Known for his villainous persona, he won numerous championships, including the NWA World Tag Team Championship. After retiring in 2013, Koloff became a born-again Christian and an ordained minister, sharing his testimony and writing books about his life and career. He passed away from liver cancer in 2017 at the age of 74.

trgt: The ZBD-03 or Type 03 (industrial designation WZ506) is a Chinese airborne infantry fighting vehicle. It features a light-weight chassis and hydropneumatic suspension for airborne operations. Early prototypes received the designation ZLC-2000.
mgt: The ZBD-03 is a Chinese airborne infantry fighting vehicle (IFV) developed to replace the BMD-3 and has been in service since 2002. Designed for air-dropping from aircraft like the Xi'an Y-20, it features a lightweight, amphibious design with hydropneumatic suspension and a Dongfeng diesel engine. Armed with a 30mm autocannon, HJ-73C anti-tank guided missiles, and a coaxial machine gun, it also includes a computerized fire control system. Despite its limited armor for air-drop compatibility, the ZBD-03 offers mobility, versatility, and firepower for rapid deployment operations.

trgt: The foramen rotundum is a circular hole in the sphenoid bone of the skull. It connects the middle cranial fossa and the pterygopalatine fossa. It allows for the passage of the maxillary nerve (V2), a branch of the trigeminal nerve.
mgt: The foramen rotundum is a circular opening in the sphenoid bone at the base of the skull. It allows passage for the maxillary nerve, an artery, and a vein. Its shape evolves from oval in the fetal period to round after birth.

trgt: Mohammed Ilyas Cherchar (born January 18, 1992) is an Algerian football player who plays for RC Arbaâ in the Algerian Ligue Professionnelle 1.
mgt: Mohammed Ilyas Cherchar (born January 18, 1992) is an Algerian footballer who plays as a defender for RC Arbaâ. A product of ASO Chlef's youth system, he also represented Algeria at the 2009 FIFA U-17 World Cup, captaining the team in their opening match.

trgt: HD 37974 (or R 126) a variable B[e] hypergiant in the Large Magellanic Cloud. It is surrounded by an unexpected dust disk.
mgt: HD 37974, also known as R126, is a massive, luminous blue supergiant star located in the Large Magellanic Cloud. Approximately 70 times the mass of the Sun and over a million times more luminous, it exhibits the B[e] phenomenon, characterized by forbidden emission lines and a dusty circumstellar disc. The star is losing mass at an extraordinary rate through powerful stellar winds and is expected to evolve into a Wolf–Rayet star. Its dusty disc, extending far beyond Pluto's orbit, challenges theories about planet formation around massive stars. R126 is also a variable star, displaying irregular brightness changes typical of α Cygni and S Doradus variables.

trgt: Robert Neelly Bellah (February 23, 1927 – July 30, 2013) was an American sociologist and the Elliott Professor of Sociology at the University of California, Berkeley. He was internationally known for his work related to the sociology of religion.
mgt: Robert N. Bellah (February 23, 1927 – July 30, 2013) was an American sociologist and influential scholar of religion, best known for coining the term "American civil religion" and for his works *Habits of the Heart* (1985) and *Religion in Human Evolution* (2011). A Harvard graduate and professor at the University of California, Berkeley, Bellah explored the intersection of religion, morality, and society, earning numerous accolades, including the National Humanities Medal. His career was marked by significant contributions to the sociology of religion and communitarian thought.

trgt: New Berlin is a borough in Union County, Pennsylvania, United States. The population was 791 at the 2020 census.
mgt: New Berlin is a small borough in Union County, Pennsylvania, United States, settled in 1792 and incorporated in 1795.

page_title: Christians, awake, salute the happy morn
trgt: "Christians, awake, salute the happy morn" is an English Christmas hymn on a text by John Byrom. It is usually sung to the tune "Yorkshire" by John Wainright.
mgt: "Christians, awake, salute the happy morn" is an English Christmas hymn written around 1750, adapted from a poem by John Byrom. It recounts the Nativity story from Luke 2 and is traditionally sung to the tune "Yorkshire," composed by John Wainwright. First published in 1766, the hymn gained popularity in England and America, appearing in major hymnals by the 19th century.

trgt: The 2022–23 Montreal Canadiens season is the franchise's 114th season since its establishment. The team's 106th season in the National Hockey League.
mgt: The 2022–23 Montreal Canadiens season was the franchise's 114th season, competing in the NHL's Atlantic Division under head coach Martin St. Louis.

trgt: Vijenac (English: The Wreath) is a biweekly magazine for literature, art and science, established in December 1993 and published by Matica hrvatska, the central national cultural institution in Croatia.
mgt: *Vijenac* is a Croatian biweekly magazine focused on literature, arts, and science, published by Matica hrvatska since 1993. It is considered the successor to the 19th-century literary magazine *Vienac*.

trgt: Stanton Peele (born January 8, 1946) is a psychologist, attorney, psychotherapist and the author of books and articles on the subject of alcoholism, addiction and addiction treatment.
mgt: Stanton Peele is an American psychologist, attorney, and addiction specialist known for his critique of the disease model of addiction and his experiential approach to understanding addictive behaviors. He has authored 14 books, including *Love and Addiction* (1975) and *Diseasing of America* (1989), and developed the Life Process Program, a non-12-step addiction treatment model. Peele argues that addiction stems from life circumstances rather than being a medical condition, advocating for self-efficacy and socialization as key to overcoming addiction. His work has been influential in challenging traditional addiction treatment paradigms.

trgt: Kleiner Tiergarten ("Small Tiergarten") is a park in Moabit, Berlin, Germany.
mgt: Kleiner Tiergarten is a historic urban park in Moabit, Berlin.

trgt: The Man Who Cheated Himself is a 1950 American crime film noir directed by Felix E. Feist, and starring Lee J. Cobb, Jane Wyatt and John Dall.
mgt: *The Man Who Cheated Himself* is a 1950 American film noir directed by Felix E. Feist and starring Lee J. Cobb, Jane Wyatt, and John Dall. The story follows a homicide detective who becomes entangled in a murder cover-up involving his lover, a wealthy socialite. Shot on location in San Francisco, the film explores themes of law, crime, and moral corruption. It received positive reviews for its engaging narrative and performances, particularly Cobb's portrayal of a conflicted detective.

trgt: Andrew Keary (born 1987) is an Irish hurler who plays for Galway Championship club Killimor. He previously lined out with the Galway senior hurling team. Keary usually lines out as a forward.
mgt: Andrew Keary (Irish: Aindriú Ó Ciara; born 1987) is an Irish former hurler who played as a left wing-forward. He achieved success with Galway's minor and under-21 teams, winning multiple All-Ireland titles.

trgt: Meckenbach is an Ortsgemeinde – a municipality belonging to a Verbandsgemeinde, a kind of collective municipality – in the Bad Kreuznach district in Rhineland-Palatinate, Germany. It belongs to the Verbandsgemeinde Kirner Land, whose seat is in the town of Kirn.
mgt: Meckenbach is a municipality in the Bad Kreuznach district of Rhineland-Palatinate, Germany. Located in the North Palatine Uplands, it has a population of 364 (as of December 2022). The village has historical roots dating back to Roman times, with evidence of copper smelting and early settlement. Meckenbach features a Romanesque Evangelical church, expanded in the 18th century, and a Stumm organ from 1836. Historically, it was part of the Waldgravial Amt of Kyrburg and later under French and Prussian administration. The municipality is governed by an eight-member council and Mayor Michael Schlarb.

trgt: The struggle for women's right to vote in Mexico dates back to the nineteenth century, with the right being achieved in 1953.
mgt: Women's suffrage in Mexico was a gradual process influenced by social, political, and revolutionary movements. While the liberal Constitution of 1857 did not explicitly bar women from voting, election laws restricted suffrage to men. Advocacy for women's rights gained momentum during the late 19th century and the Mexican Revolution, with women playing significant roles as soldaderas and activists. Efforts for suffrage intensified in the 20th century, with key milestones including the 1916 Feminist Congress of Yucatán and constitutional reforms under President Lázaro Cárdenas. Women achieved full voting rights in 1953, marking a pivotal moment in Mexico's history.

trgt: Borough Green & Wrotham railway station is located in Borough Green in Kent, England. It is 29 miles 46 chains (47.6 km) down the line from London Victoria. Train services are provided by Southeastern.
mgt: Borough Green & Wrotham railway station is located in Borough Green, Tonbridge and Malling, England, and is managed by Southeastern. Opened in 1874 as Wrotham, it was renamed in 1962. The station has two platforms and serves the Maidstone Line. Historically, it included freight facilities, which were withdrawn in 1968. The station features a ticket office, a passenger-operated ticket machine, and a modern footbridge installed in 2008. Southeastern operates services to destinations including London Victoria and Ashford International.

trgt: Gutter Phenomenon is the third studio album by American metalcore band Every Time I Die.
mgt: *Gutter Phenomenon* is the third studio album by American metalcore band Every Time I Die, released on August 23, 2005, through Ferret Records. Produced by Machine, the album blends metalcore with southern rock and features guest vocals from Gerard Way of My Chemical Romance and Daryl Palumbo of Glassjaw. It received critical acclaim, with *Revolver* naming it Album of the Year, and was supported by extensive touring, including appearances at Warped Tour and international shows.

trgt: Circumstance (Lang-fa; Lang-fr) is a 2011 French-Iranian-American dramatic film written and directed by Maryam Keshavarz starring Nikohl Boosheri, Sarah Kazemy, Reza Sixo Safai and Keon Alexander. It explores homosexuality in modern Iran, among other subjects.
mgt: *Circumstance* (Persian: شرایط) is a 2011 drama film directed by Maryam Keshavarz, exploring themes of youth, rebellion, and forbidden love in contemporary Iran. Shot in Lebanon, the film follows two teenage girls navigating societal restrictions and personal desires. It received critical acclaim, winning the Audience Award at the Sundance Film Festival and other accolades, but was banned in Iran for its portrayal of Iranian society.

trgt: Air Commodore Patrick Desmond Callaghan (16 July 1926 – 6 April 1992) was a one-star air officer in the Pakistan Air Force who is credited for his pioneering work in Flight safety in Pakistan.
mgt: Patrick Desmond Callaghan (16 July 1926 – 6 April 1992) was an Air Commodore in the Pakistan Air Force (PAF) and a pioneer officer who served from 1941 to 1971. Initially commissioned into the Royal Indian Air Force, he opted for the PAF after the partition of India. Callaghan played key roles as a flight instructor, combat pilot during the Indo-Pakistani wars of 1965 and 1971, and Chief Inspector of Aviation Safety. After retiring, he became an internationally recognized expert in air crash investigations, assisting several countries.

trgt: Pierre Martin Victor Richard de Laprade (13 January 1812 – 13 December 1883), known as Victor de Laprade, was a French poet and critic.
mgt: Victor de Laprade (1812–1883) was a French poet, critic, and politician known for his idealistic poetry inspired by nature and French provincial life.

trgt: Dolní Bečva is a municipality and village in Vsetín District in the Zlín Region of the Czech Republic. It has about 1,900 inhabitants.
mgt: Dolní Bečva is a municipality in the Zlín Region of the Czech Republic, first mentioned in 1597. It lies in the Moravian-Silesian Beskids.

page_title: 1975 Council of Ministers of East Timor
trgt: The 1975 Council of Ministers (Lang-pt, Lang-tet) was the Council of Ministers formed by the Fretilin political party in 1975 as the inaugural administration or cabinet of the Democratic Republic of East Timor proclaimed in November 1975.
mgt: The 1975 Council of Ministers of East Timor was the inaugural cabinet of the Democratic Republic of East Timor, formed by Fretilin on 1 December 1975 following its unilateral declaration of independence from Portuguese rule. Led by President Francisco Xavier do Amaral and Prime Minister Nicolau dos Reis Lobato, the council was short-lived, as Indonesia launched a full-scale invasion on 7 December 1975, leading to the establishment of a Provisional Government under Indonesian control.

trgt: James Raymond Jordan Sr. (July 31, 1936 – July 23, 1993) was the father of former basketball player Michael Jordan, and was the grandfather of former basketball players Jeffrey Jordan and Marcus Jordan.
mgt: James R. Jordan Sr. (July 31, 1936 – July 23, 1993) was an American mechanic and the father of basketball legend Michael Jordan. A lifelong basketball and baseball fan, he played a significant role in inspiring his son's athletic career. Jordan Sr. was tragically murdered in 1993 during a robbery while resting in his car. His death led to the conviction of two men, though questions about the case have persisted. He is remembered for his influence on Michael Jordan's life and career.

trgt: Ethelbert Watts (February 25, 1846 – July 13, 1919) a United States diplomat for over twenty-four years, played important roles in the Spanish–American War, Russo-Japanese War, and World War I.
mgt: Ethelbert Watts (February 25, 1846 – July 13, 1919) was an American diplomat and Civil War veteran. He served as U.S. consul-general in several countries, notably aiding American and allied interests during the Spanish–American War, Russo-Japanese War, and World War I.

trgt: Lugazi University (LU), was a private university in Uganda. The university was founded in 2007. It closed in December 2010.
mgt: Lugazi University was a private university in Lugazi, Uganda, active from 2007 to 2010, offering degrees, diplomas, and certificates.

trgt: Vladimir Lenin monument in Azov (Lang-ru) is a monument dedicated the founder of USSR Vladimir Lenin which is situated in the city of Azov, Rostov Oblast, Russia. It is officially declared as an object of cultural heritage of regional importance.
mgt: The Vladimir Lenin monument in Azov, located in Rostov Oblast, Russia, was unveiled on April 30, 1978, to commemorate Lenin's 108th birthday. Designed by sculptor Yuri Orekhov and architects Vladimir Peterburzhtsev and Alexander Stepanov, the 10.8-meter-tall monument features a half-figure of Lenin carved from Azerbaijani granite, an uncommon design for Lenin monuments. The surrounding square was renovated during its construction under the direction of architect Vladimir Fomenko.

trgt: Boulos Fahmy Eskandar (b. 1 January 1957) is a Egyptian lawyer and the current President of the Supreme Constitutional Court of Egypt.
mgt: Boulos Fahmy is an Egyptian jurist and Chief Justice of the Supreme Constitutional Court, appointed on February 9, 2022. A graduate of Cairo University, he has held various judicial roles, including vice-president of the Supreme Constitutional Court and head of the Cairo Court of Appeal. Fahmy is the first Coptic Christian to lead the court in modern Egyptian history.

trgt: Timothy John Greene (born 1969 in Cape Town, South Africa) is a South African actor, film director, and writer. He is also known as Tim Greene and cited under that name in most articles about him.
mgt: Tim Greene (born 1969) is a South African actor, film director, and writer, best known for his debut feature film *Boy Called Twist*. The film pioneered innovative crowdfunding in South African cinema and premiered in 2004.

trgt: Lectionary 34, designated by siglum l 34 (in the Gregory-Aland numbering). It is a Greek manuscript of the New Testament, on parchment leaves. Palaeographically it has been assigned to the 9th century. The manuscript is lacunose.
mgt: Lectionary 34, designated as Codex Monacensis 329, is a 9th-century Greek manuscript of the New Testament, containing lessons from the Gospels. Elegantly written in uncial script, it is housed in the Bavarian State Library.

trgt: Prakash Vir Shastri (30 December 1923 – 23 November 1977) was a Member of the Parliament of India (Sansad) and was also a leader in the Arya Samaj movement.
mgt: Prakash Vir Shastri (30 December 1923 – 23 November 1977) was an Indian politician, scholar, and orator. Serving as an Independent member of both the Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha from 1958 to 1977, he was also a prominent advocate for Hindi as India's national language and a devotee of the Arya Samaj movement. Shastri proposed the Religious Protection Bill in 1960 to safeguard religious minorities. He was Vice-Chancellor of Gurukul Vrindavan and earned recognition for his speeches, later compiled posthumously. Shastri died in a train accident in 1977.

trgt: Eric Mattson is an American firefighter and politician serving as a Democratic member of the Illinois State Senate for the 43rd District since 2022.
mgt: Eric Mattson is an American politician and firefighter serving as a Democratic member of the Illinois Senate from the 43rd district since May 2022.

trgt: Dragan Aleksić (Serbian Cyrillic: Драган Алексић; born 1 May 1970), also credited as Dragan Alekszics in Hungary, is a Serbian professional basketball coach and former player. He currently serves as the head coach for PVSK-Veolia of the Hungarian League.
mgt: Dragan Aleksić (born May 1, 1970) is a Serbian professional basketball coach and former player, currently serving as the head coach of Pécsi VSK-Veolia in the Hungarian League. A 1.95 m (6 ft 5 in) shooting guard, Aleksić had a 21-year playing career, including stints with Crvena zvezda, Spartak Subotica, and Szolnoki Olaj, among others. Notably, he set an ABA League career-high of 41 points in 2001. Transitioning to coaching in 2012, he has led teams such as Szolnoki Olaj and Soproni, and also served as a technical director for SCM U Craiova in 2016.

trgt: David Production Inc. (Japanese: 株式会社デイヴィッドプロダクション, Hepburn: Kabushiki-gaisha Deividdo Purodakushon) is a Japanese animation studio founded in September 2007 and is based in Nishitōkyō, Tokyo. Notable works from the studio include JoJo's Bizarre Adventure, Cells at Work!, and Fire Force.
mgt: David Production Co., Ltd. (株式会社デイヴィッドプロダクション) is a Japanese animation studio founded in September 2007 by Kōji Kajita and Taito Okiura. Based in Nishitōkyō, Tokyo, it became a subsidiary of Fuji TV in 2014.

trgt: Raymond Cannon (born Ulises Tildmann Cannon;: 270 September 1, 1892 – June 7, 1977) was an American actor, film director, screenwriter, journalist, and author known for his work with D. W. Griffith and Buster Keaton.
mgt: Raymond Cannon (born Ulises Tillman Cannon; September 1, 1892 – June 7, 1977) was an American actor, screenwriter, director, journalist, and author. Active in film from 1913 to 1945, he worked with notable figures like D.W. Griffith and Buster Keaton, transitioning from acting to screenwriting and directing. Later, he became a prominent sport fishing writer, authoring books and a long-running column.

trgt: Eric Reed (born 1963 or 1964) is an American horse trainer. He is the trainer of Rich Strike, the winner of the 2022 Kentucky Derby.
mgt: Eric Reed (born 1963 or 1964) is an American horse trainer and owner of Mercury Equine Center. He trained Rich Strike, the 80-1 underdog winner of the 2022 Kentucky Derby.

trgt: Nashipur is a village in the Murshidabad-Jiaganj CD block in the Lalbag subdivision of Murshidabad district in West Bengal, India.
mgt: Nashipur is a village in Murshidabad district, West Bengal, India, known for the Nashipur Rajbari, Nashipur Akhara, and historical landmarks.

trgt: Bethuel Muzeu (born 2 February 2000), also known as Muzeu Muzeu, is a Namibian footballer who plays for National First Division club Black Leopards and the Namibia national team.
mgt: Bethuel Muzeu (born 22 February 2000) is a Namibian footballer who plays as a forward for Black Leopards in South Africa and the Namibia national team. Known for his goal-scoring ability, he gained prominence at the 2022 COSAFA Cup, where he scored the decisive goal to send Namibia to the final. Muzeu began his career with Tura Magic in the Namibia Premier Football League before joining Black Leopards in 2022. He has also represented Namibia at youth and senior international levels.

trgt: Alvarado is one of the 212 municipalities of the Mexican state of Veracruz. The municipality is part of the state's Papaloapan Region and its municipal seat is established at the homonymous city of Alvarado.
mgt: Alvarado is a municipality in the state of Veracruz, Mexico, located in the Papaloapan region. Established in 1818, it spans 826.9 km² and includes 265 settlements, with its municipal seat in the city of Alvarado. The area is characterized by its coastal geography, including the Alvarado Lagoon complex formed by several lagoons and crossed by the Papaloapan and Blanco rivers. The municipality experiences a hot, sub-humid climate with a rainy season in summer. Governed by a municipal council, Alvarado is connected by federal highways and local roads, serving as a key administrative and geographical area in Veracruz.

trgt: Ari is a given name in many languages and cultures, for both men and women. It also may be a nickname for a wide variety of unrelated names.
mgt: Ari is a given name with diverse origins and meanings across cultures. In Hebrew, it means "lion," while in Scandinavian languages, it signifies "eagle." In Greek, it is often a shortened form of names derived from *áristos* ("best" or "superior") or associated with Ares, the god of war. In the Badaga language, Ari means "sun-like," and in Finnic languages, it is linked to Adrian.

trgt: Daniel P. Kristiansen (born December 13, 1962) is a former American politician of the Republican Party. He was a member of the Washington House of Representatives, representing the 39th district, and the former House Minority Leader.
mgt: Dan Kristiansen (born December 13, 1962) is an American politician who served as a Republican member of the Washington House of Representatives from 2003 to 2019, including as Minority Leader from 2014 to 2018. His tenure included controversies over campaign finance violations, false statements regarding gun violence, and ties to the NRA. Kristiansen announced his retirement in 2018, completing his term but not seeking re-election.

trgt: Colegio San Benito ("CSB") is a private Roman Catholic, university-preparatory and elementary school, founded in Mayagüez, Puerto Rico in 1965. The school was the first boys-only Catholic school founded in the area of Mayagüez.
mgt: Colegio San Benito is a private, bilingual school in Mayagüez, Puerto Rico, established in 1965. Serving grades PK-12, it features extensive facilities, strong academic performance, and notable basketball and volleyball programs.

trgt: Essert-Pittet (IPA-fr) is a former municipality in the district of Jura-Nord Vaudois of the canton of Vaud in Switzerland. In 2017 the former municipalities of Essert-Pittet and Corcelles-sur-Chavornay merged into the municipality of Chavornay.
mgt: Essert-Pittet is a former municipality in the canton of Vaud, Switzerland, located in the district of Jura-Nord Vaudois. First mentioned in 1100, it covered an area of 2.8 km², primarily used for agriculture. With a population of 164 in 2015, the municipality featured a mix of residential and agricultural life. Essert-Pittet was part of the Yverdon District until 2006, when it joined Jura-Nord Vaudois. The local economy included agriculture, construction, and education, while the population predominantly spoke French and adhered to the Swiss Reformed Church.

trgt: Empire Township is a civil township of Leelanau County in the U.S. state of Michigan. The population was 1,085 at the 2000 census. The village of Empire is located within the township.
mgt: Empire Township is a civil township in Leelanau County, Michigan, United States. Covering 42.8 square miles, it includes the village of Empire and features a mix of land and water. As of the 2000 census, the township had a population of 1,085. Established in 1864 with the founding of Burdickville, the area has a history tied to early milling industries. Today, it is known for its rural character and proximity to natural attractions.

trgt: Tech4 Carmen Contreras-Bozak, (December 31, 1919 – January 30, 2017) was the first Puerto Rican woman to serve in the U.S. Women's Army Corps (WAC) where she served as an interpreter and in numerous administrative positions.
mgt: Carmen Contreras Bozak (December 31, 1919 – January 30, 2017) was a Puerto Rican U.S. Army technician and the first Hispanic woman to serve in the Women's Army Corps during World War II. She transmitted encoded messages in General Dwight D. Eisenhower's headquarters in North Africa. After the war, she became a community leader, founding chapters of WAC Veterans and the Society of Military Widows. Contreras Bozak was buried in Arlington National Cemetery.

trgt: Gamawan Fauzi (born 9 November 1957) is an Indonesian politician.
mgt: Gamawan Fauzi is an Indonesian politician and former minister.

trgt: Valentina Ivanovna Gagarina (Lang-ru; December 15, 1935 – March 17, 2020), née Goryacheva, was the wife of the first cosmonaut Yuri Gagarin.
mgt: Valentina Ivanovna Gagarina (née Goryacheva; December 15, 1935 – March 17, 2020) was the wife of Yuri Gagarin, the first human in space. A graduate of the Orenburg Medical School, she worked in the Medical Control Laboratory of the Mission Control Center. Following her husband's historic spaceflight in 1961, she gained public attention alongside him. After Yuri Gagarin's death in 1968, she never remarried and dedicated her life to preserving his legacy, donating his personal belongings to museums and writing memoirs, including *108 Minutes and the Whole Life*. She lived in Zvezdny Gorodok and participated in events honoring her late husband until her death in 2020.

trgt: The Wills Wing U2 is an American high-wing, single-place, hang glider, designed and produced by Wills Wing of Orange, California since 2003. The aircraft is supplied complete and ready-to-fly.
mgt: The Wills Wing U2 is a recreational intermediate hang glider designed for high performance, approaching that of competition gliders. It features a variable geometry system and Dacron sailcloth wings.

trgt: Mirage: Arcane Warfare was a multiplayer action game developed by Torn Banner Studios. The game launched on Windows PCs in May 2017, but was shut down one year later.
mgt: *Mirage: Arcane Warfare* is a multiplayer action game developed and published by Torn Banner Studios, released for Windows on May 23, 2017. Built on Unreal Engine 4, the game featured melee-focused combat with magical abilities and various game modes. Despite receiving mixed reviews for its gameplay and design, it struggled commercially due to low player activity. Torn Banner supported the game with updates but ultimately removed it from sale and shut down its servers in May 2018.

trgt: Svein Finnerud (2 September 1945, Oslo – 22 June 2000) was a Norwegian jazz pianist, painter and graphic artist.
mgt: Svein Finnerud (2 June 1945 – 22 June 2000) was a Norwegian visual artist and jazz musician, known for pioneering free jazz with the Finnerud Trio and integrating multimedia art forms.

trgt: Domingos Sousa Coutinho Meneses Duarte (born 10 March 1995) is a Portuguese professional footballer who plays as a central defender for La Liga club Getafe and the Portugal national team.
mgt: Domingos Sousa Coutinho Meneses Duarte (born 10 March 1995) is a Portuguese professional footballer who plays as a centre-back for La Liga club Getafe and the Portugal national team. A product of Sporting CP's youth academy, he began his senior career with their reserve team before loan spells at Belenenses, Chaves, and Deportivo La Coruña. Duarte later joined Granada in 2019, where he played in La Liga and the UEFA Europa League, before signing with Getafe in 2022. Internationally, he debuted for Portugal in 2020 and was part of the preliminary squad for the 2022 FIFA World Cup.

trgt: Kaberamaido is a town in Eastern Uganda. It is the main municipal, administrative and commercial center of Kaberamaido District and is the location of the headquarters for the district.
mgt: Kaberamaido is a town in Eastern Uganda, located in the Teso sub-region and serving as the administrative center of Kaberamaido District. Situated at an elevation of 1,080 meters (3,540 feet), it lies approximately 163 kilometers northwest of Mbale and 64 kilometers west of Soroti. As of 2011, the town's population was estimated at 3,400.

trgt: Jean-Philippe Mateta (born 28 June 1997) is a French professional footballer who plays as a striker for Premier League club Crystal Palace. He has represented France internationally at U19 and U21 youth levels.
mgt: Jean-Philippe Mateta (born 28 June 1997) is a French professional footballer who plays as a striker for Premier League club Crystal Palace. He previously played for Lyon, Le Havre, and Mainz 05.

trgt: Air Vice-Marshal Paul Douglas Luker, CB, OBE, AFC, DL is a former Royal Air Force officer who served as Commander of Joint Helicopter Command from 2002 to 2005.
mgt: Paul Luker (born 8 July 1951) is a retired Royal Air Force air vice-marshal who served from 1968 to 2006, commanding the Joint Helicopter Command and participating in conflicts including the Gulf War, Bosnian War, and War in Afghanistan. After retiring, he held leadership roles in reserve forces organizations and charities, including serving as Chairman of Blind Veterans UK and as a Deputy Lieutenant for Hampshire.

trgt: Lincoln is an unincorporated community and census-designated place (CDP) in Lewis and Clark County, Montana, United States. As of the 2010 census, the population was 1,013.
mgt: Lincoln is a census-designated place in Lewis and Clark County, Montana, known for its historical significance, outdoor recreation, and proximity to wilderness areas. Established during the gold rush of the mid-19th century, it later became a commercial center for logging and mining. Lincoln gained national attention in 1996 with the arrest of Ted Kaczynski, the "Unabomber," near the town. It was also the site of a 5.8-magnitude earthquake in 2017. Located along Montana Highway 200, Lincoln features a humid continental climate and is near Rogers Pass, where the coldest temperature in the contiguous United States was recorded. The town is served by Lincoln Public School District and has a public library.

trgt: General Sir Daril Gerard Watson GCB CBE MC (17 October 1888 − 1 July 1967) was a senior British Army officer who saw service during both World War I and World War II.
mgt: Lieutenant General Sir Daril Watson (17 October 1888 – 1 July 1967) was a senior British Army officer who served in both World Wars. He held key commands, including GOC Western Command and Quartermaster-General.

trgt: Lilian Staple Mead (30 June 1865 – unknown) was an Australian suffragette and children's book author. She was the only female student ever educated at Adelaide's Prince Alfred College.
mgt: Lilian Staple Mead (1867–1946) was an Australian suffragist, author, and advocate for women's education and social reform. She signed the 1894 Women's Suffrage Petition and authored children's fiction.

trgt: Matthijs van Nieuwkerk (IPA-nl; born 8 September 1960) is a Dutch journalist and television presenter. He is best known for hosting the daily De Wereld Draait Door (DWDD) television show from 2005 to 2020.
mgt: Matthijs van Nieuwkerk (born 8 September 1960) is a Dutch journalist and television presenter, best known for hosting the popular talk show *De Wereld Draait Door* from 2005 to 2020. He began his career in journalism at *Het Parool*, where he became editor-in-chief in 1996, before transitioning to television as editor-in-chief of AT5 and later as a presenter for various national programs. Van Nieuwkerk also co-created and hosted shows like *Holland Sport*, *Popquiz*, and *Top 2000 à Go-Go*. In 2022, he faced accusations of workplace bullying during his time on *De Wereld Draait Door*. Beyond television, he has contributed to football magazine *Hard Gras* and written columns and books. Van Nieuwkerk divorced in 2021 and has two children.

trgt: Mouse-Warming is a 1952 Warner Bros. Looney Tunes short directed by Chuck Jones. The cartoon was released on September 8, 1952 and stars Claude Cat in his final solo cartoon.
mgt: *Mouse-Warming* is a 1952 Warner Bros. Looney Tunes animated short directed by Chuck Jones. The cartoon follows a young mouse's comedic attempts to woo a girl mouse while evading the scheming Claude Cat.

trgt: Indore Fashion Week (IFW) is an annual fashion event that takes place in Indore. It is considered a premier fashion event in central India. It is run by Storm Fashion Company (SFC) and the title sponsor is Blenders Pride.
mgt: The Blenders Pride Indore Fashion Week (BPIFW) is an annual fashion event held in Indore, India, first inaugurated in March 2012. Organized by Storm Fashion Company, it showcases collections from prominent and emerging designers across India and internationally.

trgt: Arthur is a town in Chippewa County in the U.S. state of Wisconsin. The population was 759 at the 2010 census. The unincorporated communities of Cobban, Crescent, and Drywood are located in the town.
mgt: Arthur is a town in Chippewa County, Wisconsin, United States, with a population of 759 as of the 2010 census. Founded in 1885 and named after President Chester A. Arthur, it spans 43.1 square miles, bordered by the Chippewa River. The town is served by the School District of Cadott Community and the Cornell School District.

trgt: Shigeichi Nagano (長野 重一, Nagano Shigeichi, March 30, 1925 – January 30, 2019) was a Japanese photographer. He won the Ina Nobuo Award in 1986 and had a major retrospective at the Tokyo Metropolitan Museum of Photography in 2000.
mgt: Shigeichi Nagano (1925 – January 30, 2019) was a Japanese photographer known for documenting Japan's postwar economic growth and urban life, particularly Tokyo's salarymen. He worked in publishing, film, and television, earning numerous awards and retrospectives.

page_title: Lagoa do Parado Municipal Nature Park
trgt: The Lagoa do Parado Municipal Nature Park (Lang-pt) is a municipal nature park in the state of Paraná, Brazil. It protects an area of marshland rich in biodiversity.
mgt: The Lagoa do Parado Municipal Nature Park is a protected area in Guaratuba, Paraná, Brazil, known for its rich biodiversity, including endangered species, and its role in marine life breeding.

trgt: Sophie Cook (born 4 January 1967) is a British author, broadcaster, photographer and politician.
mgt: Sophie Cook (born 4 January 1967) is a British transgender woman, author, broadcaster, mental health advocate, and former Labour political candidate.

trgt: Terry George (born 14 March 1965) is a British businessman known primarily for his appearances in television programmes. He was born and raised in Leeds, West Yorkshire.
mgt: Terry George (born 14 March 1965) is a British entrepreneur and nightclub owner from Leeds. He gained prominence through his gay nightclubs, memorial website gonetoosoon.org, and television appearances.

trgt: A number of locally-specific units of measurement were used in the Czech lands to measure length, area, capacity and so on. In 1876, the metric system was made compulsory; however, local measurements and old Viennese measurements were still in use.
mgt: The Czech units of measurement were traditional systems used in Bohemia, Moravia, Silesia, and Prague before the adoption of the metric system. These included units for length, such as the *stopa* and *loket*, and for area and capacity, like the *měřice* and *korec*.

trgt: The 1872–73 season was the second season of competitive football by Queen's Park.
mgt: The 1872–73 season saw Queen's Park F.C. reach the FA Cup semi-finals.

page_title: Hockey at the 2010 Commonwealth Games – Women's team squads
trgt: This article lists the squads of the women's hockey competition at the 2010 Commonwealth Games held in New Delhi, India from 4 to 13 October 2010.
mgt: The women's hockey tournament at the 2010 Commonwealth Games featured teams from 10 nations, each led by a head coach, with squad announcements made prior to the event.

trgt: Elizabeth Phillips Hughes MBE (12 July 1851 – 19 December 1925) was a Welsh scholar, teacher, and promoter of women's education, first principal of the Cambridge Training College for Women.
mgt: Elizabeth Phillips Hughes (12 July 1851 – 19 December 1925) was a British educator, college administrator, and advocate for women's education. She was the first principal of Cambridge Training College for Women, later renamed Hughes Hall in her honor. Hughes was the first woman at Cambridge University to achieve first-class honors in Moral Sciences. She contributed to education reform in Wales, promoted international educational initiatives, and served as a visiting professor in Japan. During World War I, she managed a Red Cross hospital and was awarded an MBE for her service.

trgt: Eileen Percy (August 1902 – 29 July 1973) was an Irish-born American actress of the silent era. She appeared in more than 60 films between 1917 and 1933.
mgt: Eileen Percy (August 1902 – July 29, 1973) was an Irish-born American actress active in silent films from 1917 to 1933. After retiring, she became a journalist.

trgt: Air Marshal Sir Peter Edward Bairsto, KBE, CB, AFC, DL (3 August 1926 – 24 October 2017) was a Royal Air Force officer who served as Deputy Commander of Strike Command from 1981 until his retirement in 1984.
mgt: Air Marshal Sir Peter Edward Bairsto (3 August 1926 – 24 October 2017) was a senior Royal Air Force officer who served from 1944 to 1984, including as Deputy Commander of Strike Command. He was a decorated veteran of the Second World War, receiving honors such as the Air Force Cross and being appointed Knight Commander of the Order of the British Empire. After retiring, he became an advisor to the Ferranti Group and served as Deputy Lieutenant of Fife.

trgt: London Bourne (1793–1869) was a former Barbadian slave who became a wealthy merchant and abolitionist.
mgt: London Bourne (1793–1869) was a Barbadian businessman, former slave, and civil rights advocate.

trgt:
Download coordinates as:

Pelican Waters is a suburb of Caloundra in the Sunshine Coast Region, Queensland, Australia. In the 2016 census, Pelican Waters had a population of 6,277 people.
mgt: Pelican Waters is a suburb of Caloundra in the Sunshine Coast Region, Queensland, Australia, established in 1995. Known for its canal estates, it had a population of 6,277 in the 2016 census.

trgt: "Dance Macabre" is a song by Swedish rock band Ghost. It was their second single off of their fourth studio album Prequelle. It topped the Billboard Mainstream Rock Songs chart for two weeks in February 2019.
mgt: "Dance Macabre" is a song by the Swedish rock band Ghost, released on May 18, 2018, as the second single from their fourth studio album, *Prequelle*. Written by Tobias Forge, Salem Al Fakir, and Vincent Pontare, the song blends hard rock with dance music influences and explores themes of the Black Plague. A disco remix by Carpenter Brut was released in August 2018. The track topped the Billboard Mainstream Rock Songs chart in 2019 and was praised as a standout on the album.

trgt: The Light Helicopter Experimental (LHX) program was a 1980s United States Army helicopter procurement project to replace the AH-1 Cobra and OH-58 Kiowa helicopters.
mgt: The Light Helicopter Experimental (LHX) program was a U.S. Army initiative launched in 1982 to develop a next-generation helicopter, ultimately resulting in the RAH-66 Comanche. Initially intended to replace multiple helicopter models, the program faced challenges including shifting requirements, technological risks, and rising costs. After selecting the Boeing-Sikorsky design in 1991, the program was terminated in 2004 due to budget constraints, evolving mission priorities, and unachievable requirements.

trgt: Paul Alden Brinegar Jr. (December 19, 1917 – March 27, 1995) was an American character actor best known for his roles in three Western series: The Life and Legend of Wyatt Earp, Rawhide, and Lancer.
mgt: Paul Alden Brinegar Jr. (December 19, 1917 – March 27, 1995) was an American character actor best known for his roles in Westerns. He appeared in over 100 Western films and television shows between 1946 and 1994, often portraying grizzled cowboy sidekicks. Brinegar is most remembered for his role as George Washington Wishbone, the cook on the CBS series *Rawhide* (1959–1966), and as Jelly Hoskins on *Lancer* (1968–1970). He also appeared in films such as *High Plains Drifter* (1973) and *Maverick* (1994). Brinegar began acting after serving in the U.S. Navy during World War II and pursued a long career in both film and television. He died of emphysema in Los Angeles at the age of 77.

trgt: Cipher runes, or cryptic runes, are the cryptographical replacement of the letters of the runic alphabet.
mgt: Cipher runes are cryptographic variations of runic alphabets, primarily preserved in Icelandic manuscripts from the 17th–18th centuries.

trgt: Laura Rajnović Evetović (Serbian Cyrillic: Лаура Рајновић Еветовић; born 1 June 1975) is a politician and administrator in Serbia. She served in the Assembly of Vojvodina from 2012 to 2020.
mgt: Laura Rajnović Evetović is a Serbian economist, politician, and chief of Subotica's communal police. She served in the Subotica city assembly and the Assembly of Vojvodina as a member of the Serbian Progressive Party.

trgt: Roberto Juan Poljak (17 September 1932, Buenos Aires; – 30 May 2019, Baltimore County) was an Argentine biophysicist and immunologist.
mgt: Roberto Poljak (1932–2019) was an Argentine biophysicist known for determining the first 3D structures of antibodies and antigen-antibody complexes.

trgt: Ä (lower case ä) is a character that represents either a letter from several extended Latin alphabets, or the letter A with an umlaut mark or diaeresis.
mgt: The letter Ä is used in various alphabets, including Finnish, Swedish, German, and Estonian, representing vowel sounds like /æ/ or [ɛ]. Originating from the ligature Æ, it evolved into two dots above "A" in German and Swedish. Ä is also used in Kazakh, Turkmen, and Cyrillic-based alphabets, and historically in Germanic umlaut contexts. It appears in languages influenced by German or Swedish and is sometimes replaced by "Ae" in limited character sets.

trgt: Marguerite Louppe (1902–1989) was a French painter, best known for her still lifes and landscapes. Her work synthesized the styles of Cubism and Purism. Louppe was married to the French artist Maurice Brianchon.
mgt: Marguerite Louppe (1902–1989) was a French painter known for her exploration of Purism, Cubism, and geometric abstraction. Her work evolved from figurative depictions of Parisian life to still lifes and landscapes, emphasizing mathematical composition and color mastery. Louppe studied at prominent Parisian art academies and exhibited widely, often alongside her husband, Maurice Brianchon. The couple's home in Périgord became a significant influence on her later work, where she focused on intimate, interior scenes and abstracted landscapes.

trgt: Lieutenant-General Sir Richard Maurice Hilton Vickers KCB CVO OBE (born 21 August 1928) is a former British Army officer who served as Director-General of Army Training from 1982 until 1983.
mgt: Sir Richard Vickers (born 21 August 1928) is a retired British Army lieutenant-general who served from 1948 to 1983, commanding key units and holding senior leadership roles, including Commandant of the Royal Military Academy Sandhurst.

trgt: Hasan Zati Sungur (March 10, 1898 – July 6, 1984) was an internationally recognized Turkish stage magician.
mgt: Hasan Zati Sungur (March 10, 1898 – July 6, 1984) was a renowned Turkish stage magician, inventor, and performer.

trgt: Hudson Township is a civil township of Charlevoix County in the U.S. state of Michigan. The population was 691 at the 2010 census.
mgt: Hudson Township is a civil township in Charlevoix County, Michigan, United States. Covering 35.47 square miles, it had a population of 691 in 2010.

trgt: Other Voices is a Canadian current affairs television series which aired on CBC Television from 1964 to 1965.
mgt: *Other Voices* was a Canadian current affairs television series hosted by Don Francks, airing on CBC Television from 1964 to 1965. The show highlighted underrepresented groups, including First Nations people, British youth subcultures, and homosexuals, while incorporating dramatic, musical, and satirical sketches. It featured notable figures such as Wyndham Lewis, Charles Mingus, and Marshall McLuhan. Despite its attempt at controversy, the series faced criticism for its handling of certain topics.

trgt: George Hall (September 21, 1795 - April 16, 1868) was an American businessman and politician who served as the first Mayor of Brooklyn.
mgt: George Hall (September 21, 1795 – April 16, 1868) was an American politician who served as the first mayor of Brooklyn from 1834 to 1835.

trgt: "Audi" is a song by American rapper Smokepurpp. It was released on May 17, 2017, as the lead single from his debut mixtape Deadstar. The track is considered one of his breakout singles, along with "Ski Mask".
mgt: "Audi" is a 2017 single by American rapper Smokepurpp from his mixtape *Deadstar*. Produced by Ronny J, the track gained popularity and was certified Gold by the RIAA. Its music video, released on May 18, 2017, has amassed over 60 million views as of June 2020. The song received positive reviews for its unorthodox style and thundering instrumental, with critics highlighting it as a fan favorite. A sequel, "Audi II," was released in 2019 ahead of Smokepurpp's debut studio album, *Deadstar 2*.

trgt: Franziska Junge (born 1981 in Zschopau, East Germany) is a German actress for theatre, film, television as well as a singer.
mgt: Franziska Junge (born 1981 in Zschopau, East Germany) is a German actor and singer known for her work in theatre, film, and television.

trgt: Michael N. Delagi (April 22, 1879 – October 7, 1957) was an American lawyer, politician, and judge from New York.
mgt: Michael N. Delagi (April 22, 1879 – October 7, 1957) was an American lawyer, judge, and politician who served in the New York State Assembly and as a Municipal Court Justice in the Bronx.

trgt: Violet Dandridge, was the pseudonym for Serena Katherine Dandridge (1878–1956), she was an American scientific illustrator, painter, naturalist, and suffragist. She was the Smithsonian Institution’s first female scientific illustrator.
mgt: Violet Dandridge (born Serena Katherine Dandridge; March 15, 1878 – November 7, 1956) was an American scientific illustrator, naturalist, and suffragist. She worked at the Smithsonian Institution, contributed to marine biology research, and advocated for women's suffrage, leading a suffragist parade in her hometown of Shepherdstown, West Virginia. Dandridge also protested environmental destruction and exhibited her artwork internationally. Her life was marked by periods of hospitalization, and her legacy includes archived works at Duke University and Shepherd University.

trgt: Flight Sergeant William Thomas Smith DCM, MM (21 October 1896 – October 1994) was a British World War I flying ace credited with five aerial victories.
mgt: William Thomas Smith (21 October 1896 – October 1994) was a British and Australian military serviceman who served in both World Wars. During World War I, he earned the Military Medal and Distinguished Conduct Medal for bravery and achieved six aerial victories as a gunner in the Royal Flying Corps and Royal Air Force. After emigrating to Australia, he joined the Royal Australian Air Force during World War II, serving until 1946 with the rank of Flight Sergeant.

trgt: John Armand Lafore Jr. (May 25, 1905 – January 24, 1993) was an American politician who was a Republican member of the U.S. House of Representatives from Pennsylvania. He was also president of the American Kennel Club.
mgt: John A. Lafore Jr. (May 25, 1905 – January 24, 1993) was an American politician, businessman, and dog breeder. A Republican, he served in the Pennsylvania House of Representatives (1950–1957) and the U.S. House of Representatives (1957–1961).

trgt: Lamakaan (Lang-ur) is an open cultural center in Banjara Hills, Hyderabad, India. The non-profit organisation provides space for events such as arts, literature, theater, music, and debates.
mgt: Lamakaan is a cultural center in Banjara Hills, Hyderabad, India, founded in March 2010 by Ashhar Farhan, Humera Ahmed, Biju Mathew, and Elahe Hiptoola. It hosts diverse events, including concerts, plays, book releases, and seminars, featuring prominent artists and theatre groups. In 2015, it faced a closure threat from the Greater Hyderabad Municipal Corporation (GHMC) over alleged nuisance complaints, but the notice was withdrawn following public protests and intervention by IT Minister K.T. Rama Rao.

trgt: Ilias Haddad (Lang-ar; born 1 March 1989, Dordrecht) is a Moroccan footballer who plays as a centre back for Union Touarga. Haddad holds both Dutch and Moroccan nationality.
mgt: Ilias Haddad (born 1 March 1989) is a Dutch professional footballer who plays as a centre back for Union Touarga. He has played for clubs in the Netherlands, Scotland, Bulgaria, and Morocco, including Telstar, St Mirren, CSKA Sofia, FC Dordrecht, AS FAR, and Raja Casablanca. Haddad represented the Netherlands at the under-18 and under-19 levels.

trgt: The Aguapeí State Park (Lang-pt) is a state park in the state of São Paulo, Brazil. It protects an area of floodplain in the Atlantic Forest biome, home to many water birds.
mgt: Aguapeí State Park (Portuguese: Parque Estadual do Aguapeí) is a protected area in the state of São Paulo, Brazil, covering 9,044 hectares (22,350 acres). Established on July 2, 1998, as compensation for environmental impacts from the Primavera Hydroelectric Power Plant, the park preserves floodplains, seasonal semi-deciduous forests, and várzea vegetation within the Atlantic Forest biome. It is home to diverse flora and fauna, including capybaras, tapirs, jabirus, and great egrets. Located near the confluence of the Aguapeí and Paraná Rivers, the park is part of the proposed Trinational Biodiversity Corridor. It supports conservation, education, recreation, and scientific research, with facilities such as an administrative headquarters and visitor center inaugurated in 2015.

trgt: The Great Seal of Japan (国璽, kokuji) is one of the national seals of Japan and is used as the official seal of state.
mgt: The State Seal of Japan, adopted in 1874, is a pure gold square seal inscribed with "Seal of Great Japan" in seal script. Created by master craftsman Abei Rekido, it is used for official state purposes, such as certifying Japanese orders. Historically significant, the seal featured prominently during the 2019 imperial transition ceremonies.

trgt: Martyrs Bus Service is a bus operator in Melbourne, Australia. It operates one route under contract to Public Transport Victoria. It is a subsidiary of the Dineen Group.
mgt: Martyrs Bus Service is a bus operator based in Lilydale, Victoria, serving eastern Melbourne. Established in 1927, it operates one route with a fleet of 42 buses.

trgt: Lady Mary Ilona Margaret Whitley (née Cambridge; 24 September 1924 – 13 December 1999) was a relative of the British royal family. The only child of the 2nd Marquess of Cambridge, she was a second cousin of Queen Elizabeth II.
mgt: Lady Mary Whitley (née Lady Mary Ilona Margaret Cambridge; 24 September 1924 – 13 December 1999) was a British aristocrat and a second cousin of Queen Elizabeth II. A great-great-great-granddaughter of King George III, she was closely connected to the royal family, serving as a bridesmaid at several royal weddings, including those of Princess Elizabeth (later Queen Elizabeth II) and the Duke of Kent. Educated at home, she spent her early years near Hyde Park and often played with her royal cousins. During World War II, she volunteered as a nurse in London. In 1951, she married Peter Whitley, with whom she had two children. Lady Mary regularly attended major royal events throughout her life but did not undertake royal duties.

trgt: Martin Schütz (14 November 1963 – 25 February 2018) was a Swiss theoretical chemist and quantum chemist.
mgt: Martin Schütz (November 14, 1963 – February 25, 2018) was a Swiss theoretical chemist and professor known for his contributions to quantum chemistry, particularly in developing local correlation methods for large molecular systems. After earning his doctorate at the University of Bern, he held research and academic positions at institutions including Lund University, the University of Stuttgart, the University of Regensburg, and Humboldt University of Berlin. Schütz's work focused on ab initio calculations of electronic structures, excited states, and intermolecular forces, advancing Post–Hartree–Fock methodologies. He passed away at the age of 54.

trgt: Kylie Nicole Sicangco Padilla (IPA-tl; born 25 January 1993) is a Filipino actress, singer and model. She is the daughter of actor Robin Padilla and Liezl Sicangco.
mgt: Kylie Nicole Sicangco Padilla (born January 25, 1993) is a Filipino actress, singer, and model. Known for her roles in *Encantadia* (2016) and *Joaquin Bordado*, she is a GMA Network contract star and a recipient of the 2008 PMPC Star Award for Best New Female Personality. Born to actors Robin Padilla and Liezl Sicangco, she spent her early years in Australia and practices Islam. Padilla was married to actor Aljur Abrenica, with whom she has two children, before their separation in 2021.

trgt: Jason Marcano (30 December 1983 – 30 May 2019) was a Trinidad and Tobago international footballer who played as a forward.
mgt: Jason Marcano (30 December 1983 – 30 May 2019) was a Trinidadian footballer who played as a forward for clubs including San Juan Jabloteh and Central, as well as the Trinidad and Tobago national team.

trgt: Rémire Island, or Eagle Island, is an island in Seychelles, lying in the Outer Islands of Seychelles, with a distance of 245 km south of Victoria, Seychelles.
mgt: Rémire Island, also known as Eagle Island, is a small island in the Seychelles' Outer Islands, part of the Amirante Islands group. Covering 0.3 km², it has a population of six (as of 2014) and features a coconut plantation, a guesthouse, and a small airstrip. Historically, the island was exploited for guano mining in the mid-20th century and later served as a presidential retreat. Today, it is managed by the Island Development Company (IDC) and is known for its rich marine life, seabirds, and small-scale farming and fishing activities.

trgt: Pornsanae Sitmonchai is a Thai Muay Thai fighter. He is known for his low kicks and aggressive fighting style.
mgt: Pornsanae Sitmonchai (born August 7, 1981) is a Thai Muay Thai fighter known for his aggressive style and international career.

trgt: Patrizio Bianchi (born 28 May 1952) is an Italian economist and academic. He has been minister of education in the Draghi Cabinet from 2021 to 2022.
mgt: Patrizio Bianchi (born 28 May 1952) is an Italian economist, professor, and politician who served as Minister of Education from 2021 to 2022 under Prime Minister Mario Draghi.

trgt: Paterson is a New Jersey Transit commuter railway station located on an elevated viaduct above Market Street in downtown Paterson, New Jersey. The railway through the station is double tracked, for north and south traffic on the NJT Main Line.
mgt: Paterson station is a commuter rail station in downtown Paterson, New Jersey, served by NJ Transit’s Main Line. Opened in 1832, it originally served the Erie Railroad and later its successors. The station features a high-level island platform and provides connections to Hoboken and Port Jervis. Historically, it was a hub for long-distance passenger and freight services, including routes to Chicago, but now exclusively serves commuter traffic. The station underwent significant changes, including elevation in the 1920s and realignments following the Erie-Lackawanna merger in 1960. It remains a key transit point in Paterson.

trgt: Soman is a power noise/techno act from Germany. They have had several hits in the Deutsche Alternative Charts.
mgt: Soman is an electronic music project by Kolja Trelle, known for its industrial sound and chart-topping releases.

trgt: Johan Fredrik Göstasson (Gson) Bergenstråhle (23 September 1926 – 10 February 2005) was a Swedish diplomat.
mgt: Fredrik Bergenstråhle (born 1927) was a Swedish diplomat and ambassador, serving in various international postings.

trgt: The Downs are an area of public open limestone downland in Bristol, England. They consist of Durdham Down to the north and east and Clifton Down to the south, separated by Stoke Road.
mgt: The Downs is a 412-acre public open space in Bristol, England, comprising Durdham Down and Clifton Down. Managed jointly since 1861, it is used for leisure, sports, events, and sightseeing.

trgt: Sendiri (Alone in the Indonesian language) is a 1984 album by Indonesian singer Chrisye. It was his ninth studio album and eight with Musica Studios as well as his third album released in 1984 after Metropolitan and Nona (Miss).
mgt: *Sendiri* is a 1984 studio album by Indonesian singer Chrisye, released by Musica Studios. Produced in collaboration with Addie MS, the album marked a return to Chrisye's romantic pop roots. Featuring the titular piano-driven ballad "Sendiri" and a duet with Vina Panduwinata, it was a commercial success, selling over 100,000 copies and winning the BASF Award for best-selling album. The album has been reissued twice, in 2004 and 2007.

trgt: The Piaggio Free is a scooter produced by italian motorcycle manufacturer Piaggio from 1992 to 2002 in Pontedera.
mgt: The Piaggio Free is a high-wheel scooter produced from 1992 to 2002, featuring two-stroke and four-stroke engine options.

trgt: Sofya Andreyevna Arzhakovskaya (Lang-ru; born 12 August 1987), known professionally as Sofya Skya (Lang-ru), is a Russian actress, ballet dancer and acting coach.
mgt: Sofya Skya (born Sofya Andreyevna Arzhakovskaya, August 12, 1987) is a Russian actress, director, acting coach, and former ballerina. A graduate of the Vaganova Academy of Russian Ballet and The Bashkir Choreographic College, she performed as a principal dancer in notable ballets before transitioning to acting. Skya gained recognition for her roles in films such as *The Tomb* (2009), *Shadows in Paradise* (2010), and *Assassins Run* (2013). She has also appeared in international productions, including *Opus of an Angel* (2017) and *Once There Was a War* (2019). Skya won the "Mrs. World" pageant in 2006 and has participated in various charity events, including performing at the 2013 Summer Universiade opening ceremony in Kazan.

trgt: Enrico Cruciani Alibrandi (9 July 1839 – 8 November 1921) was an Italian politician who served as senator and mayor of Rome.
mgt: Enrico Cruciani Alibrandi (died 1921) was an Italian engineer, politician, and mayor of Rome (1905–1907), later serving as a senator.

trgt: Israel Freedman (April 11, 1878 – March 18, 1934) was a Jewish Romanian-American Yiddish journalist.
mgt: Israel Freedman (April 11, 1878 – March 18, 1934) was a Romanian-American journalist, playwright, and Yiddish theater critic.

trgt: Herbert Willoughby Ellis FRES FGS (28 June 1869-15 October 1943) was a British entomologist.
mgt: Herbert Willoughby Ellis was a British entomologist, naturalist, and collector specializing in coleoptera.

trgt: Hualing Nieh Engle (born 11 January 1925), née Nieh Hua-ling (Chinese: 聶華苓; pinyin: Niè Huálíng; Wade–Giles: Nieh4 Hua2-ling2), is a Chinese novelist, fiction writer, and poet. She is a professor emerita at the University of Iowa.
mgt: Hualing Nieh Engle (born January 11, 1925) is a Chinese-born writer, editor, and educator, best known as the co-founder of the International Writing Program (IWP) at the University of Iowa. A prolific author of over two dozen works, including the acclaimed novel *Mulberry and Peach*, she has played a significant role in fostering cross-cultural literary exchange. After relocating to Taiwan following the Chinese Communist Revolution, she became a prominent literary editor and educator before moving to the United States in 1964. Alongside her husband, Paul Engle, she developed the IWP into a globally recognized residency for writers. Her contributions to literature and international collaboration have earned her numerous accolades, including an American Book Award and a Nobel Peace Prize nomination.

trgt: Lev Abramovich Dodin (Lang-ru, born 1944) is a modern Russian theater director, the leader of Saint Petersburg Maly Drama Theater.
mgt: Lev Dodin (born 1944) is a Russian theater director, known for his long tenure as artistic director of the Maly Drama Theater since 1982.

trgt: The former Roman Catholic Diocese of Ypres, in present-day Belgium, existed from 1559 to 1801. Its seat was Saint Martin's Cathedral in Ypres. In 1969 it was reconstituted as a titular see.
mgt: The Diocese of Ypres was established in 1557 following the destruction of the Diocese of Thérouanne and existed until 1801, when it was incorporated into the Diocese of Ghent. Cornelius Jansen, founder of Jansenism, served as its bishop.

trgt: An extraocular implant (also known as eyeball jewelry) is a cosmetic implant involving a tiny piece of decorative jewelry which is implanted within the superficial, interpalpebral conjunctiva or sclera of the human eye.
mgt: An extraocular implant, also known as eyeball jewelry, is a form of body modification developed in the Netherlands in 2002. The procedure involves implanting small platinum alloy shapes into the eye's sclera.

trgt: Cary Castle stood on Lodge Hill overlooking the town of Castle Cary, Somerset, England. It is a Scheduled Ancient Monument.
mgt: Cary Castle was a motte-and-bailey castle in Castle Cary, Somerset, England, built in the 11th or 12th century. It played a role during The Anarchy, changing hands multiple times before being abandoned by 1468. Excavations in 1890 revealed the foundations of a stone keep and baileys, though only earthworks remain today.

trgt: The FIFA Women's Club World Cup is an international women's association football competition that is proposed by the Fédération Internationale de Football Association (FIFA), the sport's global governing body. The competition is currently in only planning mode.
mgt: The FIFA Women's Club World Cup is a proposed international football competition for women's club teams, envisioned as the female equivalent of the FIFA Club World Cup. Discussions about its creation began in 2012, following the establishment of the International Women's Club Championship (IWCC) in Japan. FIFA's Women's Football Task Force and officials, including President Gianni Infantino, have since explored the tournament's potential, emphasizing its role in advancing women's football globally.

trgt: Charles Percy Abbott (born 10 June 1939) is a former Australian rules footballer who played with Hawthorn in the Victorian Football League (VFL), a polo player, and the recipient of the Australian Sports Medal.
mgt: Charles Percy Abbott (born 10 June 1939) is a former Australian rules footballer who played 17 games for Hawthorn in the Victorian Football League (VFL) from 1961 to 1963. Educated at Caulfield Grammar School and the University of Melbourne, Abbott excelled in academics, sports, and amateur football before joining Hawthorn. After retiring from football in 1964 to focus on his legal career, he became a prominent lawyer and corporate leader, holding various chairmanships and directorships. Abbott was also a skilled polo player, serving as President of the Victorian Polo Association and receiving the Australian Sports Medal in 2000 for his contributions to the sport.

trgt: Mordaunt Fenwick Bisset (1825 – 7 July 1884) of Bagborough in Somerset, 22nd Scottish feudal baron of Lessendrum in Aberdeen, Scotland, was a British Conservative Party Member of Parliament and a famous west-country Master of Staghounds.
mgt: Mordaunt Fenwick-Bisset (died 7 July 1884) was a British landowner, politician, and sportsman. He served as Member of Parliament for West Somerset (1880–1883) and as Master of the Devon and Somerset Staghounds (1855–1881), where he revitalized stag hunting on Exmoor.

trgt: Lourd Ernest Hanopol de Veyra (IPA-tl; born February 11, 1975) is a Filipino musician, emcee, poet, journalist, TV host, broadcast personality and activist who became famous as the vocalist of the Manila-based jazz rock band Radioactive Sago Project.
mgt: Lourd Ernest Hanopol de Veyra (born February 11, 1975) is a Filipino journalist, television and radio host, musician, and author. He is the vocalist of Radioactive Sago Project and has hosted various television programs, including *History with Lourd* and *Wag Po!*. A recipient of multiple Don Carlos Palanca Memorial Awards for Literature, de Veyra has published poetry collections, essays, and a novel. He graduated with a degree in journalism from the University of Santo Tomas and began his musical career with the punk band Dead Ends.

trgt: Slugterra: Ghoul from Beyond is a 2014 Canadian animated television film produced by Nerd Corps Entertainment. It premiered on March 30, 2014 on Disney XD and is the first Slugterra film.
mgt: *Slugterra: Ghoul from Beyond* is a 2014 Canadian animated television film directed by Johnny Darrell, Barry Karnowski, and Logan McPherson. Set after the events of *Slugterra*, the film follows Eli Shane and the Shane Gang as they face a new threat: the Goon, a powerful ghoul capable of controlling slugs and seeking to conquer Slugterra. The group must stop the Goon and its army while gaining a new ally, Junjie, from the Eastern Caverns. Produced by Nerd Corps Entertainment, the film premiered on Disney XD Canada on March 30, 2014, and was later released on DVD.

trgt: Patrick Pothuizen (born 15 May 1972) is a Dutch football manager and former player who is the head coach of Eerste Klasse club Achilles '29.
mgt: Patrick Pothuizen (born 15 May 1972) is a Dutch football coach and former professional player who played as a defender. He is currently the head coach of Achilles '29. Pothuizen had a notable playing career with clubs including Vitesse, NEC Nijmegen, and Twente, and holds the Eredivisie record for most yellow cards. After retiring, he transitioned into coaching, serving in various roles before managing Achilles '29.

trgt: Olia Lialina (Lang-ru, May 4, 1971, Moscow) is an Internet artist and theorist, an experimental film and video critic and curator.
mgt: Olia Lialina (born May 4, 1971) is a Russian internet artist, net art pioneer, and theorist, known for her influential work *My Boyfriend Came Back from the War*.

trgt: Gretta Kehoe-Quigley is a former camogie player, captain of the All Ireland Camogie Championship winning team in 1975, the day after she was married to Ray Quigley, the trainer of her club camogie team.
mgt: Gretta Kehoe-Quigley is a former Irish camogie player from Wexford who played as a center back. She won a Leinster senior championship in 1974 and an All-Ireland medal in 1975 alongside her sisters.

trgt: Ikhtiyar al-Din Ai-Taq was an influential amir in western Khurasan following the decline of the Seljuks, and the ruler of Gurgan and Dihistan from 1161 until 1165.
mgt: Ikhtiyar al-Din Ai-Taq was a former ghulam of Seljuk sultan Sanjar who became a military leader, establishing influence in western Khurasan after Sanjar's capture in 1153.

trgt: Elfonda Mekel, SH, (born 21 May 1970), better known by his stage name Once Mekel (IPA-id, pronounced Ohn-chè), is an Indonesian singer and former lead vocalist of Dewa 19.
mgt: Elfonda Mekel (born 21 May 1970), known professionally as Once Mekel, is an Indonesian pop and rock singer. He gained prominence as the lead vocalist of Dewa 19 from 2000 to 2011, contributing to the band's success with hit albums like *Bintang Lima*. After leaving the band to pursue a solo career, he released popular singles such as "Dealova" and "Symphony yang Indah." Once has won several awards, including Best Pop Soloist at the 2005 Anugerah Musik Indonesia.

trgt: Ward No. 8, Kolkata Municipal Corporation is an administrative division of Kolkata Municipal Corporation in Borough No. 1, covering parts of Bagbazar and Shobhabazar neighbourhoods in North Kolkata, in the Indian state of West Bengal.
mgt: Ward No. 8 of the Kolkata Municipal Corporation covers the neighborhoods of Bagbazar and Shobhabazar in Kolkata, West Bengal, India. It is part of Borough No. 1 and falls under the Shyampukur Assembly constituency and Kolkata Uttar parliamentary constituency. As of the 2011 Census, the ward had a population of 18,760 with a literacy rate of 93.57%. It is bordered by the Hooghly River and several streets, and is served by Shyampukur police station. The ward forms a municipal corporation council electoral constituency.

trgt: William Henry Belpitt (born May 3, 1860, date of death unknown) was a United States Navy sailor and a recipient of the United States military's highest decoration, the Medal of Honor.
mgt: William Henry Belpitt (May 3, 1860 – November 29, 1912) was an Australian-born sailor in the United States Navy who received the Medal of Honor for rescuing a man from drowning in Fuzhou, China, in 1884.

trgt: William Ford De Saussure (February 22, 1792 – March 13, 1870) was a United States senator from South Carolina. Born in Charleston, the son of Henry William de Saussure and Elizabeth Ford De Saussure.
mgt: William Ford De Saussure (February 22, 1792 – March 13, 1870) was an American lawyer, politician, and U.S. Senator from South Carolina, serving from 1852 to 1853. A member of the Democratic Party, he also served in the South Carolina House of Representatives and as a judge of the chancery court. De Saussure was a delegate to South Carolina's Secession Convention in 1860 and signed the Ordinance of Secession, contributing to the onset of the Civil War. He practiced law in Columbia, served as mayor of the city, and was a longtime trustee of South Carolina College.

trgt: Oxalis latifolia is a species of flowering plant in the woodsorrel family known by the common names garden pink-sorrel and broadleaf woodsorrel. It is native to Mexico and parts of Central and South America.
mgt: *Oxalis latifolia*, commonly known as broadleaf woodsorrel, is a perennial herb in the family Oxalidaceae. Native to the Americas, it spreads via bulbs and stolons and is considered an invasive species globally.

trgt:
Eddy Nait Slimani (born 25 August 1993) is a French kickboxer. He is a former WKN and WAKO-Pro world champion.

As of July 2021, he was the #9 ranked Featherweight in the world by Combat Press.
mgt: Eddy Naït Slimani (born August 25, 1993) is a French professional kickboxer competing in the super bantamweight division. Known for his orthodox stance, he has achieved multiple tournament victories and held several titles, including the WKN European and NDC -65 kg championships. Slimani has fought in prominent promotions such as Glory and Superkombat Fighting Championship, facing notable opponents like Saenchai and Masaaki Noiri. Over his career, he has amassed a professional record of 45 fights, with 38 wins, including 8 by knockout.

trgt:
Omsk hemorrhagic fever is a viral hemorrhagic fever caused by a Flavivirus.

It was found in Siberia and was named for an outbreak in the city of Omsk. First records of the new virus appeared around 1940–1943.
mgt: Omsk hemorrhagic fever (OHF) is a tick-borne viral disease caused by the Omsk hemorrhagic fever virus, a member of the *Flavivirus* family. Endemic to Western Siberia, it is transmitted through tick bites, contact with infected muskrats, or contaminated water. Symptoms include fever, hemorrhaging, neurological complications, and, in severe cases, encephalitis. While most patients recover, some may experience long-term effects such as hearing loss or psychological issues. The disease has a low fatality rate (0.5–3%) and no specific treatment, with care focused on symptom management. Prevention involves avoiding tick exposure and protective measures during activities like farming or hunting.

trgt: Albert Henri Bourderon (26 November 1858 – 2 April 1930) was a French cooper (barrel maker) and syndicalist who became a leading socialist. During World War I he supported a pacifist position in line with internationalist principles.
mgt: Albert Henri Bourderon (26 November 1858 – 2 April 1930) was a French cooper, trade unionist, socialist, and pacifist. A prominent figure in the French labor movement, he co-founded the coopers' union and played a key role in the General Confederation of Labour (CGT). During World War I, Bourderon was an outspoken pacifist, participating in the Zimmerwald Conference and co-founding the Committee for the Resumption of International Relations. After the war, he shifted toward reformism and remained active in socialist and labor causes until his death.

trgt: Alexandra Pringle, Hon. FRSL (born 1952/53), is a British publisher. She was a founding director of Virago Press and the editor-in-chief of Bloomsbury Publishing for 18 years as of March 2018.
mgt: Alexandra Pringle Hon. FRSL (born 1952 or 1953) is a British publisher and editor-in-chief of Bloomsbury Publishing since 1999. She previously worked at Virago, Hamish Hamilton, and as a literary agent.

trgt: This is a summary of 1984 in music in the United Kingdom, including the official charts from that year.
mgt: 1984 in British music was marked by record-breaking sales, iconic chart-topping singles, and the rise of major artists.

trgt: The Neradje Mosque or Neradjes (Lang-el) is a historical Ottoman-era mosque located in the old town of Rethymno, Crete, Greece. It now serves as a music school.
mgt: The Neradje Mosque, located in Rethymno, Crete, Greece, was originally a Catholic monastery before being converted into a mosque during Ottoman rule. Built in 1890 by Georgios Daskalakis, it features Renaissance architectural elements, a rectangular structure with three domes, and the tallest minaret in the town. Following the 1923 population exchange between Turkey and Greece, it was repurposed as a music school.

trgt: Veniamin Lvovich Beylin (Lang-ru; 25 August 1904 – 16 March 1982) was a Red Army major general.
mgt: Veniamin Lvovich Beylin (1904–1982) was a Soviet major general, World War II commander, and military instructor.

trgt: Acetoin, also known as 3-hydroxybutanone or acetyl methyl carbinol, is an organic compound with the formula CH3CH(OH)C(O)CH3. It is a colorless liquid with a pleasant, buttery odor. It is chiral. The form produced by bacteria is (R)-acetoin.
mgt: Acetoin, or 3-hydroxybutan-2-one, is a colorless liquid with a yogurt-like odor, commonly used as a food flavoring and fragrance. Naturally found in various fruits and vegetables, it contributes to butter flavor and is used in electronic cigarette liquids for buttery or caramel notes. Produced by fermentative bacteria, acetoin plays a role in preventing over-acidification during growth and can be metabolized into acetyl-CoA. It is also a key compound in the Voges-Proskauer test for bacterial identification.

trgt: Patrick Lichty is a conceptual media artist, activist, curator, and educator. Lichty is currently a Creative Digital Media professor at Winona State University.
mgt: Patrick Lichty (born October 5, 1962) is an American new media artist, curator, and educator known for his work in digital art, virtual reality, and augmented reality. A member of the activist collectives RTMark and The Yes Men, he has also co-founded the Second Life performance art group Second Front and contributed to the augmented reality art collective Manifest.AR. Lichty is recognized for his digital tapestries and has exhibited internationally, with works held in collections such as the Walker Art Center and the Smithsonian.

trgt: Vishwa Thulasi is a 2004 Tamil-language drama film directed by Sumathy Ram, starring Mammooty and Nandita Das. This film's score and soundtrack were composed by Ilayaraaja and M. S. Viswanathan. The film was notable for having a female director.
mgt: *Vishwa Thulasi* is a 2004 Indian Tamil-language film directed by Sumathy Ram, starring Mammootty and Nandita Das. Set between the 1940s and 1960s, it explores themes of love, societal stigma, and emotional struggles. The film features music by M. S. Viswanathan and Ilaiyaraaja and received recognition at the 2005 WorldFest Houston.

trgt: Joseph Biddle Wilkinson Jr. (February 20, 1845 – October 23, 1915) was a Member of the Board of General Appraisers.
mgt: Joseph Biddle Wilkinson Jr. (February 20, 1845 – October 23, 1915) was an American Confederate Army officer, planter, and federal judge. He served on the Board of General Appraisers from 1890 to 1899, appointed by President Benjamin Harrison. A great-grandson of James Wilkinson, he was also Chief Clerk of the New Orleans Customs House and a Virginia Military Institute graduate.

trgt: Freienfeld (IPA-de; Lang-it IPA-it) is a comune (municipality) in the province of South Tyrol in northern Italy, located about 45 kilometres (28 mi) north of the city of Bolzano.
mgt: Freienfeld (Italian: Campo di Trens) is a municipality in South Tyrol, northern Italy, located in the Eisacktal valley. It encompasses several villages, including Trens, Stilfes, and Mauls, and has a population of 2,668 (as of 2010). Historically inhabited since prehistoric times, the area features significant archaeological finds and medieval castles, such as Reifenstein and Welfenstein. Freienfeld is also home to the Sanctuary of Maria Trens, a notable pilgrimage site. The municipality was formed in 1928 by merging three former municipalities and is predominantly German-speaking.

trgt: Yann Weymouth is a St. Petersburg, Florida-based architect and the designer of the Salvador Dalí Museum. Early in his career, he served as chief of design for I. M. Pei on the Grand Louvre Project in Paris.
mgt: Yann Weymouth is an American architect known for his role as chief of design for I.M. Pei on the National Gallery of Art East Wing in Washington, D.C., and the Grand Louvre Project in Paris, including the iconic Louvre Pyramid. A graduate of Harvard University and MIT, he has held prominent positions at firms such as Arup, SOM, Arquitectonica, and HOK Architects, where he served as senior vice president and design director. Weymouth designed notable projects including the Salvador Dalí Museum in Florida and expansions of the John and Mable Ringling Museum of Art.
