Under his leadership, Vermont has won multiple America East Conference championships and made several NCAA Tournament appearances. Becker has been named America East Coach of the Year six times and is known for achieving consistent success, including numerous 20-win seasons and regular-season titles. Before Vermont, he coached at Gallaudet University and Catholic University. Becker's tenure has also been marked by controversy, including allegations of mishandling a rape accusation against a player in 2022.

trgt: Jamil Baroody (1905 – March 4, 1979) was a Saudi diplomat who served as a representative to the United Nations from the organization's first meeting in 1946 until his death.
mgt: Jamil Baroody (1905–1979) was a Lebanese-born Saudi diplomat, educator, and orator who represented Saudi Arabia at the United Nations from its founding, shaping its positions and enlivening debates.

trgt: Gaylord Dewayne Shaw (July 22, 1942 – September 6, 2015) was an American journalist who won a Pulitzer Prize for National Reporting in 1978.
mgt: Gaylord Dewayne Shaw (July 22, 1942 – September 6, 2015) was an American journalist and Pulitzer Prize winner, known for investigative reporting and breaking major news stories, including President Richard Nixon's resignation.

trgt: Bill Long (28 April 1932 – 21 May 2010) was an Irish writer and broadcaster. He often featured on RTÉ Radio 1. He was also Ireland's longest surviving heart transplant patient.
mgt: Bill Long (28 April 1932 – 21 May 2010) was an Irish writer, broadcaster, and lecturer. Known for his radio documentaries and books, including *Bright Light, White Water* and *Change of Heart*, he documented Irish lighthouses and advocated for organ donation following his heart transplant in 1994. Born in Waterford, Long worked as a journalist for several Irish newspapers before transitioning to writing and broadcasting. He also lectured in homiletics at the National University of Ireland, Maynooth, and contributed to RTÉ Radio 1's *Sunday Miscellany*.

trgt: Matheus Antunes Ribeiro (born 23 October 1993) is a Brazilian footballer who plays as a right back for Vasco da Gama.
mgt: Matheus Antunes Ribeiro (born 23 October 1993) is a Brazilian professional footballer who plays as a right back for Vasco da Gama.

trgt: St Andrew's Church, Buckland Monachorum is a Grade I listed parish church in the Church of England Diocese of Exeter in Buckland Monachorum, Devon.
mgt: St Andrew's Church, Buckland Monachorum, is a Grade I listed Church of England parish church in Devon, England, dedicated to Saint Andrew. Originally medieval, it was extensively rebuilt in the late 15th century and restored in 1868–69. The church is notable for its Drake aisle monuments, including works by John Bacon Senior and Junior, and Richard Westmacott. It houses an organ installed in 1849, later expanded, and a peal of eight bells, five of which date to 1723.

trgt: The Museum of Russian Impressionism is an art museum in Moscow dedicated to Russian art at the turn of the twentieth century.
mgt: The Museum of Russian Impressionism, located in Moscow, Russia, was founded by entrepreneur Boris Mints and opened on May 28, 2016. It showcases a collection of Russian Impressionist artworks, including pieces by Konstantin Korovin, Igor Grabar, and Valentin Serov, primarily from Mints' private collection. Housed in a restored late 19th-century Bolshevik mill, the museum also features contemporary works and has hosted exhibitions in Russia and abroad.

trgt: Karlo Matković (born 30 March 2001) is a Croatian professional basketball player for Cedevita Olimpija of the ABA League. He represents the Croatia national team internationally.
mgt: Karlo Matković (born March 30, 2001) is a Bosnian-Croatian professional basketball player for Cedevita Olimpija in the ABA League. A power forward/center, he was selected 52nd overall in the 2022 NBA Draft by the New Orleans Pelicans. Matković began his career with Cedevita and has also played on loan for OKK Beograd and Mega Basket. Internationally, he represented Bosnia and Herzegovina at youth levels and debuted for Croatia's senior team in 2022.

trgt: In mathematics, Casey's theorem, also known as the generalized Ptolemy's theorem, is a theorem in Euclidean geometry named after the Irish mathematician John Casey.
mgt: Casey's theorem is a generalization of Ptolemy's theorem in Euclidean geometry, relating the tangency and common tangents of four circles to a larger circle.

trgt: Joseph Henry Rodriguez (born December 12, 1930) is a Senior United States district judge of the United States District Court for the District of New Jersey.
mgt: Joseph Henry Rodriguez (born December 12, 1930) is a Senior United States District Judge for the District of New Jersey. Appointed by President Ronald Reagan in 1985, he served as an active judge until assuming senior status in 1998. Prior to his judicial career, Rodriguez was the Public Advocate of New Jersey from 1982 to 1985 and held leadership roles in state education and investigative commissions. A graduate of La Salle University and Rutgers School of Law–Camden, he also practiced law privately in Camden, New Jersey, for over two decades.

trgt: Hastings is a city in Mills County, Iowa, United States. The population was 152 at the time of the 2020 census.
mgt: Hastings, Iowa, is a small city in Mills County, United States, with a population of 152 as of the 2020 census. Established in 1872 following the construction of the Chicago, Burlington and Quincy Railroad, the city covers 0.41 square miles of land. It is part of the East Mills Community School District, formed in 2011 through a merger.

trgt: In topology, the Denjoy–Riesz theorem states that every compact set of totally disconnected points in the Euclidean plane can be covered by a continuous image of the unit interval, without self-intersections (a Jordan arc).
mgt: The Denjoy–Riesz theorem states that every compact, totally disconnected subset of the plane is a subset of a Jordan arc. Initially proposed by Frigyes Riesz and Arnaud Denjoy in the early 20th century, the theorem was later refined after flaws were identified in Riesz's original proof. It has applications in constructing Osgood curves and relates to the analyst's traveling salesman theorem, which characterizes subsets of curves with finite arc length.

trgt: James Adedokun Adegoke (1934–2017) was a teacher and football referee who became Apetumodu of Ipetumodu in 1992. He is from Asalu compound of Fagbemokun ruling house of Ipetumodu.
mgt: James Adedokun Adegoke Akunraaledoye II (December 25, 1934 – November 26, 2017) was the 25th Apetumodu of Ipetumodu, reigning from 1992 to 2017. His tenure brought peace and development, including the establishment of educational institutions and markets. A former teacher, sports enthusiast, and cultural advocate, he contributed significantly to Yoruba culture and sports in Nigeria.

trgt: Eric Records is an American record company that produced singles from 1968 to 1996 mainly with reissues of successful oldies, continuing today with compact disc compilations.
mgt: Eric Records is a music label founded in 1968 by Bill Buster in Philadelphia, specializing in re-releasing original versions of popular songs, later transitioning to compact discs in 1996.

trgt: Acadia Pharmaceuticals Inc. is a biopharmaceutical company headquartered in Sorrento Valley, San Diego, California.
mgt: Acadia Pharmaceuticals, founded in 1993 and headquartered in San Diego, California, is a biopharmaceutical company specializing in developing treatments for central nervous system disorders. Its flagship product, Nuplazid (pimavanserin), was approved by the FDA in 2016 for treating hallucinations and delusions associated with Parkinson's disease psychosis. The company is also conducting clinical trials for other conditions, including dementia-related psychosis and major depressive disorder. Originally established as Receptor Technologies in Vermont, Acadia relocated to California in 1997 to foster industry partnerships. It went public in 2004 and has since expanded its portfolio, including acquiring CerSci Therapeutics in 2020.

trgt: The Oregon State University Foundation is an American private nonprofit corporation dedicated to enhancing the mission of Oregon State University in Corvallis, Oregon. The OSU Foundation is governed by a volunteer Board of Trustees.
mgt: The Oregon State University Foundation, established on October 15, 1947, is a nonprofit organization based in Corvallis, Oregon, that supports Oregon State University through fundraising and resource stewardship. It manages an endowment exceeding $819 million and has led major fundraising campaigns, including The Campaign for OSU, which raised over $1.1 billion by 2014, and the ongoing Believe It campaign, launched in 2022 with a $1.75 billion goal.

trgt: Annie Brisset, a member of the Royal Society of Canada, is a Professor of Translation Studies and Discourse Theory at the School of Translation and Interpretation of the University of Ottawa, Canada.
mgt: Annie Brisset is a Canadian scholar and expert in translation studies, semiotics, and intercultural communication. She has held prominent academic and administrative roles, published extensively, and received numerous accolades, including election to the Royal Society of Canada.

trgt: Kalwaria Zebrzydowska (IPA-pl) is a town in southern Poland with 4,429 inhabitants (2007 estimate). As of 1999, it is situated in Lesser Poland or Małopolska (in Polish). Previously, the town was administered within the Voivodeship of Bielsko-Biała (1975–1998).
mgt: Kalwaria Zebrzydowska is a town in Lesser Poland, Poland, known for its 17th-century Roman Catholic monastery and calvary, a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Founded by Mikołaj Zebrzydowski, it became a significant pilgrimage site and cultural center.

trgt: Putte is part of the Dutch municipality of Woensdrecht, and had 3751 inhabitants as of 1 January 2008. The village lies on and extends over the border between the Netherlands and Belgium, the Belgian part being Putte, Kapellen.
mgt: Putte is a village in the municipality of Woensdrecht, North Brabant, Netherlands, located on the border with Belgium. First mentioned in 1277, it developed around Ravenhof Castle and along the border road. The village features historical landmarks, including the rebuilt St. Dionysius Church and the Moretusbosch forest. Putte is notable as the burial place of 17th-century painter Jacob Jordaens and for its Jewish cemeteries. During World War I, the border was sealed with the "Wire of Death."

trgt: George Stiebel (1821-1896) was a Jamaican trader and entrepreneur who became a millionaire with sea transport between North and South America and as the owner of a gold mine. Stiebel is notoriously named Jamaica's first black millionaire.
mgt: George Stiebel (1820–1896) was a Jamaican businessman and philanthropist, widely regarded as Jamaica's first black millionaire. Born to a German-Jewish father and a Jamaican mother of African descent, he built his wealth through shipping, arms trading, and gold mining. Stiebel owned numerous properties in Jamaica, including Devon House, a neoclassical mansion in Kingston that remains a historic landmark. In 1891, he was appointed a Companion of the Order of St Michael and St George by Queen Victoria.

trgt: Ya'akov Ekhoiz (Lang-he; born 29 May 1957) is an Israeli former footballer.
mgt: Ya'akov Ekhoiz (born May 29, 1957) is an Israeli former footballer and coach.

trgt: Khaled Mohamad Takaji (Lang-ar; born 18 November 1986) is a Lebanese footballer and former futsal player who plays as a forward for Lebanese Premier League club Bourj.
mgt: Khaled Mohamad Takaji (born 18 November 1986) is a Lebanese footballer and former futsal player who plays as a forward for Lebanese Premier League club Bourj. A versatile forward, he has represented Lebanon internationally in both futsal and football, captaining the futsal team and earning accolades such as the 2008 Arab Futsal Championship top scorer and Best Player awards. Takaji has played for several clubs, including Nejmeh, Ansar, and Akhaa Ahli Aley, and participated in multiple AFC Futsal Championships.

trgt: William Patrick Twomey Sr. (14 May 1899 - 25 March 1977) was an Australian rules footballer who played for Collingwood and Hawthorn in the Victorian Football League (VFL).
mgt: William Patrick "Bill" Twomey Sr. (14 May 1899 – 25 March 1977) was an Australian rules footballer and coach who played for Collingwood and Hawthorn in the Victorian Football League (VFL). A versatile and pacey player, he was part of Collingwood's 1919 premiership team and represented Victoria in 1921. After leaving football to pursue a successful athletics career, including winning the 1924 Stawell Gift, he returned as Hawthorn's captain-coach in 1933. Twomey also briefly served as a boundary umpire and was part of a prominent footballing family, with several descendants playing for Collingwood.

trgt: Annie Buller (9 December 1895 – 19 January 1973), also known as Annie Buller-Guralnick, was a union organizer as well as co-founder of the Communist Party of Canada (CPC) and manager of many CPC publications.
mgt: Annie Buller (December 9, 1895 – January 19, 1973) was a Canadian labor organizer, political activist, and founding member of the Communist Party of Canada. Known for her union organizing and advocacy for workers' rights, she played a key role in strikes and labor movements across Canada, including the 1931 Toronto dressmakers' strike and the Estevan Coal Miners Strike. Buller was imprisoned for her activism and later became a prominent figure in the Labor-Progressive Party. She remained active in political organizing, anti-war efforts, and women's campaigns until her retirement in the late 1950s.

trgt: Neodiprion abietis, commonly known as the balsam fir sawfly, is a species of insect in the family Diprionidae. It is found in North America from Canada to northern Mexico and is phytophagous, feeding on the needles of coniferous trees.
mgt: Neodiprion abietis, commonly known as the balsam fir sawfly, is a species of sawfly in the family Diprionidae. Native to North America, it primarily feeds on balsam fir and other conifers, causing significant defoliation and tree growth reduction during outbreaks. The species undergoes a complex life cycle, with larvae feeding on older foliage and exhibiting distinct physical changes across instars. Outbreaks, which can last 3–5 years, are influenced by ecological factors and can be managed using biological controls like the NeabNPV virus or insect growth regulators.

trgt: Lucas de Souza Cunha, also known as Lucas Ferrugem (born 23 January 1997) is a Brazilian footballer who plays as a centre back for Portuguese club Gil Vicente.
mgt: Lucas de Souza Cunha (born 23 January 1997) is a Brazilian professional footballer who plays as a centre back for Primeira Liga club Gil Vicente. A youth product of Mirassol and Braga, he began his senior career with Braga B, later playing for Braga's first team and on loan at Estoril and Celta Vigo B. Cunha joined Gil Vicente in 2021. Internationally, he represented Brazil at the 2017 South American U-20 Championship.

trgt: The Jack Rabbit Trading Post is a convenience store and curio shop located on former U.S. Route 66, five miles west of Joseph City, Arizona city limits off of Exit 269 on Interstate 40.
mgt: The Jack Rabbit Trading Post, established in 1949 near Joseph City, Arizona, is a historic Route 66 attraction known for its iconic "HERE IT IS" billboard, fiberglass jackrabbit, and ties to the 2006 film *Cars*.

trgt: Leucocyanidin is a colorless chemical compound that is a member of the class of natural products known as leucoanthocyanidins.
mgt: Leucocyanidin is a flavonoid compound found in various plants, involved in procyanidin synthesis and studied for its metabolic pathways.

trgt: STM (Savunma Teknolojileri Mühendislik ve Ticaret A.Ş.) is a company operating in Turkey, operating in the civil, public (including military) and private sectors.
mgt: STM (Savunma Teknolojileri Mühendislik ve Ticaret A.Ş.) is a Turkish defense company founded in 1991, specializing in naval platforms, cybersecurity, autonomous systems, radar systems, satellite technologies, and consultancy services.

trgt: "Charles" is a short story by Shirley Jackson, first published in Mademoiselle in July 1948. It was later included in her 1949 collection, The Lottery and Other Stories, and her 1953 novel, Life Among the Savages.
mgt: "Charles" is a short story by Shirley Jackson that follows a mother’s observations of her son Laurie’s behavioral changes after starting kindergarten. Laurie tells his parents about a mischievous classmate named Charles, whose antics captivate and concern them. However, a twist at the end reveals that Charles does not exist, suggesting Laurie invented him to explain his own misbehavior. The story is widely interpreted as a commentary on childhood and deception, with some alternative readings suggesting a supernatural element. It remains a popular piece in school curricula and is Jackson's second most famous short story after "The Lottery."

trgt: Gareth Williams (born 27 August 1975) is a former professional tennis player from South Africa.
mgt: Gareth Williams (born 27 August 1975) is a South African former professional tennis player specializing in doubles.

trgt: Red Obsession is a 2013 Australian documentary film which collects interviews with winemakers and wine lovers across the world. The film is narrated by Russell Crowe. Red Obsession was co-directed and co-written by David Roach and Warwick Ross.
mgt: *Red Obsession* is a 2013 Australian documentary film directed and written by David Roach and Warwick Ross, narrated by Russell Crowe. It explores the global wine industry, focusing on China's growing demand for Bordeaux wines. The film received critical acclaim for its cinematography and thought-provoking commentary on cultural and economic implications, earning a 100% approval rating on Rotten Tomatoes.

trgt: Charles Parker JP (4 March 1809 – 29 June 1898) was a New Zealand politician and a carpenter.
mgt: Charles Parker (4 March 1809 – 29 June 1898) was a New Zealand politician, builder, and property developer.

trgt: Aristides Aquino Núñez (born April 22, 1994) is a Dominican professional baseball outfielder for the Chunichi Dragons of Nippon Professional Baseball (NPB). He has played for the Cincinnati Reds of Major League Baseball.
mgt: Aristides Aquino (born April 22, 1994) is a Dominican professional baseball outfielder for the Chunichi Dragons of Nippon Professional Baseball. He previously played in Major League Baseball (MLB) for the Cincinnati Reds from 2018 to 2022. Aquino gained prominence in 2019, setting multiple records, including becoming the first MLB player to hit 10 home runs in his first 16 career games and the first rookie to hit home runs in three consecutive innings. Known for his power hitting, he also set the National League rookie record for most home runs in a month (14). After his MLB tenure, Aquino signed with the Chunichi Dragons in November 2022.

trgt: John Barnard (1705–1784) was an 18th century English shipbuilder serving the Royal Navy.
mgt: John Barnard (1705–1784) was an English shipbuilder and Master Shipwright for the Royal Navy, known for his work at Harwich Dockyard and his private shipyards in Deptford and Rotherhithe.

trgt: Daniel J. Berthiaume (born January 26, 1966) is a Canadian former professional ice hockey goaltender who played six seasons in the National Hockey League (NHL) with the Winnipeg Jets, Minnesota North Stars, Los Angeles Kings, Boston Bruins, and Ottawa Senators.
mgt: Daniel Berthiaume (born January 26, 1966) is a Canadian former professional ice hockey goaltender who played 215 NHL games for teams including the Winnipeg Jets, Minnesota North Stars, and Los Angeles Kings. Nicknamed "The Bandit," he also had a lengthy minor league career, played professional roller hockey, and later transitioned to coaching. Berthiaume currently resides in Hardy, Virginia, where he operates a fishing charter business and coaches youth hockey.

trgt: Park Sol-mi (born Park Hye-jeong on January 3, 1978) is a South Korean actress.
mgt: Park Sol-mi (born Park Hye-jeong, January 3, 1978) is a South Korean actress. She gained recognition for her roles in television dramas such as *Winter Sonata* and *Bad Girls* and made her film debut in *Dance with the Wind* (2004). Park has also pursued music, playing piano on a Japanese album, and remains active in Japan through promotions and commercials. She married actor Han Jae-suk in 2013, and the couple has one daughter.

trgt: Autumn Exhibition of Leningrad artists of 1978 (Lang-ru) opened in Exhibition Halls of the Leningrad Union of Soviet Artists and became one of the largest art exhibitions of 1978 in the USSR.
mgt: The *Autumn Exhibition of Leningrad Artists of 1978* was a significant art event held at the Leningrad Union of Soviet Artists Exhibition Halls. Featuring nearly 1,100 works by over 650 artists, it showcased paintings, sculptures, graphics, and applied arts created primarily in 1977–1978. The exhibition highlighted prominent members of the Leningrad School, including Piotr Alberti, Taisia Afonina, and Nikolai Baskakov, among others. It included diverse genres such as portraits, landscapes, still lifes, and genre scenes, with many works later acquired by museums and collectors. A catalog was published, and the event received extensive coverage in Soviet art literature.

trgt: The Insurance Act, 1938 is a law originally passed in 1938 in British India to regulate the insurance sector. It provides the broad legal framework within which the industry operates.
mgt: The *Insurance Act, 1938* is an Indian law that consolidated and amended regulations for the insurance industry. Initially influenced by British law, it established regulatory frameworks, including the Controller of Insurance. Over time, its significance evolved with the nationalization of insurance companies and the formation of the Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India (IRDAI) in 1999. The Act governs insurance operations in India, requiring companies to be Indian-registered and limiting foreign ownership, with the foreign direct investment cap increased to 74% in 2021.

trgt: Jeremy Maurice Jacobs Sr. (born January 21, 1940) is an American billionaire businessman, the owner of the Boston Bruins and chairman of Delaware North. Forbes magazine ranks him as 481st richest person in the world.
mgt: Jeremy Maurice Jacobs (born January 21, 1940) is an American billionaire businessman, chairman of Delaware North, and owner of the Boston Bruins of the National Hockey League (NHL) since 1975. He has served as chairman of the NHL Board of Governors since 2007. Jacobs is known for his significant contributions to sports, philanthropy, and business, including overseeing Delaware North's global operations in hospitality and food services. Under his ownership, the Bruins won the Stanley Cup in 2011. A major philanthropist, Jacobs and his family have donated extensively to institutions such as the University at Buffalo, where the medical school is named in their honor.

trgt: Barry Mitchell Dallas OBE JP (1 December 1926 – 21 April 1992) was a New Zealand politician and doctor. He was twice mayor of Greymouth on the West Coast of New Zealand.
mgt: Barry Mitchell Dallas OBE JP (1 December 1926 – 21 April 1992) was a New Zealand medical doctor, local government leader, and community advocate. He served as Mayor of Greymouth (1966–1968, 1980–1989) and the first Mayor of Grey District (1989–1991). Dallas was instrumental in the construction of a flood protection wall in Greymouth, which successfully mitigated the town's long-standing flooding issues. He also served on the West Coast Hospital Board and stood as a parliamentary candidate for the West Coast electorate. Dallas was appointed an Officer of the Order of the British Empire in 1990 for his contributions to local government and the community.

trgt: Conscious automatism (C.A.) is a position on the philosophic question that asks whether determinism, as distinguished from “free will”, can be considered the sole operant principle in human decision making.
mgt: Conscious automatism is a philosophical position asserting that human beings, like other animals, are conscious but act as automata, with decisions entirely determined by prior conditioning and causal antecedents. Rejecting the notion of free will, it challenges traditional concepts of moral responsibility and has significant implications for ethics and criminal justice. The term, first introduced by Thomas H. Huxley in 1874, has been further developed in modern works like *Grandest Illusion: The Seductive Myth of Free Will* by Norman Haughness, which critiques voluntarism and emphasizes the deterministic nature of human behavior.

trgt: Nathan Sawaya (born July 10, 1973) is an American artist who builds custom three-dimensional sculptures and large-scale mosaics from popular everyday items and is best known for his work with standard LEGO building bricks.
mgt: Nathan Sawaya (born July 10, 1973) is an American artist known for creating large-scale sculptures and installations using LEGO bricks. A former attorney, he gained recognition in 2004 after leaving his legal career to pursue art full-time. Sawaya's work, including his signature human form sculptures and large-scale replicas, has been exhibited globally in venues such as the Lancaster Museum of Art, Federation Square in Melbourne, and the ArtScience Museum in Singapore. His touring exhibition, *The Art of the Brick*, is one of the first major museum exhibitions to focus exclusively on LEGO as an art medium. Sawaya operates studios in Manhattan and Los Angeles, each housing over 1.5 million LEGO bricks, and has been recognized as one of the world's top LEGO builders. His work has been widely acclaimed, featured in major media outlets, and commissioned by collectors, celebrities, and institutions worldwide.

trgt: Susan Lynn Gordon (July 27, 1949 – December 11, 2011) was an American child actress who appeared in films and numerous episodes of television programs such as The Twilight Zone, My Three Sons, and The Donna Reed Show.
mgt: Susan Lynn Gordon (July 27, 1949 – December 11, 2011) was an American child actress known for her roles in films directed by her father, Bert I. Gordon, including *Attack of the Puppet People* and *The Boy and the Pirates*. She also appeared in *The Five Pennies* and numerous television series, such as *The Twilight Zone* and *Gunsmoke*. After retiring from acting, she settled in Teaneck, New Jersey, and later embraced her Jewish roots. Gordon died of thyroid cancer in 2011 and was buried in Kedumim, Israel.

trgt: Kerry Williams Kelso O'Brien (born 19 July 1951), is a former Australian politician. O'Brien was a member of the Australian Senate for the state of Tasmania from September 1996 until June 2011, representing the Australian Labor Party.
mgt: Kerry O'Brien (born 19 July 1951) is a former Australian politician who served as a Senator for Tasmania from 1996 to 2011, representing the Australian Labor Party. He held various shadow ministry roles and was Chief Government Whip in the Senate from 2007 to 2010. Before entering politics, he was a trade union official, serving as Tasmanian Branch Secretary of the Australian Liquor, Hospitality and Miscellaneous Workers' Union.

trgt: Diana Lee Inosanto is an American actress, director, stuntwoman, and martial artist. She also wrote and directed the film The Sensei (2008), as well as having written a children's book titled The Curious Mind of Sebastian in 2020.
mgt: Diana Lee Inosanto (born in Torrance, California) is an American actress, martial artist, choreographer, film director, and children's author. The daughter of martial artist Dan Inosanto and goddaughter of Bruce Lee, she has trained extensively in martial arts, including Jeet Kune Do and Eskrima. Inosanto has worked as a stunt performer, choreographer, and actress in numerous films and television productions, including her role as Magistrate Morgan Elsbeth in *The Mandalorian*. She made her directorial debut with *The Sensei* in 2008 and is active in Southern California theatre. In 2020, she published her first children's book, *The Curious Mind of Sebastian*.

trgt: The M class are a class of diesel-hydraulic locomotives built by Walkers Limited, Maryborough for the Western Australian Government Railways in 1972–1973.
mgt: The WAGR M class were diesel-electric locomotives built by Walkers Limited for the Western Australian Government Railways, primarily for shunting duties.

trgt: Oto Horvat (born 1967) is a Serbian poet and writer. He lives and works in Florence, Italy.
mgt: Oto Horvat is a poet and novelist, known for his award-winning novel *Sabo je stao* (2015).

trgt: Laurette Spang-McCook (born May 16, 1951), credited as Laurette Spang, is an American television actress. She is best known for playing the character Cassiopeia on the original Battlestar Galactica (1978).
mgt: Laurette Spang-McCook (born May 16, 1951) is an American actress best known for her role as Cassiopeia in the original *Battlestar Galactica* series. Active from 1972 to 2007, she appeared in numerous television series and films, including *Emergency!*, *The Six Million Dollar Man*, and *Charlie's Angels*. Spang began her career after being discovered by a Universal Studios talent agent and later gained recognition for her work in science fiction. She married actor John McCook in 1980, and they have three children, including actress Molly McCook.

trgt: The 1523 papal conclave elected Cardinal Giulio de' Medici as Pope Clement VII to succeed Pope Adrian VI. According to conclave historian Baumgartner, the conclave was the "last conclave of the Renaissance".
mgt: The 1523 papal conclave, held from October 1 to November 19 at the Apostolic Palace, elected Giulio di Giuliano de' Medici as Pope Clement VII. The conclave was marked by intense political maneuvering between factions supporting the Holy Roman Emperor Charles V and King Francis I of France. Despite early opposition and delays caused by the arrival of French cardinals, Medici ultimately secured the necessary votes, aided by shifting alliances and the support of the Imperial faction. His election followed the death of Pope Adrian VI and occurred during a period of significant political and military tensions in Italy.

trgt: Sir Thomas Parr (c. 1483 – 11 November 1517) of Kendal in Westmorland (now Cumbria), England, was a courtier and is best known as the father of Queen Catherine Parr, the sixth and final wife of King Henry VIII.
mgt: Sir Thomas Parr (died 11 November 1517) was an English courtier and father of Catherine Parr, the sixth wife of King Henry VIII. A member of a prominent family descended from King Edward III, Parr served as Master of the Wards, Master of the Guards, and Comptroller to the King. He was also High Sheriff of Northamptonshire and Lincolnshire. Educated in classical and modern languages, Parr emphasized education in his household, which influenced his children, including Catherine. He married Maud Green in 1508, and they had four children, three of whom survived infancy. Parr died in 1517 and was buried in St. Anne's Church, Blackfriars, London.

trgt: Annie Féolde is a French chef, best known for holding three Michelin stars at her restaurant Enoteca Pinchiorri in Florence, Italy. She was the first female chef in Italy to win that level of accolade, and only the fourth ever.
mgt: Annie Féolde is a French-born chef and restaurateur, renowned as the first female chef in Italy to earn three Michelin stars, achieved at Enoteca Pinchiorri in Florence. She co-founded the restaurant with sommelier Giorgio Pinchiorri.

trgt: Cristián Caro Cordero (born 16 February 1943) is a Chilean prelate of the Catholic Church who was Archbishop of Puerto Montt from 2001 to June 2018.
mgt: Cristián Caro Cordero is a Chilean prelate of the Catholic Church who served as Archbishop of Puerto Montt from 2001 to 2018, resigning amid broader controversies.

trgt: Infra is the 2010 album by neo-classical composer Max Richter, released on July 19, 2010 on FatCat Records. The album was reissued on April 25, 2014 on Deutsche Grammophon.
mgt: *Infra* is a 2010 studio album by contemporary classical composer Max Richter, released on FatCat Records. Inspired by a ballet production, it received critical acclaim for its emotive and atmospheric compositions.

trgt: San Michele, locally called in dialect, Gésa Granda, is the Roman Catholic parish church located in the city center at Piazza Dante Alighieri in the town Cameri, province of Novara, Piedmont, Italy.
mgt: San Michele is a historic church in Cameri, Italy, originally documented in 1100 and rebuilt between 1583 and 1591. Featuring a Latin Cross layout, it houses 17th-century altars, 19th-century frescoes, and relics of Santa Gregoria. The facade, restored in 2002, includes statues of Saints Secondo, Roch, and the Archangel Michael, along with the town's original coat of arms. The current 42-meter belltower, built in 1827, replaced an earlier structure.

trgt: Hassan Askiali Haskins Jr. (born November 26, 1999) is an American football running back for the Tennessee Titans of the National Football League (NFL). He played college football at Michigan.
mgt: Hassan Haskins (born November 26, 1999) is an American football running back for the Tennessee Titans of the NFL. A standout at Michigan, he set school records in 2021, including 20 rushing touchdowns in a single season.

trgt: Roger Shah (IPA-de; born 29 November 1972), also known as DJ Shah and Sunlounger, is a German electronic music composer and producer.
mgt: Roger Shah (born November 29, 1972) is a German electronic music producer, DJ, and composer known for his Balearic trance style. Active since 1996, he has released music under various aliases, including Sunlounger, DJ Shah, and Black Pearl, and collaborated with artists like Armin van Buuren and Tiësto. Shah has appeared in DJ Mag's Top 100 DJs list five times, peaking at #21, and is recognized for his live performances using a wireless keyboard. His notable projects include the Sunlounger albums and the "Magic Island: Music for Balearic People" compilation series.

trgt: Diether Pascual Ocampo (born July 19, 1974) is a Filipino actor, singer, and model.
mgt: Diether Ocampo (born July 19, 1974) is a Filipino actor, singer, model, and Philippine Coast Guard Auxiliary officer.

trgt: George Ege (March 9, 1748 – December 14, 1829) was a United States Congressman, elected to the House of Representatives from Pennsylvania.
mgt: George Ege (March 9, 1748 – December 14, 1829) was an American ironmaster, politician, and Federalist who served in the U.S. House of Representatives from Pennsylvania's 5th district (1796–1797) and in the Pennsylvania House of Representatives (1783). Born in Germantown, Pennsylvania, he became a prominent ironmaster, owning the Charming Forge and other properties, and was one of Berks County's wealthiest citizens. Ege also served as an associate judge in Berks County from 1791 to 1818.

trgt: Cheick Oumar Dabo (born 12 January 1981 in Bamako) is a Malian football player.
mgt: Cheick Oumar Dabo (born January 12, 1981) is a retired Malian footballer who played as a striker.

trgt: Deerfoot (1828 – 18 January 1896, Cattaraugus Reservation) was an American Seneca runner. His most noted achievements took place in England.
mgt: Deerfoot, also known as Lewis Bennett (1828–1897), was a Seneca long-distance runner renowned for his world-record performances and unique racing tactics. Competing under the nickname "Red Jacket," he gained international fame during an 1861–1863 tour of England, where he defeated many of the world's top runners and set records for 10 and 12 miles. Known for his unconventional pacing and colorful persona, Deerfoot captivated audiences and became a celebrated figure in both the United States and England.

trgt: The squad listings for the CONCACAF U-20 Championship were published on 10 February 2017.
mgt: The 2017 CONCACAF U-20 Championship squads featured teams led by 12 head coaches.

trgt: Hasan Arat (born 27 September 1959) is a Turkish Sports Administrator, businessman and former professional basketball player. He was the leader of Istanbul's Bid to host the 2020 Olympic and Paralympic Games.
mgt: Hasan Arat is a Turkish sports administrator, businessman, and former professional basketball player. He led Istanbul's bid for the 2020 Olympics, holds prominent roles in international sports organizations, and chairs various business ventures.

trgt: Oksana Alexandrovna Kostina (Lang-ru; 15 April 1972 – 11 February 1993) was a Soviet and Russian individual rhythmic gymnast. She was the 1992 World All-around champion and 1992 European All-around bronze medalist.
mgt: Oksana Kostina (15 April 1972 – 11 February 1993) was a Russian rhythmic gymnast and 1992 World All-around champion. She won five gold medals at the 1992 World Championships, including all apparatus titles. Despite her success, Kostina was overshadowed by Ukrainian gymnasts earlier in her career and was not selected for the 1992 Olympics. She aimed to compete at the 1996 Olympics but tragically died in a car crash in 1993 at the age of 20.

trgt: Edward Franc Jones (June 3, 1828 – August 14, 1913) was an American merchant, manufacturer, soldier, author and politician from New York.
mgt: Edward F. Jones (June 3, 1828 – August 14, 1913) was an American Civil War brevet brigadier general, businessman, and politician. He commanded the 6th Massachusetts Militia during its historic march through Baltimore, sparking the first bloodshed of the Civil War, and later led the 26th Massachusetts Infantry. After the war, he became a successful businessman in Binghamton, New York, known for his scale manufacturing company and the slogan "Jones pays the freight." Jones served as Lieutenant Governor of New York from 1886 to 1891 and held various civic roles, including Police Commissioner of Binghamton and Regent of the University of the State of New York. He also authored a novel and remained active in public life until losing his sight. His home, the Gen. Edward F. Jones House, is listed on the National Register of Historic Places.

trgt: Barysaw (Lang-be, IPA-be) or Borisov (Lang-ru, IPA-ru) is a city in Belarus near the Berezina River in the Minsk Region 74 km north-east from Minsk. Its population is around 145,000.
mgt: Barysaw (Belarusian: Барысаў; Russian: Борисов) is a city in Belarus, located in the Minsk Voblast. Founded in 1102, it has a rich history, including periods under the Grand Duchy of Lithuania, the Russian Empire, and Soviet rule. The city played a significant role during the Napoleonic Wars and World War II. Today, Barysaw is an industrial hub with a population of approximately 145,000 (as of 2015) and is known for its football team, BATE Borisov, which has achieved national and international success.

trgt: Abdul Qadeer Baloch (Lang-ur), better known as Mama Qadeer (Lang-ur) is a human rights activist from the Balochistan province of Pakistan.
mgt: Mama Qadeer is a Baloch human rights activist known for his protests against enforced disappearances in Balochistan, including his son's killing.

trgt: Elías Contreras (born 7 March 1997) is an Argentine professional footballer who plays as a right midfielder for Brown de Adrogué, on loan from Independiente.
mgt: Elías Contreras (born 7 March 1997) is an Argentine professional footballer who plays as a right midfielder for Brown de Adrogué, on loan from Independiente.

trgt: Jitendra Malik is an Indian-American academic who is the Arthur J. Chick Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences at the University of California, Berkeley. He is known for his research in computer vision.
mgt: Jitendra Malik (born October 11, 1960) is an Indian-American computer scientist and professor at the University of California, Berkeley, known for his pioneering contributions to computer vision, machine learning, and robotics. He has developed influential algorithms and concepts, including anisotropic diffusion, normalized cuts, and R-CNN, and his research has been widely cited. Malik has received numerous prestigious awards, including the IEEE Computer Society's Computer Pioneer Award (2019), the Allen Newell Award (2016), and the IJCAI Award for Research Excellence in AI (2018). He is a member of the National Academy of Sciences, the National Academy of Engineering, and the American Academy of Arts and Sciences. Malik also serves as Research Director at Facebook AI Research.

trgt: Lt. Col. Frank Moffat (1894-1978) was a Scottish rugby union player. He became an international referee and the 66th President of the Scottish Rugby Union. He had a distinguished military career with the Gordon Highlanders in the First World War.
mgt: Frank J. C. Moffat (1894–1978) was a Scottish rugby union player, referee, coach, and administrator, serving as the 66th President of the Scottish Rugby Union from 1952 to 1953. A wing for Watsonians, he scored 47 tries in the 1920–21 season and later became an international referee. Moffat also organized the Frank Moffat XV, a representative rugby side. Beyond rugby, he was a decorated military officer, earning the Distinguished Service Order during World War I, and worked as a mining engineer.

trgt: John Graham is a Canadian former Native American activist. He is best known for being the person who shot and killed fellow American Indian Movement activist Anna Mae Aquash.
mgt: John Graham (born 1954–1957) is a Canadian activist and former member of the American Indian Movement (AIM). He participated in various Indigenous rights protests, including the Native People's Caravan and the Beothuk Patrol. Graham was convicted in 2010 of felony murder for his role in the 1975 killing of AIM activist Anna Mae Aquash, a case that has drawn significant controversy and legal scrutiny. He is currently serving a life sentence in the South Dakota State Penitentiary. Graham has maintained his innocence, and his conviction has been the subject of multiple appeals and ongoing debate among activists and legal experts.

trgt: The Time is the second album and first full-length studio album of Chinese singer-songwriter Xu Weizhou. It is divided into four parts.
mgt: *The Time* is the second studio album by Chinese singer and actor Xu Weizhou, released in four parts from 2017 to 2018 by Baina Entertainment. The album features a mix of C-pop, rock, and EDM genres and includes singles such as "Leave Me Alone," "So What," and "Orange Sky." It achieved significant commercial success, reaching double platinum status shortly after release and topping charts on QQ Music. Xu actively participated in the album's production, including songwriting and designing the cover. The album received positive critical reception for its creativity and Xu's musical growth.

trgt: Łęczyca (IPA-pl; in full the Royal Town of Łęczyca, Lang-pl; Lang-de; Lang-he) is a town of 13,587 inhabitants in central Poland. Situated in the Łódź Voivodeship, it is the county seat of the Łęczyca County.
mgt: Łęczyca is one of the oldest cities in Poland, located in the Łódź Voivodeship. First mentioned in the 12th century, it played a significant role in Polish history as the site of the first recorded Sejm (parliament) in 1180 and the capital of the Duchy of Łęczyca during the medieval period. The city prospered between the 14th and 17th centuries, with landmarks such as the Royal Castle, built by Casimir the Great, serving as a royal residence and hosting important events, including parliamentary sessions. Łęczyca suffered destruction during the Swedish invasion and World War II, including the establishment of a ghetto and the deportation of its Jewish population to extermination camps. Today, it is known for its historical landmarks, including the castle and nearby Collegiate Church in Tum, and serves as a cultural and historical center in central Poland.

trgt: Eddy L. Harris (born January 26, 1956) is an American writer of creative nonfiction and a filmmaker.
mgt: Eddy L. Harris (born January 26, 1956) is an American writer and filmmaker known for exploring themes of race, identity, and place through memoirs and travel narratives. His debut book, *Mississippi Solo* (1988), recounts his journey down the Mississippi River by canoe and examines African-American identity. Subsequent works, including *Native Stranger* and *South of Haunted Dreams*, further explore cultural and personal reflections. Harris has received recognition in France, where he now resides, and his documentary *River to the Heart* (2017) chronicles a second Mississippi River journey.

trgt: Michael Klein (10 October 1959 – 2 February 1993) was a Romanian footballer who played as a left back.
mgt: Michael Klein (10 October 1959 – 2 February 1993) was a Romanian footballer who played as a left back. He spent most of his club career at Corvinul Hunedoara and Dinamo București, winning a league title and cup with the latter, before moving to Bayer Uerdingen in Germany. Internationally, Klein earned 89 caps for Romania, representing his country at UEFA Euro 1984 and the 1990 FIFA World Cup. Known for his consistency and dedication, he tragically died of a heart attack during training in 1993 at the age of 33. The Stadionul Michael Klein in Hunedoara is named in his honor.

trgt: Millstatt am See is a market town of the Spittal an der Drau District in Carinthia, Austria. The traditional health resort and spa town on Lake Millstatt is known for former Benedictine Millstatt Abbey, founded about 1070.
mgt: Millstatt am See is a market town in the district of Spittal an der Drau, Carinthia, Austria, located on the northern shore of Lake Millstatt. Known for its picturesque setting, the town is historically significant for the former Millstatt Abbey, a Benedictine monastery founded in the 11th century, which became a cultural and religious center in the region. Millstatt developed into a popular summer resort during the late 19th century, attracting nobility and wealthy visitors from the Austro-Hungarian Empire. Today, it is a destination for quality tourism and vacation properties, with a focus on preserving its natural and historical heritage.

trgt: Gerhard vom Rath (20 August 1830 – 23 April 1888), was a German mineralogist, born at Duisburg in Prussia.
mgt: Gerhard vom Rath (1830–1888) was a German geologist and mineralogist known for his contributions to mineralogy and crystallography.

trgt: The Indian River is a river in Lanark County in Eastern Ontario, Canada. It is in the Saint Lawrence River drainage basin and is a left tributary of the Mississippi River.
mgt: The Indian River is a river in Lanark County, Ontario, Canada, that flows from an unnamed lake in Lanark Highlands to its mouth at the Mississippi River in Mississippi Mills. Part of the Saint Lawrence River drainage basin, the river passes through notable landmarks such as the Mill of Kintail Conservation Area, which houses museums dedicated to R. Tait McKenzie and James Naismith.

trgt: Oliver County is a county located in the U.S. state of North Dakota. As of the 2020 census, the population was 1,877. Its county seat and only city is Center.
mgt: Oliver County is a rural county in North Dakota, United States, established in 1885 and named after politician Harry S. Oliver. Its seat and largest city is Center, which coincidentally lies near the geographic center of North America. The county, part of the Bismarck Metropolitan Statistical Area, spans 731 square miles, with a population of 1,877 as of 2020. Known for its agricultural landscape and proximity to the Missouri River, Oliver County has a predominantly German and Norwegian heritage. It is politically Republican-leaning and features a consolidated school district serving its residents.

trgt: Abdallah Ben Abdel Mohsen At-Turki (Lang-ar, born 4 August 1940) is a Muslim religious leader from Saudi Arabia who has been General Secretary of the Muslim World League.
mgt: Abd Allah al-Turki is a Saudi Arabian Islamic scholar and academic who has held prominent positions, including Minister of Islamic Affairs and Secretary-General of the Muslim World League.

trgt: Dale Albert Kimball (born November 28, 1939) is a Senior United States district judge of the United States District Court for the District of Utah.
mgt: Dale Albert Kimball (born November 28, 1939) is a senior United States district judge for the District of Utah, appointed by President Bill Clinton in 1997. He previously practiced law, taught at Brigham Young University, and co-founded a prominent law firm. Known for presiding over high-profile cases, including the Brian David Mitchell trial, Kimball has also contributed extensively to legal scholarship and community service.

trgt: Michael Atwell Slater (died 2 February 1842) was a Royal Navy Officer and hydrographic surveyor particularly noted for his survey work in the north-east of Engand and the east of Scotland.
mgt: Michael Atwell Slater (died February 2, 1842) was a British Royal Navy officer and hydrographer. Promoted to Commander in 1837, he contributed to Admiralty Charts and coastal surveys of Britain.

trgt: John Oliver Nestor (November 7, 1912 – May 1, 1999) was a U.S. Food and Drug Administration medical officer and whistleblower.
mgt: John Nestor (1913–1999) was an American physician, FDA whistleblower, and advocate for strict adherence to speed limits while driving.

trgt: Margarita Iosifovna Aliger (Russian: Маргари́та Ио́сифовна Алиге́р, IPA: [mərɡɐˈrjitə ɪˈosjɪfəvnə ɐljɪˈɡjɛr]; October 7 [O.S. September 24] 1915 – August 1, 1992) was a Soviet and Russian poet, translator, and journalist.
mgt: Margarita Iosifovna Aliger (October 7, 1915 – August 1, 1992) was a Soviet poet, essayist, and journalist. Known for her patriotic and wartime poetry, including the celebrated poem "Zoya" (1942), she explored themes of Soviet heroism and societal reflection. Aliger also wrote essays on Russian literature and travel. She faced personal tragedies, including the loss of her husbands and children, and is buried in Peredelkino.

trgt: Markku Peltoniemi (born 8 February 1948) is a Finnish former footballer and a current sporting director for Helsingin Jalkapalloklubi
mgt: Markku Peltoniemi (born 8 February 1948) is a Finnish former footballer and long-serving sporting director of HJK Helsinki.

trgt: DeForest is a village in Dane County, Wisconsin, United States, along the Yahara River. The population was 10,811 at the 2020 census. It is part of the Madison Metropolitan Statistical Area.
mgt: DeForest is a village in Dane County, Wisconsin, United States, with a population of 10,811 as of the 2020 census. Named after Isaac De Forest, who purchased the land in 1856, the village spans 8.57 square miles and is part of the Madison Metropolitan Area. DeForest is served by the DeForest Area School District and the DeForest Area Public Library, which reflects the area's Norwegian heritage in its design. The village is accessible via major highways, including I-39/90/94 and US-51, and is near Dane County Regional Airport.

trgt: The Rafz train crash occurred at approximately 6.43 am on 20 February 2015. An S-Bahn and an Interregio express train collided at Rafz railway station in Rafz, Canton of Zürich, Switzerland.
mgt: The Rafz train crash occurred on 20 February 2015 in Rafz, Switzerland, when an Interregio train collided with an S-Bahn train in a side collision. Six people were injured, including the Interregio train driver, who was seriously hurt. The crash caused partial derailment, track damage, and structural damage to a nearby bridge. An investigation by the Swiss Transportation Safety Investigation Board was launched, focusing on potential signal issues and safety equipment failures.

trgt: Black Noise: Rap Music and Black Culture in Contemporary America is a 1994 book by Tricia Rose. It was released in hardback on April 29, 1994 through Wesleyan University Press.
mgt: *Black Noise: Rap Music and Black Culture in Contemporary America* is a 1994 book by Tricia Rose that critically examines rap music, black culture, and their intersections with American society. Widely reviewed and praised, it received a 1995 American Book Award and is considered a foundational text in hip-hop studies.

trgt: Mindy Lubber is President of Ceres, a non-profit sustainability advocacy organization based in Boston, Massachusetts. Lubber became President of Ceres in January 2014 and was a speaker at the Sustainable Innovation Forum of the 2015 United Nations Climate Change Conference.
mgt: Mindy Lubber is an American environmentalist and business leader known for her work in sustainability and corporate responsibility. She has held roles at the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency, founded Green Century Capital Management, and advises multinational corporations on sustainable business strategies.

trgt: Larissa Takchi (born 1997) is an Australian cook who won the eleventh series of MasterChef Australia in 2019. At 22 years old, to date she was the youngest contestant to win the competition.
mgt: Larissa Takchi (born 1997) is an Australian television cook and winner of *MasterChef Australia* 2019. Of Lebanese descent, she managed her family’s café before competing and winning with her innovative "Szechuan Pavlova."

trgt: Frank Schaffer Besson Jr., CBE (May 30, 1910 – July 15, 1985) was a United States Army general.
mgt: Frank S. Besson Jr. (1910–1985) was a United States Army general and logistics innovator, serving as the first commander of the Army Materiel Command.

trgt: The Animal Welfare Act 2006 (c 45) is an Act of the Parliament of the United Kingdom.
mgt: The Animal Welfare Act 2006 is a United Kingdom law that consolidated and updated animal welfare legislation, replacing the Protection of Animals Act 1911. It introduced a duty of care for animal owners, defined "protected animals," and established the "five welfare needs." The Act prohibits tail docking for cosmetic reasons (with exceptions for working dogs) and restricts the sale and gifting of animals to children under 16. It grants inspectors and constables powers to address animal suffering, enforce regulations, and prosecute offences, with penalties including fines and imprisonment.

trgt: Moritz Schuppert (1817 – May 2, 1887) was an American surgeon, anti-vaccinationist and early advocate of antisepsis.
mgt: Moritz Schuppert (1817 – May 2, 1887) was a German-born surgeon in New Orleans, known for introducing antisepsis.

trgt: Thierry Joseph-Louis Escaich (born 8 May 1965) is a French organist and composer.
mgt: Thierry Escaich is a French composer, organist, and improviser known for his diverse works.

trgt: Paul Pressler is the chair of the board of directors of eBay. He was previously CEO of Gap, Inc., chairman of Walt Disney Parks and Resorts and a partner at the private equity firm Clayton, Dubilier & Rice.
mgt: Paul Pressler is an American businessman known for his leadership roles in major companies, including Disney, Gap Inc., and eBay. He served as chairman of Walt Disney Parks and Resorts, overseeing expansions like Disney California Adventure and Downtown Disney. Pressler later became CEO of Gap Inc., where he managed the company's restructuring efforts. He has also held board positions at Avon, David’s Bridal, and SiteOne Landscape Supply, and became eBay's board chair in 2020.

trgt: James Harold Bradley (Mar 4, 1906 – Nov. 10, 1984), was a pianist and the Founder and Principal of the Bradley Institute for Music Education Research.
mgt: Harold Bradley (1906–1984) was a Canadian pianist, music educator, and founder of the Bradley Institute for Music Education Research in Niagara Falls, Ontario. A protégé of renowned French pianist Isidor Philipp, Bradley developed innovative teaching methods emphasizing early childhood music education and ambidextrous piano technique. His work connected music to cognitive development, psychology, and character building, influencing generations of students and educators. Bradley also performed widely, taught extensively, and served on the Niagara Falls Board of Education for 24 years. His institute operated until 2008, continuing his legacy in music education.

trgt: Judith Magdolna Laqueur-Révész (15 July 1915 – 17 April 2018) was a Hungarian-Dutch potter and sculptor.
mgt: Judith Magdolna Révész (1915–2018) was a Hungarian-Dutch ceramicist and sculptor known for her earthenware and bronze portraits, often inspired by Hungarian folk art. Born in Budapest, she moved to the Netherlands in 1920 and later studied under sculptor Mark Vedres in Hungary. Révész worked as a freelance potter in Amsterdam, collaborated with notable artists, and exhibited widely, including a duo exhibition with M.C. Escher. Her notable works include a tile picture for Prince Bernhard's retreat, now housed in the Dutch Tile Museum.

trgt: Isaac Hawkins Browne, FRS (7 December 1745 – 30 May 1818) was a British Tory politician, industrialist, essayist, and a lord of the manor of Badger, Shropshire.
mgt: Isaac Hawkins Browne (1745–1818) was an English coal owner, landowner, and politician who served as a Tory Member of Parliament for Bridgnorth from 1784 to 1812. He was instrumental in developing coal mining and ironworks on his Shropshire estates during the Industrial Revolution and oversaw significant improvements to his Badger estate, including the expansion of Badger Hall and the creation of picturesque landscapes. Browne was also a Fellow of the Royal Society and published essays on religion, morality, and metaphysics. Known for his conservative views, he supported the established order in Parliament and opposed Catholic emancipation and electoral reform. He retired from public life in 1812 and died in 1818.

trgt: Léon Spilliaert (also Leon Spilliaert; 28 July 1881 – 23 November 1946) was a Belgian symbolist painter and graphic artist.
mgt: Léon Spilliaert (28 July 1881 – 23 November 1946) was a Belgian symbolist and expressionist painter known for his melancholic, dreamlike works and introspective self-portraits.

trgt: Adil Rza bey oglu Isgandarov (Lang-az; 5 May 1910 – 18 September 1978) was a Soviet and Azerbaijani director, stage and film actor and pedagogue. People's Artist of the USSR (1959).
mgt: Adil Isgandarov (May 5, 1910 – September 18, 1978) was an Azerbaijani actor, theater and film director, and pedagogue. He directed the Azerbaijan State Academic National Drama Theatre and Azerbaijanfilm.

trgt: Drexel-Alvernon was a census-designated place (CDP) in Pima County, Arizona, United States. The population was 4,192 at the 2000 census.
mgt: Drexel-Alvernon is a former census-designated place (CDP) in Pima County, Arizona, covering 0.9 square miles with a 2000 population of 4,192.

trgt: Doon Valley is one of the nine electoral wards of East Ayrshire Council. Created in 2007, the ward elects three councillors using the single transferable vote electoral system.
mgt: Doon Valley is an electoral ward in East Ayrshire, Scotland, established in 2007. It includes Dalmellington, Drongan, and Patna, with three councillors representing the area.

trgt: Petra Kronberger (born 21 February 1969, in Pfarrwerfen) is an Austrian former alpine skier, who participated in all disciplines. She was the first female alpine skier to win in all five World Cup events.
mgt: Petra Kronberger (born February 21, 1969) is a retired Austrian alpine skier and Olympic champion. Competing from 1987 to 1992, she won two Olympic gold medals, one World Championship gold, and three consecutive World Cup overall titles (1990–1992). Kronberger was the first skier in the modern era to win races in all five alpine disciplines in a single season. After retiring at age 23, she pursued academic studies, worked in adult education, and contributed to cultural and sporting organizations, including the Austrian Ski Federation.

trgt: The red flour beetle (Tribolium castaneum) is a species of beetle in the family Tenebrionidae, the darkling beetles. It is a worldwide pest of stored products, particularly food grains, and a model organism for ethological and food safety research.
mgt: The red flour beetle (*Tribolium castaneum*) is a small beetle of the family Tenebrionidae, known for its reddish-brown color and global distribution in stored food products. Originating from the Indo-Australian region, it is a major pest of grains, flour, and other stored commodities, causing significant economic losses. The species exhibits complex mating behaviors, including polyandry and polygamy, which enhance genetic diversity and reproductive success. It has also been extensively studied as a model organism in genetics and developmental biology, with its genome sequenced in 2008.

trgt: The Roy model is one of the earliest works in economics on self-selection due to A. D. Roy. The basic model considers two types of workers that choose occupation in one of two sectors.
mgt: The Roy model is an economic framework introduced by Andrew Roy to analyze self-selection in occupational choices based on individual skills and technology. It was later formalized mathematically by George Borjas to study immigration, showing how selection mechanisms influence expected wages. The model has since been applied in various fields, including labor economics, where it underpins methods like the Heckman correction and serves as an alternative to the LATE framework.

trgt: The Chicago Ballers were a basketball team based in Chicago, Illinois. The team competed in the Junior Basketball Association (JBA), a league created for high school and junior college players as an alternative to the National Collegiate Athletic Association (NCAA).
mgt: The Chicago Ballers were a professional basketball team based in Chicago, Illinois, that competed in the Junior Basketball Association (JBA) during its inaugural 2018 season. Founded by LaVar Ball, the team was coached by Nicolas Colon.

trgt: Ellen Battelle Dietrick (1847–1895) was an American suffragist and author who was active in the movement's organizations in Kentucky and Massachusetts. She was a core member of the group that published The Woman's Bible in the 1890s.
mgt: Ellen Battelle Dietrick (1847 – November 25, 1895) was an American suffragist, writer, and social reformer. She was a founding vice-president of the Kentucky Equal Rights Association and held leadership roles in national and regional suffrage organizations, including the National American Woman Suffrage Association. Dietrick established organizations to support women, such as educational unions and homes for the elderly, and campaigned for civic reforms. She contributed to *The Woman's Bible* and authored works on labor relations and women's roles in Christianity, including *Women in the Early Christian Ministry*.

trgt: Hillhead High School is a day school in Glasgow, Scotland, on Oakfield Avenue, neighbouring the University of Glasgow.
mgt: Hillhead High School is a coeducational secondary school in Glasgow, Scotland, established in 1885. It became a comprehensive school in 1972, serving Glasgow's West End and beyond. The school operates from two buildings: the X-shaped, category B listed Main Building, completed in 1929, and the Terrace Building, acquired around 2001. Hillhead High School offers a range of facilities and extracurricular activities, including athletics clubs.

trgt:
Siegfried Müller is a retired German sidecarcross passenger and, together with his driver Reinhard Böhler, the first-ever World Champion in the sport.

He has also won the German national sidecarcross championship in 1980.
mgt: Siegfried Müller is a retired German sidecarcross passenger who, alongside driver Reinhard Böhler, became the first-ever FIM Sidecarcross World Champion in 1980. He also won the German championship that year.

trgt: Leon Mike (born 4 September 1981) is an English football manager and former professional player, who last managed North West Counties Football League side Burscough.
mgt: Leon Mike (born 4 September 1981) is an English former professional footballer who played as a striker for clubs including Manchester City, Aberdeen, and Mossley.

trgt: Andrea Debono (7 November 1821 – 29 October 1871), also known as Latif Effendi, was a Maltese trader and explorer who was one of the first Europeans to explore the area around the White Nile in the mid-19th century.
mgt: Andrea Debono (7 November 1821 – 29 October 1871) was a Maltese trader, explorer, and British Consular Agent in Khartoum. Known for his ivory trade and Nile explorations, he was the first European to explore the Sobat River. Debono also contributed to geographical knowledge of Lake Albert and the Semliki River. Despite his achievements, his reputation was tarnished by accusations of involvement in the slave trade, though charges were later dropped. He died in Cairo in 1871.

page_title: Soviet Union military aircraft designation systems
trgt: Pre-revolutionary Imperial Russia (before 1917) did not have a single national unified system but instead relied on those provided by the manufacturers of the aircraft, like Sikorsky Ilya Muromets or Anatra Anasal.
mgt: The Soviet Union's military aircraft designation systems evolved over time, beginning with a function-based system in 1923 and transitioning in 1940 to a system using design bureau abbreviations and sequential numbers.

trgt: Crystina Poncher (born July 20, 1984) is currently a commentator, host and reporter for Top Rank Boxing on ESPN. She previously served as host, reporter and correspondent for NFL Network and NFL.com.
mgt: Crystina Poncher is an American sports broadcaster and journalist, known as the only female play-by-play boxing broadcaster with Top Rank. She has also worked with the NFL and Fox Sports.

trgt: Raymond Duchamp-Villon (5 November 1876 – 9 October 1918) was a French sculptor.
mgt: Raymond Duchamp-Villon (1876–1918) was a French Cubist sculptor known for *The Large Horse* and his contributions to modern art.

trgt: Jamiul Uloom Jawadia, also known as Jawadia Arabic College, is a Deeni Madarasa (religious educational institution) of Shia Muslims in Banaras (Varanasi) India. Major course of studies include Jurisprudence, Theology and Islamic Literature.
mgt: Jawadia Arabic College, established in 1928 in Banaras, Uttar Pradesh, India, by Maulana Syed Mohammad Sajjad Al-Husaini, is a prominent institution for higher Islamic studies, focusing on Shia jurisprudence, theology, and literature.

trgt: Leslie Walter Gandar (26 January 1919 – 16 December 1994) was a New Zealand politician of the National Party.
mgt: Les Gandar (1919 – 16 December 1994) was a New Zealand politician, cabinet minister, educator, and diplomat who served as High Commissioner.

trgt: The story of Little Muck is a fairy tale written by Wilhelm Hauff. It was published in 1826 in a collection of fairy tales and tells the story of an outsider called Little Muck.
mgt: "Little Muck" is a German fairy tale by Wilhelm Hauff, originally published in *Arabian Days' Entertainments*. Set within a frame story, it follows the adventures of Little Muck, a physically misshapen but resourceful man who acquires magical items—a pair of slippers and a walking stick—that grant him extraordinary abilities. After being betrayed by a king, Muck uses enchanted figs to exact revenge and reclaim his items, ultimately living a prosperous but solitary life. The tale conveys themes of superficial judgment, justice, and the transformative power of storytelling, while also serving as a critique of incompetent rulers. It is associated with the Aarne–Thompson–Uther tale type ATU 566.

trgt:
Homoisoflavonoids (3-benzylidenechroman-4-ones) are a type of phenolic compounds occurring naturally in plants.

Chemically, they have the general structure of a 16-carbon skeleton, which consists of two phenyl rings (A and B) and heterocyclic ring (C).
mgt: Homoisoflavonoids are a class of naturally occurring flavonoid-related compounds found in various plants, including Portulaca oleracea, Caesalpinia sappan, and members of the Asparagaceae family. They exhibit diverse biological activities, such as anti-angiogenic, antimycobacterial, and cytotoxic properties. Homoisoflavonoids can be synthesized from compounds like 2'-hydroxydihydrochalcones and are categorized into subtypes, including homoisoflavanones, which are prominent in plants like Hyacinthaceae. These compounds have potential applications in cancer treatment and other medicinal uses.

trgt: Dottle is the remaining plug of unburnt tobacco and ashes left in the bottom of a tobacco pipe when it has been smoked.
mgt: Dottle refers to the unburned tobacco residue left in a pipe after smoking. It can result from improper packing, excessive moisture, or rapid smoking.

trgt: Stevie Anderson (born May 12, 1970) is a former American football wide receiver who played in the National Football League.
mgt: Stevie Anderson (born May 12, 1970) is a former American football wide receiver who played in the NFL from 1993 to 1997.

trgt: The Manned Spaceflight Engineer Program was an effort by the United States Air Force to train American military personnel as payload specialists for United States Department of Defense missions on the Space Shuttle program.
mgt: The Manned Spaceflight Engineer (MSE) Program was a United States Air Force initiative to train military personnel as payload specialists for classified missions aboard NASA's Space Shuttle. Established in 1979, the program aimed to support Department of Defense (DoD) payloads, with 32 MSEs selected over its duration. The program faced challenges, including strained relations with NASA, internal Air Force debates on the value of manned spaceflight, and delays in shuttle launches. Following the Challenger disaster in 1986 and a shift back to unmanned rockets, the MSE program ended in 1988, with only two MSEs having flown in space.

trgt: The United States Space Force's 4th Electromagnetic Warfare Squadron (4 EWS) is an offensive space electromagnetic warfare unit formerly located at Holloman Air Force Base, New Mexico, stood up operations July 1, 2014 at Peterson Space Force Base, Colorado.
mgt: The 4th Electromagnetic Warfare Squadron is a United States Space Force unit based at Peterson Space Force Base, Colorado. Activated in 1990, it conducts offensive counterspace operations and space situational awareness, supporting global and theater campaigns.

trgt: Sina Karimian (born 23 January 1988) is an Iranian kickboxer, fighting out of Power of Dream Gym. He is first and current K-1 WORLD GP Cruiserweight Champion.
mgt: Sina Karimian (born January 23, 1988) is an Iranian professional kickboxer and the inaugural K-1 Cruiserweight champion. Competing since 2018, he has achieved notable victories, including multiple title defenses and wins against prominent fighters such as Hisaki Kato and Kyotaro Fujimoto. Known for his orthodox stance and fighting out of Iran, Karimian has a professional record of 14 wins, including 7 by knockout, and 2 losses.

trgt: Comproportionation or synproportionation is a chemical reaction where two reactants containing the same element but with different oxidation numbers, form a compound having an intermediate oxidation number. It is the opposite of disproportionation.
mgt: Comproportionation is a chemical reaction where two species of the same element in different oxidation states react to form a single species in an intermediate oxidation state, often analyzed using Frost diagrams.

page_title: MAGI '900- Museum of Artistic and Historical Excellence
trgt: The Museo MAGI ‘900 is a private museum in Pieve di Cento, Emilia-Romagna, Italy.
mgt: The Museo MAGI '900 is a contemporary art museum in Italy, featuring over 2,000 works.

trgt: Freud's inner circle or Secret Committee consisted of Sigmund Freud's most trusted psychoanalysts.
mgt: The Secret Committee was a group of loyal psychoanalysts formed by Sigmund Freud in 1912 to safeguard psychoanalytic theory. Proposed by Ernest Jones, the committee initially included Jones, Sándor Ferenczi, Otto Rank, Hanns Sachs, and Karl Abraham, each receiving a golden ring symbolizing their commitment. Max Eitingon joined in 1919. The group aimed to address potential deviations from psychoanalytic principles and solidify Freud's inner circle, partly isolating Carl Jung. Despite functioning effectively for a decade, internal conflicts led to its dissolution in 1924. It was later reconstituted with Anna Freud replacing Rank, continuing its influence on psychoanalytic discourse.

trgt: The 62nd Ohio Infantry Regiment was an infantry regiment in the Union Army during the American Civil War.
mgt: The 62nd Ohio Infantry Regiment was a Union Army regiment during the American Civil War, active from 1861 to 1865.

trgt: Alimi Ballard (born October 17, 1977) is an American television actor. He is best known for his role as FBI agent David Sinclair on the CBS police procedural Numb3rs (2005–2010).
mgt: Alimi Ballard (born October 17, 1977) is an American actor known for roles in *Loving*, *Numb3rs*, and *Dark Angel*. He has also appeared in *NCIS* and *CSI: Crime Scene Investigation*.

trgt: In Search of Sunrise (abbreviated ISOS) is a trance music mix-compilation music CD series released on SongBird, a subdivision of Black Hole Recordings originally founded by Tiësto.
mgt: *In Search of Sunrise* is a trance music compilation series launched in 1999, originally mixed by Tiësto. Later installments featured Richard Durand and Markus Schulz as curators.

trgt: Sprung is an American crime comedy television series created by Greg Garcia. It premiered on the streaming service Amazon Freevee on August 19, 2022.
mgt: *Sprung* is an American crime comedy television series created, written, and directed by Greg Garcia. Starring Garret Dillahunt and Martha Plimpton, the show premiered on Amazon Freevee on August 19, 2022. The single-camera sitcom follows Jack, a recently released prisoner navigating life outside prison during the early days of the COVID-19 pandemic. Filmed in Pittsburgh, the series features a cast including Shakira Barrera, Phillip Garcia, and James Earl, with nine episodes in its first season.

trgt: Luise Koch (11 October 1860 Bremen – 14 March 1934 in Bremen) was a German educator, politician (DDP) and women's rights activist.
mgt: Luise Koch (11 October 1860 – 14 March 1934) was a German politician, suffragist, and educator who advocated for women's voting rights.

trgt: Perla is a Mexican telenovela produced by TV Azteca and starred Silvia Navarro and Leonardo Garcia as protagonists.
mgt: *Perla* is a 1998 Mexican telenovela created by Enrique Torres and produced by TV Azteca. Starring Silvia Navarro and Leonardo García, the series follows Perla, a young woman who assumes her late friend's identity to navigate a world of power, betrayal, and family secrets. The show aired on Azteca Trece and ran for 230 episodes.

trgt: Saint Paul Cathedral is the mother church of the Diocese of Pittsburgh in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania. St. Paul's parish was established in 1833.
mgt: Saint Paul Cathedral, located in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania, is the mother church of the Diocese of Pittsburgh. Founded in 1834, the current Gothic Revival structure, designed by Egan and Prindeville, was completed in 1906 and inspired by Cologne Cathedral. It features limestone construction, twin spires, and a capacity of 2,600 worshipers. The cathedral, situated in the Oakland neighborhood, replaced earlier iterations downtown, which were destroyed or relocated due to urban development. It houses a renowned Beckerath pipe organ and became a contributing property in the Schenley Farms Historic District in 1983.

trgt: The Oberkommando der Luftwaffe (lit. 'Upper Command of the Air Force'; abbreviated OKL) was the high command of the air force (Luftwaffe) of Nazi Germany.
mgt: The *Oberkommando der Luftwaffe* (OKL) was the High Command of Nazi Germany's Luftwaffe, established in 1935 to oversee military aviation operations. It reported to the *Oberkommando der Wehrmacht* (OKW) and played a key role in organizing and commanding air fleets, air districts, and operational units during World War II. Initially part of Hermann Göring's Ministry of Aviation, the OKL evolved into a distinct military command structure, though its organization remained fragmented. It was disbanded in 1945 following Germany's defeat.

trgt: Dmitry Tikhonovich Lisanevich (Lang-ru, 1778 – 1825) was a prominent general of Imperial Russian Army. He is best known for negotiation of The Treaty of Kurakchay and killing of Ibrahim Khalil Khan of Karabakh.
mgt: Dmitry Tikhonovich Lisanevich (1778 – July 1825) was a Russian Imperial general known for his role in the Caucasian War and other military campaigns, including the Russo-Persian and Russo-Turkish wars. Born into a poor noble family in Voronezh, he joined the military in 1793 and rose through the ranks, earning recognition for his bravery and leadership, such as during the capture of Ganja in 1804. He was involved in significant events, including the controversial killing of Ibrahim Khalil Khan in 1806. Promoted to lieutenant general in 1824, Lisanevich was appointed commander of the Caucasian Line, where he faced uprisings in Kabarda and Chechnya. He was mortally wounded in 1825 during a conflict in Chechnya. Known for his temper and nicknamed "Mad Major," Lisanevich was a decorated officer, receiving honors such as the Order of St. George and the Order of St. Vladimir.

trgt: Nothing's Real is the debut studio album by English singer Shura, released on 8 July 2016 by Polydor Records. The album was met with positive reviews from music critics.
mgt: *Nothing's Real* is the debut studio album by English singer-songwriter Shura, released on 8 July 2016 through Polydor Records. Recorded between 2014 and 2016, the electropop album features production by Shura, Greg Kurstin, Joel Pott, and Al Shux. It received critical acclaim for its '80s-inspired sound and introspective songwriting, with praise highlighting its catchy pop tracks and Shura's fresh approach to modern pop. The album debuted at number 13 on the UK Albums Chart and includes singles such as "Touch," "2Shy," and "What's It Gonna Be?"

trgt: Calamus is a genus of flowering plants in the palm family Arecaceae that are among several genera known as rattan palms. There are an estimated 400 species in this genus, all native to tropical and subtropical Asia, Africa, and Australia.
mgt: Calamus is a genus of climbing palms in the family Arecaceae, comprising over 400 species. These dioecious plants are known for their slender, reedy stems, which can grow up to 200 meters long, and their spiny climbing adaptations. Some species, such as *Calamus draco*, produce a red resin called "Dragon's blood." The stems are widely used for various purposes, including canes and furniture.

trgt: Yambio Airport is an airport serving Yambio in South Sudan.
mgt: Yambio Airport is a public, civilian airport in Yambio, South Sudan.

trgt: Eleanor Reissa is an American actress, singer, theatre director, playwright, librettist, choreographer, translator, and author based in New York City. She works and performs in English and Yiddish speaking stages, and also interprets and performs Yiddish theatre and songs.
