It debuted at number 10 on the US Billboard 200.

trgt: Paulus Vallius (Paolo Valla, Paulus Valla, Paulus de la Valle, Paulus de Valle) (1561-1622) was an Italian Jesuit logician.
mgt: Paulus Vallius was a Roman philosopher and lecturer at the Collegio Romano, whose Thomist teachings influenced Galileo's logical treatises.

trgt: François Picquet (IPA-fr; 4 December 1708 – 15 July 1781) was a French Sulpician priest who emigrated to Montreal, Canada, in 1734.
mgt: François Picquet (December 4, 1708 – 1781) was a French Sulpician missionary who worked in New France, notably founding Fort de La Présentation in 1749.

trgt: The Weatherly Model 201 is a 1960s American agricultural monoplane designed and built by the Weatherly Aviation Company of Hollister, California.
mgt: The Weatherly 201 is an American agricultural aircraft designed in the 1960s for crop dusting and liquid spraying, certified in 1967.

trgt: William Beau Mirchoff (/ˈmɜːrtʃɒf/; born January 13, 1989) is an Canadian-American actor best known for his role in the MTV series Awkward, his role as Jamie Hunter in Good Trouble, and his role in Now Apocalypse on Starz.
mgt: Beau Mirchoff (born January 13, 1989) is an American-Canadian actor known for his roles in television and film. He gained recognition for starring in MTV's *Awkward* and has appeared in *Desperate Housewives*, *The Fosters*, and *Good Trouble*.

trgt: Majha Hoshil Na (‹See RfD› transl.- Will You Be Mine?) is an Indian Marathi television drama that aired on Zee Marathi. The Story revolves around the Sai, Aditya and his Maternal uncles.
mgt: *Majha Hoshil Na* is an Indian Marathi-language television drama series that aired on Zee Marathi from 2 March 2020 to 28 August 2021. Produced by Ocean Films Company and directed by Aniket Sane, the show follows the love story of Sai, a wealthy woman, and Aditya, a middle-class man, as they navigate societal and familial challenges before and after their marriage. The series, set in Mumbai and other locations like Dapoli and Manali, gained popularity for its engaging storyline and became the top-rated Marathi show in 2020.

trgt: John Zachary Young FRS (18 March 1907 – 4 July 1997), generally known as "JZ" or "JZY", was an English zoologist and neurophysiologist, described as "one of the most influential biologists of the 20th century".
mgt: John Zachary Young FRS (18 March 1907 – 4 July 1997) was a British zoologist, anatomist, and physiologist known for his pioneering research on the nervous system, particularly his discovery of the squid giant axon and synapse, which influenced Nobel Prize-winning work on nerve signal transmission. He served as Professor of Anatomy at University College London (1945–1974) and later became Professor Emeritus. Young's contributions extended to nerve regeneration research during World War II and studies on the brain and central nervous system. He authored influential textbooks, delivered the BBC Reith Lectures in 1950, and was President of the Marine Biological Association. Young received numerous honors, including the Royal Medal and Linnean Medal, and his legacy is commemorated through the J. Z. Young Chair at UCL.

trgt: Alfred Hart Everett (11 October 1848 – 18 June 1898) was a British civil servant and administrator in Borneo as well as being a naturalist and natural history collector.
mgt: Alfred Hart Everett (1848–1898) was a British naturalist and colonial administrator known for his zoological collections in Borneo and the Philippines. He served under the White Rajahs of Sarawak and later in British North Borneo, contributing significantly to ornithology and mammalogy.

trgt: Richard Gordon Guindon (December 2, 1935 – February 27, 2022) was an American cartoonist best known for his gag panel Guindon. Guindon's cartoons have appeared in the Minneapolis Tribune, The Realist, and the Detroit Free Press.
mgt: Dick Guindon (December 2, 1935 – February 27, 2022) was an American cartoonist best known for his self-titled cartoon series, *Guindon*. His work appeared in publications like *The Nation*, *Playboy*, and *The Realist*.

trgt: Kate Addo (born 23 July 1972) is a Ghanaian journalist, broadcaster and public relations practitioner. She is currently the Director of Public Affairs of the Parliament of Ghana.
mgt: Kate Addo (born 23 July 1972) is a Ghanaian journalist, broadcaster, and public relations practitioner. She is the Director of Public Affairs at the Parliament of Ghana and founder of the Duo Concept Foundation.

trgt: HMS Modeste was a 64-gun third rate ship of the line of the Royal Navy. She was previously the 64-gun Modeste, of the French Navy, launched in 1759 and captured later that year.
mgt: HMS *Modeste* was a 64-gun third-rate ship of the line originally built for the French Navy and launched in 1759. Captured by the Royal Navy during the Battle of Lagos in August 1759, she was commissioned as HMS *Modeste* in 1760. The ship served in the Mediterranean and the Caribbean, participating in notable actions such as the capture of Martinique in 1762. After periods of active service and being laid up, she was converted into a receiving ship in 1778 and remained in this role until being broken up in 1800.

trgt: Maria Silva Cruz (20 April 1915 – 23 August 1936) was a Spanish anarchist and a hero of the Casas Viejas Uprising in Spain. She was also known as "La Libertaria."
mgt: Maria Silva Cruz (20 April 1915 – 23 August 1936), also known as "La Libertaria," was a Spanish anarchist and participant in the 1933 Casas Viejas uprising. She survived the violent suppression of the revolt, during which her grandfather's house was burned by government forces. Later imprisoned and involved with the anarchist movement, she lived in Andalusia with her partner, Miguel Pérez Cordon, and their son. Following the fascist occupation of Ronda in 1936, Silva Cruz was arrested and executed by firing squad. Her remains were never identified, and she was not officially declared dead until 2010.

trgt: Samuel Lord (1803-1889) was a British-born American retail millionaire who founded Lord & Taylor. It was the oldest luxury department store in the United States.
mgt: Samuel Lord (1803–1889) was a British-American entrepreneur and co-founder of the iconic American department store Lord & Taylor. Born in Saddleworth, Yorkshire, he emigrated to the United States in 1826, establishing a dry goods store in Manhattan that evolved into the renowned retail chain. After retiring in 1862, he returned to England, where he pursued horticulture until his death in 1889.

trgt: Come is an American alternative rock band, formed in Boston by Thalia Zedek (vocals, guitar), Chris Brokaw (guitar, vocals), Arthur Johnson (drums), and Sean O'Brien (bass).
mgt: Come is an American alternative rock and blues rock band formed in Boston, Massachusetts, in 1990. Known for their dark, emotionally intense sound and the interplay between Thalia Zedek's distressed vocals and the guitar work of Zedek and Chris Brokaw, the band released four critically acclaimed albums, including their debut *11:11* (1992). They toured with notable acts like Sonic Youth and Nirvana and received praise from artists such as Kurt Cobain and Bob Mould. After disbanding in 2001, Come reunited for several performances, including a 2013 tour celebrating the 20th anniversary of *11:11*.

trgt: György Szondy was a Hungarian soldier and the captain of Drégely Castle. He was a respected soldier, even by his Turkish foes, whose recognition can be seen by his burial by Hadim Ali Pasha with full military honours.
mgt: György Szondy (1500 – July 9, 1552) was a Hungarian military captain and national hero known for his defense of Drégely Castle during the Ottoman–Hungarian Wars. Despite being vastly outnumbered, Szondy and his small garrison resisted a 12,000-strong Ottoman force led by Hadim Ali Pasha in the 1552 siege. Refusing to surrender, Szondy fought valiantly until his death in battle. His bravery earned the respect of his Ottoman adversaries, who buried him with full military honors. Szondy's legacy endures as a symbol of courage, celebrated annually in Drégelypalánk, Hungary.

trgt: TVR Cultural (IPA-ro) was the cultural channel of Romania's government-funded television network Televiziunea Română. It provided cultural news, documentaries about the arts, as well as various shows, musicals and theatrical pieces. It was closed in September 2012.
mgt: TVR Cultural is a Romanian television channel dedicated to culture and the arts, originally launched in 2002 by Televiziunea Română. It ceased broadcasting in 2012 due to financial difficulties but was relaunched in 2022, offering programming in both SD and HD formats.

trgt: Edith Guerrier (1870–1958) was a pioneer in the field of library science. Guerrier is best known for developing progressive library programs in the 1890s, including a reading program and a pottery studio for girls of Boston's North End, an urban immigrant center during the Progressive Era.
mgt: Edith Guerrier (1870–1958) was an American librarian, social reformer, and advocate for immigrant communities. She is best known for founding the Saturday Evening Girls, a reading club for immigrant girls in Boston's North End, which evolved into the Paul Revere Pottery, providing artistic and economic opportunities. Guerrier's innovative library programs and community initiatives emphasized education, cultural integration, and gender equality.

page_title: Kidnapping of Alexandra Măceșanu and Luiza Melencu
trgt: The kidnapping of Alexandra Măceșanu and Luiza Melencu happened in Caracal, Olt County, Romania on 25 July 2019. Romanian police were heavily criticized for taking 19 hours to locate and search the building where she was held captive. The handling of the case met widespread outrage and protests, which led to the dismissal of Interior Minister Nicolae Moga and other officials.
mgt: The kidnapping of Alexandra Măceșanu and Luiza Melencu refers to the 2019 abductions of two Romanian teenagers, Alexandra Măceșanu and Luiza Melencu, by Gheorghe Dincă, a 65-year-old mechanic. Alexandra managed to call emergency services while in captivity, but authorities delayed their response, leading to widespread criticism. Dincă confessed to murdering both girls, though doubts remain about his claims, with allegations of human trafficking and corruption surrounding the case. The incident sparked national outrage, protests, and government resignations.

trgt: Mount Kineo is a prominent geological feature located on a 1,150-acre (470 ha) peninsula that extends from the easterly shore of Moosehead Lake in the northern forest of Maine. With 700-foot (210 m) cliffs rising straight up from the water, it is the central feature of Mount Kineo State Park, a protected area of 800 acres (320 ha) managed by the Maine Department of Agriculture, Conservation and Forestry.
mgt: Mount Kineo is a prominent mountain in Piscataquis County, Maine, with an elevation of 1,789 feet (545 m) and a rich geological and cultural history. Known for its large rhyolite formation, used by Indigenous peoples for crafting tools and weapons, the mountain has been a significant site for centuries. Accessible only by water, it is part of a state park featuring hiking trails and the historic Mount Kineo Golf Course, established in 1893.

trgt: Erdinç Tekir (born November 17, 1966) is a Turkish member of the IHH of Abkhaz origin. He was previously convicted for his participation at the Black Sea hostage crisis. He worked for the IHH since he was released from prison and joined the Gaza Flotilla.
mgt: Erdinç Tekir (born November 17, 1966) is a Turkish activist and former member of the Islamic youth organization Milli Gençlik Vakfı. He participated in the Georgian–Abkhazian conflict and was involved in the 1996 hijacking of the MV *Avrasya*. After serving prison time, he joined the İHH Humanitarian Relief Foundation, where he worked on various campaigns. Tekir was aboard the *Mavi Marmara* during the 2010 Gaza flotilla raid, where he was injured by Israeli forces.

page_title: King Mongkut's Institute of Technology Ladkrabang
trgt: King Mongkut's Institute of Technology Ladkrabang (KMITL or KMIT Ladkrabang for short) is a research and educational institution in Thailand. It is situated in Lat Krabang District, Bangkok approximately 30 km east of the city center. The university consists of nine faculties: engineering, architecture, science, industrial education and technology, agricultural technology, information technology, food industry, liberal arts, and medicine.
mgt: King Mongkut's Institute of Technology Ladkrabang (KMITL) is a public research university in Bangkok, Thailand, established in 1960. Initially founded as a telecommunications training center with Japanese support, it evolved into a leading institution for science and technology education. KMITL offers diverse programs, conducts advanced research, and collaborates with international partners, including Japan and Carnegie Mellon University, to drive innovation and development.

trgt: Ko.Si. Mani (13 September 1929 – 2 December 2016) was an Indian politician who was the minister for co-operation, statistics and ex-servicemen in the Tamil Nadu state of India between 2006 and 2011 Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam (DMK) regime. He was instrumental in developing kumbakonam fisheries market and darasuram vegetable market.
mgt: Ko. Si. Mani (13 September 1929 – 2 December 2016) was an Indian politician and a senior member of the Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam (DMK). Representing the Kumbakonam constituency, he was elected to the Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly four times and served as a minister. His career included allegations of corruption and legal controversies.

page_title: Zar je važno da l' se peva ili pjeva... World Tour
trgt: Zar je važno da l' se peva ili pjeva. World Tour is the fifth headlining concert tour by Serbian singer Lepa Brena, in support of her eighteenth studio album, Zar je važno dal se peva ili pjeva (2018). The tour is set to begin on 11 November 2017, in Vienna, Austria, at the Lugner City. The tour was also included some festival concerts.
mgt: The *Zar je važno da l' se peva ili pjeva... World Tour* was a concert tour by Serbian singer Lepa Brena, held from November 11, 2017, to August 10, 2022. Supporting her album of the same name, the tour included 95 shows across Europe, North America, and Australia. Highlights included sold-out performances at Belgrade's Štark Arena and adjustments due to the COVID-19 pandemic.

trgt: Brooke High School is a public high school in Wellsburg, West Virginia, United States. It is the only high school in the Brooke County School District. Athletic teams compete as the Brooke Bruins in the WVSSAC Class AAA as well as the Ohio Valley Athletic Conference.
mgt: Brooke High School is a public, coeducational high school in Wellsburg, West Virginia, established in 1969 through the consolidation of three local schools. Part of the Brooke County School District, it serves grades 9–12 and is known for its athletic achievements, including state championships in basketball, football, and cheerleading, as well as its award-winning marching band.

trgt: 1939 Battle of Westerplatte, originally titled in Poland as Tajemnica Westerplatte ("The Secret of Westerplatte"), is a 2013 Polish-Lithuanian war film written and directed by Paweł Chochlew. It portrays the Battle of Westerplatte between the Polish Army and Nazi German forces at the start of World War II in 1939. The film focuses on the conflict between Westerplatte commander Henryk Sucharski (Michał Żebrowski) and his deputy Franciszek Dąbrowski (Robert Żołędziewski).
mgt: *1939 Battle of Westerplatte* is a 2013 Polish-Lithuanian war film directed by Paweł Chochlew, depicting the Battle of Westerplatte during the Nazi invasion of Poland. The film sparked controversy for its perceived revisionist portrayal of the battle, with some critics labeling it "anti-Polish" and offensive to the honor of Polish soldiers. Despite this, others praised it as a tribute to the defenders' heroism. The film's title and marketing underwent changes to address criticism.

trgt: Dr Abdulai Osman Conteh (born 5 August 1945) is a lawyer and politician from Sierra Leone. He is a former vice president of Sierra Leone, who served under President Joseph Saidu Momoh before he was ousted by the military junta in 1992. Conteh later spent a considerable number of years in Belize, where he served as the country's chief justice before returning to Sierra Leone.
mgt: Abdulai Osman Conteh (born August 5, 1945) is a Sierra Leonean lawyer, politician, and jurist who served as the 9th Chief Justice of Belize from 2000 to 2010. He previously held prominent political roles in Sierra Leone, including Minister of Foreign Affairs, Minister of Finance, Attorney-General, and First Vice-President. Conteh is known for his landmark rulings on constitutional and indigenous land rights in Belize and his judicial service in the Caribbean.

trgt:
Prince Global Sports, LLC is an American sporting goods manufacturing company based in Atlanta, Georgia. Founded in 1970, Prince's range of products includes rackets, footwear, apparel, tennis balls, pickleball paddles, stringing machines, hats and bags.

Prince is the leading global manufacturer of tennis rackets.
mgt: Prince Sports is an American sporting goods company founded in 1970 by Robert H. McClure in Princeton, New Jersey. Known for its innovative tennis equipment, including the first oversized tennis racket, the company produces rackets, footwear, apparel, and accessories. After undergoing multiple ownership changes and a Chapter 11 bankruptcy in 2012, Prince was acquired by Authentic Brands Group and later licensed to the Waitt Company. In 2014, the company relocated its headquarters to Atlanta, Georgia, citing the city's vibrant tennis community.

trgt: Emotional is the debut album by American R&B singer Carl Thomas. It was released by Bad Boy Records and Arista Records on April 18, 2000, in the United States. Emotional earned Thomas a nomination in the Best R&B/Soul Album at the 2001 Soul Train Music Awards, also garnering him a Male & Best R&B/Soul or Rap New Artist nod. "I Wish" was also nominated for Best R&B/Soul Single.
mgt: *Emotional* is the debut studio album by American R&B singer Carl Thomas, released on April 18, 2000, by Bad Boy and Arista Records. Featuring production from Sean Combs, Mike City, and others, the album blends romantic themes with influences from 1970s and 1980s soul. It received critical acclaim, with *Rolling Stone* naming it one of the Top 50 Albums of 2000. The singles "I Wish" and "Summer Rain" achieved chart success.

trgt: Gambling in New Jersey includes casino gambling in Atlantic City, the New Jersey Lottery, horse racing, off-track betting, charity gambling, amusement games, and social gambling. New Jersey's gambling laws are among the least restrictive in the United States. In 2013, the state began to allow in-state online gambling. Five years later, the state won a lawsuit that dismantled Nevada's monopoly on legal sports betting.
mgt: Gambling in New Jersey has a long history, with the state often being more permissive than others. Early lotteries funded military efforts and educational institutions, while horse racing dates back to the 1830s. Modern gambling expanded with the legalization of racetrack betting, the New Jersey Lottery, and Atlantic City casinos. The state also pioneered online gambling and legalized sports betting in 2018, becoming a leader in both industries.

trgt:
The University of Silesia in Katowice (Lang-pl) is an autonomous state-run university in Silesia Province, Katowice, Poland.

The university offers higher education and research facilities. It offers undergraduate, masters, and PhD degree programs, as well as postgraduate, postdoctoral research, habilitation, and continuous education and training programs.
mgt: The University of Silesia in Katowice (Uniwersytet Śląski w Katowicach) is a public university in Poland, established on June 8, 1968. With campuses in Katowice, Sosnowiec, Cieszyn, and Chorzów, it offers diverse programs across multiple faculties. The university emphasizes international collaboration, ecological challenges, and regional traditions, and is home to the modern Scientific Information Centre and Academic Library.

trgt: Ningali Josie Lawford, also known as Ningali Lawford-Wolf and Josie Ningali Lawford, (1967 – 11 August 2019) was an Aboriginal Australian actress known for her roles in the films Rabbit-Proof Fence (2002), Bran Nue Dae (2009), and Last Cab to Darwin (2015), for which she was nominated for the AACTA Award for Best Actress in a Leading Role.
mgt: Ningali Josie Lawford (1967 – 11 August 2019) was an Australian actor and member of the Walmadjari people. Known for her work in theatre, film, and television, she gained acclaim for her one-woman show *Ningali*, which toured internationally. Lawford appeared in films such as *Rabbit-Proof Fence* and *Last Cab to Darwin* and voiced Nanna in *Little J & Big Cuz*.

trgt: Hansdiha Junction railway station is a railway station on the Dumka–Bhagalpur line of Sahibganj loop under Malda railway division of the Eastern Railway Zone. The station is situated beside Hansdiha-Ramgarh Road, Kasba, Hansdiha in Dumka district in the Indian state of Jharkhand.
mgt: Hansdiha railway station is a railway station in Dumka district, Jharkhand, India, operated by the Eastern Railway zone of Indian Railways. Opened in 2012, it lies on the Dumka–Bhagalpur line and is part of the under-construction Jasidih–Pirpainti line.

trgt:
Stephen James Kmetko (born February 16, 1953) is an American broadcast and entertainment journalist. First working as a news anchor and reporter in the US midwest, Kmetko moved to Los Angeles, California and embarked on a 30-year career as an entertainment journalist and television news show host.

Kmetko came out as gay in 1999, becoming one of the first prominent openly gay journalists in America.
mgt: Steve Kmetko (born February 16, 1953) is an American broadcast journalist and entertainment reporter known for his work as an anchor on E! News Daily and E! News Weekend, where he covered major events like the Academy Awards and Cannes Film Festival. Beginning his career in local news, he transitioned to entertainment reporting, hosting programs such as *Studio 22* and contributing to *CBS This Morning*. Kmetko later worked with Q Television Network and KTTV Fox 11 News. Openly gay, he has been an advocate for LGBTQ+ visibility in media.

trgt: Uzel Holding (also known as Uzel Group and Uzel Corporation) was one of Turkey's oldest and largest manufacturing companies. Its main sector was agricultural machinery, although it eventually branched out into the automotive industry and real estate through its venture capital arm, Argentum. The company was founded in 1937. However, its roots went back to the middle of the nineteenth century. Uzel Holding collapsed in 2010 and was declared bankrupt in July 2012.
mgt: Uzel Holding was a Turkish agricultural machinery and automotive company founded in 1937 by Ibrahim Uzel in Bursa, Turkey. It grew from producing leaf springs to becoming one of the world's largest tractor manufacturers, with operations in over 75 countries and notable acquisitions, including Gebrüder Holder GmbH and a stake in the Ursus Tractor Factory. Despite its global success, financial difficulties and internal disputes led to its collapse in 2011 and bankruptcy in 2012.

trgt: The second season of the American reality competition show Top Chef Masters was announced on October 22, 2009. In addition, it was announced that Gail Simmons would be added as a critic. The season premiered on April 7, 2010 with 22 chefs competing against each other in weekly challenges. In the season finale that premiered on June 9, 2010, Marcus Samuelsson was crowned Top Chef Master.
mgt: The second season of *Top Chef Masters* premiered on Bravo from April 7 to June 9, 2010, featuring 22 chefs competing in culinary challenges to win money for their chosen charities. Hosted in a 10-episode format, the season included Quickfire and Elimination challenges, with the latter determining progression to the Champions' Round. Marcus Samuelsson emerged as the winner, earning $100,000 for his charity.

trgt: Ruby Romaine is a fictional character portrayed by Tracey Ullman on her show Tracey Takes On. The character became so popular that HBO greenlit a pilot for a potential Ruby Romaine spin-off series resulting in the one-off television special, Tracey Ullman in the Trailer Tales in 2003. Ruby is a self-proclaimed "star maker".
mgt: Ruby Romaine is a fictional character portrayed by Tracey Ullman in the television series *Tracey Takes On...* and its spin-off *Tracey Ullman in the Trailer Tales*. A brash, hard-living Hollywood makeup artist, Ruby has worked in the film and television industry for over 50 years, with over 720 credits to her name. Known for her colorful personality, Ruby recounts her tumultuous life, including her early days in Hollywood, numerous affairs with actors, and her unconventional family history. The character was inspired by real-life Hollywood makeup artists and figures from Ullman’s experiences, blending humor and satire to create a vivid portrayal of "Hollywood white trash."

trgt: Princess Jane Mpologoma Nabanakulya is a daughter of Uganda's first President Mutesa II of Buganda. She was born in Sunga Village, Buyaga County, Bunyoro-Kitara Kingdom, on April 12, 1964 by Omuzaana Naome Nanyonga, a midwife of Major General Sir Mutesa II of Buganda.
mgt: Jane Mpologoma Nabanakulya (born April 12, 1964) is a Ugandan princess, nurse, and pastor. The daughter of Mutesa II of Buganda and Naome Nanyonga, she grew up in Sunga Village, Uganda, and pursued nursing studies in Uganda, Greece, and Sweden. She currently resides in London, United Kingdom, where she co-pastors a Manmin Church with her husband, David Segawa Mukasa.

trgt: Masters students are an important element of the Aston Centre for Europe and are actively encouraged to take part in the events, seminars, debates and workshops that ACE offers throughout the academic year. ACE delivers a suite of 5 academic programmes at Masters (MA) level.
mgt: The Aston Centre for Europe (ACE) is a research center at Aston University, established in 2009. Co-directed by Professors Simon Green, John Gaffney, and Dr. Nat Copsey, it focuses on European policy, integration, and leadership, offering conferences, workshops, and collaborative Master's programs.

trgt: Otto Eisenschiml (June 16, 1880 – December 7, 1963) was an Austrian-born chemist and industrial executive in the American oil industry, and a controversial author. He may be best known for his provocative 1937 book on the assassination of Abraham Lincoln in which he proposed that a senior member of Lincoln's Cabinet orchestrated the plot to kill the president.
mgt: Otto Eisenschiml (June 16, 1880 – December 7, 1963) was an Austrian-American industrial chemist, inventor, and historian. Known for his work in the chemical and oil industries, he also gained fame for his controversial theories on the assassination of Abraham Lincoln, published in his 1937 book *Why Was Lincoln Murdered?*. His theories, though widely discredited, sparked significant public interest and inspired further works in popular culture.

trgt: Melchior Inchofer or Imhofer, in Hungarian: Inchofer Menyhért (c. 1584 – 28 September 1648) was an Austrian-Hungarian Jesuit. He played an important part in the trial of Galileo, by his arguments, later published in his Tractatus Syllepticus, that Galileo was an advocate of the Copernican system. His role in the Galileo affair is being reassessed in the light of fresh documentary evidence.
mgt: Melchior Inchofer (1584–1648) was a Hungarian Jesuit theologian, writer, and professor known for his role in the 1633 trial of Galileo, where he concluded that Galileo's *Dialogue* supported Copernicanism. Born to Lutheran parents, he converted to Catholicism and joined the Jesuits, teaching philosophy and theology. Inchofer authored significant works on ecclesiastical history, Latin, and astronomy, and contributed to the study of historical sources.

trgt: Wintley Augustus Phipps, Sr. (born January 7, 1955) is a Trinidadian-American singer, songwriter, record producer, and minister, and founder of the U.S. Dream Academy, Songs of Freedom Publishing Company, and Coral Records Recording Company. He features a booming bass-baritone voice, usually singing inspirational gospel music. He is an ordained Seventh-day Adventist minister.
mgt: Wintley Augustus Phipps (born January 7, 1955) is a Trinidadian-American gospel singer, minister, and entrepreneur. A Seventh-day Adventist pastor, he has performed for numerous U.S. presidents and global figures, including Nelson Mandela and Mother Teresa. Phipps is the founder of the U.S. Dream Academy, a nonprofit supporting at-risk youth through mentoring and education.

trgt: The Braille pattern dots-1456 (⠹) is a 6-dot braille cell with both top, the middle right, and bottom right dots raised, or an 8-dot braille cell with both top, the upper-middle right, and lower-middle right dots raised. It is represented by the Unicode code point U+2839, and in Braille ASCII with a question mark:?.
mgt: Braille pattern dots-1456 is a braille cell used in unified international braille to represent the voiceless dental fricative /θ/. It is also utilized in 8-dot braille systems, such as Gardner-Salinas and Luxembourgish Braille, and is related to various patterns in Japanese kantenji braille for representing kanji characters.

trgt: Eumenes fraternus is a species of potter wasp in the subfamily Eumeninae of the family Vespidae. It is native to the eastern United States and Canada. The female builds a miniature pot out of mud in which it lays an egg and places a live caterpillar. Its developing larva feeds on this whereas the adult wasp feeds primarily on nectar.
mgt: *Eumenes fraternus* is a species of potter wasp in the family Vespidae, found in the eastern United States and Canada. Recognizable by its slender first abdominal segment and black coloration with ivory markings, it builds distinctive pitcher-shaped mud nests for its larvae. Adults primarily feed on nectar and pollen, while larvae consume paralyzed caterpillars provided by the female.

trgt: Christian Poncelet (24 March 1928 – 11 September 2020) was a conservative French politician. A member of President Nicolas Sarkozy's Union for a Popular Movement (UMP), he was President of the Senate from 1998 to 2008. In addition to being a Senator, he was Mayor of Remiremont (Vosges) and has been the President of the General Council of Vosges.
mgt: Christian Poncelet (24 March 1928 – 11 September 2020) was a French politician who served as President of the French Senate from 1998 to 2008. A member of the Union for a Popular Movement (UMP), he represented Vosges in the National Assembly and later in the Senate. Poncelet was awarded the Grand Cross of the Royal Norwegian Order of Merit in 2000.

trgt:
Harvey Livett (born 4 January 1997) is a professional rugby league footballer who plays as a second-row forward or centre for the Huddersfield Giants in the Betfred Super League, and the England Knights at international level.

He played for the Warrington Wolves in the Super League, and spent time on loan from Warrington at the Rochdale Hornets in the Championship and Hull Kingston Rovers in the Super League.
mgt: Harvey Livett (born 4 January 1997) is an English professional rugby league player who plays as a second-row or centre for the Huddersfield Giants in the Super League. He began his career with the Warrington Wolves, making his Super League debut in 2017 and playing in the 2018 Challenge Cup Final. Livett has also played for Hull Kingston Rovers and Salford, and represented the England Knights internationally.

page_title: Volleyball at the 2020 Summer Olympics – Men's Asian qualification
trgt: The Asian Qualification Tournament for the 2020 Men's Olympic Volleyball Tournament was a volleyball tournament for men's national teams held in Jiangmen, China from 7 to 12 January 2020. 8 teams played in the tournament and the winners Iran qualified for the 2020 Summer Olympics (postponed to 2021 due to the COVID-19 pandemic).
mgt: The Men's Asian qualification tournament for volleyball at the 2020 Summer Olympics was held in Jiangmen, China, from 7 to 12 January 2020. Eight teams competed, with Iran winning their second title and China finishing as runners-up. The tournament featured 15 matches and served as a qualifier for the Olympics.

trgt: Eugène-René Poubelle (15 April 1831 – 15 July 1907) was a French lawyer and diplomat who introduced waste containers to Paris and made their use compulsory. This introduction was so innovative at the time that Poubelle's surname became synonymous with waste bins (la poubelle) and remains the most common French word for a bin.
mgt: Eugène-René Poubelle (15 April 1831 – 15 July 1907) was a French lawyer, university lecturer, and government administrator, best known for introducing the dustbin, or "poubelle," to France during his tenure as préfet of the Seine. His 1884 decree mandated waste sorting and collection in Paris, revolutionizing urban sanitation. Poubelle also championed direct sewer connections to combat cholera. Later, he served as ambassador to the Vatican and advocated for Southern France's wine industry. His legacy includes the widespread use of the word "poubelle" for trash bins and commemorations such as a Parisian street and a Google Doodle celebrating his 190th birthday.

trgt:
Blaže Koneski (Lang-mk; 19 December 1921 – 7 December 1993) was a Macedonian poet, writer, literary translator, and linguistic scholar.

His major contribution was to the codification of standard Macedonian. He is the key figure who shaped Macedonian literature and intellectual life in the country however he has also been accused of serbianizing the Macedonian standard language.
mgt: Blaže Koneski (born Blagoje Ljamević; 19 December 1921 – 7 December 1993) was a Macedonian writer, translator, linguistic scholar, and key figure in the codification of the standard Macedonian language. A professor and rector at Ss. Cyril and Methodius University of Skopje, he contributed significantly to Macedonian literature and linguistics. Koneski authored influential works on Macedonian grammar and history, translated major literary works, and received numerous international awards. Despite his achievements, his role in standardizing Macedonian has been criticized for alleged Serbianization.

trgt: Sex, Money and Hip-Hop is the second studio album by rapper and Slaughterhouse member KXNG CROOKED (also known as Crooked I). The album was released on December 16, 2014 and it was executive produced by KXNG CROOKED himself along with Mike Smith and Jonathan Hay.
mgt: *Sex, Money and Hip-Hop* is a studio album by American rapper KXNG Crooked, released on December 16, 2014. Described as a "mixtape experience," the album features collaborations with Slaughterhouse and includes socially conscious tracks like "I Can't Breathe," addressing the Eric Garner case.

trgt: Wrocław Global Forum (WGF) is an annual transatlantic conference centered on topics related to democracy, prosperity and security. Launched in 2010, the event is held in Wrocław, Poland. It is organized by the Atlantic Council, the Polish Institute of International Affairs and the city of Wrocław. The most recent Forum took place on June 2–3, 2016.
mgt: The Wrocław Global Forum (WGF) is an annual international conference held in Wrocław, Poland, since 2010. Organized with support from the Atlantic Council and other institutions, it focuses on transatlantic dialogue, global challenges, and security issues. The event features high-profile speakers, the Atlantic Council's Freedom Awards, and is supported by governments and corporate sponsors.

page_title: Our Lady and St Peter's Church, Bridlington
trgt: Our Lady and St Peter's Church is a Roman Catholic parish church in Bridlington, East Riding of Yorkshire, England. It was built from 1893 to 1894 in the Gothic Revival style. It is located on the corner of Victoria Road and Wycliffe Lane, close to the town centre. It is a Grade II listed building.
mgt: Our Lady and St Peter's Church is a Roman Catholic parish church in Bridlington, England. Built in the Gothic Revival style, it was designed by Smith, Brodrick & Lowther and completed in 1894. The church, dedicated to Mary and Saint Peter, is Grade II listed and serves the Our Lady Star of the Sea parish alongside churches in Eastfield and Filey.

page_title: Faculty of Informatics and Statistics, University of Economics in Prague
trgt: The Faculty of Informatics and Statistics (FIS VŠE), also known as the School of Informatics and Statistics, is the fourth of six faculties at Prague University of Economics and Business. The faculty was established in 1991, following the dissolution of the Faculty of Direction. Its academic focus is informatics, statistics, econometrics and other mathematical methods applied to business practice. The faculty has eight departments and several research laboratories, and hosts around 2,500 students across its programs.
mgt: The Faculty of Informatics and Statistics at the University of Economics in Prague, established in 1991, is a public institution located in Prague, Czech Republic. Led by Dean prof. Ing. Jakub Fischer, Ph.D., the faculty has 2,490 students and offers Bachelor, Master, and doctoral programs in fields such as Informatics, Econometrics, and Statistics. It collaborates with institutions like the Czech Technical University, the Academy of Sciences, the Czech Statistical Office, and international universities.

trgt: Joel Spitzer (born c. 1957) is an American smoking cessation educator. He currently serves as technical advisor at WhyQuit.com, a free nicotine dependence recovery website. All of his videos and writings on WhyQuit.com end with his advice, "Never Take Another Puff!"
mgt: Joel Spitzer is an American smoking cessation educator and advocate known for promoting the "cold turkey" method of quitting smoking. Since 1972, he has conducted over 350 stop-smoking clinics and written extensively on smoking cessation, including the free book *Never Take Another Puff*, which has been downloaded over 2.4 million times. Spitzer began his career as a volunteer speaker for the American Cancer Society at age 14 and later coordinated smoking prevention programs at Rush North Shore Medical Center and other health departments. He opposes nicotine replacement therapies, advocating instead for quitting without gradual reduction.

trgt: Dead P.A. is a live electronic musical group typically referred to as a Live P.A. based out of Houston, Texas. Dead P.A. was founded in January 2003 by Jason Walsh and George Blitch. Dead P.A. performs live dancefloor oriented music and is distinguished for thematic sets. Their music is self described as "Drum and Breaks."
mgt: Dead P.A. is an electronic dance music act from Houston, Texas, formed in 2003 by Jason Walsh and George Blitch. Known for their dynamic live performances, the group incorporates continuous mixes, theatrics, and live percussion. They have toured extensively, performed at major festivals, and received accolades, including a Revolutions Music Award for "Best Live Performance" in 2009.

trgt: The 2nd Infantry Brigade was a formation of the New Zealand Military Forces raised for service abroad with the New Zealand Expeditionary Force during the First World War. As part of the New Zealand Division, it participated in several major battles on the Western Front from 1916 to 1918 before being disbanded in 1919. There was also a 2nd Infantry Brigade in the New Zealand Territorial Force that existed from 1924 to 1964.
mgt: The 2nd Infantry Brigade was a formation of the New Zealand Military Forces that served during the First World War and later as part of the Territorial Force. Formed in 1916, it was part of the New Zealand Division and fought on the Western Front in major battles such as Messines and Passchendaele. Disbanded in 1919, it was re-established in 1924 as a Territorial Force unit, serving until its final disbandment in 1964.

trgt: 2011 MD is a bright micro-asteroid, classified as near-Earth object of the Apollo and Amor group, respectively. On 27 June 2011, at around 17:00 UTC (13:00 EDT), the object passed exceptionally close to Earth's surface at a distance of approximately 12,000 kilometers (7,500 mi), roughly the diameter of the Earth.
mgt: 2011 MD is a small near-Earth asteroid, approximately 6 meters (20 feet) in diameter, discovered on June 22, 2011, by the Lincoln Near-Earth Asteroid Research (LINEAR) program. Initially mistaken for space debris, it was later confirmed as an asteroid. During its close approach to Earth on June 27, 2011, it passed within 0.1 lunar distances, altering its orbital period. Observations revealed it to be a porous rubble pile with a density similar to water. The asteroid posed no threat to Earth, as it would have burned up in the atmosphere if it entered. In 2014, NASA identified 2011 MD as a potential candidate for the Asteroid Redirect Mission.

trgt: Zhenskii vestnik (Russian: Women's Herald) was a Russian language monthly feminist magazine which was published in Saint Petersburg in the period 1904–1917. Its subtitle was Soiuz zhenshchin, Jus suffragii. The magazine billed itself as monthly social scientific and literary journal on equality and advancement of women.
mgt: *Zhenskii vestnik* was a Russian feminist magazine founded by Mariia Ivanovna Pokrovskaia in 1904 and published monthly in Saint Petersburg until 1917. It served as the official organ of the Union of Women until 1907, promoting women's independence and critiquing male-dominated political movements.

trgt: Lakes Mall was an enclosed shopping mall in Lauderdale Lakes, Florida. Opened in 1972, the shopping center initially featured Jefferson Ward and Britt's discount stores, and later included the first locations for the retail chains Office Depot and Sports Authority. The complex was torn down in 1995 and redeveloped as a strip mall.
mgt: Lakes Mall was a shopping center in Lauderdale Lakes, Florida, that opened in 1972 and was demolished in 1996. Originally anchored by Jefferson Ward and Britt's, the mall faced declining business in the 1980s due to competition and tenant closures. After partial redevelopment, the site now hosts retailers like Ross Dress for Less and Burlington Coat Factory.

trgt: Binghamstown (Irish: An Geata Mór) is a townland and village in County Mayo, Ireland. It lies on the R313 regional road on the Mullet Peninsula, near the town of Belmullet. The townland of Binghamstown has an area of approximately 3 square kilometres (1 sq mi), and had a population of 106 people as of the 2011 census. Binghamstown is in the electoral division of An Geata Mór Thuaidh.
mgt: Binghamstown (Irish: An Geata Mór) is a townland and village in County Mayo, Ireland, located on the Mullet Peninsula near Saleen Harbour. Established in 1796 by Major Denis Bingham as a "landlord village" for the Bingham estate, it was originally named Ballymacshedon before being renamed. The village features historical connections to the Bingham family, including the now-ruined Binghamstown House, and is associated with the Irish name meaning "the Big Gate."

trgt: Josef Kiefel (2 October 1909 – 11 March 1988) was a German communist who participated in the German Resistance against Hitler and fought with Soviet partisans against the Nazis. After the creation of East Germany in 1949, Kiefel served as a Stasi officer.
mgt: Josef Kiefel (October 2, 1909 – 1988) was a German Communist and intelligence officer. After fleeing to the Soviet Union during the Nazi rise to power, he served as a Soviet-trained partisan during World War II. Postwar, he helped establish East Germany's state security infrastructure and led the Stasi's counterintelligence department from 1953 to 1960.

trgt: Hochschild Kohn's, also known as Hochschild-Kohn or simply Hochschild's, was a 20th-century American department store chain based in Baltimore, Maryland. It was started in 1897 as a partnership between Max Hochschild, Benno Kohn, and his brother Louis B. Kohn. Hochschild-Kohn & Company opened that year with a downtown-Baltimore store on the northwest corner of Howard and Lexington Streets. The chain closed in 1984.
mgt: Hochschild Kohn's was a Baltimore-based department store chain founded in 1897 by Max Hochschild, Benno Kohn, and Louis B. Kohn. Once the city's largest department store, it expanded into suburban locations, pioneering retail growth in the region. The company faced financial challenges during the Great Depression and underwent ownership changes before closing in 1984. Its Belvedere store was listed on the National Register of Historic Places in 2003.

trgt: The Lipetsk fighter-pilot school (Lang-de), also known as WIWUPAL from its German codename Wissenschaftliche Versuchs- und Personalausbildungsstation "Scientific Experimental and Personnel Training Station", was a secret training school for fighter pilots operated by the German Reichswehr at Lipetsk, Soviet Union, because Germany was prohibited by the Treaty of Versailles from operating an air force and sought alternative means to continue training and development for the future Luftwaffe. It is now the site of Lipetsk Air Base.
mgt: The Lipetsk fighter-pilot school was a secret German military aviation training facility operated in the Soviet Union from 1926 to 1933. Established under the Treaty of Rapallo, it allowed Germany to circumvent the Treaty of Versailles' restrictions on military aviation. Located at Lipetsk Air Base, the school trained 120 German fighter pilots, hundreds of personnel, and also supported Soviet pilot training. It closed in 1933 due to shifting political dynamics and the rise of the Nazi regime.

trgt:
Nassau County Route 8 is an unsigned county road in Nassau County, New York. It travels between Old Country Road (CR 25) and Rockaway Avenue (CR E06) in Garden City and Northern Boulevard (NY 25A) in Manhasset.

Nassau County Route 8 travels along Herricks Road and Shelter Rock Road, is roughly five miles (8.0 km) in length, and is maintained by the Nassau County Department of Public Works.
mgt: County Route 8 (CR 8) is a 5-mile (8.05 km) county road in Nassau County, New York, maintained by the Nassau County Department of Public Works. It consists of two segments: Herricks Road and Shelter Rock Road, running through several communities, including Garden City, Mineola, and North Hills. Notably, the Herricks Road grade crossing was the site of a fatal 1982 train crash, leading to its eventual replacement with an underpass in 1998.

trgt: Worms Blast is a puzzle video game for Microsoft Windows, PlayStation 2, GameCube, Game Boy Advance and Mac OS X released in 2002, developed by Team17, and published by Ubi Soft. The Mac version was developed and published by Feral Interactive.
mgt: *Worms Blast* is a 2002 puzzle video game developed by Team17 and published by Ubi Soft, with Feral Interactive handling the Mac OS X version. Part of the *Worms* series, it was released for multiple platforms, including Windows, PlayStation 2, GameCube, Game Boy Advance, and Mac OS X. The gameplay combines elements of *Puzzle Bobble* with unique mechanics, such as weapon-based block destruction and character-specific stats. It features single-player puzzle modes, multiplayer battles, and the distinctive "BLAST mode." While praised for its innovative gameplay, it received criticism for its high difficulty level, which some reviewers found frustrating for novice players.

trgt: Charles Percy Snow, Baron Snow, Kt, CBE, FRSL (15 October 1905 – 1 July 1980) was an English novelist and physical chemist who also served in several important positions in the British Civil Service and briefly in the UK government. He is best known for his series of novels known collectively as Strangers and Brothers, and for The Two Cultures, a 1959 lecture in which he laments the gulf between scientists and "literary intellectuals".
mgt: C. P. Snow, Baron Snow (15 October 1905 – 1 July 1980), was an English physicist, novelist, and public intellectual. He is best known for his "Strangers and Brothers" series of novels, which explore academic and political life, and for his influential Rede Lecture, *The Two Cultures* (1959), which highlighted the divide between the sciences and the humanities. A Fellow of Christ's College, Cambridge, Snow also held prominent civil service roles, was knighted in 1957, and became a life peer in 1964.

trgt: The Gonzaga Bulldogs (/ɡənˈzæɡə/) (also known unofficially as the Zags) are the intercollegiate athletic teams representing Gonzaga University, located in Spokane, Washington, United States. Gonzaga competes in the National Collegiate Athletic Association (NCAA) Division I as a member of the West Coast Conference.
mgt: The Gonzaga Bulldogs are the athletic teams representing Gonzaga University in Spokane, Washington. Competing in the NCAA's West Coast Conference (WCC), the Bulldogs field 18 varsity teams. Known for their powerhouse men's basketball program, Gonzaga has made 22 consecutive NCAA tournament appearances and achieved national prominence. The university's mascot is Spike the Bulldog, with "Zags" serving as an unofficial nickname. Historically, Gonzaga also had notable football and boxing programs, with football ending in 1941 and boxing in 1952.

trgt: The Cello Concerto is a composition for cello and orchestra by the Finnish composer Esa-Pekka Salonen. The work was co-commissioned by the Chicago Symphony Orchestra, the New York Philharmonic, the Barbican Centre, and the Elbphilharmonie. It was completed in 2017 and was first performed by the cellist Yo-Yo Ma and the Chicago Symphony Orchestra under the direction of Salonen on March 9, 2017. The piece is dedicated to Yo-Yo Ma.
mgt: The *Cello Concerto* by Esa-Pekka Salonen is a 2017 composition for solo cello and orchestra, incorporating live electronics. Written for cellist Yo-Yo Ma, the concerto is structured in three movements and explores themes of cosmic imagery and opposing forces, blending lyrical passages with dynamic, dance-like sections. Praised for its innovative orchestration and technical demands, the work has received mixed critical reception, with some highlighting its creativity and others questioning its emotional depth.

trgt:
The 1912–13 season was Blackpool F.C.'s 16th season (13th consecutive) in the Football League. They competed in the twenty-team Division Two, then the second tier of English football, finishing bottom. Their application for re-election was successful.

George Wilson was the club's top scorer, with ten goals.
mgt: The 1912–13 season was Blackpool F.C.'s 16th season in the Football League. Competing in the Second Division, they finished 20th and last, resulting in relegation. George Wilson was the team's top scorer with ten goals. In the FA Cup, they exited in the first round.

trgt: The Caister Murdoch System (CMS) was a major natural gas collection, processing and transportation system in the UK sector of the southern North Sea. It comprised 11 platforms, 8 subsea wellhead completions and interconnecting pipelines centered about 155 km east of Flamborough Head Yorkshire. It operated from 1993 to 2018.
mgt: The Caister Murdoch System (CMS) was a network of offshore natural gas fields in the North Sea, developed by Conoco and Total to exploit the Caister and Murdoch fields discovered between 1968 and 1985. Production began in 1993, with gas transported to the Theddlethorpe gas terminal in Lincolnshire. The system expanded to include additional fields and infrastructure, including compression facilities, until production ceased in 2018 following the terminal's closure. Decommissioning of platforms and subsea structures is ongoing.

trgt: Araxá (IPA-pt) is a municipality in Western Minas Gerais state, Brazil. Its estimated population by IBGE (Brazilian Institute of Geography and Statistics) in 2020 is 107,337 inhabitants and the area of the municipality is 1,283 square kilometres (495 sq mi), with 3.45 square kilometres (1.33 sq mi) making up the urban perimeter.
mgt: Araxá is a municipality in the state of Minas Gerais, Brazil, located at an elevation of 973 meters (3,192 ft) with a population of 107,337 (2020). Known for its spa with medicinal mud and mineral waters, it is a center for tourism, mining, and agriculture. The city hosts the Grande Hotel, a historic landmark, and is a major producer of niobium and phosphate. Araxá is also a regional hub for education and healthcare, with a high Human Development Index (HDI) of 0.772 (2010).

trgt: Tamara Bowie (born June 3, 1981) is an American former professional basketball player. In 2007, she was named the Úrvalsdeild kvenna Foreign Player of the Year after leading the league in scoring and rebounds. She played professionally in Iceland, Latvia, Israel, Greece and Bulgaria, where she won the national championship in 2011.
mgt: Tamara Bowie (born June 3, 1981) is an American former professional basketball player and college standout. She played for the Ball State Cardinals from 1999 to 2003, becoming the program's all-time leading scorer and a two-time Mid-American Conference Player of the Year. Professionally, she played internationally in Iceland, Israel, and Bulgaria, earning accolades such as Úrvalsdeild Foreign Player of the Year and leading Grindavík in scoring and rebounds. Bowie also helped WBC Neftokhimik Burgas win the Bulgarian League championship in 2011.

trgt: Swing Kids is a 1993 American drama film directed by Thomas Carter and starring Christian Bale, Robert Sean Leonard and Frank Whaley. In pre-World War II Germany, two high school students attempt to be swing kids by night and Hitler Youth by day, a decision that acutely impacts their friends and families.
mgt: *Swing Kids* is a 1993 American drama film directed by Thomas Carter and written by Jonathan Marc Feldman. Set in 1939 Nazi Germany, the film follows a group of teenagers in Hamburg who embrace American swing music and culture while grappling with the pressures of the Hitler Youth. Starring Robert Sean Leonard, Christian Bale, and Frank Whaley, the film explores themes of resistance, friendship, and ideological conflict. Despite its historical backdrop and a soundtrack featuring swing music, *Swing Kids* received mixed reviews, with a 56% score on Rotten Tomatoes and criticism for its screenplay, though some praised its performances and research. The film grossed $5.6 million against a $12 million budget.

trgt: Maine's Italian sandwich, sometimes referred to as the Maine Italian sandwich, is an American submarine sandwich in Italian-American cuisine prepared on a long bread roll or bun with meats, cheese and various vegetables. The Maine Italian sandwich was supposedly invented in Portland, Maine.
mgt: The Maine Italian sandwich is a traditional sandwich originating in Portland, Maine, consisting of a long bread roll filled with ham, cheese, tomato, onion, green bell pepper, olives, and oil. It was invented by Giovanni Amato, founder of Amato's sandwich shops.

trgt: Amanda Jaqueline Paswall (née DaCosta; born 7 October 1989) is an American-born Portuguese former professional footballer who played as a midfielder for Washington Spirit, Chicago Red Stars and Boston Breakers in the National Women's Soccer League (NWSL), Liverpool Ladies in England's FA WSL, the Washington Freedom and MagicJack in the Women's Professional Soccer (WPS) and for the Portuguese national team.
mgt: Amanda Jaqueline DaCosta (born October 7, 1989) is a retired professional soccer player and coach who played as a midfielder. She had a successful collegiate career at Florida State University before playing for clubs in the United States and Europe, including Liverpool Ladies and multiple National Women's Soccer League teams. Internationally, she represented the United States at youth levels and later played for Portugal, earning 19 caps.

trgt: Hardoi is a village in Maharajganj block of Rae Bareli district, Uttar Pradesh, India. Located on the road from Maharajganj to Bachhrawan, Hardoi historically served as the seat of a pargana and has a now-ruined fort built in the early 1400s under the Jaunpur Sultanate. As of 2011, its population is 2,308, in 415 households.
mgt: Hardoi is a village in Maharajganj block of Raebareli district, Uttar Pradesh, India. Covering an area of 5.264 square kilometers, it had a population of 2,308 as of the 2011 census. Historically, it was founded by a Bhar named Hardeo and played a role in regional conflicts, including resisting Ghazi Sayyid Salar Masud in the 11th century.

trgt: Robert McLeod Eversz (born April 22, 1954) is an American fiction writer, screenwriter, and educator. The author of the Nina Zero novels, he is a co-founder of the Prague Summer Program for writers, a study abroad program for creative writers in the English language.
mgt: Robert Eversz is an American author and educator, best known for his award-winning crime novel series featuring paparazza Nina Zero, set in Southern California. Born in Great Falls, Montana, he earned degrees from UC Santa Cruz and UCLA. Eversz co-founded the Prague Summer Program, teaches at the UCLA Extension Writers' Program, and has served as a visiting professor at Western Michigan University. His novels, including *Shooting Elvis* and *Killing Paparazzi*, have received critical acclaim and been translated into fifteen languages.

trgt: Crown Prince Hyoryeong (born 4 June 1149), personal name Wang Gi was a Goryeo Royal Prince as the first and oldest son of King Uijong and Queen Janggyeong who later deposed from his position alongside his father and his only son by Jeong Jung-bu and Yi Ui-bang in 1170.
mgt: Crown Prince Hyoryeong (born Wang Gi, June 4, 1149 – fl. 1170) was the crown prince of the Goryeo dynasty from 1153 to 1170, during the reign of his father, King Uijong. His tenure ended following a military coup that deposed Uijong, leading to Hyoryeong's imprisonment and exile. His ultimate fate remains unknown.

trgt: Greenhill is a small area of Edinburgh, the capital of Scotland. Situated south of the city centre, Greenhill is normally taken to be part of Bruntsfield, which skirts it to the north. Greenhill borders Marchmont and The Grange to the east, Morningside to the south, and Merchiston, beyond Holy Corner, to the west. It comprises a mixture of Georgian and Victorian villas and some tenement housing.
mgt: Greenhill is a historic area in Edinburgh, Scotland, named after the former Greenhill estate, established in 1586 when the Burgh Muir was feued. The estate was originally owned by Thomas Aikenhead, an Edinburgh merchant and skinner, and later passed to John Livingston, an apothecary. Notable features include a stone carving of the manor house and Livingston's 1645 mausoleum. Greenhill became part of Edinburgh's controlled parking zone in 2006.

trgt: AdGear is an online advertising technology company and the first official advertising technology provider for the Government of Canada. The privately owned company was founded in 2008 by Yves Poiré, Vlad Stesin and Bosko Milekic in Montreal, Canada, and it now operates offices in Montreal.
mgt: AdGear is a digital advertising technology company that provides real-time advertising products, including a demand-side platform and ad servers. Founded in Canada, it powered ad delivery for major publications like *La Presse* and *Toronto Star* before being acquired by Samsung Electronics in 2016.

trgt: Ma Zhou (601–648), courtesy name Binwang, formally the Duke of Gaotang (高唐公), was a Chinese politician who served as a chancellor during the reign of Emperor Taizong in the Tang dynasty. He was initially a commoner and a guest of the general Chang He (常何), and after Chang submitted suggestions that Ma drafted, Emperor Taizong was impressed and retained Ma as an imperial official, eventually rising to the post of chancellor.
mgt: Ma Zhou (601–648), courtesy name Binwang, was a Chinese politician and scholar of the Tang dynasty. Born into a poor family in Qinghe Commandery, he gained recognition for his intellect and moral character, eventually catching the attention of Emperor Taizong. Serving as a trusted advisor and official, Ma was known for his well-reasoned petitions advocating frugality, administrative reforms, and social stability. He held several prominent positions, including deputy head of the legislative bureau and minister of civil service affairs. Despite suffering from chronic illness, Ma remained a close confidant of Emperor Taizong, who personally attended to his care. Ma died in 648 and was posthumously honored as the Duke of Gaotang.

trgt: William F. Lee (born 1950) is an American intellectual property and commercial litigation trial attorney. As co-managing partner of WilmerHale, Lee was the first Asian-American to lead a major American law firm. He is also a Senior Fellow of the Harvard Corporation, the governing board of Harvard University.
mgt: William F. Lee (born 1950) is an American lawyer, academic, and former government official. A prominent trial attorney, he has served as co-managing partner of WilmerHale and represented major clients, including Apple in high-profile patent litigation. Lee has also held academic roles at Harvard Law School and leadership positions at Harvard University.

trgt:
Bobby Beck is the CEO and cofounder of the online animation school, Animation Mentor, which was started in March, 2005. It was the first post-secondary school that helped students to pursue a career in animation. At the school, students work with mentors from major studios in a production-style environment, and graduate with a professional demo reel.

Beck lives in Oakland, CA, he spends his spare time kiteboarding, playing music, doing random art projects and treasure hunting.
mgt: Bobby Beck is an animator and the CEO and President of Animation Mentor, an online animation school headquartered in Emeryville, California. He began his career as an animator in the late 1990s, working at Tippet Studios, Walt Disney Feature Animation, and Pixar, where he contributed to films such as *Toy Story 2*, *Monsters, Inc.*, and *Finding Nemo*. In 2004, Beck left Pixar to focus on Animation Mentor, which he co-founded to provide mentorship-based animation education to students worldwide.

trgt: William Henry Seaman (November 15, 1842 – March 8, 1915) was an American lawyer and judge from Wisconsin. He served as a United States federal judge for 22 years, first in the Eastern District of Wisconsin, then on the Seventh Circuit.
mgt: William Henry Seaman (November 15, 1842 – March 8, 1915) was an American lawyer, judge, and Civil War veteran who served as a federal judge on the United States Court of Appeals for the Seventh Circuit from 1905 to 1915. Previously, he was a United States district judge for the Eastern District of Wisconsin and the 22nd mayor of Sheboygan, Wisconsin. A Democrat, Seaman also served in local government and was a delegate to the 1888 Democratic National Convention.

trgt: Joachim Herrmann (born 21 September 1956) is a German politician. He is a member of the CSU party. Herrmann is an MP in the Landtag, the parliament of Bavaria since 1994. Since 16 October 2007 he has been Minister of the Interior of the state of Bavaria.
mgt: Joachim Herrmann (born 21 September 1956) is a German politician and member of the Christian Social Union (CSU). He has served as the Bavarian State Minister of the Interior since 2007 and as Additional Deputy Minister President of Bavaria since 2008. Herrmann has held various leadership roles within the CSU, including faction leader in the Landtag of Bavaria (2003–2007) and Deputy General Secretary (1997–1998). He has been a member of the Landtag since 1994, representing Erlangen-Stadt. Herrmann has participated in federal coalition negotiations and served as a delegate to multiple Federal Conventions. During his tenure, he has overseen responses to significant incidents of violence in Bavaria and has taken strong positions on issues such as EU-Turkey relations. Born in Munich, Herrmann is a lawyer, married, and has three children.

trgt: Jack Ross (born 6 November 1962 in Auckland), is a New Zealand poet, novelist, translator, editor and lecturer in creative writing. A trilogy of novels has been published by two different publishers, Alan Brunton's Bumper Books (Volume 1) and Titus Books.
mgt: Jack Ross is a New Zealand writer, editor, and academic known for his contributions to experimental literature. Raised on Auckland's North Shore, he earned a PhD in English and Comparative Literature from the University of Edinburgh. Ross has edited several literary magazines, including *brief* and *Landfall*, and is a lecturer in Academic and Creative Writing at Massey University.

trgt:
The Canadian Paediatric Society (CPS) is a national association of paediatricians.

As a voluntary professional association, the CPS represents more than 3,000 paediatricians, paediatric subspecialists, paediatric residents, and other people who work with and care for children. The CPS is governed by an elected Board of Directors representing each province and territory.
mgt: The Canadian Paediatric Society (CPS), founded in 1922, is a national organization based in Canada dedicated to advancing child and youth health. It focuses on professional education, advocacy, public education, and research. The CPS administers the Canadian Immunization Monitoring Program, Active (IMPACT) and developed the Education Program for Immunization Competencies (EPIC).

trgt: Fatal Deviation is a low-budget cult film produced and set in Trim, County Meath, Ireland. Produced in 1998, it enjoys the distinction of being Ireland's first full-length martial arts film. The film stars real-life martial arts enthusiast James Bennett. The film was conceived by James Bennett. The film was also panned by critics and many have considered this film to be one of the worst films ever made. The movie went straight to video without a theatrical release.
mgt: *Fatal Deviation* is a 1998 Irish martial arts action film directed by Simon Linscheid, Shay Casserley, and James Bennett, who also stars in the lead role. The film follows Jimmy Bennett, a young man returning to his hometown to uncover the truth about his father's death while facing a local gang and competing in a deadly martial arts tournament. Produced on a minimal budget, the film has gained cult status for its amateur production and "so bad it's good" reputation. A sequel is in development.

trgt: Xbase++ is an object oriented programming language which has multiple inheritance and polymorphism. It is based on the XBase language dialect and conventions. It is 100% Clipper compatible language supporting multiple inheritance, polymorphism, object oriented programming. It supports the xBase data types, including Codeblocks. With Xbase++ it is possible to generate applications for Windows NT, 95, 98, Me, 2000, XP, VISTA and Windows 7, 8, 10.
mgt: Xbase++ is a programming language and development platform designed as a successor to Clipper, supporting its syntax and features. It includes tools like a visual development environment, an integrated debugger, and a resource compiler. Xbase++ supports Replaceable Database Drivers (RDD) for various database formats, including DBF and SQL servers. It emerged after Computer Associates abandoned Clipper, leading to the development of alternative solutions.

trgt: The 1999 Brickyard 400, the 6th running of the event, was a NASCAR Winston Cup Series race held on August 7, 1999 at Indianapolis Motor Speedway in Speedway, Indiana. Contested at 160 laps on the 2.5 mile (4.023 km) speedway, it was the 20th race of the 1999 NASCAR Winston Cup Series season. Dale Jarrett of Robert Yates Racing won the race.
mgt: The 1999 Brickyard 400 was the 20th race of the 1999 NASCAR Winston Cup Series, held at the Indianapolis Motor Speedway in Speedway, Indiana. Dale Jarrett won the race, leading 117 laps and becoming the second two-time winner of the event. A late caution allowed leaders to pit, with Jarrett's strategic two-tire stop securing his victory over competitors like Jeff Burton and Bobby Labonte.

page_title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit season 8
trgt: The eighth season of the television series, Law & Order: Special Victims Unit premiered September 19, 2006 and ended May 22, 2007 on NBC. The series remained in its 10pm/9c Tuesday timeslot. With the introduction of a new partner for Detective Stabler, early episodes of season 8 took on a significantly different focus when compared to those of previous seasons.
mgt: The eighth season of *Law & Order: Special Victims Unit* premiered on NBC on September 19, 2006, and concluded on May 22, 2007, with 22 episodes. The season featured cast changes, including Connie Nielsen temporarily replacing Mariska Hargitay during her maternity leave, and introduced Adam Beach as Detective Chester Lake. Notable guest stars included Leslie Caron, who won an Emmy, and Chris "Ludacris" Bridges.

trgt: Red God (1954–1979) was a Thoroughbred race horse foaled in Kentucky who competed in England and the United States but who is best known as the sire of Blushing Groom who prominent turfman Edward L. Bowen calls one of the great international sires of the 20th century.
mgt: Red God (foaled 1954) was an American-bred, British-trained Thoroughbred racehorse and successful sire. Owned and bred by Harry F. Guggenheim, he won notable races including the Richmond Stakes and Roseben Handicap. Retired in 1960, he stood at stud in Ireland, producing multiple stakes winners.

trgt:
Robert Žák (born 6 May 1966) is a Czech football manager and former player.

As a player, he played in the Czechoslovak First League until its conclusion in 1993, after which he continued in the Gambrinus liga for one season before finishing his professional playing career in 1994.

He has managed three clubs in the Gambrinus liga, firstly České Budějovice followed by Most and most recently Bohemians Prague.
mgt: Robert Žák (born 6 May 1966) is a Czech former footballer and manager. As a midfielder, he played 111 matches and scored 18 goals in the Czechoslovak First League, representing Slavia Prague, Hradec Králové, and České Budějovice. He later managed several clubs, including České Budějovice, Most, Bohemians Prague, and Litvínov, though his managerial stints were often short-lived due to poor team performances.

trgt: The Cizeta-Moroder V16T, now known as just the Cizeta V16T, is an Italian sports car (built from 1991 to 1995) developed by automotive engineer Claudio Zampolli in a joint venture with music composer Giorgio Moroder and designed by Marcello Gandini. It was the only product of the Cizeta company. It was developed by a group of ex-Lamborghini employees and initially introduced in Los Angeles in December 1988.
mgt: The Cizeta-Moroder V16T is a rare Italian sports car produced by Cizeta Automobili from 1991 to 2003, with only 13 units built, including one Spyder. Designed by Marcello Gandini, it features a unique 6.0 L V16 engine mounted transversely, producing 540 hp and achieving a top speed of 328 km/h (204 mph). Initially a collaboration between Claudio Zampolli and Giorgio Moroder, the partnership dissolved after one prototype.

trgt:
Uckfield Town Council governs the five wards of Uckfield town. The council appoints one councillor who is then known as the Mayor of Uckfield. The current Mayor of Uckfield is Councillor Jackie Love.

Uckfield town council comprises five wards – North, East, West, New Town, and Ridgewood – electing between two and four town councillors each.

Clockwise, from the north, it borders the communities of Maresfield, Buxted, Framfield and Little Horsted.
mgt: Uckfield Town Council is the local governing body for the town of Uckfield in East Sussex, England. Comprising 15 councillors elected from five wards, the council is led by Mayor Jackie Love and Deputy Mayor Donna French. The council includes representatives from the TRUST Independent group, Labour Party, Conservative Party, and Liberal Democrats. Meetings are held at the Civic Centre in Uckfield. The most recent election was held on 2 May 2019.

trgt: The Postcard Killings is a 2020 American crime film directed by Danis Tanović, starring Jeffrey Dean Morgan, Famke Janssen and Cush Jumbo, and based on the 2010 novel The Postcard Killers by James Patterson and Liza Marklund. The film was released on March 13, 2020, receiving negative reviews from critics.
mgt: *The Postcard Killings* is a 2020 American crime thriller film directed by Danis Tanović, based on the novel *The Postcard Killers* by Liza Marklund and James Patterson. Starring Jeffrey Dean Morgan, Famke Janssen, and Cush Jumbo, the film follows a New York detective investigating his daughter's murder during her honeymoon in Europe. Despite its premise, the film received generally unfavorable reviews, holding a 22% approval rating on Rotten Tomatoes and a score of 29 on Metacritic.

trgt: Isometric projection is a method for visually representing three-dimensional objects in two dimensions in technical and engineering drawings. It is an axonometric projection in which the three coordinate axes appear equally foreshortened and the angle between any two of them is 120 degrees.
mgt: Isometric projection is a method of visually representing three-dimensional objects in two dimensions, where the scale along each axis is equal, and the angles between axes are 120°. First formalized in the 19th century, it has been widely used in engineering, architecture, and art. Unlike perspective projection, isometric projection maintains consistent object sizes regardless of depth, which is useful for technical drawings but can create visual distortions. It has also been employed in video games and pixel art to create a three-dimensional effect, though often using variations like dimetric projection.

trgt: Pope Benedict V (Lang-la; died 4 July 965) was the bishop of Rome and ruler of the Papal States from 22 May to 23 June 964, in opposition to Leo VIII. He was overthrown by Emperor Otto I. His brief pontificate occurred at the end of a period known as the Saeculum obscurum.
mgt: Pope Benedict V (died 4 July 965) was pope from 22 May to 23 June 964. Elected by the Roman people in opposition to Pope Leo VIII, his papacy was marked by conflict with Holy Roman Emperor Otto I, who besieged Rome and deposed him. Stripped of his papal title, Benedict was exiled to Hamburg, where he died in 965. Renowned for his learning, he was nicknamed "Grammaticus" and served as a cardinal-deacon before his brief and contested papacy.

trgt: David Jonathan Heyman (born 26 July 1961) is a British film producer and the founder of Heyday Films. Heyman secured the rights to the Harry Potter film series in 1999 and went on to produce all eight installments of the franchise. He also received three Academy Award nominations for his work on the films Gravity, Once Upon a Time in Hollywood, and Marriage Story.
mgt: David Jonathan Heyman (born 26 July 1961) is a British film producer and founder of Heyday Films. He is best known for producing the *Harry Potter* film series and its spin-off *Fantastic Beasts* films. Heyman has also produced critically acclaimed works such as *Gravity*, *Paddington*, and *Once Upon a Time in Hollywood*, earning multiple Academy Award nominations. He resides in London with his wife, Rose Uniacke.

trgt: Walton Castle was a Saxon Shore Fort in the Roman province of Britannia. The fort was reused by the Normans who used it as the bailey for a castle. It stood 30 metres above sea level but was destroyed by coastal erosion in the 18th century. It was located in the village of Walton, Suffolk, now part of Felixstowe (grid reference TM32253580).
mgt: Walton Castle was a Roman Saxon Shore fort located on the cliffs at Felixstowe, Suffolk, likely built in the late 3rd century. It featured flint-faced walls with brick bonding and forward-projecting towers. Reused by the Normans as a castle bailey, it was dismantled in the 12th century. Coastal erosion led to its gradual destruction, with remnants visible during exceptionally low tides.

trgt: Ali Abdel Aal Sayyed Ahmed (Lang-ar; born 29 November 1948) is an Egyptian law professor and politician. He was Speaker of the House of Representatives between 10 January 2016 and 12 January 2021. As law professor he worked at Ain Shams University and specialized in constitutional law.
mgt: Ali Abdel Aal (born 29 November 1948) is an Egyptian legal scholar and politician who served as the Speaker of the House of Representatives from 2016 to 2021. A constitutional law professor at Ain Shams University, he contributed to drafting the 2014 Egyptian Constitution and Ethiopia's 1995 Constitution.

trgt: The National Center for Health Statistics (NCHS) is a U.S. government agency that provides statistical information to guide actions and policies to improve the public health of the American people. It is a unit of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) and a principal agency of the U.S. Federal Statistical System. It is headquartered at University Town Center in Hyattsville, Maryland, just outside Washington, D.C.
mgt: The National Center for Health Statistics (NCHS), established in 1960, is a U.S. federal agency under the Department of Health and Human Services and part of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) since 1987. Headquartered in Hyattsville, Maryland, it collects and analyzes health data through surveys, administrative sources, and partnerships. Its major programs include the National Vital Statistics System, National Health Interview Survey, National Health and Nutrition Examination Survey, and National Health Care Surveys.

trgt:
Eastoft is a village and civil parish in North Lincolnshire, England. It is situated within the Isle of Axholme, 3 miles (5 km) north-east from Crowle, and on the A161 road.

The 2001 census recorded a parish population of 378, increasing to 431 at the 2011 census.
mgt: Eastoft is a village and civil parish in North Lincolnshire, England, situated near the border with the East Riding of Yorkshire. With a population of 431 as of the 2011 census, it is a linear settlement along the A161 road. Historically, Eastoft was part of both Lincolnshire and Yorkshire until becoming a separate parish in 1855. The village features notable landmarks, including the Grade II listed St Bartholomew's Church, built in 1855, and two historic Eastoft Halls. Eastoft has a rich history tied to drainage works by Cornelius Vermuyden in the 17th century and was served by a railway station from 1903 to 1965. The village also includes a primary school, a war memorial, and a nature reserve, Eastoft Meadow. Actress Gina Bramhill is among its notable residents.

trgt: Vladimir Nikolaevich Nemukhin (Lang-ru; February 12, 1925 in village Priluki, Kaluga Oblast, RSFSR, USSR – April 18, 2016 Moscow, Russia) was a Russian artist. Nemukhin was associated with the 'Lianozovo Group' along with Oscar Rabin, Valentina Kropivnitskaya [ru] (1924-2008) and Lidiya Masterkova.
mgt: Vladimir Nemukhin (1925–2016) was a Russian nonconformist artist and a key figure in the Soviet Union's unofficial art movement.
