mgt: Grace Yun Yi is a Chinese-Canadian statistician and professor at the University of Western Ontario, known for her contributions to statistical theory, particularly in missing and mismeasured data, and biostatistics. She earned degrees from Sichuan University, York University, and the University of Toronto, where she completed her Ph.D. in 2000. A Fellow of the American Statistical Association and the Institute of Mathematical Statistics, Yi has received several prestigious awards, including the CRM–SSC Prize.

trgt: Ginataang ampalaya, is a Filipino vegetable stew made from bitter melon and tinapa (smoked fish) in coconut milk, bagoong alamang (shrimp paste), and spices. The dish can also be made with pork or shrimp and other vegetables. The dish is characteristically savory and slightly bitter due to the ingredients used. It is a type of ginataan.
mgt: Ginataang ampalaya is a Filipino dish made with bitter melon (ampalaya) cooked in coconut milk, often with tinapa (smoked fish), onions, garlic, and shrimp paste or fish sauce. Variations may include pork, shrimp, or additional vegetables like moringa leaves and eggplants. The dish is typically served over rice and can be spiced with peppers or ginger.

trgt: Hyper Combat Unit Dangaioh (破邪大星ダンガイオー, Haja Taisei Dangaiō) is a Japanese anime OVA series produced and animated by AIC and Artmic and released in Japan in 1987. Dangaioh featured character designs by creator Toshiki Hirano, mechanical designs by Shoji Kawamori, and animation direction by Masami Ōbari.
mgt: *Dangaioh* (破邪大星ダンガイオー, *Haja Taisei Dangaiō*) is a Japanese original video animation (OVA) series in the mecha and science fiction genres, directed by Toshiki Hirano and released from 1987 to 1989. The story follows four psychic warriors who unite their powers to form Dangaioh, a powerful mecha, to combat the tyranny of Captain Galimos and his henchman, Gil Berg. The series gained attention for its dynamic animation, music by Chumei Watanabe, and subsequent international releases, including English subtitled and dubbed versions. It inspired merchandise, a sequel series titled *Great Dangaioh* (2001), and appearances in video games like *Super Robot Wars*.

trgt: Aquinas Walter Richard Sipe (December 11, 1932 – August 8, 2018) was an American Benedictine monk-priest for 18 years (1952–1970 at Saint John's Abbey, Collegeville, Minnesota), a psychotherapist and the author of six books about Catholicism, the clerical sexual abuse in the Catholic Church, and clerical celibacy.
mgt: Richard Sipe (1932–2018) was an American psychotherapist, former priest, and expert on the sexual behavior of Catholic clergy. Known for his 25-year study on celibacy, he found that over half of priests had sexual relationships. Sipe testified in numerous abuse cases, contributed to media investigations, and influenced the Pulitzer Prize–winning Boston Globe exposé depicted in the film *Spotlight*.

trgt:
Steilacoom (/ˈstɪləkəm/) is a town in Pierce County, Washington, United States. The population was 6,727 at the 2020 census. Steilacoom incorporated in 1854 and became the first incorporated town in what is now the state of Washington. It has also become a bedroom community for service members stationed at Joint Base Lewis–McChord.

Based on per capita income, Steilacoom ranks 61st of 522 areas ranked in the state of Washington.
mgt: Steilacoom is a town in Pierce County, Washington, United States, with a population of 6,727 as of the 2020 census. Incorporated in 1854, it is the oldest incorporated town in Washington. Originally inhabited by the Steilacoom people, the town's name derives from a Native American word or a local chief's name. Steilacoom is known for its historic sites, including the state's oldest Catholic and Protestant churches, and its early role in Washington's development.

trgt: Amyntaio (Lang-el, before 1928: Σόροβιτς - Sorovits; Bulgarian/Macedonian: Суровичево, Сорович), is a town and municipality in the Florina regional unit of Macedonia, Greece. The population of Amyntaio proper is 4,306, while that of the entire municipality is 16,973 (2011). The town is named after the ancient king of Macedon and grandfather of Alexander the Great, Amyntas III.
mgt: Amyntaio (Greek: Αμύνταιο) is a town and municipality in the Florina regional unit of West Macedonia, Greece. Situated at an elevation of 589 meters, it covers a municipal unit area of 249.9 km² and had a population of 7,612 as of 2011. The area is notable for archaeological discoveries, including Neolithic and Iron Age settlements, and a Roman villa.

trgt: Rami Mehmed Pasha (1645–1706) was an Ottoman statesman and poet who served as Grand Vizier (1703) and governor of Cyprus and of Egypt (1704–06). He was known as a poet of divan literature (the epithet Rami, meaning "Obedient", is his pen name in his poems).
mgt: Rami Mehmed Pasha (1645–1706) was an Ottoman statesman, diplomat, and poet who served as Grand Vizier of the Ottoman Empire in 1703 and later as Governor of Egypt. He played a key role in the Treaty of Karlowitz negotiations and authored a book on the peace talks.

trgt: The 9th Marine Regiment was an infantry regiment of the United States Marine Corps. Formed during World War II, it served until 1994, when it was deactivated during the post Cold War drawdown. Battalions of the Ninth Marine Regiment, but not the regiment itself, were reactivated from 2007 to 2014 as part of the Marine Corps' growth during the wars in Iraq and Afghanistan. The battalions have subsequently been deactivated again.
mgt: The 9th Marine Regiment, nicknamed the "Striking Ninth," was an infantry regiment of the United States Marine Corps, active at various times between 1917 and 2015. It served in major conflicts, including World War II, the Vietnam War, and Operation Desert Storm. The regiment played a key role in battles such as Bougainville, Guam, Iwo Jima, and Operation Dewey Canyon, earning numerous commendations. It was inactivated in 1994 and reactivated from 2007 to 2015.

trgt: North Hollywood High School (NHHS) is a public high school in the North Hollywood neighborhood of Los Angeles, California, United States. It is located in the San Fernando Valley and enrolls approximately 2,500 students. Several neighborhoods, including most of North Hollywood, Valley Village, Studio City and Sun Valley, send students to this school. NHHS is accredited by the Western Association of Schools and Colleges. The school principal is Ricardo Rosales.
mgt: North Hollywood High School (NHHS) is a public high school in North Hollywood, Los Angeles, California, established in 1927. Part of the Los Angeles Unified School District, it serves grades 9–12 and has an enrollment of 2,579 students as of 2019–20. The school offers three magnet programs—Highly Gifted Magnet, Zoo Magnet, and STEM Magnet—alongside Small Learning Communities. Known for its academic and extracurricular achievements, NHHS has received high rankings and offers extensive Advanced Placement courses and competitive academic teams.

trgt: Baasskap (IPA-af) (also spelled baaskap), literally "boss-ship" or "boss-hood", is an Afrikaans term that was used during apartheid to describe the social, political and economic domination of South Africa by its minority white population generally and by Afrikaners in particular. The term is sometimes translated to the English-language term "white supremacy" and functioned either as a description or an endorsement of white minority rule in South Africa.
mgt: Baasskap was a faction of apartheid ideology in South Africa, primarily supported by members of the National Party and state institutions. It advocated for systematic racial segregation to maintain Afrikaner dominance in economic and political spheres while allowing controlled black labor participation in the economy. Prominent proponents included Prime Minister J.G. Strydom and Minister of Justice C.R. Swart, with Hendrik Verwoerd refining its intellectual framework.

trgt: Tamika Tremaglio is the current executive director of the National Basketball Players Association (NBPA). Tremaglio was selected by the players to replace Michele Roberts on September 22, 2021, and officially stepped into the role upon Roberts'retirement in early January 2022. Prior to joining the NBPA as executive director, Tremaglio, who retired from Deloitte LLP, served as the Managing Principal for Deloitte's Greater Washington offices.
mgt: Tamika Tremaglio is an American business executive and attorney who serves as the executive director of the National Basketball Players Association (NBPA). Born in St. Mary’s County, Maryland, she holds degrees from Mount St. Mary’s University, the University of Maryland School of Law, and the University of Baltimore. Tremaglio previously worked at Deloitte, where she became Managing Principal for the Greater Washington Area in 2017.

trgt: Patrick Kwok-Choon (born 19 November 1988) is a Canadian actor, best known for his recurring roles as Perry Crofte in the television adaptation of the horror Western comic book miniseries Wynonna Earp and as lieutenant commander Gen Rhys in the CBS science fiction drama Star Trek: Discovery.
mgt: Patrick Kwok-Choon (born November 19, 1988) is a Canadian actor and director known for his work in theatre, television, and film. A graduate of Concordia University, George Brown Theatre School, and the Canadian Film Centre, he has appeared in productions such as *War Horse*, *Kim's Convenience*, and *Star Trek: Discovery*.

trgt: Muhammad bin Saud Al Muqrin (Lang-ar Muḥammad bin Suʿūd Āl Muqrin; 1687–1765), also known as Ibn Saud, was the emir of Diriyah and is considered the founder of the First Saudi State and the Saud dynasty, which are named for his father, Saud bin Muhammad Al Muqrin. His reign lasted between 1727 and 1765.
mgt: Muhammad bin Saud Al Muqrin (1687–1765) was the founder of the First Saudi State and the Emir of Diriyah from 1727 to 1765. He formed a historic alliance with Islamic reformer Muhammad bin Abdul Wahhab, establishing the foundation for the modern Saudi state. His governance, based on Islamic principles, shaped the enduring structure of Saudi rule.

trgt: ER is an American drama series that aired on NBC from September 19, 1994, until April 2, 2009. The series featured a large ensemble cast that changed dramatically over its long run. The main cast was augmented by a wealth of recurring characters and award-winning guest stars.
mgt: The cast of *ER*, a medical drama that aired for 15 seasons from 1994 to 2009, featured a large ensemble of actors over its run. The original main cast included Anthony Edwards, George Clooney, Sherry Stringfield, Noah Wyle, Eriq La Salle, and Julianna Margulies. Over the years, the show introduced numerous new characters, including Gloria Reuben, Alex Kingston, Maura Tierney, and Goran Visnjic, among others. Many cast members departed throughout the series, with some returning for guest appearances, including in the series finale. The show also featured notable guest stars, several of whom received Emmy recognition for their performances.

trgt: Joseph Harmatz (Lang-he; 23 January 1925 – 22 September 2016) was a Lithuanian-born Jew who fought as a partisan fighter during World War II. After the war, he joined Nakam and plotted acts of revenge that were aimed at killing Nazis and other Germans to avenge Jewish deaths during the Holocaust. After emigrating to Israel, he headed World ORT, a Jewish non-profit organization that promotes education and training in communities around the world.
mgt: Joseph Harmatz (January 23, 1925 – September 22, 2016) was a Lithuanian-born Jewish partisan, Holocaust survivor, and leader of Nakam, a group dedicated to avenging the deaths of six million Jews during the Holocaust. After surviving the Vilnius Ghetto and joining anti-Nazi guerrillas, Harmatz participated in efforts to poison Nazi war criminals, including a failed plot to poison German water supplies. He later emigrated to Israel, leading World ORT from 1960 to 1994 and aiding Jewish resettlement.

trgt: DXWT (92.3 FM), broadcasting as 92.3 Wild FM, is a radio station owned and operated by the UM Broadcasting Network. It serves as the flagship station of the Wild FM network. The station's studio is located at the UMBN Media Center, C. Bangoy cor. Palma Gil St., Davao City, and its transmitter is located along Broadcast Ave., Shrine Hills, Matina, Davao City.
mgt: DXWT (92.3 FM), branded as 92.3 Wild FM, is a radio station in Davao City, Philippines, owned by the UM Broadcasting Network. Established in 1949 as DXMC on AM, it transitioned to FM in 1988 and adopted its current call sign. Known for its dance-leaning Contemporary Hit Radio format, it pioneered innovative programming and promotions, becoming a leading station in Mindanao.

trgt: Edward A. Keenan (October 15, 1894 – April 12, 1970) was an American politician who served as the 34th Mayor of Burlington, Vermont. Keenan was the last Republican elected to the mayoralty of Burlington until Peter C. Brownell was thirty years later.
mgt: Edward A. Keenan (October 15, 1894 – April 12, 1970) was an American politician who served as the 34th mayor of Burlington, Vermont, from 1963 to 1965. A Republican, he also served on the Burlington Board of Aldermen and was active in state politics.

trgt: Christopher (Chris) van Wyk (19 July 1957 – 3 October 2014) was a South African children’s book author, novelist and poet. Van Wyk is famous for his poem "In Detention" on the suspicious deaths that befell South African political prisoners during Apartheid. He was also an editor at Ravan Press.
mgt: Christopher van Wyk (19 July 1957 – 3 October 2014) was a South African author, poet, and editor, best known for his autobiographical works *Shirley, Goodness and Mercy* and *Eggs to Lay, Chickens to Hatch*. His writing, often rooted in his childhood in Riverlea, Johannesburg, explored themes of identity and community. Van Wyk gained prominence during the post-Soweto uprising literary movement, publishing poetry and children's literature that reflected Black Consciousness and defiance. He won several awards, including the Olive Schreiner Prize and the Sanlam Literary Award, and abridged Nelson Mandela's *Long Walk to Freedom* for children. Posthumously, he was honored with an honorary Doctor of Literature degree from the University of the Witwatersrand in 2019.

trgt: Ebenezer Norman is a Liberian philanthropist, humanitarian, public speaker, and founder of the education non-profit A New Dimension of Hope (NDHope). He is involved in humanitarian efforts in Liberia and throughout West Africa, notably for efforts to build schools in attenuated or war-torn communities after the Second Liberian Civil War in 2003 and the Ebola virus epidemic in Liberia in 2015.
mgt: Ebenezer Norman (born June 10, 1981) is a Liberian humanitarian activist and founder of New Dimension of Hope (NDHope), an organization focused on constructing and managing schools in Liberia to meet international academic standards. Inspired by the aftermath of the Second Liberian Civil War, Norman built his first school in Troyah Town, Liberia, despite challenges such as the Ebola outbreak and property disputes. His efforts have garnered international support, including from Nobel Peace Prize Laureate Leymah Gbowee. Norman, a Global Goodwill Ambassador and recipient of the LOOP Award, continues to expand educational opportunities in Liberia while residing in Denver, Colorado.

trgt: Hyme House, at 3 Fitzjohn's Avenue, Hampstead, England, was the London home of society portrait painter Philip de László. He painted many of his portraits at sittings in the studio and gardens of Hyme House. Sitters included royalty, celebrities, businesspeople and politicians from the 1920s and 1930s.
mgt: Hyme House, located on Fitzjohn's Avenue in Hampstead, London, is a historic Arts and Crafts-style villa built in 1886. It was home to renowned portrait painter Philip de László from 1921 to 1937, where he painted notable figures, including members of the British royal family. After de László's death, the house was acquired by the Sisters of Mercy of the Holy Cross in 1938, serving as a nursing home during World War II and later as a convent and girls' school. In 1985, it became the Fitzjohn's Lodge Hotel before being converted back to residential use in 2006.

trgt: CubETH is a Swiss satellite project. As of 2015, it is being developed by several Swiss academic and industrial partners. The spacecraft is a single unit CubeSat and therefore 10 x 10 x 10 cm (3.9 x 3.9 x 3.9 in) in size. Its main goal is to prove the feasibility of attitude and orbit determination with low cost commercial off-the-shelf GNSS-receivers.
mgt: CubETH is a 1U CubeSat technology demonstration mission led by ETH Zurich, with a planned duration of 18 months. The mission aims to demonstrate the use of low-cost GNSS receivers for satellite positioning in space, with onboard computation of orbit and attitude. Developed in collaboration with Swiss academic and industrial partners, CubETH was scheduled for launch in 2017.

trgt: The Gateway, also known as Curtains, is a 2015 horror film that was directed by Jaron Henrie-McCrea. The film had its world premiere on August 31, 2015 at Film4 FrightFest and stars Danni Smith and Tim Lueke as two activists that investigate a series of disappearing shower curtains in Smith's apartment.
mgt: *The Gateway* (originally titled *Curtains*) is a 2015 horror film that follows Danni, a former hospice nurse turned whale conservation activist, who discovers a mysterious portal in her shower. Her investigation leads to deadly consequences, including encounters with monsters and a sinister figure known as the Pale Man. The film received mixed reviews, with critics praising its originality and charm but noting its limited appeal and unconventional narrative.

trgt: The BB 1800 were motor luggage vans of SNCF. These were the so-called "first-series" Z 5000 locomotives ordered by the Chemins de fer de l'Ouest. They were put into service, with running numbers Z 5001 to Z 5010, on 12 April 1900, on the line from Paris-Invalides to Issy-Plaine, which was extended to Meudon on 1 July 1901 then to Versailles RG on 31 May 1902.
mgt: The SNCF Class BB 1800, originally designated as Z 5000, was a series of early electric locomotives introduced in 1901 for France's first electrified rail services. Featuring a wooden body with steel framing, they operated on 600 V DC third rail and were later modified with updated motors and electrical equipment. Renumbered over time, they served in passenger and shunting roles until their withdrawal in 1956.

trgt: Geoffrey Sayre-McCord (né McCord, born December 10, 1956) is an American philosopher who works in moral theory, ethics, meta-ethics, the history of ethics, and epistemology. He teaches at the University of North Carolina, Chapel Hill. He is also the director of the Philosophy, Politics and Economics Society.
mgt: Geoffrey Sayre-McCord (born December 10, 1956) is an American philosopher and professor known for his work on moral realism and David Hume's moral theory. He is the Morehead-Cain Alumni Distinguished Professor of Philosophy at the University of North Carolina, where he directs the Philosophy, Politics, and Economics Program.

page_title: Wally Bear and the NO! Gang
trgt: Wally Bear and the NO! Gang is an educational Nintendo Entertainment System game that was released in 1992 exclusively for a North American audience. It was not licensed by Nintendo. The game teaches children to say no to potentially harmful drugs like tobacco, alcohol and marijuana. Wally Bear and the NO! Gang was designed in cooperation with, and endorsed by, the American Medical Association and the National Clearinghouse for Alcohol and Drug Information.
mgt: *Wally Bear and the NO! Gang* is a 1992 side-scrolling video game developed by American Game Cartridges and published by American Video Entertainment for the Nintendo Entertainment System. The game follows Wally Bear, a skateboarding bear, as he promotes anti-drug and alcohol messages while traveling to his uncle's party. Despite mixed reviews at release, the game gained attention for its educational themes. Wally Bear and related characters were later used by the National Clearinghouse for Alcohol and Drug Information (NCADI) for substance abuse prevention campaigns, evolving into the "Wally Bear and the KNOW Gang" initiative. The franchise was eventually retired in 2004 and replaced by the "Building Blocks for a Healthy Future" program.

trgt: Anne Line Gjersem (born 6 January 1994) is a Norwegian retired figure skater. She is the 2015 Nordic silver medalist, the 2015 Denkova-Staviski Cup bronze medalist, and a three-time Norwegian national champion. She represented Norway at the 2014 Winter Olympics and has reached the free skate at six ISU Championships.
mgt: Anne Line Gjersem (born January 6, 1994) is a retired Norwegian figure skater and the first Norwegian to compete in Olympic figure skating since 1964, finishing 23rd at the 2014 Winter Olympics in Sochi. A four-time Norwegian national champion, she achieved Norway's best World Championship result in ladies' skating since the 1940s, placing 17th in 2015.

trgt: Bobby is a 2002 Indian Telugu-language romance film written and directed by Sobhan and produced by K. Krishna Mohan Rao. The movie stars Mahesh Babu, Aarthi Agarwal, Raghuvaran, and Prakash Raj while Brahmanandam, Sunil, Pragathi, Ravi Babu, and Meher Ramesh play supporting roles. The soundtrack of the movie was scored by Mani Sharma, the cinematography was done by Venkat R. Prasad, and editing was handled by S. Sudhakar Reddy. The film released on 31 October 2002.
mgt: *Bobby* is a 2002 Indian Telugu-language romantic drama film directed and written by Sobhan. Starring Mahesh Babu and Aarthi Agarwal, the film follows the love story of Bobby, the son of a wealthy businessman, and Bhagyamati, the daughter of a mafia leader, whose fathers are arch rivals. Despite their families' enmity, the couple's love leads to reconciliation. Produced by K. Krishna Mohan Rao with music by Mani Sharma, the film received mixed reviews for its lack of originality.

trgt: Woodall services is a motorway service station on the M1 motorway in Rotherham close to Sheffield in England. It lies between junctions 30 and 31. It was opened in 1968 by Trust House Forte but was renamed Welcome Break after the takeover of the company. It takes its name from the nearby village of Woodall.
mgt: Woodall services is a motorway service area on the M1 in South Yorkshire, England, operated by Welcome Break. Opened in 1968, it features facilities including KFC and Burger King, with both sides connected by a footbridge. Known for its popularity with coaches, it also includes murals by David Fisher reflecting local history.

trgt: Ian Simmonds (born 1966), also known as Juryman, is a Welsh-born electronic musician. He is one of a few talents "to successfully breach the chasm that keeps DJ culture from more tangible, organic realms." His complex musical landscapes are often accompanied by his deep calm voice, making poetic, political and social statements at the same time. He is a self-taught musician, singing and playing trumpet, bass guitar, piano and is a producer with numerous releases, contributions and co-productions.
mgt: Ian Simmonds (born 1966), also known as Juryman, is a Welsh electronic and trip-hop musician, producer, and DJ. He began his career in the late 1980s as a founding member of the collective Sandals, which emerged from London's acid house and rave scenes. Simmonds later gained recognition for his innovative solo work, releasing albums under his own name and the Juryman moniker. Since 2005, he has been based in Germany, collaborating with local musicians and exploring jazz influences through his project Wise in Time.

trgt: Ronald Welch (14 December 1909 – 5 February 1982) was the pseudonym of Welsh writer Ronald Oliver Felton TD, who wrote in English. He is best known for children's historical fiction. He won the 1956 Carnegie Medal from the Library Association for the year's best children's book by a British author, for Knight Crusader, the first in his so-called Carey Family series of novels.
mgt: Ronald Welch was the pen name of Ronald Oliver Felton, a Welsh author and teacher born in Aberavon, West Glamorgan. A veteran of the Second World War, he served as a major in the Welch Regiment, which inspired his pseudonym. Welch was headmaster of Okehampton Grammar School in Devon and is best known for his historical novels, including the Carey family series.

trgt: NoGravity is a space flight simulation and space shooter developed by realtech VR, a Montreal based, French Canadian independent computer games company. The game was ported to many platforms over the years as it was open sourced in 2005. NoGravity drew comparisons with the Wing Commander series.
mgt: *No Gravity* is a 3D space shooter video game developed and published by realtech VR. Originally released in the late 1990s as *SpaceGirl* for BeOS, it was later renamed *No Gravity* and open-sourced in 2005. The game features mission-based gameplay with objectives like destroying enemy ships and escorting allies. It was ported to various platforms, including PSP, iOS, and Android, with an extended version titled *No Gravity: The Plague of Mind* released in 2009. The classic version has been downloaded over 270,000 times and received mixed reviews, with the PSP version earning a 65% rating on Metacritic.

trgt: Muslim Community Radio (MCR) was a radio station based in London, United Kingdom founded in 1998. The station used to run through FM radio, at 87.7 FM (formerly 1143AM, 87.8 FM & 101.4 FM prior to that), and only broadcast annually during the holy month of Ramadan. The slogan of the radio station is 'Ramadan Special'.
mgt: Muslim Community Radio (MCR) is a London-based Islamic radio station broadcasting on 1143 AM in English and Bengali. Established in 1998, it operates from the London Muslim Centre in Tower Hamlets and is run by the Islamic Forum Europe with volunteer presenters. MCR features a variety of programmes, including religious sessions, children's shows, and community-focused content.

trgt: Carroll Ashmore Campbell Jr. (July 24, 1940 – December 7, 2005), was an American Republican Party politician who served as the 112th governor of South Carolina from 1987 to 1995. Prior to this, he served as a member of the South Carolina Senate representing the 2nd district from 1977 to 1978 and as a congressman representing South Carolina's 4th district from 1979 to 1987.
mgt: Carroll Ashmore Campbell Jr. (July 24, 1940 – December 7, 2005) was an American politician and member of the Republican Party who served as the 112th governor of South Carolina from 1987 to 1995. Known for his leadership during Hurricane Hugo and his role in bringing BMW's first U.S. manufacturing plant to South Carolina, he also chaired the National Governors Association. After leaving office, Campbell worked as a lobbyist and was diagnosed with Alzheimer's disease in 2001.

page_title: Church of Nossa Senhora do Rosário (Calheta)
trgt: The Church of Nossa Senhora do Rosário (Lang-pt) is a 16th-century Azorean temple in the civil parish of Nossa Senhora do Rosário, municipality of Calheta, being reconstructed after the Mandado de Deus earthquake that affected the islands of São Jorge and Pico.
mgt: The Church of Nossa Senhora do Rosário (Church of Our Lady of the Rosary) is a Roman Catholic church located in Topo, São Jorge, in the Azores, Portugal. Originally built in the 16th century, it was reconstructed after the 1757 "Mandado de Deus" earthquake under architect José de Avelar de Melo. The church features a Roman architectural style, a prominent bell tower, and a richly decorated interior with Baroque influences, including gilded retables, painted panels, and silver-plated lamps.

trgt: Chukwuedu Nathaniel II Nwokolo (19 April 1921 – 18 May 2014) was a Nigerian physician specialist in tropical diseases. He was recognised for discovering and mapping out the area of paragonimiasis lung disease in Eastern Nigeria, with a study of the disease in Africa and clinical research for its control. He founded SICREP: Sickle Cell Research Programme to effectively fight the disease in Nigeria and globally.
mgt: Chukwuedu Nwokolo (19 April 1921 – 18 May 2014) was a Nigerian medical doctor, professor of medicine, and pioneering researcher. He was instrumental in establishing medical schools in Nigeria, including at the University of Nigeria, Nsukka, where he became the first head of the Department of Medicine. Renowned for his research in gastroenterology and tropical diseases, he was a Rockefeller Foundation fellow and advisor to the World Health Organization.

trgt: The 17th Arizona State Legislature, consisting of the Arizona State Senate and the Arizona House of Representatives, was constituted in Phoenix from January 1, 1945 to December 31, 1946, during the third of Sidney Preston Osborn's four consecutive terms as Governor of Arizona. The number of senators and house members remained constant at 19 and 58, respectively. The Democrats controlled one hundred percent of the senate. while the Republicans gained a single house seat.
mgt: The 17th Arizona State Legislature, consisting of the Arizona State Senate and House of Representatives, convened from January 1, 1945, to December 31, 1946. The Legislature, based at the Arizona State Capitol in Phoenix, was entirely controlled by the Democratic Party, with all 19 Senate seats and 57 of 58 House seats held by Democrats. It held one regular session from January 8 to March 9, 1945, and three special sessions in 1945 and 1946.

trgt: Road Rules: Down Under is the sixth season of the MTV reality television series Road Rules. It took place exclusively in Hawaii for part of the first episode, with the rest of the season exclusively in Oceania. This season is often noted as Road Rules: Australia.
mgt: *Road Rules: Down Under* is the sixth season of MTV's reality television series *Road Rules*. It aired from June 22 to September 7, 1998, and featured cast members Chadwick Pelletier, Christina Pazsitzky, Kefla Hare, Piggy Thomas, Shayne McBride, and Susie Meister. The season consisted of 13 episodes.

trgt: Ali Massoud Ansari FRSE (Lang-fa, born 24 November 1967 in Rome) is the Professor in Modern History with reference to the Middle East at the University of St Andrews in Scotland, where he is also the founding director of the Institute for Iranian Studies.
mgt: Ali Massoud Ansari (born 24 November 1967) is a British-Iranian historian and expert in Iranian studies. He is a professor at the University of St Andrews and an Associate Fellow at Chatham House. Ansari has written extensively on Iranian history and politics, contributing to publications such as *The Guardian* and *The Independent*. He earned his PhD from the School of Oriental and African Studies and was elected a Fellow of the Royal Society of Edinburgh in 2016.

trgt:
The Mascom Top 8 Cup is a football cup competition in Botswana. It was created in 2012, and features the top eight finishers from the Botswana Premier League.

Since 2013, the winners qualify for the CAF Confederation Cup, as the FA Challenge Cup is not played. This was stopped in 2019 with the return of the FA Cup.
mgt: The Mascom Top 8 Cup is a football tournament in Botswana, established in 2012, featuring the top eight teams from the previous Premier League season. Matches include two-legged ties for the quarter-finals and semi-finals, with a single final. The competition has seen notable moments, including a controversial 2018–19 semi-final. Orapa United are the current champions.

trgt:
Oak is a discontinued programming language created by James Gosling in 1989, initially for Sun Microsystems' set-top box project. The language later evolved to become Java.

The name Oak was used by Gosling after an oak tree that stood outside his office.
mgt: Oak was a programming language developed by Sun Microsystems in 1991 as part of the Green project, led by James Gosling, to create software for smart appliances. Renamed Java in 1994, it became the foundation for the widely-used Java platform.

trgt: The Murakumo-class destroyers (叢雲型駆逐艦, Murakumo-gata kuchikukan) ("Gathering Clouds") were a class of six torpedo boat destroyers (TBDs) of the Imperial Japanese Navy, built in Britain in 1897–99. The class is also sometimes referred to as the Shinonome-class destroyers (東雲駆逐艦, Shinonome-gata kuchikukan)("Daybreak"). All were named after celestial phenomena.
mgt: The *Murakumo*-class destroyers were a group of six destroyers built for the Imperial Japanese Navy by John I. Thornycroft & Company in England between 1898 and 1899. Based on the Royal Navy's "Thirty Knotters," they featured a flush deck design and were armed with a QF 12-pounder gun, five QF 6-pounder Hotchkiss guns, and two 18-inch torpedo tubes. The class saw action during the Russo-Japanese War, including the Battle of Tsushima, and later served in World War I. Reclassified as third-class destroyers in 1912, the vessels were gradually retired or repurposed, with the last being decommissioned in 1925.

trgt: William O. Powell II (born March 9, 1988) is a Canadian football running back for the Ottawa Redblacks of the Canadian Football League (CFL). He has also been a member of the Saskatchewan Roughriders, Arizona Cardinals, Philadelphia Eagles, Houston Texans and Detroit Lions.
mgt: William O. Powell II (born March 9, 1988) is an American professional gridiron football running back for the Ottawa Redblacks of the Canadian Football League (CFL). After playing college football at Kansas State, Powell had stints in the NFL before joining the CFL in 2015. He has played for the Ottawa Redblacks, where he won a Grey Cup in 2016, and the Saskatchewan Roughriders, leading the league in rushing touchdowns in 2019. Powell rejoined the Redblacks in 2022.

trgt: Brontotheriidae is a family of extinct mammals belonging to the order Perissodactyla, the order that includes horses, rhinoceroses, and tapirs. Superficially, they looked rather like rhinos, although they were actually more closely related to horses; Equidae and Brontotheriidae make up the suborder Hippomorpha. They lived around 56–34 million years ago, until the very close of the Eocene.
mgt: Brontotheriidae, also known as brontotheres, is an extinct family of large, herbivorous mammals that lived during the Eocene epoch (56–34 million years ago). Members of this family, such as Megacerops, were characterized by massive bodies, distinctive bony horns, and teeth adapted for shearing vegetation. Brontotheres likely became extinct due to their inability to adapt to changing climates and tougher vegetation.

trgt: Khan Bahadur Abū Muḥammad ʿAbdul Ghafūr (1833-1889), better known by his pen name Nassakh (Lang-ur), was a British Indian officer, writer, literary critic and collector. He is best known for his magnum opus Sukhan-e-Shuara (Lang-ur Speech of Poets) which was a biography of prominent Urdu and Persian poets. He organised mushaira in places where he worked; inspiring young Urdu poets in Bengal.
mgt: Khan Bahadur Nassakh (1833–1889), born Abū Muḥammad ʿAbdul Ghafūr, was a Bengali Muslim poet, government officer, and scholar of Urdu and Persian. He served as a deputy magistrate in the Bengal Presidency and was known for his contributions to Urdu literature, including works like *Daftar-e-Bemisal* and *Sukhan-e-Shuara*. Nassakh also translated Persian poetry and critiqued notable poets, earning praise from figures like Ghalib.

trgt: Elections to the Legislative Assembly of the Indian state of Mysore were held on 26 March 1952. 394 candidates contested for 99 seats in 80 constituencies in the Assembly. There were 19 two-member constituencies and 61 single-member constituencies, accounting for 99 seats.
mgt: The 1952 Mysore State Legislative Assembly election was held on 26 March 1952 to elect 99 members to the Mysore Legislative Assembly. The Indian National Congress (INC) emerged victorious, and K. Hanumanthaiah became the Chief Minister, succeeding K. Chengalaraya Reddy.

trgt: La Vinia Delois Jennings is an American literary scholar and critic of twentieth-century American literature and culture, currently a Distinguished Humanities Professor at the University of Tennessee, and also formerly a Lindsay Young Professor and a 1998 Fulbright Senior Lecturer appointed to the University of Málaga in Spain.
mgt: La Vinia Delois Jennings is an American literary scholar, author, and professor specializing in 20th-century American literature and African American studies. A professor at the University of Tennessee, she has received numerous accolades, including the Toni Morrison Society Book Prize and the Jefferson Prize. Her works explore themes of race, culture, and identity.

trgt: The Kinburn Spit (Lang-uk) is a spit in Mykolaiv Raion, Mykolaiv Oblast, Ukraine. It occupies the westernmost part of the Kinburn Peninsula, being its natural extension stretching west into the Black Sea between the Dnieper-Bug estuary and the Bay of Yahorlyk. It is approximately 10 kilometres (6.2 mi) long, with a width of about 1 kilometre (0.62 mi) at its base, narrowing to about 100 metres (330 ft) in its western half.
mgt: The Kinburn Spit (Ukrainian: Кінбурнська коса) is a narrow peninsula in Mykolaiv Oblast, Ukraine, extending into the Black Sea. Known for its unique ecosystem, it is part of the Regional Landscape Park of Kinburn Foreland, which protects its diverse flora and fauna, including rare and endangered species. Historically significant, the spit was the site of an Ottoman fort and later became a natural park. During the 2022 Russian invasion of Ukraine, it was occupied by Russian forces and became a strategic military site, leading to environmental damage and ongoing military operations.

page_title: Hong Kong motion picture rating system
trgt: The Hong Kong motion picture rating system (Chinese: 香港電影分級制度; pinyin: Xiānggǎng diànyǐng fēnjí zhìdù; Jyutping: hoeng1 gong2 din6 jing2 fan1 kap1 zai3 dou6) is a legal system of movie screening and rating. An official government agency issues ratings for any movie that will be shown in Hong Kong cinemas.
mgt: The Hong Kong motion picture rating system, established on November 10, 1988, under the Movie Screening Ordinance, classifies films to guide viewers and protect minors from inappropriate content. Administered by the Office for Film, Newspaper and Article Administration, it features four levels, with Level III restricting access to adults only.

trgt: The National Democratic Party (NDP) was a right wing political party that operated in the United Kingdom during the 1960s and 1970s. The NDP sought to position itself as an early rival to the National Front although ultimately it failed to challenge the position of this group.
mgt: The National Democratic Party (NDP) was a far-right political party in the United Kingdom, officially established in 1966 by Dr. David Brown, who had previously contested elections under the NDP name. The party sought to unite various nationalist groups but struggled with internal divisions and competition from the National Front (NF). It achieved some electoral success, notably in Southampton Itchen during the 1970 general election, but its influence waned in the 1970s due to defections, failed merger attempts with the NF, and the rise of other far-right groups. The NDP dissolved by the late 1970s.

trgt: A composite film is a feature film whose screenplay is composed of two or more distinct stories. More generally, composite structure refers to an aesthetic principle in which the narrative structure relies on contiguity and linking rather than linearity. In a composite text or film, individual pieces are complete within themselves, yet they form a whole work that is greater than the sum of its individual parts.
mgt: A composite film is a cinematic work composed of multiple interrelated stories that form a cohesive whole through unifying elements such as recurring characters, shared themes, or common settings. These films may feature individually titled segments, as seen in *Paris, je t'aime* and *Pulp Fiction*, or remain untitled, like *Love Actually*. Story sequencing, thematic connections, and musical scores—whether unified, composite, or thematic variations—enhance the film's coherence and emotional impact.

trgt: The 2013 Kashiwa Reysol season was Kashiwa Reysol's 3rd season back in the J.League Division 1 since promotion in 2010, making it their 41st season in the top flight overall. They finished 10th in the league, won the J.League Cup and were knocked out of the Emperor's Cup and AFC Champions League at the fourth round Semifinals respectively.
mgt: The 2013 season was Kashiwa Reysol's third consecutive campaign in the J.League Division 1 under manager Nelsinho Baptista. The team finished 10th in the league, won the J.League Cup, and reached the semifinals of the AFC Champions League. Masato Kudo was the season's top scorer with 27 goals in all competitions.

page_title: Higher Vocational Agricultural School of Bevalala
trgt: Higher Vocational Agricultural School of Bevalala – Ecole Professionnelle Supérieure Agricole de Bevalala (EPSA) – in Bevalala, Madagascar, was in 1957 a Jesuit outreach to assist farmers and craftsmen with marginal income to increase their income. It has grown to offer a 3-year bachelor's degree.
mgt: The Higher Vocational Agricultural School of Bevalala, located in Antananarivo, Madagascar, is a Jesuit Catholic institution offering BAC+3 programs in agronomy and technical education. Established in 1993, it evolved from the Rural Learning Centre, expanding its focus to include diverse agricultural and technical training.

trgt: Dance of the Tiger (Swedish: Den Svarta Tigern) is a novel by Finnish palaeontologist Björn Kurtén, published in 1978 and English translation in 1980. It is a prehistoric novel dealing with the interaction between Neanderthals and Cro-Magnons. A sequel, Singletusk, published in 1982, continues the story of the family.
mgt: *Dance of the Tiger* is a 1980 novel by Finnish paleontologist Björn Kurtén, set 35,000 years ago in Ice Age Scandinavia. The story follows a Cro-Magnon named Tiger as he confronts a tyrannical hybrid and explores interactions between Cro-Magnons and Neanderthals. The novel is noted for its scientific plausibility and depiction of interbreeding theories.

trgt: Nagorik Natya Sampradaya is a Bangladeshi theatre company. Established in 1968, it was the first theatre group in Dhaka and started performing plays on stage in 1972. The chairperson of the group is the actor and director Aly Zaker and his wife, actress Sara Zaker, is the vice-chairperson. As of December 2018, Nagorik had performed 46 different productions.
mgt: Nagorik Natya Sampradaya (নাগরিক নাট্য সম্প্রদায়) is one of Bangladesh's oldest and most prominent theatre groups, founded in 1968 by Fazle Lohani, Ataur Rahman, and Zia Haider. Known for staging iconic productions of both Bangla and international plays, the troupe performed Bangladesh's first ticketed play in 1973. In 2018, it celebrated its Golden Jubilee with cultural events and new initiatives.

trgt: The Gator Guard Drill Team, abbreviated GGDT, is a precision drill team and military fraternity based at the University of Florida. The team is named after the Florida Gators, the mascot of the University of Florida. Founded in 1953, the Gator Guard is most widely known for its annual performances in the New Orleans Mardi Gras Parades. In 2016, the team consisted of 31 active male and female members.
mgt: The Gator Guard Drill Team, established in 1953 at the University of Florida, is a precision drill and color guard team formed by ROTC cadets. Known for its performances at the University of Florida's Homecoming Parade and New Orleans Mardi Gras, the team uses M1903 rifles with bayonets. Modeled after the 3rd U.S. Infantry Regiment, "The Old Guard," it annually trains in Washington, D.C., and honors alumnus Mark T. Barrett with a memorial ceremony.

trgt: His Majesty's Hired armed cutter Sandwich served the Royal Navy from 23 May 1798 until the French frigate Créole captured her on 14 June 1799. She then served in the French Navy until the Royal Navy recaptured her on 15 October 1803. The Navy purchased her in 1804 and she served for some months in 1805 as HMS Sandwich before she was sold in Jamaica. During this period she captured three small French privateers in two days.
mgt: The hired armed cutter *Sandwich* was a British naval vessel acquired on 22 May 1798 and armed with 2 4-pounder guns and 10 12-pounder carronades. Under British command, she captured the Dutch hoy *Hoop* in 1798 but was herself captured by the French on 14 June 1799. The French Navy retained her name and used her until 1803, when she was recaptured by the Royal Navy. Purchased in 1804, *Sandwich* served briefly before being sold in Jamaica in 1805.

trgt: The Captain Benjamin Williams House, also known as deKoven House or DeKoven Community Center, is a historic house at 27 Washington Street in Middletown, Connecticut. Built in the late 18th century, it is a particularly fine example of late Georgian architecture, and was listed on the National Register of Historic Places in 1978. It is now owned and operated by the Rockfall Foundation and operated as a community center.
mgt: The Captain Benjamin Williams House is a historic Georgian-style home located at 27 Washington Street in Middletown, Connecticut. Built around 1797 for sea captain Benjamin Williams, it reflects Middletown's prominence as a shipping center in the 18th century. The house features distinctive architectural details, including brownstone quoining and a fanlight entrance. Added to the National Register of Historic Places in 1978, it has undergone significant restorations and now serves as a site on the Middletown Heritage Trail.

trgt: Vena, currently DNA Latin Group, is an American bachata group formed by members Lenny Santos and Max Santos from Aventura alongside ex-vocalist Steven Tejada from Xtreme. They are known for their hits such as "Sangre de Mis Venas", "Corazon de Hierro (featuring Teodoro Reyes & Frank Reyes)", "Señora", "Dile a El", "Por Mentiras", among others. VENA alongside Larry Hernandez were nominated for a Billboard Premious Musica Mexicana award in 2012.
mgt: Vena was a bachata group formed in 2011 in the Bronx, New York, by Lenny Santos and Max Santos, former members of Aventura, alongside Steve Styles from Xtreme. Known as a "Bachata Supergroup," they released several hits, including "Señora," "Ya No," and "Corazón de Hierro." After lineup changes and Steve's departure in 2015, the group rebranded as DNA Latin Group in 2018, continuing to release music.

trgt:
Goodwin Park is a city park and golf course located in Hartford and Wethersfield, Connecticut. Opened to the public in 1901, it covers 227 acres (92 ha) and offers a playground, community pool and a 27-hole golf course.

In December each year, the park hosts Holiday Lights Fantasia, a Greater Hartford tradition since 1999 where illuminated structures festoon the interior road and thousands of cars stream through the park to enjoy 1 million lights.
mgt: Goodwin Park is a 237-acre urban park located in Hartford and Wethersfield, Connecticut, established in 1901. Originally named South Park, it was renamed in honor of Reverend Francis Goodwin, a key advocate for Hartford's park system. Designed by Frederick Law Olmsted, the park features a grand meadow, tree plantations, and water features. The park's central area, once an airstrip, now houses the Goodwin Park Golf Course, a 27-hole facility with historic roots dating back to the late 1800s.

trgt: "Rollin' in my 5.0" is a hip hop song written by American rapper Vanilla Ice. The song samples "Fly Like an Eagle" by the Steve Miller Band. The studio version of the song was only found on the single as it was originally intended to be featured on Mind Blowin. It is about Vanilla Ice's 5.0 Liter Foxbody Mustang (Ford Mustang (third generation)).
mgt: "Rollin' in My 5.0" is a 1991 single by American rapper Vanilla Ice, released as part of his live album *Extremely Live*. The song, a rap ballad, narrates Ice's adventures cruising in Miami, street racing, and thanking fans for his success. Accompanied by a music video, it gained popularity in the early 1990s but was retired by 1992. The track is featured on the 2008 compilation *The Best of Vanilla Ice*.

trgt:
The 354th Fighter Squadron (354 FS) is part of the 355th Fighter Wing at Davis–Monthan Air Force Base, Arizona. It operates A-10 Thunderbolt II aircraft conducting close air support missions.

The squadron conducts Close Air Support, Air Interdiction, Forward Air Control – Airborne, and Combat Search and rescue for theater commanders worldwide.
mgt: The 354th Fighter Squadron, nicknamed the "Bulldogs," is a United States Air Force unit based at Davis–Monthan Air Force Base, Arizona. Active since 1942, it has participated in major conflicts including the European Theater of Operations and the Vietnam War. Currently part of the 355th Operations Group, it conducts fighter and forward air control training.

trgt: 16th Street Park, also known as Dominico – 16th Street Park, is a municipal park in Bayonne, New Jersey. It is located on the west side of the city along the Newark Bay across from Port Newark–Elizabeth Marine Terminal. It is a component of the Hackensack RiverWalk.
mgt: 16th Street Park, officially named DiDomenico – 16th Street Park, is a public park in Bayonne, New Jersey, developed as a Works Progress Administration project. It features various recreational facilities, including sports courts, fields, walking paths, and a municipal pool. The park honors former mayor G. Thomas DiDomenico.

trgt: Ching Cheung-ying (Chinese: 程張迎, born 31 May 1958) is a Hong Kong politician and retired schoolteacher. He is formerly the representative of the Sun Tin Wai constituency of Sha Tin District Council and the former chairperson of the Council. He is a member of the Democratic Party.
mgt: Ching Cheung-ying is a Hong Kong educator and politician. A former Chinese Language and History teacher at Diocesan Boys' School, he opposed its transition to a fee-paying model. He has served as a Sha Tin District councillor since 1985 and was awarded the Medal of Honour in 2007.

trgt: Martin Straka (born September 3, 1972) is a Czech former ice hockey center who most recently played for HC Plzeň 1929 of the Czech Extraliga. He is also the club's general manager and co-owner, having bought a 70% share of the team in 2009. Straka played in the National Hockey League (NHL) from 1992 until 2008 with the Pittsburgh Penguins, Ottawa Senators, New York Islanders, Florida Panthers, Los Angeles Kings and New York Rangers.
mgt: Martin Straka (born September 3, 1972) is a Czech former professional ice hockey forward who played in the National Hockey League (NHL) for 15 seasons. Drafted 19th overall by the Pittsburgh Penguins in 1992, he also played for the Ottawa Senators, New York Islanders, Florida Panthers, Los Angeles Kings, and New York Rangers. Straka was known for his speed and two-way play, achieving a career-high 95 points in the 2000–01 season. Internationally, he won gold at the 1998 Winter Olympics and the 2005 World Championships. After returning to the Czech Extraliga in 2008, he won a league title with HC Škoda Plzeň in 2013. Straka retired in 2014.

trgt: Among the paintings attributed to Giovanni da Rimini (fl. 1292–1336) are two panels from a former diptych, dated to 1300–1305, of which the left wing is in the collection of the National Gallery, London, and the right that of the Galleria Nazionale d'Arte Antica, Palazzo Barberini, Rome.
mgt: The *Diptych by Giovanni da Rimini* is a pair of early 14th-century panel paintings attributed to the Italian painter Giovanni da Rimini, a key figure in the Riminese School influenced by Giotto and Byzantine art. The left wing, *Scenes from the Lives of the Virgin and other Saints*, is housed in the National Gallery, London, while the right wing, *Scenes from the Life of Christ*, is in the Galleria Nazionale d'Arte Antica, Rome. The diptych features intricate, jewel-like depictions of religious scenes, reflecting the donor's wishes and showcasing Giovanni's mastery. The left wing was acquired by the National Gallery in 2015 after a temporary export bar, with financial assistance from Ronald Lauder. Giovanni's work, including this diptych, has been pivotal in reconstructing the early Riminese School and its connections to Giotto's innovations.

trgt: The Platte River Campground Site, designated 20BZ16, is an archaeological site located along the Platte River, within the Sleeping Bear Dunes National Lakeshore near Empire, Michigan. It is significant as a largely intact record of prehistoric life over a long span of time. It was listed on the National Register of Historic Places in 1990.
mgt: The Platte River Campground Site, located along the Platte River within the Sleeping Bear Dunes National Lakeshore in Michigan, is an archaeological site spanning 9 acres. Occupied intermittently from A.D. 130 to 1269 during the Middle and Late Woodland periods, it later became part of the logging community of Edgewater. Archaeological excavations in 1987 uncovered prehistoric and historic artifacts, highlighting its significance. The site was added to the National Register of Historic Places in 1990.

trgt: Rosa Amelia Guzmán was a Salvadoran journalist, feminist and suffragette. Her 1950 speech to the Constituent Assembly was instrumental in women gaining, not just the right to vote, but the rights of citizenship on 14 September. She was one of the first four women elected to serve in the Legislative Assembly of El Salvador.
mgt: Rosa Amelia Guzmán (later Rosa Amelia Guzmán de Araujo) was a Salvadoran feminist, activist, and politician who played a key role in advancing women's rights in El Salvador. She co-founded the journal *Tribuna Femenina* and the Salvadoran Feminist League, advocating for women's suffrage and legal equality. In 1956, she became one of the first female deputies in El Salvador's Legislative Assembly.

trgt: "Romeo & Juliet" is a song recorded by Kosovo-Albanian rapper Mozzik and Swiss-Albanian rapper Loredana. An Albanian and German rap song, it was solely written by the rappers themselves. German producers Miksu and Macloud were additionally hired for the song's composition and production process.
mgt: "Romeo & Juliet" is a 2019 hip hop song by Mozzik and Loredana, released independently on February 1, 2019. Written by the duo and produced by Miksu and Macloud, the track follows their previous collaboration, "Bonnie & Clyde." Its music video has garnered significant attention on YouTube.

trgt: Klagenfurt Athletic Sports Club or EC KAC (Lang-de) is a professional ice hockey team in the ICE Hockey League. The team plays their home games in Klagenfurt, Carinthia, Austria at Stadthalle Klagenfurt. EC KAC has won the most Austrian ice hockey Championships, a total of 32 times including 11 consecutive titles from 1964 to 1974, and four consecutive from 1985 to 1988. Most recently they won it in 2021.
mgt: EC KAC (Klagenfurt Athletic Sports Club) is a professional ice hockey team based in Klagenfurt, Carinthia, Austria. Founded in 1909, it is the oldest and most successful ice hockey club in Austria, competing in the ICE Hockey League. The team has won a record 32 Austrian championships, with its first title in 1934 and its most recent in 2021. Their home games are played at Stadthalle Klagenfurt.

trgt: Sheddrick Tobias "Buck" Gurley (born April 7, 1978) is a former American football defensive tackle. Gurley played college football for Florida. He played professionally in the NFL for the Miami Dolphins, Chicago Bears, and Tampa Bay Buccaneers and was part of the 2002 Buccaneers team that won Super Bowl XXXVII.
mgt: Buck Gurley (born April 7, 1978) is a former American football defensive tackle and current coach. He played college football for the Florida Gators, winning an SEC Championship in 2000. Gurley had a brief NFL career, including a stint with the Tampa Bay Buccaneers during their Super Bowl XXXVII-winning season.

trgt: Wittering is a village and civil parish in the City of Peterborough unitary authority area in the ceremonial county of Cambridgeshire, England. The village is about 3 miles (5 km) south of the market town of Stamford in neighbouring Lincolnshire and about 9 miles (14 km) west of Peterborough's urban sprawl.
mgt: Wittering is a village and civil parish in Cambridgeshire, England, with a population of 2,297 as of the 2001 census. Its Church of England parish church, All Saints, dates back to the Anglo-Saxon period and is a Grade I listed building. RAF Wittering, established in 1916, has played significant roles in military aviation history, including as a base for the UK's V bomber force and later as "The Home of the Harrier."

trgt: Rhyssa persuasoria, also known as the sabre wasp, is a species belonging to the family Ichneumonidae subfamily Rhyssinae. Members of this subfamily, including those of Rhyssa and the allied Megarhyssa, are also known collectively as giant ichneumonid wasps or giant ichneumons.
mgt: *Rhyssa persuasoria* is a large ichneumon wasp species in the family Ichneumonidae, found across Europe, North Africa, the Near East, and parts of the Indomalayan and Australasian realms. It inhabits coniferous and mixed woodlands. Females use their long ovipositors to lay eggs on wood-boring larvae, which serve as food for their offspring. Adults, active from July to August, feed on carbohydrates like honeydew.

trgt: Karli Coburger (born 4 October 1929) is a former Major general in the East German Ministry for State Security (MfS / "Stasi"). In 1984 he became, as matters turned out, the last head of the service's Central Department for surveillance, fact finding and arrests ("Hauptabteilung / Section VIII").
mgt: Karli Coburger (born October 4, 1929) is a former senior intelligence officer of East Germany's Ministry for State Security (Stasi). Rising to the rank of major general, he led Section VIII, overseeing surveillance, investigations, and arrests. Coburger was implicated in human rights abuses, including psychological torture, and was later convicted of complicity in the perversion of justice.

trgt: Dick Tracy vs. Crime, Inc. (1941) is a Republic Movie serial based on the Dick Tracy comic strip. It was directed by the team of William Witney and John English with Ralph Byrd reprising his role from the earlier serials. It was the last of the four Dick Tracy serials produced by Republic, although Ralph Byrd went on to portray the character again in two features and on television.
mgt: *Dick Tracy vs. Crime, Inc.* is a 1941 Republic Pictures film serial directed by William Witney and John English. Starring Ralph Byrd as the titular detective, the serial follows Dick Tracy as he battles a villain known as The Ghost, who possesses the ability to become invisible. Praised for its action and suspense, it is considered one of the best Dick Tracy serials. It was re-released in 1952 and later on DVD.

trgt: The qualification for the 2023 BAL season, also known as Road to BAL 2023, is the 3rd edition of the qualifying tournaments for the Basketball Africa League. The tournaments are organised by FIBA Africa and are held from 10 October to 27 November 2022.
mgt: The 2023 BAL qualification, also known as the Road to BAL 2023, was the qualifying tournament for the 2023 Basketball Africa League (BAL), organized by FIBA Africa. Held from October 10 to November 27, 2022, it featured 21 teams across multiple divisions, with several teams withdrawing before the games. The tournament included group stages in various African cities and an Elite 16 round, where additional teams joined through qualification or wild cards. The East and West Division games determined the final qualifiers for the BAL.

trgt: Pashchimottanasana (Lang-sa), Seated Forward Bend, or Intense Dorsal Stretch is a seated forward-bending asana in hatha yoga and modern yoga as exercise. Janusirsasana is a variant with one knee bent out to the side; Upavishthakonasana has the legs straight and wide apart.
mgt: Paschimottanasana, or Seated Forward Bend, is a classical yoga asana described in the 15th-century *Hatha Yoga Pradipika*. The pose involves bending forward from a seated position to stretch the back of the body. Variations include twists, leg positions, and balancing forms.

trgt: Egon Albrecht-Lemke (19 May 1918 – 25 August 1944) was a German-Brazilian Luftwaffe fighter pilot and recipient of the Knight's Cross of the Iron Cross during World War II. Albrecht claimed 25 aerial victories, 10 over the Western Front and 15 over the Eastern Front.
mgt: Egon Albrecht-Lemke (19 May 1918 – 25 August 1944) was a German Luftwaffe Hauptmann and flying ace during World War II. Born in Curitiba, Brazil, he flew combat missions on both the Eastern and Western Fronts, achieving 25 aerial victories and destroying numerous ground targets. Albrecht-Lemke was awarded the Knight's Cross of the Iron Cross in 1943 for his achievements. He served as a squadron leader and later as a group commander, flying aircraft such as the Messerschmitt Bf 110 and Bf 109. He was killed in action on 25 August 1944 after being shot down by USAAF P-51 Mustangs near Creil, France.

trgt: Emerich (Imre) Vogl (born 12 August 1905 in Temesvár, Austria-Hungary (now Romania) – died 29 October 1971 in Bucharest, Romania) was a Romanian football player and coach of Banat Swabian ethnicity who was a member of Romanian team which participated at the 1930 FIFA World Cup in Uruguay and 1934 FIFA World Cup in Italy.
mgt: Emerich Vogl (12 August 1905 – 29 October 1971) was a Romanian footballer and manager, known for his versatility as a defender and midfielder. He played for Chinezul Timișoara and Juventus București, winning multiple Liga I titles. Vogl represented Romania in the 1930 and 1934 FIFA World Cups and later managed both Juventus București and the Romania national team.

trgt: Nikaho (にかほ市, Nikaho-shi) is a city located in Akita Prefecture, Japan. As of 31 December 2019, the city had an estimated population of 24,291 in 9,371 households, and a population density of 100 persons per km2. The total area of the city is 241.13 square kilometres (93.10 sq mi).
mgt: Nikaho (にかほ市) is a city in Akita Prefecture, Japan, located in the Tōhoku region. Established on October 1, 2005, through the merger of Nikaho, Kisakata, and Konoura, it covers an area of 241.13 km² and had a population of 24,291 as of December 2019. Bordered by the Sea of Japan and Yamagata Prefecture, Nikaho features a humid subtropical climate and is partially within Chōkai Quasi-National Park. The city’s economy is driven by agriculture, commercial fishing, and industry, including a TDK electronics factory. Nikaho is also known for Mount Chōkai, a popular destination for hiking and skiing.

trgt: "7empest" (pronounced "tempest") is a song by American rock band Tool. Clocking in at over 15 minutes in length, it is the final song on the band's fifth studio album, Fear Inoculum. The song peaked at number 6 on the Billboard Hot Rock Songs chart and was cited by critics as a standout track from the album. It later won the Grammy Award for Best Metal Performance.
mgt: "7empest" is a song by the American rock band Tool, released on August 30, 2019, as part of their album *Fear Inoculum*. Spanning 15 minutes and 43 seconds, the track is noted for its complex structure, shifting time signatures, and aggressive vocals. Widely acclaimed, it was praised as the standout track of the album by multiple publications and won the Grammy Award for Best Metal Performance in 2020.

trgt:
Opendatasoft is a French company that offers data sharing software. Based in Paris, it also has a subsidiary in Boston (United States) and an office in Nantes (France).

Opendatasoft has developed a tool for sharing and reusing the data of companies and public administrations. Its software lets you organize, share, and visualize any type of data. The software can be used by both private companies and public entities
mgt: Opendatasoft is a global software-as-a-service (SaaS) company founded in 2011 by Jean-Marc Lazard, Franck Carassus, and David Thoumas. Headquartered in Boston, Massachusetts, and Paris, France, it provides data-sharing and visualization solutions for public and private organizations. Known for its open data platforms, Opendatasoft serves over 280 customers worldwide, including cities, governments, and corporations, and hosts the annual "Data on Board" summit to promote data sharing.

trgt: Ninnes is a locality at the northeastern corner of Yorke Peninsula and western side of the Mid North of South Australia. It lies where the Upper Yorke Road from Kulpara to Bute is crossed by the road from Paskeville to Lochiel. The dominant industry is broadacre grain and sheep farming.
mgt: Ninnes is a rural locality in South Australia, situated within the District Council of Barunga West. Established in the 1860s, it was historically significant for its early settlement and the proclamation of the Hundred of Ninnes in 1874. Once the seat of the District Council of Ninnes, the locality's boundaries were formalized in 1998.

trgt: Ro-45 was an Imperial Japanese Navy Kaichū type submarine of the K6 sub-class. Completed and commissioned in January 1944, she served in the central Pacific Ocean during World War II and was sunk in April 1944 during her first combat sortie.
mgt: The Japanese submarine *Ro-45* was a Kaichū-type (K6 subclass) submarine of the Imperial Japanese Navy during World War II. Commissioned in January 1944, it operated briefly before being sunk on April 30, 1944, near Truk by U.S. Navy forces during airstrikes by Task Force 58. All 74 crew members were lost, and the submarine was officially declared presumed lost in May 1944. *Ro-45* was stricken from the Navy list in July 1944.

trgt: Marco Pinotti (born 25 February 1976 in Osio Sotto, Lombardy) is an Italian former road racing cyclist, who competed as a professional between 1999 and 2013. An individual time trial specialist, Pinotti was a six-time Italian Time Trial Champion (2005, 2007, 2008, 2009, 2010 and 2013).
mgt: Marco Pinotti (born February 25, 1976) is a retired Italian professional road cyclist and time trial specialist. Known as "L'Ingegnere" (The Engineer) for his analytical approach, he achieved notable success in the Giro d'Italia, winning two individual time trial stages and two team time trial stages. Pinotti also held the race leader's pink jersey during the 2007 Giro. Over his career, he won six Italian National Time Trial Championships and excelled in stage races and one-day events. After retiring in 2013, he transitioned to a role in sports science.

trgt:
Infobox baseball season yearly The 1907 Los Angeles Angels season was the fifth season for the Los Angeles Angels playing in the Pacific Coast League (PCL). The Angels compiled a 115–74 record and won the PCL pennant. The team played its home games at Chutes Park and Prager Park in Los Angeles.

Henry Berry was the team's manager. In January 1907, he also purchased a controlling interest in the team.
mgt: The 1907 Los Angeles Angels season was part of the Pacific Coast League (PCL) and featured standout performances from both pitchers and position players. Pitcher Dolly Gray led the team with 32 wins and a 1.71 ERA, while "Sleepy Bill" Burns and Judge Nagle also contributed strong seasons. Among position players, Pop Dillon and Gavvy Cravath excelled offensively, with Cravath tying for the league lead in doubles. Walter Carlisle led the PCL in home runs and runs scored.

trgt: Owain Sebastian Yeoman is a Welsh actor. His credits include The Nine, Kitchen Confidential, AMC's Turn (as Benedict Arnold) and the HBO series Generation Kill. Additionally, he portrayed CBI Agent Wayne Rigsby in The Mentalist and portrayed Benny Gallagher in Emergence.
mgt: Owain Sebastian Yeoman (born 2 July 1978) is a Welsh actor known for his roles in film and television. He made his film debut in *Troy* and gained prominence as Wayne Rigsby in *The Mentalist*. Yeoman has also appeared in *Generation Kill*, *American Sniper*, and *Turn: Washington's Spies*. A graduate of Oxford University and the Royal Academy of Dramatic Art, he initially pursued a career in banking before transitioning to acting. Yeoman is a vegetarian and has been featured in a PETA campaign.

trgt: The crested servaline genet (Genetta cristata), also known as the crested genet, is a genet species endemic to Nigeria and Cameroon. As the population has declined due to loss of habitat, it is listed as Vulnerable on the IUCN Red List. It was first recorded in the Mamfe Division in Cameroon and initially considered a subspecies of the servaline genet (Genetta servalina). But now it is regarded as a distinct species.
mgt: The crested servaline genet (*Genetta cristata*) is a small carnivorous mammal in the family Viverridae, native to southern Nigeria and western Cameroon. It inhabits primary and secondary forests, as well as bush–mango plantations, at elevations up to 1,000 meters. Characterized by its khaki-colored fur, dark spots, and a nuchal crest, it is classified as Vulnerable by the IUCN due to habitat loss from agriculture, oil production, and hunting.

trgt: Robert Simpson Neighbors (November 3, 1815 – September 14, 1859) was an Indian agent and Texas state legislator. Known as a fair and determined protector of Indian interests as guaranteed by treaty, he was murdered for his beliefs by a Texan who disagreed with giving any rights to the Comanches.
mgt: Robert Simpson Neighbors (November 3, 1815 – September 14, 1859) was an American Indian agent, soldier, and politician in Texas. Known for his advocacy for Native Americans, he implemented the "field system," directly engaging with tribes in their homelands. Neighbors played a key role in establishing Indian reservations in Texas and relocating tribes to Indian Territory. His efforts to protect Native Americans made him a controversial figure, ultimately leading to his assassination in 1859.

trgt: None but One is a studio album released by American singer Jean Ritchie in 1977 on Sire LP record SA-7530. The album was a stylistic departure for Ritchie in that it featured "modern" instruments and production values. It resulted in significant critical acclaim, but the album suffered serious distribution problems. A song from the album, "Now is the Cool of the Day" became an anthem for environmentalists opposed to mountaintop removal.
mgt: *None but One* is a 1977 studio album by American folk musician Jean Ritchie, released by Sire Records. Departing from her traditional acoustic style, the album incorporates electric instruments and higher production values while maintaining Appalachian influences. Featuring social commentary and themes of racial harmony and environmentalism, it includes the title track and an environmental hymn later adopted by activists. Critically acclaimed, it received top folk music awards but faced limited commercial success due to distribution issues.

trgt: Steinfurt-Burgsteinfurt station is the main station of the town of Steinfurt in western Munsterland in the German state of North Rhine-Westphalia and is located in the district of Burgsteinfurt. The station is a former railway junction on the Münster–Enschede railway. The Coesfeld–Rheine railway and the Borken–Steinfurt railway are closed and largely dismantled.
mgt: Steinfurt-Burgsteinfurt station is a railway station in Steinfurt, North Rhine-Westphalia, Germany, located on the Münster–Enschede line. Opened on 30 September 1875, it became a junction station with the addition of several lines, though many were later closed. Renamed in 2004, it now serves regional rail services and is remotely controlled from Coesfeld.

trgt: Margaret Bernadine Hall (10 March 1863 – 2 January 1910) was an English painter who spent most of her career in Paris. Few of her works have survived, but she is notable for her 1886 painting Fantine, which hangs in the Walker Art Gallery, Liverpool, England. The subject of the painting is Fantine, a character in Victor Hugo's 1862 novel Les Misérables.
mgt: Margaret Bernadine Hall (1863–1910) was a British painter known for her works in social realism, including *Fantine* and *Les Abandonées*. Born in Liverpool, she studied art in Paris and exhibited internationally, including at the Paris Salon. Hall traveled extensively before returning to England, where she died in Hampstead Heath. Few of her paintings survive, with *Fantine* housed at the Walker Art Gallery.

trgt: Colonel Sir John Wallace Pringle, CB, FRGS (23 May 1863 – 16 July 1938) was a British engineer who was Chief Inspecting Officer of the Railways Inspectorate of the Ministry of Transport from 1916 to 1929. As such he was in charge of investigations into a number of serious railway accidents in the UK.
mgt: Sir John Wallace Pringle CB (1863 – 16 July 1938) was a British army officer, railway engineer, and civil servant. He served in the Royal Engineers, contributing to the Uganda railway survey and the Hyderabad-Godavari Valley Railway. Later, as Chief Inspecting Officer for the Ministry of Transport, he led railway safety investigations and modernization efforts.

trgt: Jelena Jovanović (Serbian Cyrillic: Јелена Јовановић; born 1980) is a politician in Serbia. She has served in the Assembly of Vojvodina since 2016 as a member of the Serbian Progressive Party. Jovanović is the first Roma woman to serve in the provincial assembly.
mgt: Jelena Jovanović is a Serbian politician and advocate for the Roma community, serving as a member of the Assembly of Vojvodina since 2016. A member of Serbia's Roma National Council, she has focused on education and combating anti-Roma discrimination in Serbia.

trgt: Antasena-class combat boat is a combat boat built by North Sea Boats and designed by LOMOcean Design for the Indonesian armed forces. The manufacturer designated it as Combat Boat X-18 / CB X-18. Initially, this class of ships was developed from a tank boat program from a consortium consisting of PT Lundin (North Sea Boats), PT Pindad, PT LEN, and PT Hariff Daya Tunggal Engineering, with John Cockerill as the turret supplier.
mgt: The **Antasena-class combat boat** is an Indonesian catamaran-type combat vessel developed by North Sea Boats and first unveiled in 2016. Designed for amphibious operations in shallow waters, it features advanced composite materials, a Cockerill 3030 turret, and optional integrated drones. The boat can carry up to 60 troops and is powered by twin MTU diesel engines, achieving speeds of up to 50 knots. The first prototype underwent sea trials in 2021.

trgt:
Kettle War infobox

The Kettle War (Lang-nl) was a military confrontation between the troops of the Holy Roman Empire and the Republic of the Seven Netherlands on 8 October 1784. It was named the Kettle War because the only shot fired hit a soup kettle.
mgt: The Kettle War was a brief and largely bloodless conflict in 1784 between the Dutch Republic and the Austrian Netherlands, sparked by disputes over the Scheldt River's closure to shipping. The conflict ended with the Treaty of Fontainebleau, reaffirming the river's closure and compensating Austria.

trgt: Airblue Limited (stylized as airblue) is a private Pakistani airline with its head office on the 12th floor of the Islamabad Stock Exchange (ISE) Towers in Islamabad, Pakistan. Airblue operates scheduled domestic and international flights, the latter to Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates.
mgt: Airblue is a private Pakistani airline founded in 2003, with its headquarters in Islamabad. It commenced operations in 2004 and operates a fleet of 12 aircraft serving 26 destinations, including domestic and international routes. Known for innovations like e-ticketing and wireless check-in, Airblue also introduced a frequent flyer program, "Blue Miles." The airline initially expanded rapidly, competing with Pakistan International Airlines and launching international flights in 2005. Despite challenges, including the discontinuation of UK operations, Airblue remains a key player in Pakistan's aviation industry. Its subsidiary, Airbahn, operates in the United States.

trgt: Helene Hewitt OBE is a British climate scientist who is a research fellow at the Met Office. Her research considers climate and ocean models. Hewitt serves on the CLIVAR Ocean Model Development Panel. She was awarded an Order of the British Empire in the 2022 Birthday Honours.
mgt: Helene Hewitt OBE is a British climate scientist specializing in ocean and sea ice modeling. She is the Head of Ocean Modelling at the Met Office Hadley Centre, where she develops climate models, including HadGEM3. Hewitt contributed to the IPCC Sixth Assessment Report and was honored with an OBE in 2022.

trgt: Ahmed Javed (born 2 January 1956) is a retired Indian police officer and civil servant who was the 39th Commissioner of Police, Mumbai, India. He was succeeded by Dattatray Padsalgikar. He is former Indian Ambassador to Saudi Arabia. Javed is the second former Mumbai police commissioner to get a diplomatic posting after J. F. Ribeiro who served as Indian ambassador to Romania between 1989–1993.
mgt: Ahmed Javed (born January 2, 1956) is a retired Indian Police Service (IPS) officer and diplomat who served as the 39th Police Commissioner of Mumbai from September 2015 to January 2016 and as the 20th Indian Ambassador to Saudi Arabia from December 2015 to April 2019. A 1980 batch IPS officer, he held various key positions in law enforcement and introduced innovative policing initiatives, including Mumbai Police's popular Twitter handle.

trgt: Tomás Francisco Reilly (December 20, 1908 – July 21, 1992) was an American-born bishop in the Catholic Church. He served as Prelate of San Juan de la Maguana in the Dominican Republic from 1956–1969 and the first bishop of the Diocese of San Juan de la Maguana from 1969–1977.
mgt: Tomás Francisco Reilly, C.Ss.R. (December 20, 1908 – July 21, 1992), was an American-born Catholic bishop and member of the Redemptorists. He served as the first Bishop of San Juan de la Maguana in the Dominican Republic from 1969 to 1977, after previously leading the prelature since 1956.

trgt: Stefi Baum (born December 11, 1958 in Chicago, Illinois) is an American astronomer. The American Astronomical Society honored her work by awarding her the Annie J. Cannon Prize in 1993. Baum helped to develop the Hubble Space Telescope and, starting in 2004, was the director of Rochester Institute of Technology’s Chester F. Carlson Center for Imaging Science.
mgt: Stefi Baum (born December 11, 1958) is an American astronomer and academic, currently serving as the dean of the Faculty of Science and a professor of physics and astronomy at the University of Manitoba. She earned her BA in physics from Harvard University and a PhD in astronomy from the University of Maryland. Baum has held various prominent positions, including roles at the Space Telescope Science Institute (STScl) and the U.S. Department of State, and has conducted research in the Netherlands.

trgt: Benedicto “Bong” Ducat (born May 6, 1957) is a Filipino impressionist painter. He is now teaching at the University of Santo Tomas College of Fine Arts and Design.
