He is noted for his renderings of plants, flowers and nature in droplet-like brush strokes and bright colors. In college, Ducat won the University of Santo Tomas Benavides Civic Award.
mgt: Benedicto "Bong" Ducat (born May 6, 1957) is a Filipino painter and art professor known for his works such as *The Joyful Moods of Nature*, *Forest Leaves*, and *Lotus*. A graduate of the University of Santo Tomas College of Fine Arts, he has held various roles in art education and craftsmanship and currently teaches at his alma mater.

trgt: Icon is a compilation album by American country artist Trisha Yearwood. It was released on August 31, 2010 via MCA Nashville Records and charted on the Billboard country albums chart. It was one of several compilations released by MCA following Yearwood's departure from the label in 2007. It contained a series of Yearwood's biggest hits from her years at the label.
mgt: *Icon* is a compilation album by American country artist Trisha Yearwood, released on August 31, 2010, by MCA Nashville. Part of MCA's *Icon* series, the album features 12 of Yearwood's hit singles recorded between 1991 and 2001, produced by various collaborators, including Garth Fundis and Tony Brown. It peaked at number 69 on the *Billboard* Top Country Albums chart and received positive reviews.

trgt: The Schöneberger Sängerknaben were a German boys' choir from Berlin, named after the Bezirk Schöneberg [de]. The choir performed with about 30 boys at a time. They wore short black trousers, black blazers with emblems, white shirts and white knee socks. The repertoire initially included German folk songs, Berlin popular songs and Schlager, later also opera choruses and other classical works.
mgt: The Schöneberger Sängerknaben was a German boys' choir founded by Gerhard Hellwig on November 12, 1947. Initially performing in hospitals and public spaces, the choir gained prominence through opera performances, including at the Deutsche Oper Berlin and the Bayreuth Festival. They appeared in films, recorded songs, and toured internationally. The choir ceased operations following Hellwig's death on January 15, 2011.

trgt: Acalymma vittatum, the striped cucumber beetle, is a beetle of the family Chrysomelidae and a serious pest of cucurbit crops in both larval and adult stages. It is distributed from eastern North America to the Rocky Mountains. It is replaced in the west by Acalymma trivittatum, a duller species often with greyish or pale white elytra rather than yellow.
mgt: *Acalymma vittatum*, commonly known as the striped cucumber beetle, is a small beetle in the family Chrysomelidae. Native to North America, it is a significant agricultural pest of cucurbit crops, causing damage through foliar and root feeding and transmitting bacterial wilt (*Erwinia tracheiphila*). Control methods include pesticides, trap crops, biological agents, and vermicompost fertilizers.

trgt: Alan Arnett McLeod, VC (20 April 1899 – 6 November 1918) was a Canadian soldier, aviator, and a recipient of the Victoria Cross, the highest award for gallantry in the face of the enemy that can be awarded to British and Commonwealth forces. McLeod served as a pilot in the Royal Flying Corps and later the Royal Air Force during the First World War.
mgt: Alan Arnett McLeod VC (April 20, 1899 – November 6, 1918) was a Canadian First World War flying ace and the youngest Canadian to receive the Victoria Cross. Serving with the Royal Flying Corps, he displayed extraordinary bravery during a mission in March 1918, saving his observer and surviving a fiery crash despite severe injuries. McLeod died at age 19 from the Spanish flu. He is commemorated through numerous tributes, including a street, a school building, and his induction into the Canadian Aviation Hall of Fame.

trgt:
The team rosters for the 2001 South American U-20 Championship football tournament held in Ecuador. The ten national teams involved in the tournament were required to register a squad of 20 players; only players in these squads were eligible to take part in the tournament.

Players name marked in bold have been capped at full international level.
mgt: The 2001 South American U-20 Championship squads featured the national under-20 football teams of ten South American countries competing in the tournament. Each team, led by their respective coaches, included a roster of young players representing clubs from their home countries and abroad. Notable participants included future stars who would go on to achieve success in professional football.

trgt: The Northampton Red Sox were a minor league baseball team based in Cape Charles, Virginia, which lies within Northampton County, Virginia, in 1927 and 1928. The Northampton teams played as exclusively as members of the Class D level Eastern Shore League.
mgt: The Northampton Red Sox were a minor league baseball team based in Cape Charles, Virginia, that played in the Class D Eastern Shore League from 1927 to 1928. The team, unaffiliated with the Boston Red Sox, played home games at Patton Field. After finishing last in their inaugural season, the Red Sox led the league in 1928 when it folded midseason. The franchise and the league ceased operations that year, and Cape Charles has not hosted another minor league team.

trgt: Sultan of Selangor (سلطان سلاڠور) is the title of the constitutional ruler of Selangor, Malaysia who is the head of state and head of the Islamic religion in Selangor. The current monarch, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah ascended the throne on the death of his father, on 22 November 2001.
mgt: The Sultan of Selangor is the hereditary monarch and head of state of the Malaysian state of Selangor. Established in 1766, the Selangor Sultanate traces its lineage to a Bugis dynasty. The Sultan serves as the Head of Islam in Selangor and holds significant constitutional and ceremonial roles, including appointing the Menteri Besar and conferring state honors. The current Sultan, Sharafuddin Idris Shah, has reigned since 2001. The Sultanate's official residences include Istana Alam Shah in Klang and Istana Bukit Kayangan in Shah Alam. The Sultan of Selangor is also a member of Malaysia's Conference of Rulers, eligible to be elected as the Yang di-Pertuan Agong.

trgt: Mariano Ezequiel Arcioni (born 2 April 1970) is an Argentine politician who has served as the governor of Chubut Province since 1 November 2017. From 2015 to 2017, he was the province's vice governor under Mario Das Neves, whose office Arcioni took upon Das Neves's death on 31 October 2017.
mgt: Mariano Ezequiel Arcioni (born 2 April 1970) is an Argentine lawyer and politician serving as the Governor of Chubut Province since 2017. A member of the We Are All Chubut party, he previously served as Vice Governor under Mario Das Neves. His tenure has been marked by financial crises and controversial mining policies.

trgt: Jun Nishida (西田潤, Nishida Jun, 1977 – March 26, 2005) was a Japanese ceramicist. He is best known for his massive conceptual pottery pieces, which experiment with the material capacities of clay and the imaginative forms that ceramics could take amid the intense thermochemical conditions of the kiln. Throughout his brief yet productive career, Nishida pushed the boundaries of contemporary ceramics, challenging conventions of scale, abstraction, and method to produce a radically new visual language of pottery.
mgt: Jun Nishida (1977–2005) was a Japanese ceramic artist known for his radical and experimental approach to ceramics, which he described as "excavation." Born in Osaka, he studied at Kyoto Seika University, earning BFA and MFA degrees. Nishida's work, influenced by the postwar avant-garde Sōdeisha movement, challenged traditional notions of ceramics by reshaping and fracturing kiln-fired pieces. His best-known series, *Zetsu* (2000–2005), features massive, texturally dynamic sculptures. Nishida died at age 28 in a kiln explosion in Java, Indonesia.

trgt: In statistics, the strictly standardized mean difference (SSMD) is a measure of effect size. It is the mean divided by the standard deviation of a difference between two random values each from one of two groups. It was initially proposed for quality control and hit selection in high-throughput screening (HTS) and has become a statistical parameter measuring effect sizes for the comparison of any two groups with random values.
mgt: The strictly standardized mean difference (SSMD) is a statistical parameter used in high-throughput screening (HTS) assays to measure the differentiation between two groups, such as positive and negative controls. Defined as the ratio of the mean to the standard deviation of the difference between two groups, SSMD is widely applied for quality control and hit selection in HTS experiments. It offers advantages over traditional metrics like the Z-factor by accounting for variability in both groups and providing a probabilistic basis for interpretation.

trgt: Robinsons Summit Center, formerly known as the JG Summit Center, is an office skyscraper located in Makati, Philippines. It is owned by JG Summit Holdings, Inc., and developed by its real estate arm, Robinsons Land Corporation. It stands at 174 meters (571 ft), it is currently the 9th-tallest complete building in Makati, and is one of the tallest buildings in the Philippines.
mgt: The Robinsons Summit Center is a 38-story office skyscraper located on Ayala Avenue in Makati, Philippines. Completed in 2001, the 174-meter (570.87 ft) building was designed by Hellmuth, Obata + Kassabaum in collaboration with W.V. Coscolluela & Associates. Owned by Robinsons Land Corporation, it features a double-height lobby, advanced elevator systems, a helipad, and amenities like a Fitness First branch and ample parking.

trgt:
First Manhattan Co. (FMC) was founded in 1964 by a group of financial industry executives led by David "Sandy" Gottesman. FMC remains an owner-operated investment advisory firm. Robert Gottesman, David Gottesman's son, is currently the firm's CEO.

FMC is based in New York City.
mgt: First Manhattan Co. (FMC) is a financial services and asset management firm founded in 1964 by David Gottesman and headquartered in New York City. The firm manages over $16 billion in assets (as of 2013) for high-net-worth individuals, trusts, retirement accounts, and institutional clients, employing a long-term, value-oriented investment strategy. Known for its ties to Berkshire Hathaway and Warren Buffett, FMC focuses on fundamental analysis and U.S. equity and fixed-income markets. The firm is led by CEO Robert Gottesman and a management committee. In 2013, FMC played a prominent role in a proxy contest to restructure the leadership of biotech company VIVUS, Inc.

trgt: Early relations between India and the Soviet Union were hostile and based on mutual suspicion. While relations were established in 1947 after India's independence, Soviet leader Joseph Stalin had a negative view of Mahatma Gandhi, the Indian National Congress, and Jawaharlal Nehru, whom he viewed as tools of the British and monopoly capitalism. Following Stalin's death relations became warmer with close cooperation between the two states. Relations ended in 1991 with the dissolution of the Soviet Union.
mgt: India–Soviet Union relations were marked by a strong and strategic partnership from 1955 until the dissolution of the Soviet Union in 1991. The relationship began with reciprocal visits by Indian Prime Minister Jawaharlal Nehru and Soviet leader Nikita Khrushchev in 1955, fostering economic, military, and diplomatic cooperation. Key milestones included Soviet support for India during the Kashmir dispute, the 1971 Indo-Soviet Treaty of Friendship, and collaboration on defense projects like the MiG-21. Relations remained robust through the Cold War, despite global geopolitical shifts.

trgt: The Nieuport-Macchi Parasol was a single-engine observation aircraft produced by the Italian aeronautical company Nieuport-Macchi in the early 1910s. It was the first aircraft designed independently by Macchi, which until then had only produced aircraft under license. The Parasol operated in front-line service in 1915 during the early stages of Italy's participation in World War I.
mgt: The Nieuport-Macchi Parasol was an Italian monoplane developed in 1913 by Nieuport-Macchi, based on the Nieuport IV. Featuring a parasol wing for improved visibility, it was powered by an 80-horsepower Gnome rotary engine. Used briefly during World War I for reconnaissance and artillery spotting, it was retired in late 1915 due to instability and high accident rates.

trgt: Hallie Flanagan Davis (August 27, 1889 in Redfield, South Dakota – June 23, 1969 in Old Tappan, New Jersey) was an American theatrical producer and director, playwright, and author, best known as director of the Federal Theatre Project, a part of the Works Progress Administration (WPA).
mgt: Hallie Flanagan (August 27, 1889 – June 23, 1969) was an American theatrical producer, director, playwright, and author, best known for directing the Federal Theatre Project during the Great Depression. A pioneer of experimental theater, she was the first woman awarded a Guggenheim Fellowship to study theater in Europe. Flanagan also founded the Vassar Experimental Theatre and later served as Dean and professor at Smith College. Her leadership of the Federal Theatre Project brought innovative, affordable theater to millions, though it faced political controversy and was ultimately shut down.

trgt: Sharp Gallaher Racing (formerly known as Eddie Sharp Racing) is a disbanded NASCAR team based in Mooresville, North Carolina that competed in the ARCA Racing Series and the NASCAR Camping World Truck Series. The team fielded the No. 6 Chevrolet Silverado for Justin Lofton and various other drivers and the No. 8 Chevrolet for Max Gresham.
mgt: Sharp Gallaher Racing was an American stock car racing team that competed in the NASCAR Camping World Truck Series from 2010 to 2013. Founded by Eddie Sharp in 2005, the team achieved two race victories and one pole position. Renamed Sharp Gallaher Racing in 2013 after partnering with Bill Gallaher, the team ceased operations later that year.

trgt: Doğuş Balbay (IPA-tr; born 21 January 1989) is a Turkish professional basketball player and the team captain for Anadolu Efes Istanbul of the Turkish Basketbol Süper Ligi (BSL) and the EuroLeague. He is 6 ft 1 in (1.85 m) tall and he plays at the point guard position.
mgt: Doğuş Balbay (born January 21, 1989) is a Turkish professional basketball player who plays as a point guard for Anadolu Efes in the Turkish Basketball Super League (BSL) and the EuroLeague. A two-time EuroLeague champion, Balbay is known for his defensive prowess and leadership on the court.

trgt: TV Sound and Image, British Television, Film and Library Composers 1956-80 is a 2012 compilation album released by Soul Jazz Records. The album consists of a style of music known as Library music, music developed by production houses with the intent that the recordings would be licensed to various film, television, and radio programs.
mgt: *TV Sound and Image* is a compilation album featuring library music, released on July 3, 2012, by Soul Jazz Records. Compiled by Stuart Baker, Pete Reilly, and Steve Platt, the album showcases themes and incidental music from the golden age of television. It received critical acclaim for its nostalgic appeal, funk-inspired tracks, and enduring musical quality.

trgt: Susan Jean Silver (born July 17, 1958) is an American music manager, best known for managing Seattle rock bands such as Soundgarden, Alice in Chains and Screaming Trees. Silver also owns the company Susan Silver Management, and co-owns the club The Crocodile in Seattle. Silver was named "the most powerful figure in local rock management" by The Seattle Times in 1991.
mgt: Susan Jean Silver (born July 17, 1958) is an American music manager and entrepreneur, best known for managing influential rock bands such as Soundgarden, Alice in Chains, and Screaming Trees during the rise of the Seattle grunge scene. She co-owns the Seattle music venue The Crocodile and co-manages Alice in Chains. Silver retired in 1998 but returned to music management in 2005.

page_title: George Washington (copy of bust by Houdon)
trgt: George Washington (bust by Houdon) is a public artwork that is a limited edition copy of an original work by French neoclassical sculptor Jean Antoine Houdon that is on display inside the Indiana Statehouse, which is located in Indianapolis, Indiana, United States. The artwork was cast a solid piece of plaster that has been painted white.
mgt: The *George Washington* bust at the Indiana Statehouse is a 1932 plaster copy of Jean Antoine Houdon's original 1785 sculpture, created from life at Mount Vernon. Commissioned for the George Washington Bicentennial Celebration, it features Washington in a toga-like drape. The bust is owned by the Indiana Historical Bureau and is displayed on the Statehouse's fourth floor.

trgt: Great Bliss, Vol. 1 is an album by American jazz saxophonist David S. Ware recorded in 1990 and released on the Swedish Silkheart label. Great Bliss was conceived as a two-installment project and marks the debut of the David S. Ware Quartet, one of the most highly acclaimed musical groups of the decade.
mgt: *Great Bliss, Vol. 1* is a 1991 jazz album by saxophonist David S. Ware, recorded in January 1990 and released on the Silkheart label. Featuring pianist Matthew Shipp, bassist William Parker, and drummer Marc Edwards, it marked the debut of Ware's renowned quartet. The album showcases Ware's multi-instrumental talents and innovative compositions.

trgt: Senegalese Americans are an ethnic group of Americans of Senegalese descent. In the surveys of 2019, 18,091 people claimed to be of Senegalese origin or descent in the United States. However, many West Africans trafficked by enslavers to the United States were also of Senegalese origin (arriving together with Africans of other origins who came by way of Senegalese ports). Thus many African Americans may also have some ancestors of this country.
mgt: Senegalese Americans are an ethnic group in the United States with origins in Senegal, West Africa. The community traces its roots to the transatlantic slave trade, during which many West Africans, including Senegalese, were trafficked to the Americas. In modern times, Senegalese immigration to the U.S. increased in the 20th century, with many settling in New York City, Chicago, and other urban areas. Predominantly Muslim, Senegalese Americans maintain cultural ties through language, religion, and organizations like Tékkil, which supports professional and community development.

trgt: Titus Haterius Nepos was an eques who held a number of imperial Roman positions during the reigns of Trajan and Hadrian. The two most important posts were praefectus vigilum or commander of the vigiles or nightwatch, and praefectus or governor of Egypt (120-124). According to Fergus Millar, Nepos was the first eques who was promoted from regular procuratorial posts into a proper secretarial appointment.
mgt: Titus Haterius Nepos was a Roman official and military officer who served as the prefect of Roman Egypt from 120 to 121 CE. His career, documented in an inscription from Fulginiae and a letter from Vindolanda, included roles such as military tribune, procurator of Roman Armenia, and praefectus vigilum. While governor of Egypt, he famously visited the Colossi of Memnon and heard the statue "sing."

trgt: GLtron is a 3D snake game based on the light cycle portion of the film Tron. The game is free and open-source software and has been ported to many mobile and non-mobile operating systems such as Windows, MacOS, Symbian and Android over the years.
mgt: GLtron is an open-source 3D snake game inspired by the light cycle sequence from the film *Tron*. Originally developed as a university project by Andreas Umbach in 1998, it was released under the GPL-2.0-or-later license in 1999. The game features various gameplay modes, customizable arena sizes, and a soundtrack by Finnish musician Peter Hajba. It has been ported to multiple platforms, including Linux, Windows, macOS, and mobile systems like Android. Praised for its graphics and sound, GLtron has been downloaded over 1.5 million times from SourceForge and is included in several Linux distributions.

trgt: The Amos Baldwin House is a historic house at 92 Goshen Street East in Norfolk, Connecticut, United States. Built about 1765, it is an important surviving example of colonial architecture in the community, and is one of its oldest buildings with a gambrel roof. It was listed on the National Register of Historic Places in 2016.
mgt: The Amos Baldwin House, located in Norfolk, Connecticut, is a historic Colonial-style home built around 1765. Added to the National Register of Historic Places in 2016, it is one of Norfolk's oldest surviving buildings. The house has ties to notable figures, including Amos Baldwin, a 19th-century farmer and politician, and later served as a private boys' school.

trgt: Elections to the Legislative Assembly of the Indian state of Hyderabad were held and Sri Burgula Rama Krishna Rao took oath as First Chief Minister of Hyderabad State on 6 March 1952. 564 candidates competed for the 175 seats in the Assembly. There were 33 two-member constituencies and 109 constituencies single-member constituencies.
mgt: The 1952 Hyderabad State Legislative Assembly election was the first election held in Hyderabad State after its integration into India. The Indian National Congress won a majority, securing 93 of 175 seats with 41.86% of the vote. The People's Democratic Front, representing communists, won all seats in Nalgonda district. Following the States Reorganisation Act of 1956, Hyderabad State was merged into Andhra Pradesh.

trgt: Kurihara (栗原市, Kurihara-shi) is a city located in Miyagi Prefecture, Japan. As of 30 April 2020, the city had an estimated population of 66,565, and a population density of 83 persons per km2 in 24,994 households. The total area of the city is 804.97 square kilometres (310.80 sq mi). Parts of the city are within the borders of the Kurikoma Quasi-National Park.
mgt: Kurihara (栗原市) is a city in Miyagi Prefecture, Japan, located in the Tōhoku region. Established on April 1, 2005, through the merger of several towns and a village, it functions as a unitary administrative area. Known for its agriculture, particularly rice production, Kurihara also features natural attractions such as Mount Kurikoma, Lake Izunuma, and hot spring resorts. The city experiences a humid subtropical climate with distinct seasonal changes. Historically, the area was part of Mutsu Province and later controlled by the Date clan during the Edo period.

trgt: Kfar Dunin (Lang-ar) is a village in Lebanon, 102 km from the capital Beirut and 580 m from sea coast remote. Adjacent villages: Deir Kifa, Alkaline, Khirbat Silm, Der Ntar. It is considered first of villages in Bent Jbeil district of Nabatieh Governorate. Its population is 6000 people.
mgt: Kfar Dounin (Arabic: كفردونين) is a village in the Bint Jbeil District of the Nabatieh Governorate in southern Lebanon, situated at an elevation of 580 meters. Historically, it was noted in the late 19th century for its Metawileh population and remnants of a demolished church.

trgt:
Trabzonspor Club Women's Football Team (Lang-tr) is the women's football team of Trabzonspor in Trabzon, Turkey. It was established on 16 October 2007.

Right after its foundation, the team was invited to join the Turkish Women's Football Super League. However, the league was cancelled in 2003 but re-established for the 2007–08 season. Trabzonspor were the champions in the 2008–09 season, which was the second year of the league.
mgt: Trabzonspor Kulübü Kadın Futbol Takımı is the women's football team of Trabzonspor, based in Trabzon, Turkey. Founded in 2007, the team won the Turkish Women's Football League in the 2008–09 season, becoming the first Turkish club to win both men's and women's premier league titles. Re-established in 2021, they currently compete in the Turkish Women's Football Super League. The team also participated in the 2009–10 UEFA Women's Champions League qualifiers.

trgt: Yankee Pasha is a 1954 American romantic adventure film directed by Joseph Pevney and starring Jeff Chandler, Rhonda Fleming and Mamie Van Doren. Shot in technicolor, it was produced and distributed by Hollywood studio Universal Pictures. The film is based on the 1947 novel Yankee Pasha by Edison Marshall.
mgt: *Yankee Pasha* is a 1954 American adventure film directed by Joseph Pevney and based on Edison Marshall's novel of the same name. Starring Jeff Chandler, Rhonda Fleming, and Mamie Van Doren, the film follows fur trapper Jason Starbuck's quest to rescue his love, Roxana, from pirates and slavery in Morocco. Produced by Universal Pictures, it marked one of Van Doren's early major roles.

trgt: Gavan McCormack is a researcher specializing in East Asia who is Emeritus Professor and Visiting Fellow, Division of Pacific and Asian History of the Australian National University. He is also a coordinator of an award-winning open access journal The Asia-Pacific Journal: Japan Focus.
mgt: Gavan McCormack is an Australian historian and academic specializing in modern East Asian history, particularly Japan. A professor emeritus at the Australian National University, he has published extensively on Japanese political, intellectual, and environmental history. McCormack is known for his critiques of Japanese economic policies, environmental exploitation, and U.S. foreign policy.

trgt: Vicente Greco (February 3, 1888 – October 5, 1924) was an Argentinian composer, conductor, and bandoneon player of tango music. He had a significant role in the spread of tango music from the suburbs into the cities, where it became very popular.
mgt: Vicente Greco (1888–1924) was an Argentine tango musician, composer, and bandleader, credited with forming the first *orquesta típica*, a traditional tango orchestra. A self-taught bandoneonist, he composed influential tangos like "Rodríguez Peña" and "El flete," and collaborated with Carlos Gardel.

trgt: Endohedral fullerenes, also called endofullerenes, are fullerenes that have additional atoms, ions, or clusters enclosed within their inner spheres. The first lanthanum C60 complex called La@C60 was synthesized in 1985. The @ (at sign) in the name reflects the notion of a small molecule trapped inside a shell. Two types of endohedral complexes exist: endohedral metallofullerenes and non-metal doped fullerenes.
mgt: Endohedral fullerenes are a class of fullerenes in which atoms, ions, or small molecules are encapsulated within the carbon cage. These structures include endohedral metallofullerenes, doped with metals like scandium or lanthanum, and non-metal doped fullerenes, containing noble gases or reactive elements like nitrogen. They exhibit unique physical and chemical properties, such as stability, charge transfer, and quantum mechanical phenomena, making them valuable for applications in materials science, quantum research, and medical imaging.

trgt: Rola Saad (Lang-ar) (born in Achrafieh, Beirut, Lebanon on September 9, 1948) is a Lebanese pan-Arab television personality and owner of Vanilla Productions. She is well known for her appearance in her number-one show in the middle east Star Academy Arab World since 2004 on Lebanese Broadcasting Corporation (LBC). In July 2011, she completed the production of 8 full seasons of Star Academy.
mgt: Rola Saad (Arabic: رولا سعد; born September 19, 1948) is a Lebanese television producer and media icon known for her innovative and successful productions in the Middle East. Her portfolio includes over 20 works spanning variety shows, reality TV, drama series, and documentaries. She is best known for directing *Star Academy*, as well as producing *Celebrity Duets* and *Splash*, pioneering reality television in the region.

trgt: Bakhtyuras Shampekuly Besikbayev (Lang-kk; 1920 – 26 June 1941) was a gunner and radio operator in the 207th Long-Range Bomber Aviation Regiment during the Second World War. He was posthumously declared both a Hero of the Russian Federation and a Hero of Kazakhstan after the dissolution of the Soviet Union.
mgt: Bakhtyuras Besikbayev (1920 – June 26, 1941) was a Kazakh radio operator and gunner in the Soviet Air Force during World War II. Serving in the 207th Long-Range Bomber Aviation Regiment, he was killed in action during a mission against German forces. Initially misidentified, his crew was later recognized for their heroism, leading to Besikbayev being posthumously awarded the titles Hero of Kazakhstan and Hero of the Russian Federation.

trgt: Islesboro is a town in Waldo County, Maine, United States, comprising Islesboro Island and several smaller islands. The population was 583 at the 2020 census. It has a summer colony accessible by state ferry service from Lincolnville Beach three miles to the west, by private boat, or by air taxi service. Home to Warren Island State Park, Islesboro includes the village of Dark Harbor.
mgt: Islesboro is a town in Waldo County, Maine, located on Islesboro Island in Penobscot Bay. Incorporated in 1789, it was originally a shipping hub before becoming a resort community with luxurious summer homes. The town covers 68.88 square miles, with 14.29 square miles of land. As of 2020, its population was 583. Islesboro Central School serves students from kindergarten through 12th grade, including tuition-paying mainland students.

trgt: Antiscopa elaphra is a moth in the family Crambidae. It is endemic to New Zealand and has been observed in both the North and South Islands. Adults of this species are on the wing from August until March and are attracted to light. In 2020 this species had its DNA barcode sequenced.
mgt: *Antiscopa elaphra* is a species of moth in the family Crambidae, endemic to New Zealand. First described by Edward Meyrick in 1884, it is characterized by its small size, long palpi, triangular forewings, and fragile appearance. The species is active year-round and is attracted to light.

trgt: Joseph Holston (born Joseph Deweese Holston Jr., April 6, 1944) is an American painter and printmaker known for his portrayals of the African American experience, using vivid colors and expressive lines in a cubist-abstractionist style. His media include painting, etching, silk screen, and collage.
mgt: Joseph Holston is an American painter and printmaker known for his vibrant use of color, expressive forms, and exploration of Black identity. Raised in Chevy Chase, Maryland, he transitioned from commercial art to fine art in the 1970s, gaining recognition for his etchings and paintings, which are included in major collections such as the Smithsonian American Art Museum and The Phillips Collection. Holston's work has been exhibited widely, including solo shows at the Butler Institute of American Art and the Reginald F. Lewis Museum. His notable project, *Color in Freedom: Journey along the Underground Railroad* (2008), is a visual narrative of the journey from slavery to freedom, which has toured internationally, including an exhibition at the United Nations in Geneva. Holston has also served as an artist-in-residence at several institutions and continues to be celebrated for his contributions to American art and African American history.

trgt: Kizzuwatna (or Kizzuwadna; in Ancient Egyptian Kode or Qode), is the name of an ancient Anatolian kingdom in the 2nd millennium BC. It was situated in the highlands of southeastern Anatolia, near the Gulf of İskenderun, in modern-day Turkey. It encircled the Taurus Mountains and the Ceyhan River. The centre of the kingdom was the city of Kummanni, in the highlands. In a later era, the same region was known as Cilicia.
mgt: Kizzuwatna was an ancient kingdom located in southeastern Anatolia, near the Taurus Mountains, during the 2nd millennium BC. Known for its rich resources, including silver mines and fertile plains, it was home to a diverse population of Hurrians, Luwians, and Hittites. Kizzuwatna played a strategic role in the power struggles between the Hittites and the Mitanni, forming alliances and treaties with both. It later became part of the Hittite Empire, contributing troops and resources, including advanced iron weaponry, to major conflicts like the Battle of Kadesh. After the Hittite Empire's collapse, the region became part of the Neo-Hittite kingdom of Quwe.

trgt: The Action Group on Erosion, Technology, and Concentration (ETC), is an international organization dedicated to "the conservation and sustainable advancement of cultural and ecological diversity and human rights." "ETC" is intended to be pronounced "et cetera." ETC often publishes opinions on scientific research by its staff and board members in topics including community and regional planning, ecology and evolutionary biology, and political science.
mgt: The ETC Group (Action Group on Erosion, Technology and Concentration) is an international civil society organization focused on environmental and social justice issues, particularly in agriculture and emerging technologies. Founded as the Rural Advancement Foundation International (RAFI), its work includes advocacy on farmers' rights, opposition to genetic engineering, and critiques of synthetic biology and geoengineering. The group coined the term "biopiracy" and has influenced international treaties.

trgt: Skeletonization is the state of a dead organism after undergoing decomposition. Skeletonization refers to the final stage of decomposition, during which the last vestiges of the soft tissues of a corpse or carcass have decayed or dried to the point that the skeleton is exposed. By the end of the skeletonization process, all soft tissue will have been eliminated, leaving only disarticulated bones.
mgt: Skeletonization is the process by which a body decomposes into a skeleton, influenced by factors such as climate, temperature, and burial conditions. It can take weeks in tropical climates or years in colder regions. Skeletons are analyzed in forensic and archaeological contexts to determine factors like age, sex, trauma, and time of death. Proper preservation and ethical considerations are essential in skeletal research.

trgt: Ethel Esther Page (née Blunt; 20 September 1875 – 26 May 1958) was the first wife of Sir Earle Page, the 11th Prime Minister of Australia. She worked as a nurse before her marriage. Page supported her husband during his long political career, though never living in Canberra, and was politically active herself, serving on the state executive of the Country Party and as president of its women's section. She was also involved in various community organisations.
mgt: Ethel Esther Page (née Blunt; 20 September 1875 – 26 May 1958) was an Australian nurse, political activist, and the wife of Sir Earle Page, the 11th Prime Minister of Australia. She served as the prime minister's spouse during his brief tenure in April 1939. A trained nurse, she was active in public life, founding the Women's Country Club and serving as the inaugural president of the Women's Country Party. She was also involved in the Country Women's Association and the National Council of Women.

trgt: Joseph Lawson Edward Howze (born Lawson Edward Howze, August 30, 1923 – January 9, 2019) was an African-American prelate of the Catholic Church. He served as the first Bishop of Biloxi from 1977 to 2001, and was the first openly Black Catholic bishop of a US diocese.
mgt: Joseph Lawson Howze (August 30, 1923 – January 9, 2019) was an American prelate of the Catholic Church and the first Black bishop to lead a U.S. diocese. He served as the first bishop of the Diocese of Biloxi from 1977 to 2001, following earlier roles as a priest and auxiliary bishop.

trgt: Artillerie-Inrichtingen was a Dutch state-owned artillery, small arms, and munitions company which also produced machine tools and was founded in 1679 in Delft, Netherlands. The company was split in 1973 with its defense related businesses becoming Eurometaal and its civilian manufacturing becoming Hembrug Machine Tools. During its years of operation as Artillerie Inrichtingen, the company manufactured armaments and an array of other industrial outputs for the Dutch Army and the Royal Dutch East Indies Army (KNIL).
mgt: Artillerie-Inrichtingen (A.I.) was a Dutch state-owned arms and munitions manufacturer founded in 1679 in Delft by Willem III of Orange. It played a significant role in producing artillery, small arms, and precision machinery, including the AR-10 battle rifle, which influenced modern firearm designs. Relocated to Zaandam in 1899, the company expanded its operations and contributed to Dutch military efforts. After World War II, it diversified into civilian products. A.I. was split in 1973 into Eurometaal and Hembrug Machine Tools, with the latter continuing to produce precision lathes.

trgt: Fawley is a village and civil parish in West Berkshire, England. The hub of the village is centred 3.5 miles (5.6 km) east of Lambourn and has a sub-community within its bounds, Little or South Fawley. It includes the depopulated small hill settlement of Whatcombe. Fawley is the inspiration for "Marygreen" in Thomas Hardy's Jude the Obscure.
mgt: Fawley is a small village and civil parish in West Berkshire, England, located in the Berkshire Downs. Covering an area of 8.78 km², it had a population of 165 as of the 2011 census. The village is divided into North Fawley (Great Fawley) and South Fawley (Little Fawley). Its Church of England parish church, St. Mary the Virgin, was designed by George Edmund Street and built in 1865–66, incorporating fragments of an earlier medieval church. Fawley has historical ties to notable figures, including Thomas Hardy, whose fictional character Jude Fawley in *Jude the Obscure* was inspired by the village.

trgt: Climax is a village in Kalamazoo County in the U.S. state of Michigan. The population was 767 at the 2010 census. The village is located within Climax Township on the northern boundary with Charleston Township, and is roughly 12 miles (19 km) west of Battle Creek and 16 miles (26 km) east of Kalamazoo. Climax shares a school district with the neighboring town of Scotts.
mgt: Climax is a village in Kalamazoo County, Michigan, United States. Incorporated in 1899, it has a total area of 1.05 square miles and a population of 712 as of the 2020 census. Settled in the early 19th century, it was named in 1834 by the Eldred family. Climax is notable for being the first community in Michigan to receive free rural mail service in 1896.

trgt: Farma 12 - City vs Village is the twelfth season of the Slovak version of The Farm reality television show based on the Swedish television series of the same name. The show began filming in August 2020 and premiered on August 31, 2020 on Markíza.
mgt: *Farma* season 12 was the twelfth installment of the Slovak reality television series, airing on Markíza from August 31 to December 11, 2020. Hosted by Eva "Evelyn" Kramerová, the season featured 20 contestants divided into "City" and "Village" teams, competing under contrasting living conditions. Xénia Gregušová emerged as the winner.

trgt: John James Hughes (1814 – 17 June 1889) was a Welsh engineer, businessman and founder of the city of Donetsk. The village was originally named Yuzovka (Hughesovka, Lang-ru) after Hughes, ("Yuz" being a Russian approximation of Hughes), formally becoming a town in May 1917 and later, in 1924, being renamed to Stalino (Lang-uk); in 1961, the name was changed to Donetsk.
mgt: John James Hughes (1814 – 17 June 1889) was a Welsh engineer and businessman, best known as the founder of the city of Donetsk, Ukraine (formerly Hughesovka). Born in Merthyr Tydfil, Wales, he gained prominence for his innovations in ironworks and armaments. In 1869, Hughes moved to the Russian Empire, where he established a major metallurgical complex and an industrial settlement that grew into a thriving city.

trgt: Waiau is a small town in north Canterbury, in the South Island of New Zealand. It lies 30 kilometres (19 mi) east of Hanmer Springs on the northern bank of the Waiau Uwha River, some 30 kilometres (19 mi) from the river's mouth. There is a small supermarket, a DIY store and a petrol station.
mgt: Waiau is a small rural town in the Hurunui District of Canterbury, New Zealand, located on State Highway 70. It has a population of 255 (2018 census) and is known for its history as the terminus of the Waiau Branch railway (1919–1978). The town was heavily impacted by the 2016 Kaikoura earthquake, which caused significant damage to buildings and infrastructure. Waiau features a primary school, a community swimming pool built in 2019, and the historic Waiau Lodge Hotel, which was destroyed by fire in 2021. The town's name, meaning "flowing water" in Māori, reflects its cultural heritage.

trgt: Hugh Alan Leslie OBE (17 April 1900 – 2 September 1974) was an Australian politician. He was a member of the Country Party and served terms in the Western Australian Legislative Assembly (1943–1949) and House of Representatives (1949–1958, 1961–1963). He was a newspaper editor before entering politics.
mgt: Hugh Leslie OBE (17 April 1900 – 2 September 1974) was an Australian politician and newspaper editor. A member of the Australian Country Party, he served as the federal Member of Parliament for Moore from 1949 to 1958 and again from 1961 to 1963. Born in Durban, South Africa, Leslie emigrated to Australia in 1923 and became active in politics after a career in journalism. He also served in the military during World War II, losing his right leg in Libya. Leslie was later recognized with an Order of the British Empire (OBE) in 1967.

trgt: The Department of Social Security (the DSS) was a government department in Australia, which administered the Social Security system between 1972 and 1998. The department was one of several new departments established by the Whitlam Government and was managed by the Minister for Social Security.
mgt: The Department of Social Security (DSS) was an Australian government department established on 19 December 1972, succeeding the Department of Social Services and parts of the Department of Health. Headquartered in Canberra, it managed social security policies and programs, including income support and equity initiatives. The department played a key role in implementing reforms such as the Disability Support Pension and the Access and Equity program, which improved services for disadvantaged groups, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander communities and migrants. In 1997, its service delivery functions were transferred to Centrelink, and the DSS was dissolved on 21 October 1998, with its remaining responsibilities assumed by the Department of Family and Community Services.

trgt:
Witch World is a science fantasy novel by American writer Andre Norton, published as a paperback original by Ace Books in 1963. It inaugurated the Witch World series and established a setting that she eventually shared with other writers.

The first hardcover edition was published by Gregg Press of Boston in 1977 as #1 in a set of seven called "The Witch World Novels of Andre Norton". Later it appeared in three-novel omnibus editions and in audiobook.
mgt: *Witch World* is a 1963 science fantasy novel by American author Andre Norton, marking the first installment in the *Witch World* series. The story follows Simon Tregarth, a disgraced World War II veteran who escapes assassins by entering a magical world through a mystical gateway. In this new realm, he allies with the witches of Estcarp to combat threats from neighboring lands and the technologically advanced Kolder. The novel was nominated for the 1964 Hugo Award and remains a classic of the genre.

trgt:
The Störtebeker Festival (Lang-de) is an yearly open-air theatre festival in Germany. It is based on stories around the medieval German privateer Klaus Störtebeker and his Victual Brothers, who later turned to pirates.

Founded in 1959 as part of an East German cultural initiative, the festival has become Germany's most successful open-air theatre event, and is broadcast by public television network NDR. It is held in the small town of Ralswiek on the isle of Rügen.
mgt: The Störtebeker Festival is an annual open-air theatre event held in Ralswiek, Germany, on the island of Rügen. Established in 1959 as the "Rügenfestspiele," it has evolved into a major cultural attraction, dramatizing the legend of the pirate Klaus Störtebeker and his exploits in the Baltic Sea. Since 1993, the festival has drawn millions of visitors, with over 7.3 million attendees by 2016. It features large-scale productions with actors, extras, horses, and ships.

trgt: SMS Eber was the last of the six gunboats of the Iltis class of the German Imperial Navy prior to and during World War I. Other ships of the class are SMS Iltis, SMS Luchs, SMS Tiger, SMS Jaguar and SMS Panther. They were built between 1898 and 1903. All of them served primarily overseas, in the German colonies. Eber had a crew of 9 officers and 121 men.
mgt: SMS *Eber* was an Iltis-class gunboat of the Imperial German Navy, launched in 1903 and primarily used for colonial service. Initially stationed off the west coast of Africa, she conducted surveys, protected German interests, and responded to crises, including the Agadir Crisis. During World War I, *Eber* transferred her armament to the auxiliary cruiser *Cap Trafalgar*. Disarmed and stranded in Brazil, she was scuttled by her crew in 1917 to avoid capture.

trgt: The Ricoh 2A03 or RP2A03 (NTSC version) / Ricoh 2A07 or RP2A07 (PAL version) is an 8-bit microprocessor manufactured by Ricoh for the Nintendo Entertainment System video game console. It was also used as a sound chip and secondary CPU by Nintendo's arcade games Punch-Out!! and Donkey Kong 3.
mgt: The Ricoh 2A03 is an 8-bit microprocessor launched in 1983, co-developed by Nintendo and Ricoh for the Nintendo Entertainment System (NES). Based on the MOS Technology 6502, it features a modified instruction set, integrated sound hardware with five audio channels, and support for game controller polling. Regional variations, such as the Ricoh 2A07, were adapted for PAL systems.

trgt: Proserpine was launched at Amsterdam in 1801 as a 32-gun frigate. The Royal Navy captured her in May 1804 at the capture of Surinam and took her into service as HMS Amsterdam. She sailed to England where she became a guard and storeship at Cork. She was sold in 1815.
mgt: The Dutch frigate *Proserpine* was launched in 1801 for the Batavian Republic and captured by the British in 1804 during the capture of Surinam. Renamed HMS *Amsterdam*, it served as a storeship and guardship before being sold in 1815. It had a burthen of 849 tons and carried 24-pounder carronades.

trgt: Edwin "Honest Ed" Mirvish, OC CBE OOnt (July 24, 1914 – July 11, 2007) was an American-Canadian businessman, philanthropist and theatrical impresario who lived in Toronto, Ontario. He is known for his flagship business, Honest Ed's, a landmark discount store in downtown Toronto, and as a patron of the arts, instrumental in revitalizing the theatre scene in Toronto.
mgt: Ed Mirvish (July 24, 1914 – July 11, 2007) was a Canadian entrepreneur, philanthropist, and theatre impresario, best known as the founder of the iconic discount store Honest Ed's in Toronto. Renowned for his publicity stunts and generosity, he revitalized Toronto's theatre scene by saving the Royal Alexandra Theatre and building the Princess of Wales Theatre.

trgt: Attabad Lake (Lang-ur) is a lake located in the Gojal region of Hunza Valley in Gilgit−Baltistan, Pakistan. It was created in January 2010 as the result of a major landslide in Attabad. The lake has become one of the biggest tourist attractions in Gilgit−Baltistan, offering activities like boating, jet-skiing, fishing and other recreational activities.
mgt: Attabad Lake, also known as Gojal Lake, is a lake in the Hunza Valley of Gilgit-Baltistan, Pakistan, formed on January 4, 2010, by a massive landslide in Attabad village. The disaster blocked the Hunza River, creating a 21-kilometer-long lake that displaced thousands, submerged villages, and disrupted the Karakoram Highway. The lake has since become a significant tourist attraction.

trgt: DWOW (103.5 FM), on-air as K-Lite 103.5 (pronounced as one-oh-three-five), is a radio station owned and operated by the Advanced Media Broadcasting System. Its studios are located at the AMBS Media Center, 2nd Floor, Starmall EDSA Shaw, EDSA cor. Shaw Blvd., Mandaluyong, and its transmitter is located at Unit 906A, Paragon Plaza Building, EDSA cor. Reliance St., Mandaluyong. The station operates daily from 6:00 AM to 12:00 MN.
mgt: DWOW (103.5 FM), branded as K-Lite 103.5, is a radio station in Mandaluyong, Philippines, serving Mega Manila and surrounding areas. Owned by Advanced Media Broadcasting System, it airs an adult contemporary and OPM format in English. Established on December 18, 1979, the station has undergone multiple rebrandings, including Heart FM, Max FM, and Wow FM, before returning to its K-Lite branding in 2013.

trgt: Under the Whyte notation for the classification of steam locomotives, 0-8-0 represents the wheel arrangement of no leading wheels, eight powered and coupled driving wheels on four axles and no trailing wheels. Locomotives of this type are also referred to as eight coupled.
mgt: The 0-8-0 is a steam locomotive wheel arrangement with eight driving wheels and no leading or trailing wheels. Initially used for mainline freight haulage, it later became popular for heavy switching duties. Known for its high tractive effort and adhesive weight, the 0-8-0 was widely used in countries such as the United States, Germany, Russia, and South Africa. Notable examples include the USRA 0-8-0 in the United States, the Prussian G8.1 in Germany, and the O-class in Russia. The design was less common in lighter railways, where smaller configurations like the 0-6-0 were preferred.

trgt: Mystic Comics is the name of three comic book series published by the company that eventually became Marvel Comics. The first two series were superhero anthologies published by Marvel's 1930-1940s predecessor, Timely Comics, during what fans and historians call the Golden Age of comic books. The third, simply titled Mystic, was a horror fiction-suspense anthology from Marvel's 1950s forerunner, Atlas Comics.
mgt: *Mystic Comics* was a comic book series published by Timely Comics, the predecessor of Marvel Comics, during the Golden Age of Comic Books. The first volume (1940–1942) was a superhero anthology introducing notable characters like the Destroyer, the Blazing Skull, and the Black Widow, the first super-powered female character in comics. A second volume (1944–1945) featured characters such as the Angel and the Young Allies. A later series titled *Mystic* (1951–1957), published by Atlas Comics, was a horror-suspense anthology that transitioned to comply with the Comics Code Authority.

trgt: The Anthony Massad Three-Decker is a historic triple-decker house in Worcester, Massachusetts. Built about 1912, it was cited as a good local example of Colonial Revival styling when it was listed on the National Register of Historic Places in 1990. Many details, including Tuscan columns on the porch and modillion blocks in the eaves, have been lost or obscured by subsequent exterior changes (see photo).
mgt: The Anthony Massad Three-Decker is a historic three-story residential building located at 14 Harlow Street in the Brittan Square area of Worcester, Massachusetts. Built around 1912 in the Colonial Revival style, it features a hip roof, asymmetrical facade, and stacked porches. Originally owned by Anthony Massad, a hotel proprietor, the building housed tenants working in skilled blue- and white-collar jobs. It was added to the National Register of Historic Places in 1990.

trgt: Gemeinhardt Co. is a manufacturer of flutes and piccolos. These musical instruments are developed by this company for all levels of musicians, beginners to professionals. It is owned by its major supplier, Angel Industries Co. Ltd of Taiwan, widely acknowledged as the premier manufacturer of woodwind musical instruments. The Gemeinhardt Company is headquartered in Elkhart, Indiana, where many of its instruments are still made.
mgt: Gemeinhardt is an American musical instrument manufacturer founded in 1948 by Kurt Gemeinhardt, a fourth-generation flute maker from Germany. Based in Elkhart, Indiana, the company produces flutes, piccolos, and other woodwind instruments. Known for its high-quality beginner and professional flutes, Gemeinhardt combines American-made components with assembly by its parent company, Angel Industries of Taiwan. The company also owns the Roy Seaman Piccolo brand and introduced the Kurt Gemeinhardt Generation Series in 2014.

trgt: Maris Harvey Taylor (June 5, 1876 – May 16, 1982), also known as M. Harvey Taylor or Harvey Taylor, was an American politician from Pennsylvania who served as a Republican member of the Pennsylvania Senate for the 15th district from 1941 to 1964 including as President pro tempore from 1945 to 1964. He was a political boss who held multiple political positions in Dauphin County and statewide.
mgt: M. Harvey Taylor (June 5, 1876 – May 16, 1982) was an American politician, businessman, and Republican leader who served as a Pennsylvania State Senator from 1941 to 1964, including as President Pro Tempore for 16 years. Known as the political boss of Dauphin County, he played a key role in state politics and infrastructure development. The M. Harvey Taylor Memorial Bridge is named in his honor.

trgt: Berlin, also known as the Long-a-Coming Depot, is located in Berlin, Camden County, New Jersey, United States, between East Taunton and Washington Avenues, abutting the New Jersey Transit Atlantic City Line tracks. The station was built in 1856 and added to the National Register of Historic Places on February 14, 1997 for its significance in transportation.
mgt: The Long-a-Coming Depot, built in 1856 in Berlin, New Jersey, is a historic Italianate-style train station originally serving the Camden and Atlantic Railroad. Renamed Berlin Station in 1867, it closed in the 1960s. Restored in 1994 by the Long-a-Coming Historical Society, it now serves as a meeting space. It was added to the National Register of Historic Places in 1997.

trgt: Ultrasound-modulated optical tomography (UOT) is a form of tomography involving ultrasound. It is used in imaging of biological soft tissues and has potential applications for early cancer detection. Like optical techniques, this method provides high contrast, and the use of ultrasound also provides high resolution.
mgt: Ultrasound-modulated optical tomography (UOT) is an imaging technique used to visualize soft tissue by combining laser light and focused ultrasound. It offers greater penetration depth compared to traditional optical methods and relies on scattered photons within tissue to generate images. UOT operates through mechanisms involving ultrasonic modulation of light intensity, phase, and speckle patterns, enabling the reconstruction of internal tissue properties. Analytical models and advanced techniques, such as time-resolved frequency-swept UOT, enhance resolution and imaging capabilities. First proposed in 2013, UOT has potential applications in biomedical imaging, including virus detection.

trgt: On July 9, 2014, a mass shooting occurred in a home located in northern Harris County, Texas, near the Spring census-designated place, a suburban area of the Greater Houston area, leaving six family members dead, four of them children, and a lone survivor. 34-year old Ronald Lee Haskell, was apprehended after a standoff that lasted several hours. Haskell was related to the victims via his ex-wife.
mgt: The 2014 Harris County shooting occurred on July 9, 2014, near Spring, Texas, when Ronald Lee Haskell killed six members of the Stay family, including four children, in an execution-style mass shooting during a domestic dispute. A 15-year-old survivor, Cassidy Stay, alerted police, leading to Haskell's capture after a standoff. Haskell was convicted of capital murder in 2019 and sentenced to death.

trgt: Kamaha Castle (鎌刃城, Kamaha jō) was a Sengoku period yamashiro-style Japanese castle located in the Banba neighborhood of the city of Maibara, Shiga Prefecture, in the Kansai region of Japan. Its ruins have been protected as a National Historic Site since 2005.
mgt: Kamaha Castle (鎌刃城) was a yamashiro-style Japanese castle located in Maibara, Shiga Prefecture, Japan. Built in the 15th century by the Hori clan, it played a strategic role during the Sengoku period, controlling a key route between Kyoto and eastern Japan. The castle changed hands multiple times, involving prominent clans such as the Rokkaku, Asai, and Oda. It was abandoned in 1574 after losing its strategic importance. Today, only fragments of stone walls remain, and the site is recognized as one of the Continued Top 100 Japanese Castles.

trgt:
The Reverend Lord Frederick de Vere Beauclerk (8 May 1773 – 22 April 1850), a 19th-century Anglican priest, was an outstanding but controversial English first-class cricketer, the leading "amateur" player of the Napoleonic period.

Lord Frederick played for 35 years from 1791 to 1825, and served as President of Marylebone Cricket Club (MCC) for 1826–27.
mgt: Lord Frederick de Vere Beauclerk (8 May 1773 – 22 April 1850) was an English nobleman, Anglican clergyman, and prominent cricketer. A controversial figure, he played first-class cricket from 1791 to 1825, primarily for the Marylebone Cricket Club (MCC), and was known as a skilled all-rounder and tactician. Despite his clerical career, Beauclerk prioritized cricket, becoming one of the sport's most divisive figures due to his competitive nature and alleged match-fixing.

trgt: Joseph Tate is an American attorney who represented government official Lewis Libby in the CIA leak grand jury investigation. Tate is a partner with the law firm Dechert LLP in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania. He specializes in defending clients against charges of complex white collar crime, in particular, antitrust and other regulatory issues. Previously, Tate worked in the Antitrust Division of the United States Department of Justice.
mgt: Joseph Tate is an American attorney who graduated from Villanova University in 1963 and its School of Law in 1966. He served as Lewis Libby's attorney during the CIA leak grand jury investigation, publicly denying Libby's involvement as journalist Judith Miller's confidential source. Tate maintained that Libby had provided a written waiver, and Miller was eventually released from jail after a phone call facilitated by their attorneys.

page_title: Eu Quero Tchu, Eu Quero Tcha
trgt: "Eu Quero Tchu, Eu Quero Tcha" (I Want Tchu, I Want Tcha), full title "Eu Quero Tchu Eu Quero Tcha (Tchu Tcha Tcha)" is a single by Brazilian Sertanejo universitário duo João Lucas & Marcelo, released in 2012, and written by Shylton Fernandes. The song was a hit in Brazil, following the success of similar songs such as Michel Teló's "Ai Se Eu Te Pego" and Gusttavo Lima's "Balada", though not as successful in Europe as them.
mgt: "Eu Quero Tchu, Eu Quero Tcha" is a 2012 single by Brazilian sertanejo duo João Lucas & Marcelo, written by Shylton Fernandes. The song gained widespread popularity with its soccer-themed music video featuring Brazilian footballer Neymar, who performs the signature "Tchu Tcha Tcha" dance. It was featured in the telenovela *Avenida Brasil* and referenced in Brazilian pop culture, including *The Simpsons*. The track inspired international adaptations and covers, including versions by Pitbull, Flavel & Neto, and others, further cementing its global appeal.

trgt: Mike Mineo is an American singer-songwriter and multi-instrumentalist born in Hollywood, Florida. His musical style encompasses avant-garde, pop, folk, soul, and jazz. He has recorded and self-released four albums on his own independent record label Nevernothing Records, LLC. In March 2016 Mike Mineo moved to Nashville, Tennessee, to further his music career.
mgt: Mike Mineo is an American singer-songwriter from Hollywood, Florida, known for his eclectic style and dynamic performances. He released his debut album *Eccentricity* in 2010, followed by *Beach Season* (2011), *Big Big Star* (2013), and *1* (2016). His music has been featured on college radio, television, and major events like SunFest, and he has opened for the Steve Miller Band.

trgt: Bygdøy Royal Estate (Lang-no), also known as the Bygdø Royal Farm, is a Kongsgård estate and manor house that occupies a large part of the northwestern part of the Bygdøy peninsula in Oslo, Norway. It is the official summer residence of the King of Norway.
mgt: Bygdøy Royal Estate is a historic Baroque estate in Oslo, Norway, owned by the Norwegian royal family. Originally linked to a Cistercian monastery, it became royal property in the 13th century. The current main building, completed in 1733, has served as a royal summer residence and underwent significant restorations in recent years. The estate features gardens, organic farming, and public access to certain areas, including a café and farm facilities.

trgt: Maharishi Institute of Management (MIM) is a group of private institutes founded by Maharishi Mahesh Yogi with locations in Maharishi Nagar(NOIDA), Bhopal, Indore, Hyderabad, Bengaluru and Chennai, India. The Institute is accredited by the All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE) and offers Graduate and Post Graduate courses in Management and Computer Science. Maharishi Institute of Management is a part of Maharishi Mahesh Yogi's global education movement. MIM is established under the auspices of Maharishi Shiksha Sansthan (MSS).
mgt: The Maharishi Institute of Management (MIM) is a private educational institution in India, established in 1993 under the Maharishi Shiksha Sansthan. With campuses in Bhopal, Indore, Noida, Bangalore, Hyderabad, and Chennai, it offers graduate and postgraduate programs in management and computer science, accredited by state universities and the All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE). MIM emphasizes Consciousness-Based Education, incorporating Transcendental Meditation and Vedic Management principles, aiming to provide both career-oriented and life-oriented education to address global challenges while preserving cultural integrity.

trgt: Soyuz TMA-07M (Lang-ru) was a spaceflight launched to the International Space Station in 2012 which transported three members of the Expedition 34 crew to the station. The Soyuz remained docked to the space station and served as an emergency escape vehicle for the Expedition 35 increment, before returning its crew to Earth in May 2013.
mgt: Soyuz TMA-07M was a 2012–2013 crewed spaceflight to the International Space Station (ISS) operated by Roscosmos. Launched on December 19, 2012, aboard a Soyuz-FG rocket from the Baikonur Cosmodrome, it carried Roman Romanenko, Chris Hadfield, and Thomas Marshburn. The spacecraft docked with the ISS for 143 days before safely returning to Earth on May 14, 2013.

trgt:
Kuressaare (IPA-et) is a town on Saaremaa island in Estonia. It is the administrative centre of Saaremaa Parish and the capital of Saare County. Kuressaare is the westernmost town in Estonia. The recorded population on 1 January 2018 was 13,276.

The town is situated on the southern coast of Saaremaa island, facing the Gulf of Riga of the Baltic Sea, and is served by the Kuressaare Airport, Roomassaare harbour, and Kuressaare yacht harbour.
mgt: Kuressaare is a town on Saaremaa Island in Estonia, first appearing on maps in 1154. Historically known as Arensburg, it developed around Kuressaare Castle, a medieval fortress now housing the Saaremaa Regional Museum. The town has been under Danish, Swedish, and Russian control, gaining its civic charter in 1563 and self-governing status in 1990. Kuressaare is known for its cultural events, including the Saaremaa Opera Days, and is served by Kuressaare Airport and local bus connections.

trgt: Nick Rotteveel (IPA-nl; born January 6, 1989), professionally known as Nicky Romero or Monocule, is a Dutch DJ, record producer and remixer from Amerongen, Utrecht Province. He has worked with, and received support from DJs, such as Tiësto, Fedde le Grand, Sander van Doorn, David Guetta, Calvin Harris, Armand van Helden, Avicii and Hardwell. He currently ranks at number 26 on DJ Mag's annual Top 100 DJs poll. He is known for his viral hit song "Toulouse".
mgt: Nicky Romero (born Nick Rotteveel on January 6, 1989) is a Dutch DJ, record producer, and remixer known for his contributions to electronic dance music (EDM). Rising to prominence with hits like "Toulouse" and his collaboration with Avicii, "I Could Be the One," Romero has achieved international success, including chart-topping releases and performances at major festivals like Tomorrowland and Ultra Music Festival. He founded Protocol Recordings in 2012, a successful record label, and hosts the weekly radio show Protocol Radio.

page_title: Acts of Parliament of the United Kingdom relating to the European Communities and the European Union
trgt:
This is a list of current, amended, spent and repealed Acts of the Parliament of the United Kingdom relating to its former membership and current relationship to the European Communities and the European Union from 1972 onwards.

Some of the Acts (particularly the European Communities Act 1972 and the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018) represent some of the most important constitutional legislation formerly or currently on the statute book.
mgt: The Acts of Parliament of the United Kingdom relating to the European Communities and the European Union encompass legislation governing the UK's accession to the European Communities (later the European Union), its membership, and subsequent developments. Key acts include the European Communities Act 1972, which facilitated the UK's entry in 1973, and later legislation addressing referendums, European Parliament elections, the European Economic Area, EU treaty amendments, and the UK's withdrawal from the EU.

trgt: Nancy Duff Campbell is an American lawyer and a founder and co-president of the National Women's Law Center. Campbell has focused on women's law and public policy issues and has participated in the development of legislative initiatives and litigation regarding women's rights, emphasizing issues affecting low‐income women, and has authored articles on women's legal issues.
mgt: Nancy Duff Campbell is an American attorney, law professor, and women's rights advocate. She co-founded the National Women's Law Center in 1981, focusing on gender equality and economic justice. Campbell has participated in landmark Supreme Court cases and policy initiatives, including tax reforms and child support enforcement, significantly advancing legal protections for women and families.

trgt: Emilie Tolnay (born Emilie Müller: 6 October 1901 - 5 July 1944) was an Austrian hairdresser who by the time of the German annexation in 1938 had become a resistance activist. She died on the guillotine in the Vienna district court complex.
mgt: Emilie Tolnay (née Müller; ? – July 5, 1944) was an Austrian resistance activist during World War II. A member of the Communist resistance, she was arrested for anti-Nazi activities and sentenced to death by the People's Court. Tolnay was executed by guillotine in Vienna.

trgt: The 2014 Mountain Province Bus accident was a bus accident that occurred when a Florida Trans bus fell off a road in Sitio Paggang, Talubin, Bontoc, Mountain Province of the Philippines. The accident occurred on February 7, 2014. Fourteen people were killed and more than 32 people were injured. Comedian Tado and two more artists died in this accident.
mgt: The 2014 Mountain Province bus accident occurred on February 7, 2014, in Sitio Paggang, Talubin, Bontoc, Mountain Province, Philippines, when a GV Florida Transport bus fell 100 to 120 meters off a mountain road. The accident resulted in 14 deaths, including prominent figures such as comedian Tado, and 32 injuries. Mechanical issues were suspected as the cause.

trgt: Saga (born 1975) is a Swedish white nationalist singer-songwriter. She started as the vocalist for Symphony of Sorrow, but has since become known for her tribute CDs to the band Skrewdriver and her solo projects, promoting white nationalism and anti-communism. She is one of the most successful Swedish white nationalist singers.
mgt: Saga (born 1975) is a Swedish singer associated with white power music and the nationalist movement. Active since 2000, she gained international recognition through tribute albums and performances across Europe and the United States. Her music was controversially cited by Anders Behring Breivik in his 2011 manifesto, which she publicly condemned, retreating from public life afterward.

trgt: Quintus Pompeius Falco (c. 70 – after 140 AD) was a Roman senator and general of the early 2nd century AD. He was governor of several provinces, most notably Roman Britain, where he hosted a visit to the province by the Emperor Hadrian in the last year. Falco achieved the rank of suffect consul for the nundinium of September to December 108 with Marcus Titius Lustricus Bruttianus as his colleague.
mgt: Quintus Pompeius Falco (c. 70 – after 143) was a Roman senator, general, and governor who held several prominent positions during the reigns of Trajan and Hadrian. He served as governor of Judea, Moesia Inferior, and Britain, where he suppressed a rebellion. Falco also oversaw the construction of the Via Traiana and later governed the province of Asia. He married Sosia Polla, linking him to prominent Roman families, and retired to his estate near Tusculum.

trgt: Gold Hill, also known as Goldhill, Gold Mine, or Gold Ridge, is an unincorporated community north-centrally located in Lee County, Alabama, United States, just a few hundred feet south of the Chambers County line. It is part of the Columbus, Georgia-Alabama Metropolitan Area. Today, Gold Hill lies mostly in the corporate limits of Auburn.
mgt: Gold Hill is an unincorporated community in Lee County, Alabama, United States. Settled in the 1830s, it was named after an early settler, Goldsmith, rather than the gold mined nearby during the Alabama Gold Rush. The community, also known as Gold Ridge in the 1870s, is home to historical landmarks, including the now-demolished 140-year-old barn.

trgt: This article is about the phonology of Bernese German. It deals with current phonology and phonetics, including geographical variants. Like other High Alemannic varieties, it has a two-way contrast in plosives and fricatives that is not based on voicing, but on length. The absence of voice in plosives and fricatives is typical for all High German varieties, but many of them have no two-way contrast due to general lenition.
mgt: Bernese German phonology is characterized by several distinctive features, including a vowel length opposition independent of vowel quality, a rich system of diphthongs and triphthongs, and unique consonantal traits. Notable aspects include the vocalization of /l/ to [w], the velarization of /nd̥/ to [ŋː], and the reduction of /ŋk͡x/ to [jɣ̊] in certain dialects. Stress patterns favor initial stress in loanwords, and vowel lengthening and shortening occur in specific contexts, distinguishing Bernese German from other Alemannic dialects.

trgt: The House of Zogu, or Zogolli during Ottoman times and until 1922, is an Albanian dynasty whose roots date back to the early 14th century. The family provided the first president and the short-lived modern Albanian Kingdom with its only monarch, Zog I of Albania (1928–1939).
mgt: The House of Zogu is a former Albanian royal dynasty founded in the 13th century by Zogu Pasha. Its most notable member, King Zog I, ruled Albania from 1928 until his deposition in 1939. The current head is Crown Prince Leka II.

trgt: Entrenching battalions were temporary units formed in the armies of the British Empire during the First World War. Entrenching Battalions were trained as infantry, but were primarily utilized for manual labour duties such as trench repair, wire laying, road making and assisting tunnellers, pioneers, engineers and signals. The Battalions were also used as pools of men from which drafts of replacements could be drawn by conventional infantry battalions.
mgt: Entrenching battalions were temporary units formed during World War I by the British, Canadian, and New Zealand armies to perform labor-intensive tasks such as improving defenses and serving as reserve forces. The British Army established them in 1916, disbanding them in 1918 after the German spring offensive. The Canadian Expeditionary Force created similar units in 1916, disbanding them in 1917, while New Zealand formed entrenching battalions in 1918, disbanding them later that year.

trgt: Lord Forbes was launched at Chester in 1803 as a West Indiaman. She soon became an "armed defense ship", but by 1805 had returned to being a West Indiaman. She made two voyages as an "extra" ship for the British East India Company (EIC). She continued trading with India until 1817 when she sustained damage on her way to Bengal. There she was surveyed, condemned and sold.
mgt: The *Lord Forbes* was a British merchant ship launched in 1803 and employed in various roles, including as an armed defense ship during the Napoleonic Wars and later as an East Indiaman for the British East India Company (EIC). She completed two voyages for the EIC between 1811 and 1814 before transitioning to private trade. In 1817, after sustaining damage en route to Bengal, she was condemned and sold for breaking up.

trgt:
Cuck is a 2019 American thriller film directed by Rob Lambert from a screenplay by Lambert and Joe Varkle. It stars Zachary Ray Sherman, Timothy V. Murphy, Sally Kirkland and Monique Parent.

The film had its world premiere at the Cleveland International Film Festival on March 28, 2019 and it was released on October 4, 2019.
mgt: *Cuck* is a 2019 American psychological drama film directed by Rob Lambert and co-written with Joe Varkle. The film follows Ronnie, a mentally unstable loner who becomes immersed in extremist internet communities, leading to a destructive unraveling of his identity. Starring Zachary Ray Sherman, the film explores themes of masculinity, online radicalization, and alienation. Released by Gravitas Ventures, it received mixed reviews for its performances and atmosphere but was criticized for its plot and tone.

trgt: Pudliszki Sp. z o.o. is a brand and producer in the food industry of fruit and vegetables. Presently, the brand is under the American company Heinz, which holds considerable shares in the company. Pudliszki focuses on the production of tomato goods (ketchup and concentrate) and vegetables (peas, beans and corn).
mgt: Pudliszki is a Polish food company founded in 1920 and headquartered in Pudliszki, Poland. Initially established as a sugar refinery in 1847, it transitioned to food production in 1923 under Stanisław Fenrych. Known for its tomato products, the company was nationalized post-World War II and privatized in 1994. Since 1997, it has been majority-owned by Heinz.

trgt: Springfield Central Railway Station is the terminus station of the Springfield Line in Queensland, Australia. It serves Springfield Central (the central suburb of Greater Springfield) and other nearby suburbs in the City of Ipswich. Brisbane-bound services depart half-hourly during off-peak times, with most travelling to Kippa-Ring. The station was opened in December 2013 after the line was extended from Richlands.
mgt: Springfield Central railway station is an elevated commuter rail station located in Springfield Central, Queensland, Australia. Opened on December 2, 2013, as part of the Darra to Springfield Transport Corridor project, it serves the Springfield line and is operated by Queensland Rail. The station features a 12-meter-wide island platform, parking facilities, bus connections, and accessibility features, catering to the growing western Brisbane region.

trgt: Code R is an American action-adventure television series that aired on CBS from January 21 to June 10, 1977. Code R focuses on the emergency services (police, fire, and ocean rescue) of the California Channel Islands. The series starred James Houghton, Martin Kove and Tom Simcox and ran for a single season of thirteen episodes.
mgt: *Code R* is a short-lived American action-adventure television series that aired on CBS from January 21 to June 10, 1977. Produced by Warner Bros. Television, the show followed the emergency services of a fictional island, featuring police, fire, and ocean rescue teams. Despite international screenings, *Code R* was canceled after 13 episodes due to low ratings.

trgt: Gerard Alsteens (born 3 August 1940, in Auderghem) is a Belgian political cartoonist, graphic artist and painter who works under the pseudonym "GAL". which, artistically speaking, share a closer resemblance to paintings than regularly drawn cartoons. Throughout the years his work has received several prizes and awards.
mgt: GAL (born 1940 in Auderghem, Brussels) is a Belgian cartoonist and illustrator known for his socially and politically engaged work. Active since 1960, his cartoons have appeared in prominent publications like *Knack* and *De Zwijger*. His distinctive style blends artistic techniques with sharp political commentary, often addressing issues like apartheid and far-right politics. GAL has contributed to numerous social causes, including Oxfam and Amnesty International, and his work has been featured globally. In 2019, he received an honorary doctorate from the Free University in Brussels.

trgt: Elizabeth Marian Swaney OLY (born 30 July 1984) is an American born-and-raised freestyle skier who competed for Hungary in the 2018 Winter Olympics in the women's halfpipe based on her Hungarian ancestry. She was unable to qualify for the 2014 Winter Olympics for Venezuela in both skeleton and freestyle skiing, and qualified for the 2018 Winter Olympics in the women's halfpipe, in which she placed last.
mgt: Elizabeth Marian Swaney (born July 30, 1984) is an American freestyle skier who represented Hungary in the women's halfpipe at the 2018 Winter Olympics. Known for her unconventional path to Olympic qualification, she utilized the quota system and strategic participation in events to secure her spot. Despite criticism for her performance, she received support from fellow athletes. Swaney is also a Harvard graduate, marathoner, and former skeleton competitor.

trgt:
Beta Delphini (β Delphini, abbreviated Beta Del, β Del) is a binary star in the constellation of Delphinus. It is the brightest star in Delphinus.

The two components of the system are designated Beta Delphini A (officially named Rotanev /ˈroʊtənɛv/, which is historically the name of the system) and B.
mgt: Beta Delphini, also known as Rotanev, is a binary star system in the constellation Delphinus, located approximately 101 light-years from Earth. Discovered as a binary in 1873, it consists of two F-type stars with an orbital period of 26.66 years. The primary star is a giant with 1.75 times the Sun's mass, while the secondary is a subgiant with 1.47 times the Sun's mass.
