The system's name, Rotanev, originated as a reversed Latinized form of the name of Niccolò Cacciatore, an astronomer who worked on the Palermo Star Catalogue. In 2016, the International Astronomical Union officially approved the name Rotanev for the primary star.

trgt: The Technological Educational Institute of Peloponnese (Lang-el; formerly Technological Educational Institute of Kalamata, Τεχνολογικό Εκπαιδευτικό Ίδρυμα Καλαμάτας, TEIKAL) was one of the 15 Technological Educational Institutes in Greece. Its main campus and administrative centre was located in Kalamata and one department was located in Sparta.
mgt: The Technological Educational Institute of Peloponnese (TEI of Peloponnese) was a public university in Kalamata, Greece, active from 1989 to 2019. Originally established as a faculty of the TEI of Patras, it became an independent institution in 1989. The institute comprised four schools and six departments, including programs in engineering, information technology, and health sciences. It underwent significant restructuring under the "ATHENA" Reform Plan in 2013 and received a positive evaluation in 2016. In 2019, it was abolished, with its School of Engineering absorbed by the University of Peloponnese.

trgt: World Wide Adventures is a generalized name applied to Warner Bros. live-action short films of the 1960s. Usually, the trade magazines like BoxOffice only listed the one-reelers (running about 10 minutes in length) under this heading, with the longer films simply dubbed “specials.” For the most part, this was a handy marketing logo for a wide range of shorts of the documentary genre.
mgt: *World Wide Adventures* was a series of live-action short films distributed alongside Looney Tunes and Merrie Melodies cartoons during the mid-20th century. Produced by Warner Bros., these featurettes, often travelogues, were created by in-house producers Cedric Francis and William L. Hendricks. Though largely forgotten, some titles, like *Kingdom of the Saguenay*, have been rediscovered as extras on Turner Classic Movies.

trgt: The olfactory tract is a bilateral bundle of afferent nerve fibers from the mitral and tufted cells of the olfactory bulb that connects to several target regions in the brain, including the piriform cortex, amygdala, and entorhinal cortex. It is a narrow white band, triangular on coronal section, the apex being directed upward.
mgt: The olfactory tract is a neural structure in the brain's olfactory system, located in the olfactory sulcus on the inferior surface of the frontal lobe. It divides into medial and lateral striae, connecting to various brain regions involved in olfaction. Damage to the olfactory tract can cause ipsilateral anosmia, as seen in conditions like Kallmann syndrome.

trgt:
Mohamed Gasana Tchité (born 31 January 1984) is a Burundian–Belgian footballer who plays as a striker.

He spent most of his professional career in Belgium, with Standard Liége, Anderlecht, Club Brugge, Sint-Truiden and White Star Bruxelles, and in Spain with Racing de Santander.
mgt: Mohamed Gasana Tchité (born January 31, 1984) is a retired Burundian professional footballer who played as a striker. He had a notable club career in Belgium, Spain, and Romania, representing teams such as Standard Liège, Anderlecht, Racing Santander, and Club Brugge. Tchité won individual accolades, including the Belgian Professional Footballer of the Year and the Belgian Ebony Shoe. Despite his desire to represent Belgium internationally, FIFA deemed him ineligible due to his prior appearances for Burundi and a change of allegiance to Rwanda.

trgt: Sylvia Fine Kaye (August 29, 1913 – October 28, 1991) was an American lyricist, composer, and producer. Many of her compositions and productions were performed by her husband, comedian Danny Kaye. Fine was a Peabody Award-winner and was nominated for two Academy Awards and two Emmys during her career. She won an Emmy award in 1976 for children's special.
mgt: Sylvia Fine (August 29, 1913 – October 28, 1991) was an American composer, lyricist, and producer, best known for her work managing the career of her husband, entertainer Danny Kaye. She wrote many of his songs for film and television, earning multiple award nominations and a Peabody Award. Fine also produced and taught musical comedy, leaving a lasting legacy in entertainment.

trgt:
Henry Nicholas John Gunther (June 6, 1895 – November 11, 1918) was an American soldier and possibly the last soldier of any of the belligerents to be killed during World War I. He was killed at 10:59 a.m., about one minute before the Armistice was to take effect at 11:00 a.m.

Gunther had recently been demoted, and was seeking to regain his rank just before the war ended.
mgt: Henry Nicholas John Gunther (June 6, 1895 – November 11, 1918) was an American soldier and the last known combat death of World War I. Born in Baltimore, Maryland, to a German-American family, he was drafted in 1917 and served in the 313th Infantry Regiment. Demoted from sergeant to private, Gunther sought to redeem himself and was killed charging a German machine gun position one minute before the Armistice took effect. Posthumously, he was restored to the rank of sergeant and awarded the Distinguished Service Cross. Memorials honor him in both France and the United States.

trgt: Advance America (formerly Citizens Concerned for the Constitution) is a conservative political advocacy group in the U.S. state of Indiana. Advance America claims that it is a "non-partisan tax exempt, educational organization." It claims affiliation with approximately 4,000 Indiana churches, nearly one third of all churches in the state of Indiana.
mgt: Advance America is an Indiana-based conservative advocacy group founded in 1980 by attorney Eric Miller. Governed by a board of pastors, it focuses on issues related to family and religious freedom. The group publishes voter guides, monitors state legislation, and campaigns on topics such as religious freedom laws, same-sex marriage bans, and education policies.

trgt: Walter Gilbert Alexander I (December 3, 1880 – February 5, 1953) was an American physician and Republican Party politician from New Jersey. He was president of the National Medical Association and the first African American to serve in the New Jersey Legislature.
mgt: Walter G. Alexander (December 3, 1880 – February 5, 1953) was an American physician, civil rights leader, and politician. He was the first African American to serve in the New Jersey Legislature, representing Essex County in the General Assembly from 1920 to 1922.

trgt: Bajrang Punia (born 26 February 1994) is an Indian freestyle wrestler, who competes in the 65-kg weight category. At the 2020 Tokyo Olympics, Punia won a bronze medal by defeating Kazakhstan's Daulet Niyazbekov with a wide 8-0 margin. Punia is the only Indian wrestler to win 4 medals at the World Wrestling Championships.
mgt: Bajrang Punia (born 26 February 1994) is an Indian freestyle wrestler and Olympic medalist. He has won multiple medals at prestigious international events, including a bronze at the 2020 Tokyo Olympics, silver at the 2018 World Wrestling Championships, and gold at the 2018 Asian Games and 2022 Commonwealth Games. Known for his dominance in the 65 kg category, Punia has also claimed numerous Asian and Commonwealth titles. Born in Khudan, Haryana, he began wrestling at a young age, inspired by his father, and trained at Chhatrasal Stadium. Punia is married to fellow wrestler Sangeeta Phogat and works as a Gazetted Officer in the Indian Railways.

trgt: Baguma Crescent, popularly known as Beenie Gunter, is a Ugandan reggae dancehall artist, commonly known for his hit songs Pon Mi and Olina Work, the latter, a collaboration with Nigerian artist Skales. He released his 25 track debut studio album No Fear in 2019 that featured artists like Skales and Lydia Jazmine.
mgt: Beenie Gunter, born Crescent Baguma on February 23, 1993, in Kyenjojo, Uganda, is a reggae and dancehall musician. Active since 2013, he gained prominence with hits like "Pon Mi" and "Olina Work," collaborating with artists such as Skales, Eddy Kenzo, and Beenie Man. He founded Guntalk City in 2015 and released his debut album *No Fear* in 2019.

trgt: Dale Harris Van Sickel (November 29, 1907 – January 25, 1977) was an American college football, basketball and baseball player during the 1920s, who later became a Hollywood motion picture actor and stunt performer for over forty years. Van Sickel played college football for the University of Florida, and was recognized as the first-ever first-team All-American in the history of the Florida Gators football program.
mgt: Dale Van Sickel (November 29, 1907 – January 25, 1977) was an American athlete and Hollywood stuntman. He was a standout football player at the University of Florida, becoming the school's first first-team All-American in 1928 and later being inducted into the College Football Hall of Fame in 1975. After a brief coaching career, he transitioned to Hollywood, performing stunts in over 140 films and co-founding the Stuntmen's Association of Motion Pictures.

trgt: Helmut Rahn (16 August 1929 – 14 August 2003), known as Der Boss (The Boss), was a German footballer who played as a forward. He became a legend for having scored the winning goal in the final of the 1954 FIFA World Cup (West Germany vs. Hungary 3–2). Rahn, along with the German team, were decorated by the President of the Federal Republic of Germany in 1972.
mgt: Helmut Rahn (16 August 1929 – 14 August 2003) was a German footballer who played as a wing forward and earned legendary status for his decisive role in West Germany's victory at the 1954 FIFA World Cup, famously scoring the winning goal in the final, known as the "Miracle of Bern." Nicknamed "Der Boss," Rahn played for clubs including Rot-Weiss Essen and 1. FC Köln, and scored 21 goals in 40 international matches.

trgt: Marahoué National Park is a national park in Ivory Coast. It was established in 1968, and covers 1,010 km2 (390 sq mi). However, it has lost nearly all its forest cover in the first two decades of the 21st century. Over the years, the park has been damaged and occupied by people and no longer provides suitable habitat for chimpanzees or many other large animals.
mgt: Marahoué National Park is a protected area in central Ivory Coast, established in 1968 and covering 1,010 km². Located near Bouaflé, it features diverse habitats, including gallery forests, savannas, and wetlands. Once heavily forested, the park has suffered extensive deforestation, illegal agriculture, and habitat loss, leading to the disappearance of species like the West African chimpanzee. It remains home to various mammals and 287 recorded bird species.

trgt: Oscar "Richard" Serag Garin Jr. (born December 11, 1969) is a Filipino politician and civil engineer serving as the mayor of Miagao since 2022. He was previously the Representative of Iloilo's 1st district, Vice Governor of Iloilo, member of Iloilo's provincial board, and Mayor of Guimbal.
mgt: Richard Garin (born December 11, 1967) is a Filipino politician and civil engineer currently serving as the mayor of Miagao, Iloilo, since 2022. He previously held various political positions, including Vice Governor of Iloilo (2010–2013), Representative of Iloilo's 1st district (2013–2019), and Mayor of Guimbal (1998–2007). A member of the Garin political family, he has authored significant social legislations and is married to Janette Garin, a former Secretary of Health and current congresswoman.

trgt: The England Boxing National Amateur Championships previously known as the ABA Championships is the premier boxing tournament hosted annually by England Boxing. The Championships are 'open' class: any boxer who is registered with a club registered with England Boxing (aged over 17 years by the 1 October) can enter.
mgt: The England Boxing National Amateur Championships, formerly known as the ABA Championships, is an annual amateur boxing competition in England, inaugurated in 1881. Organized by England Boxing, it features multiple weight categories and has undergone numerous changes, including rebranding in 2019. The event has historically been a key pathway for English boxers.

trgt: May Song Vang (February 5, 1951 – August 5, 2013) was an American Hmong community leader and activist. She was the widow of General Vang Pao, a former member of the Royal Lao Army and prominent Hmong American leader, who died in 2011. May Song Vang became a more prominent symbol of the Hmong American community in California and the rest of the United States after the death of her husband.
mgt: May Song Vang (February 5, 1951 – August 5, 2013) was a Laotian-born Hmong American leader, midwife, nurse, and physician assistant. A pioneer for women in Laos, she defied societal norms to pursue education and later married General Vang Pao, a prominent Hmong military leader. After immigrating to the United States, she became a key figure in the Hmong American community, advocating for education and founding the General Vang Pao Foundation.

trgt: Muhammad Said al-Attar (Lang-ar) (26 November 1927 – 20 November 2005) was the acting Prime Minister of Yemen for five months in 1994. During his career, he held various ministerial positions in Yemen and represented his country at the United Nations. From 1974 to 1985, Muhammad Said Al-Attar was Executive Secretary of the United Nations Economic Commission for Western Asia (currently ESCWA).
mgt: Muhammad Said al-Attar (1927 – November 20, 2005) was a Yemeni economist, diplomat, and politician who held prominent roles in Yemen and international organizations. He served as Deputy Prime Minister, Minister of the Economy, and Yemen's Permanent Representative to the United Nations. Al-Attar was the first Executive Secretary of the United Nations Economic Commission for Western Asia and received the French Legion of Honour.

trgt:
The AN/FPS-24 Radar was a long range early warning radar used by the United States Air Force Air Defense Command. It used a two-frequency signal in order to avoid fluctuation loss, which causes signals on single-frequency radars to fade in and out as the target moves. Reducing this effect results in a much steadier signal.

General Electric was the primary contractor for the design, which operated in the very high frequency (VHF) at 214 to 236 MHz.
mgt: The AN/FPS-24 radar was a VHF early warning radar system developed by General Electric and deployed by the United States Air Force between 1958 and 1962. Twelve units were constructed, featuring a powerful 5 MW transmitter and a large rotating antenna with a range of 250 nautical miles. Despite its advanced capabilities, the system faced significant design and operational challenges, including bearing failures. Some units were later repurposed for joint USAF-FAA surveillance, and several radar towers remain as historic landmarks.

trgt: Sendle is an Australian-based company which provides courier services within the country, focusing primarily on small to medium-sized businesses and online retailers. It claims to be the first courier company in Australia to offer fully carbon neutral services. While its delivery services are worldwide - with the exception of Russia and Belarus - its operational staff are based in Australia, New Zealand, the Philippines and the United States.
mgt: Sendle is an Australian private logistics company founded in 2014 by James Bradfield Moody, Sean Geoghegan, and Craig Davis. Headquartered in Sydney, it provides parcel and package delivery services across Australia. In 2015, it became the country's first carbon-neutral delivery service. Sendle has partnered with major e-commerce platforms like eBay and Shopify and has raised significant capital, including $35 million in 2021, to support its growth and expansion.

trgt: The Great Sandy Desert (GSD) is an interim Australian bioregion, located in the northeast of Western Australia straddling the Pilbara and southern Kimberley regions and extending east into the Northern Territory. It is the second largest desert in Australia after the Great Victoria Desert and encompasses an area of 284,993 square kilometres (110,036 sq mi). The Gibson Desert lies to the south and the Tanami Desert lies to the east of the Great Sandy Desert.
mgt: The Great Sandy Desert is a vast desert region in northern Western Australia and the Northern Territory, covering an area of 284,993 km² (110,036 sq mi). Characterized by large ergs and longitudinal dunes, it is home to features like the Wolfe Creek Crater. The region is sparsely populated, with Indigenous Australian communities, including the Martu and Pintupi peoples, and mining operations. The desert experiences extreme heat, seasonal rainfall, and unique flora and fauna, including spinifex vegetation and diverse wildlife.

trgt: Kerry Condon (born 9 January 1983) is an Irish actress. She is best known for her role as Octavia of the Julii in Rome, Stacey Ehrmantraut in Better Call Saul, and the voice of F.R.I.D.A.Y. in various films in the Marvel Cinematic Universe. She is also the youngest actress ever to play Ophelia in a Royal Shakespeare Company production of Hamlet.
mgt: Kerry Condon (born 9 January 1983) is an Irish actress known for her work in film, television, and theater. She gained recognition for her stage roles, including Mairead in *The Lieutenant of Inishmore* and Ophelia in *Hamlet* with the Royal Shakespeare Company, and won awards for *The Cripple of Inishmaan*. Condon's film credits include *Ned Kelly*, *Unleashed*, and *The Last Station*. She is also known for voicing F.R.I.D.A.Y. in the Marvel Cinematic Universe and for her roles in the HBO/BBC series *Rome* and *The Walking Dead*.

trgt:
UPC Nederland (Lang-en) was the second largest cable operator in the Netherlands, providing cable television (digital and analogue), broadband Internet, and telephone service to both residential and commercial customers.

In early 2015, UPC Nederland started merging with Ziggo. The name UPC was finally phased out in favor of Ziggo on 13 April 2015.
mgt: UPC Nederland was a Dutch telecommunications company founded in 1998 and headquartered in Amsterdam. A subsidiary of Liberty Global, it provided cable television, broadband internet, and telephony services. Formed through the consolidation of regional cable operators, it introduced innovations like EuroDOCSIS 3.0 and the Horizon set-top box. In 2015, it merged with Ziggo.

trgt: Empire Day was a 7,241 GRT cargo ship that was built in 1941 as a CAM ship by William Doxford & Sons Ltd, Sunderland, Co Durham, United Kingdom for the Ministry of War Transport (MoWT). In 1943, she was converted to a standard cargo ship. She served until August 1944 when she was torpedoed and sunk by U-198.
mgt: MV *Empire Day* was a British CAM ship and cargo vessel built in 1941 by William Doxford & Sons Ltd in Sunderland for the Ministry of War Transport. Initially armed with a Hawker Sea Hurricane, she served in numerous convoys during World War II, transporting goods such as grain and coal. On 7 August 1944, while en route from Mozambique to Aden, she was torpedoed and sunk by German submarine U-198 off the coast of Tanganyika. All 42 aboard survived, though her Chief Officer was later killed when U-198 was sunk.

trgt: Major Edward Tempest Tunstall North JP (31 January 1900 – 1 January 1942) was a British Conservative Party politician. He was the Member of Parliament (MP) for Nuneaton in Warwickshire from 1931 until 1935. He was killed in action in 1942, aged 41.
mgt: Edward North (1900 – 1 January 1942) was a British Conservative politician and military officer. He served as the Member of Parliament for Nuneaton from 1931 to 1935, later serving as a Major in the Royal Armoured Corps during World War II.

trgt: Robert Gilbert Haney, Jr. (March 15, 1926 – November 25, 2004) was an American comic book writer, best known for his work for DC Comics. He co-created the Teen Titans as well as characters such as Metamorpho, Eclipso, Cain, and the Super-Sons.
mgt: Bob Haney (March 15, 1926 – November 25, 2004) was an American comic book writer known for his extensive work with DC Comics from the 1950s to the 1980s. He co-created notable characters and teams, including the Teen Titans, Metamorpho, and Eclipso, and contributed significantly to titles such as *The Brave and the Bold* and *Aquaman*. Haney's writing often addressed youth culture and social issues, though he was known for disregarding continuity. Beyond comics, he wrote for animated series like *ThunderCats* and *The New Adventures of Superman*. Haney received several accolades, including the Inkpot Award and the posthumous Bill Finger Award.

trgt: Molecular electronic transitions take place when electrons in a molecule are excited from one energy level to a higher energy level. The energy change associated with this transition provides information on the structure of a molecule and determines many molecular properties such as colour. The relationship between the energy involved in the electronic transition and the frequency of radiation is given by Planck's relation.
mgt: A molecular electronic transition refers to the movement of an electron between energy levels within a molecule, often studied using ultraviolet–visible spectroscopy. These transitions, such as σ → σ*, π → π*, and n → σ*, occur in chromophores, which absorb light in specific regions of the electromagnetic spectrum. Factors like solvent effects and molecular structure influence these transitions, which are associated with broad absorption bands rather than narrow spectral lines.

trgt: CollabNet VersionOne is a software firm headquartered in Alpharetta, Georgia, United States. CollabNet VersionOne products and services belong to the industry categories of value stream management, devops, agile management, application lifecycle management (ALM), and enterprise version control. These products are used by companies and government organizations to reduce the time it takes to create and release software.
mgt: CollabNet, founded in 1999 by Tim O'Reilly, Brian Behlendorf, and Bill Portelli, is a private software company headquartered in Alpharetta, Georgia. Specializing in agile management, DevOps, and value stream management, it serves global enterprises and government organizations. CollabNet merged with VersionOne in 2017 and later became part of Digital.ai in 2020 following mergers with XebiaLabs and Arxan.

trgt: Might and Magic: Heroes Kingdoms (originally Heroes of Might and Magic Kingdoms) was a real-time strategy massively multiplayer online game based on the Heroes of Might and Magic series. The game was announced in 2007 with closed betas running until 2009. It was released in France on 3 November 2009. It closed its servers on 1 September 2014.
mgt: *Might and Magic: Heroes Kingdoms* was a browser-based real-time strategy massively multiplayer online game developed and published by Ubisoft. Released on November 3, 2009, in France, it was part of the *Heroes of Might and Magic* series. Players managed cities, heroes, and resources while forming alliances to achieve victory. The game operated on a free-to-play model with optional subscriptions and virtual goods. It was discontinued on September 1, 2014.

trgt: Veronika Ivanovskaia (born 1 February 1995) is a German pool player. She began to play in 2003, and later became part of the German national team. She was the winner of the 2018 European Pool Championships eight-ball event, and the runner-up at the 2017 Austria Open.
mgt: Veronika Ivanovskaia (born February 1, 1995) is a German professional pool player of Russian descent. A former junior European champion, she has won multiple titles, including the 2018 European Pool Championship in eight-ball. Ivanovskaia also competes internationally in World and Euro Tour events.

trgt:
Luro, officially Luro Administrative Post (Lang-pt, Lang-tet), is an administrative post (and was formerly a subdistrict) in Lautém municipality, East Timor; Luro is also the name of two of its towns (Upper and Lower Luro).

The main river of the administrative post is the Adafuro, running between Wairoque and Cotamutu and through Afabubu. Principal crops grown in the subdistrict include corn, rice, coconuts, cassava and other vegetables.
mgt: Luro is an administrative post in Lautém Municipality, East Timor, covering an area of 125.4 square kilometers with a population of 7,124 as of 2015. Located in a valley surrounded by hills, its highest point is Legumau at 1,228 meters. The region is divided into six sucos and relies on agriculture, with rice, corn, and livestock being key to its economy.

trgt: HNoMS Rauma was an Otra-class minesweeper built in 1939 for the Royal Norwegian Navy. Captured by the Germans during the 1940 invasion of Norway and renamed Kamerun, she was returned to the Norwegians after the end of the Second World War and recommissioned in 1947. Rauma remained in service until being sold for scrapping in 1963.
mgt: HNoMS *Rauma* was a Norwegian Otra-class minesweeper launched in 1939 and commissioned in early 1940. Shortly after entering service, it participated in the defense of Horten during the German invasion of Norway, damaging enemy vessels before being seized by German forces. Renamed *Kamerun*, it served in the Kriegsmarine and later the German Mine Sweeping Administration. Returned to Norway post-war, *Rauma* was recommissioned in 1947, rebuilt as a minelayer training ship in 1949, and decommissioned in 1959 before being scrapped in 1963.

trgt: AFSCME Council 31 is the Illinois state chapter of the American Federation of State, County and Municipal Employees (AFSCME), a union of public service workers in the public, private and non-profit sectors. AFSCME Council 31 has "100,000 active and retired members", including "approximately 40,000 state employees working in more than 50 departments, authorities, boards, and commissions under the authority of the Governor."
mgt: AFSCME Council 31 is the Illinois chapter of the American Federation of State, County and Municipal Employees (AFSCME), a major public sector labor union in the United States. Established in 1942, it has played a significant role in advocating for collective bargaining rights, particularly through milestones like the Illinois Public Labor Relations Act of 1983. The council has been involved in political organizing, protests, and legal disputes, including standoffs with Illinois governors over labor contracts and layoffs.

trgt: Srikanth Kidambi (born 7 February 1993) is an Indian badminton player. A former world no. 1, Kidambi was awarded the Padma Shri, India's fourth highest civilian award, in 2018. and the Arjuna award in 2015. In 2021, he became the first Indian to reach the World Championship final in the men's singles discipline.
mgt: Srikanth Kidambi (born 7 February 1993) is an Indian badminton player and former world number one in men's singles. He is the first Indian male to win a silver medal at the BWF World Championships (2021) and played a key role in India's historic Thomas Cup victory in 2022. Kidambi has won multiple prestigious titles, including four Superseries titles in 2017, equaling a record held by legends like Lin Dan and Lee Chong Wei. Coached by Pullela Gopichand, he has also earned medals at the Commonwealth Games and South Asian Games, solidifying his status as one of India's most accomplished badminton players.

trgt: Alturas de Macchu Picchu is a studio album by the Chilean rock band Los Jaivas, released in October 1981 on SyM label. It is a concept album that musicalizes the homonymous poem by Pablo Neruda that appeared in his 1950 book Canto General. The album mixed electric, electronic and folk instruments. Prior to its launch, an television special recorded in the ruins of Machu Picchu was presented on Chilean and Peruvian television.
mgt: *Alturas de Macchu Picchu* is a 1981 concept album by the Chilean band Los Jaivas, based on Pablo Neruda's poem of the same name from his 1950 work *Canto General*. Recorded in Paris and during tours, the album blends rock, folk, and Andean music, featuring traditional instruments alongside electric and electronic elements. Acclaimed for its innovation, it is considered a landmark in Latin American music and was ranked the second-best Chilean album of all time by *Rolling Stone* in 2008.

trgt: Dacrydium cupressinum, commonly known as rimu, is a large evergreen coniferous tree endemic to the forests of New Zealand. It is a member of the southern conifer group, the podocarps. The former name "red pine" has fallen out of common use.
mgt: Dacrydium cupressinum, commonly known as rimu, is a slow-growing coniferous tree native to New Zealand, found in lowland and montane forests across the North, South, and Stewart Islands. It can grow up to 50 meters tall and live for 800–900 years. Rimu plays an ecological role as a food source for birds, including the endangered kakapo, and has historical significance for Māori, who used its heartwood and bark. While once widely harvested for timber, rimu is now protected in public forests, with limited logging permitted on private land. It is also cultivated as an ornamental tree.

trgt:
Kogiidae is a family comprising at least two extant species of Cetacea, the pygmy (Kogia breviceps) and dwarf (K. sima) sperm whales. As their common names suggest, they somewhat resemble sperm whales, with squared heads and small lower jaws, but are much smaller in size, with much shorter skulls and more notable dorsal fins than sperm whales. Kogiids are also characterized by a "false gill slit" behind their eyes.

A number of genera are extinct.
mgt: Kogiidae is a family of small cetaceans within the superfamily Physeteroidea, which includes the pygmy sperm whale (*Kogia breviceps*) and the dwarf sperm whale (*Kogia sima*). These deep-diving, squid-eating whales are known for their unique adaptations, including a spermaceti organ for sound production, a modified colon that releases a red "ink" to deter predators, and high encephalization quotients. Found in oceans worldwide except polar regions, kogiids face threats from fishing entanglement, plastic ingestion, and commercial hunting.

trgt: Four Lane Ends is a Tyne and Wear Metro station, serving the suburbs of Benton and Longbenton, North Tyneside in Tyne and Wear, England. It joined the network on 11 August 1980, following the opening of the first phase of the network, between Haymarket and Tynemouth via Four Lane Ends.
mgt: Four Lane Ends Interchange is a transport hub in Longbenton, North Tyneside, England, serving as both a Tyne and Wear Metro station and a bus station. Opened in 1980, it features step-free access, a multi-storey car park, cycle facilities, and art installations. It provides frequent Metro and bus services connecting Newcastle and North Tyneside.

trgt: Fight Sports is a Grappling and Brazilian jiu-jitsu academy and team based out of Miami, Florida. It was founded in 2011 by multiple time world champion Roberto "Cyborg" Abreu. Fight Sport's competition team includes a number of jiu-jitsu's top competitors, including many ADCC qualifiers. Fight Sports is also an internationally known Jiu Jitsu affiliation with 32 schools worldwide.
mgt: Fight Sports is a Brazilian jiu-jitsu (BJJ) academy and team founded in 2011 in Miami, Florida, by six-time World No-Gi and ADCC champion Roberto "Cyborg" Abreu. The organization has grown to include over 30 affiliates across four continents. While known for its competitive success, Fight Sports has faced controversy over its handling of sexual abuse allegations.

trgt:
Osterheide is an unincorporated area in the district of Heidekreis, in Lower Saxony, Germany.

The 177.99 square kilometres (68.72 sq mi) area has 2,463 inhabitants (as at 31 December 2020). Its administrative seat is the village of Oerbke; other villages are Ostenholz and Wense. The districts of Osterheide and Lohheide cover the Bergen-Hohne Military Training Area.
mgt: Osterheide is an unincorporated area in the Heidekreis district of Lower Saxony, Germany, covering 177.99 km² with a population of 4,437 as of 2022. Established in 1945 after the division of the Bergen Area Estate, it includes the NATO Bergen-Hohne training ranges. Its administration is governed by federal regulations for unincorporated areas.

trgt: In computational geometry, a bitonic tour of a set of point sites in the Euclidean plane is a closed polygonal chain that has each site as one of its vertices, such that any vertical line crosses the chain at most twice.
mgt: A bitonic tour is a specific type of traveling salesman tour in which the points are visited in a strictly left-to-right and right-to-left order. The optimal bitonic tour minimizes the total length of such a path and can be computed using a dynamic programming algorithm. The problem is often attributed to Jon L. Bentley but was first described in the 1990 textbook *Introduction to Algorithms*. The optimal bitonic tour has no self-crossings and can serve as an optimal traveling salesperson tour under certain conditions. Variants of the problem and related optimization criteria have been explored, including applications in algorithmic contests.

trgt: The divisions of Punjab (Lang-ur), are the first-order administrative bodies of the Punjab Province of Pakistan. In total, there are 10 divisions, which are further divided into districts ranging from three to six per division, depending upon area. Divisions are governed by Commissioners while districts are governed by Deputy Commissioners.
mgt: The divisions of Punjab, Pakistan, are the first-level administrative units of the province, currently numbering 10 as of 2022. Originating during the Mughal and British eras, divisions were restructured post-independence, with their number gradually increasing. Each division is overseen by a commissioner and further subdivided into districts, tehsils, and union councils.

page_title: Up the Elephant and Round the Castle
trgt: Up the Elephant and Round the Castle is a British television sitcom, which aired from 1983 to 1985, and was produced by Thames Television for the ITV network. Starring comedian Jim Davidson, who played the role of Jim London, the show spawned a sequel, Home James!, which was also made by Thames. Home James ran from 1987 to 1990.
mgt: *Up the Elephant and Round the Castle* is a British sitcom that aired on ITV from 30 November 1983 to 7 November 1985. Starring Jim Davidson, the series follows Jim London, a mischievous young man who inherits a dilapidated Victorian house in south London and navigates comedic misadventures involving the police, drinking, and women. The show ran for three series with 22 episodes and featured a theme tune composed by Keith Emerson. Produced by Thames Television, it was later released on DVD, with the complete series reissued in 2016.

trgt: Potamia (Greek: Ποταμιά, Turkish: Potamya, Bodamya and Dereliköy) is a village in the north-east of Cyprus in the district of Nicosia, close to the Green Line separating it from the area of the breakaway, unrecognised Northern Cyprus. Together with Pyla and Rizokarpaso, Potamia forms one of the few remaining ethnically mixed communities in Cyprus, made up of Greek and Turkish Cypriots.
mgt: Potamia (Greek: Ποταμιά, Turkish: Potamya) is a village in the Nicosia District of Cyprus, notable for its history of coexistence between Greek and Turkish Cypriots. Once a royal residence of the Lusignan Dynasty, it was home to Queen Catherine Cornaro and features the remains of a palace. The village promotes bicommunal cooperation, exemplified by its bilingual radio station, Radio Potamia.

trgt: Woodford Island is an inland island in the Far-North Coast of New South Wales, Australia. It is formed by the Clarence River where it splits into the South Arm and North Arm at the small village of Brushgrove then reforms at the town of Maclean. The island is the largest in NSW, either inland or offshore, at 37 km2.
mgt: Woodford Island is a river island located in the Clarence River in New South Wales, Australia. It is the largest inland island in the Southern Hemisphere and home to approximately 300 residents. The island features a mix of floodplains and a central mountain ridge, supporting industries like sugarcane farming and cattle production. Historically significant, it was an early European settlement and a hub for timber and agriculture. Key access points include two bridges and a ferry, and notable sites include Brushgrove, Ilarwill, and the Maclean District Golf Course.

trgt: Erik Rauch (May 15, 1974 – July 13, 2005) was an American biophysicist and theoretical ecologist who worked at NECSI, MIT, Santa Fe Institute, Yale University, Princeton University, and other institutions. Rauch's most notable paper was published in Nature and concerned the mathematical modeling of the conservation of biodiversity.
mgt: Erik Rauch (May 15, 1974 – July 13, 2005) was an American biophysicist, theoretical ecologist, and entrepreneur. He earned degrees from Yale University and MIT, where his research focused on evolving systems. Rauch co-founded MetaCarta and contributed to spatial information processing. He died in a hiking accident at age 31.

trgt: The Heian-jingu Shrine (平安神宮, Heian-jingū) is a Shinto shrine located in Sakyō-ku, Kyoto, Japan. The Shrine is ranked as a Beppyō Jinja (別表神社) (the top rank for shrines) by the Association of Shinto Shrines. It is listed as an important cultural property of Japan.
mgt: Heian Shrine (平安神宮, Heian-jingū) is a Shinto shrine in Kyoto, Japan, established in 1895 to commemorate the 1100th anniversary of Heian-kyō, the former capital of Japan. Dedicated to Emperor Kanmu and later Emperor Kōmei, the shrine features a partial reproduction of the Heian Palace at 5/8 scale, designed by Itō Chūta. Its architecture reflects the Heian Period, including one of Japan's largest torii gates. The shrine's expansive garden, created by Ogawa Jihei VII, includes ponds fed by the Lake Biwa Canal and supports rare species. Heian Shrine hosts major festivals, including the Jidai Matsuri, and serves as a venue for traditional weddings and cultural events.

trgt:
The Black Country derby is the local derby between English football teams West Bromwich Albion and Wolverhampton Wanderers, eleven miles (18 km) apart in the Black Country of the West Midlands.

The most successful side in the Black Country derby is West Bromwich Albion, losing just three of the past 23 matches between the two sides. Wolves are currently in the Premier League, and West Brom in the EFL Championship following their relegation in 2021.
mgt: The Black Country derby is a football rivalry between West Bromwich Albion and Wolverhampton Wanderers, two of the oldest clubs in English football, based in the Black Country region of England. First contested in 1883, the derby has been played 162 times, with Albion leading in victories. The rivalry peaked in the 1950s when both clubs competed at the top of English football. Matches are known for their intensity, passionate fanbases, and occasional hooliganism.

trgt: Soul Power is the debut studio album by American singer Curtis Harding, released May 6, 2014, by Burger Records. It encompasses a wide breadth of genres, though it is primarily inspired by soul music and his mother's gospel singing. The album received generally favorable reviews from music critics.
mgt: *Soul Power* is the debut studio album by American musician Curtis Harding, released on May 6, 2014, by Burger Records. Recorded at Living Room Studios in Atlanta, the album blends soul with influences from gospel, indie rock, garage, and disco. Harding's versatile vocals and genre-spanning compositions received critical praise, though some critics noted a lack of innovation.

trgt: The 2004 New Zealand rugby league season was the 97th season of rugby league that had been played in New Zealand. The main feature of the year was the fifth season of the Bartercard Cup competition that was run by the New Zealand Rugby League. The Mt Albert Lions won the Cup by defeating the Marist Richmond Brothers 40–20 in the Grand Final.
mgt: The 2004 New Zealand rugby league season featured domestic, international, and regional competitions. The New Zealand national team participated in the Tri-Nations tournament, finishing third, and hosted the Pacific Cup. Domestically, the Bartercard Cup saw team changes, with the Canterbury Bulls making the playoffs. The New Zealand Warriors struggled in the NRL, finishing 14th, while regional titles were claimed by Mt Albert Lions, Riccarton, and Ngongotaha Chiefs.

page_title: Committee of the Verkhovna Rada on issues of ecological policy
trgt: The Committee of the Verkhovna Rada of Ukraine on Environmental Policy, Nature Resources Utilization and Elimination of the Consequences of Chornobyl Catastrophe (Lang-uk, Komitet Verkhovnoi Rady Ukrainy z pytan’ ekolohichnoi polityky, pryrodokorystuvannia ta likvidatsii naslidkiv Chornobyl’s’koi katastrofy) is a standing committee of the Verkhovna Rada, Ukraine's unicameral parliament. The committee consists of 15 people's deputies in the parliament's 8th convocation. Its entire composition was approved on December 4, 2014.
mgt: The Committee of the Verkhovna Rada of Ukraine on Environmental Policy, Nature Resources Utilization, and Elimination of the Consequences of the Chornobyl Catastrophe is a parliamentary body established on June 28, 1990. It oversees Ukraine's environmental policies, natural resource management, and disaster response, including Chornobyl. The committee has played a key role in Ukraine's environmental legislation and international commitments, such as ratifying the UN Framework Convention on Climate Change in 1996.

trgt: Menodora is a genus of perennial plants and shrubs in the olive family Oleaceae. Its 23 species (as per Green 2003) are found in the temperate Americas and in southern Africa. These are uniform species of deserts and arid grasslands or savannas.
mgt: *Menodora* is a genus of flowering plants in the family Oleaceae, characterized by bisexual flowers with bright yellow or white corollas and bilobed fruit capsules. The genus, named from the Greek words for "force" and "gift," was first described in 1809 in Mexico. It exhibits a unique amphitropical distribution, with species found in the Americas and Africa, likely due to long-distance dispersal rather than continental drift. Taxonomically, *Menodora longiflora* was once proposed as a separate genus but is now included within *Menodora* based on morphological and molecular evidence.

trgt:
Lloyd John Quinan (born 1957) is a Scottish broadcaster and pro-independence politician. He was a Scottish National Party (SNP) Member of the Scottish Parliament (MSP) for the West of Scotland region from 1999 to 2003.

Born and raised in Edinburgh, Quinan is a longtime campaigner for Scottish independence, initially joining the Scottish National Party in 1974, later leaving in 2003. In 2015 the SNP selected him as a list candidate for the Lothian region.
mgt: Lloyd Quinan (born 1957) is a Scottish actor, television presenter, director, and former politician. He served as a Member of the Scottish Parliament (MSP) for the West of Scotland region from 1999 to 2003 as a member of the Scottish National Party (SNP). Before his political career, Quinan worked extensively in theatre and television, including as a presenter for STV. An active campaigner, he was involved in anti-nuclear protests and supported Scottish independence.

trgt:
Hunsrückisch is a German dialect spoken in the Hunsrück region of Germany (Rhineland-Palatinate). This mountainous region of Germany has long been an exporter of emigrants to Brazil, United States, Canada, Australia and other parts of the world.

Hunsrückisch was spoken in Edgar Reitz's acclaimed television series Heimat.
mgt: Hunsrückisch is a German dialect spoken primarily in the Hunsrück region of Germany and by descendants of German immigrants in southern Brazil, where it is known as Hunsrik. The dialect lacks a standardized form and varies significantly across localities. In Germany, it is influenced by historical regional divisions and neighboring French, while in Brazil, it has incorporated elements from Portuguese, Amerindian languages, and other German dialects. Hunsrückisch features unique phonological, grammatical, and lexical characteristics, including variations in gender, tenses, and plural formation. Efforts to document and preserve the dialect continue in both Germany and Brazil.

trgt: Lygodium microphyllum (commonly known as, variously, climbing maidenhair fern, Old World climbing fern, small-leaf climbing fern, or snake fern) is a climbing fern originating in tropical Africa, Southeast Asia, Melanesia and Australia. It is an invasive weed in Florida where it invades open forest and wetland areas. The type specimen was collected in the vicinity of Nabúa, on the island of Luzon in the Philippines by Luis Née.
mgt: Lygodium microphyllum, commonly known as Old World climbing fern, is a species of fern in the family Lygodiaceae. Native to tropical Africa, Asia, Australia, and Pacific islands, it has become invasive in regions like the Caribbean and southeastern United States, where it damages ecosystems by outcompeting native plants and altering fire dynamics. Efforts to control its spread include the use of biological agents such as insects and mites.

trgt: Brian Edward O'Connor (born February 14, 1953), known professionally as Brian Brucker O'Connor or Brian O'Connor, is an American actor, comedian and guidance counselor. His best known roles include Biddle in Beverly Hills Cop II (1987) and Schemer on Shining Time Station (1989–1993). O’Connor briefly reprised his role as Schemer in a 2020 parody of Thomas and the Magic Railroad entitled “The Magic Railroad Parody” which aired exclusively on YouTube.
mgt: Brian Edward O'Connor (born February 14, 1953) is an American actor, comedian, and guidance counselor, best known for his role as Horace Schemer on the children's television series *Shining Time Station* (1989–1993). A graduate of the University of Massachusetts, he co-founded the comedy troupe Slap Happy and has appeared in films such as *Beverly Hills Cop II* (1987). O'Connor later became a guidance counselor and performs with the band The 6660s.

trgt: KushCo Holdings is an American company that sells packaging, containers, and other ancillary products for the cannabis industry. It is based in Garden Grove, California and is publicly traded on the New York OTCQB marketplace under the stock ticker symbol, KSHB.
mgt: KushCo Holdings, formerly known as Kush Bottles, is a publicly traded company (OTCQB: KSHB) founded in 2010 by Nicholas Kovacevich and Dallas Imbimbo in Santa Ana, California. Specializing in cannabis-related packaging and ancillary products, the company has expanded through acquisitions, including Dank Bottles, CMP Wellness, and Summit Innovations. KushCo played a key role in advocating for child-resistant cannabis packaging regulations and launched an eCommerce platform in 2015. By 2017, it had sold over 100 million products and served 4,000 cannabis clients. The company continues to grow, entering the Cannabidiol (CBD) market and expanding its distribution network.

trgt: Qi Xin (simplified Chinese: 齐心; traditional Chinese: 齊心; born November 3, 1926) is a Chinese author and member of the Chinese Communist Party (CCP), who wrote various articles on her husband, Chinese communist revolutionary Xi Zhongxun. She is the mother of Xi Jinping, current General Secretary of the Chinese Communist Party, also known as the paramount leader.
mgt: Qi Xin (born November 3, 1926) is a Chinese Communist revolutionary and the widow of Xi Zhongxun, a prominent Chinese Communist Party (CCP) leader. She played an active role in the Second Sino-Japanese War and later worked in education and consultancy roles within the CCP. Qi is the mother of Xi Jinping, the current General Secretary of the CCP.

trgt: Uriah Francis Abshier (September 17, 1849 – May 23, 1934) was a state legislator from the state of Oregon. He served one two-year term in the Oregon House of Representatives as a Republican legislator, representing a large rural district in south-central Oregon. He was the postmaster for Silver Lake, Oregon and owned ranch property near that town. Later, he ran a hardware store in Centerville, Washington.
mgt: Uriah F. Abshier (September 17, 1849 – May 23, 1934) was an American politician, businessman, and farmer. He served as a Republican member of the Oregon House of Representatives from 1885 to 1886, representing Lake and Klamath counties. Abshier was also active in local politics, serving as Lake County commissioner, assessor, and postmaster. Later, he became a successful hardware store owner in Washington and a fruit farmer in California.

trgt: UFOria is a science fiction comedy film written and directed by John Binder and starring Fred Ward, Harry Dean Stanton, Harry Carey, Jr. and Cindy Williams. The film includes small appearances by Peggy McKay, Joe Unger, Hank Worden, Charlotte Stewart, and Alan Beckwith. Filming was completed in 1981, but the film was not theatrically released until 1985. It was released only on VHS in 1987 by MCA Home Video, now known as Universal Studios Home Entertainment.
mgt: *UFOria* is a 1984 comedy film written and directed by John Binder, starring Fred Ward, Cindy Williams, and Harry Dean Stanton. The story follows a drifter who falls in love with a UFO-obsessed supermarket clerk while assisting a faith-healing con man. Despite production challenges and limited theatrical release, the film garnered positive reviews, with critics praising its originality and performances. It was later released on VHS in 1987 but has not been issued on DVD or Blu-ray due to music licensing costs.

trgt: The Multi Fibre Arrangement (MFA) governed the world trade in textiles and garments from 1974 through 1994, imposing quotas on the amount developing countries could export to developed countries. Its successor, the Agreement on Textiles and Clothing (ATC), expired on 1 January 2005.
mgt: The Multi Fibre Arrangement (MFA) was an international trade agreement established in 1974 to regulate the global textile and clothing industry. It aimed to manage imports from developing countries to developed nations through quotas, addressing the competitive advantage of low labor costs in developing economies. While the MFA restricted exports from many developing countries, it also facilitated growth in nations like Bangladesh, which benefited from preferential treatment. The agreement was phased out under the World Trade Organization's Agreement on Textiles and Clothing, with quotas ending in 2005. This led to significant shifts in global trade, including increased competition from China and challenges for smaller economies.

trgt: Barbara Keiler (born April 7, 1953 in New York City, United States), known more widely by her pseudonym Judith Arnold, is a best-selling American author of crime fiction and over eighty-five romance novels. She has been writing since 1983, and has also been published under the pen names Ariel Berk and Thea Frederick.
mgt: Judith Arnold is the pen name of Barbara Keiler (born April 7, 1953), an American romance novelist who has written over 85 novels since 1983. A three-time Romantic Times award-winner and former president of Novelists, Inc., she has also been nominated for the RITA Award and the RWA Lifetime Achievement Award.

trgt: The Maquis des Glières was a Free French Resistance group, which fought against the 1940–1944 German occupation of France in World War II. The name is also given to the military conflict that opposed Resistance fighters to German, Vichy and Milice forces.
mgt: The Maquis des Glières was a French Resistance group active during World War II in the Haute-Savoie region. From January to March 1944, approximately 450 maquisards, led by Lieutenant Tom Morel and later Captain Maurice Anjot, resisted German and Vichy French forces on the Glières Plateau. Despite being outnumbered and enduring harsh conditions, they collected Allied parachute drops and carried out sabotage operations. The resistance was ultimately crushed by a German offensive on March 26, 1944, resulting in heavy casualties. However, their efforts became a propaganda victory, boosting the French Resistance.

trgt: 2002 Euler is a stony background asteroid from the inner regions of the asteroid belt, approximately 17 kilometers (11 miles) in diameter. It was discovered on 29 August 1973, by Russian astronomer Tamara Smirnova at the Crimean Astrophysical Observatory in Nauchnyj, and assigned the prov. designation 1973 QQ1. It was named after Swiss mathematician Leonhard Euler.
mgt: (2002) Euler is a stony asteroid in the inner region of the main asteroid belt, discovered on August 29, 1973, by Tamara Smirnova at the Crimean Astrophysical Observatory. Named after Swiss mathematician and astronomer Leonhard Euler, it measures approximately 14.5–19.8 kilometers in diameter and orbits the Sun every 3.76 years with low eccentricity.

trgt: Martina Carraro (born 21 June 1993) is an Italian swimmer. She competed at the 2020 Summer Olympics, in 100 m breaststroke and 200 m breaststroke. She was European champion in short course in the 100 m breaststroke at the 2019 European Short Course Swimming Championships in Glasgow, Scotland.
mgt: Martina Carraro (born 21 June 1993) is an Italian competitive swimmer specializing in breaststroke. She has represented Italy at multiple international competitions, including the 2016 and 2020 Summer Olympics. Carraro has won numerous medals, including a bronze at the 2019 World Championships and several European Championship podium finishes.

trgt: "Love Song" is the fourteenth single by Japanese rock band Luna Sea, released on November 8, 2000. It turned out to be the band's last single, as they held a press conference on the day of its release and announced they would be disbanding. They later reunited in 2010, and released "The One -Crash to Create-" in 2012. "Love Song" reached number 4 on the Oricon Singles Chart, and charted for five weeks.
mgt: "Love Song" is a single by Japanese rock band Luna Sea, released on November 8, 2000. Written primarily by Sugizo, the song features an acoustic foundation with a band arrangement and guitar solo. Released during the *Lunacy* recording period but excluded from the album, its release coincided with the band's announcement of their disbandment. The single includes two B-sides, "Into the Sun" and "Until the Day I Die," and its music video was directed by Shūichi Tan.

trgt: The Parapetí River is a river in Bolivia that has its source on the eastern side the Andes, flows through the Gran Chaco of Bolivia and ends in the marshes of the Bañados de Izozog depression. The drainage basin is 25,300 km2, and with the inclusion of the Bañados of Izozog is 61,903 km2.
mgt: The Parapetí River is a 500 km (310 mi) long river in Bolivia, originating in the Cordillera Central and flowing into the Bañados del Izozog wetlands in the Gran Chaco region. These wetlands, primarily fed by the river, are internationally protected under the Ramsar Convention and partially included in the Kaa-Iya del Gran Chaco National Park.

trgt: Holy Saviour Monastery of Julfa (Lang-hy) was an Armenian Apostolic monastery, located north-west of Jugha cemetery at the end of the gorge, 3 km away from the cemetery, on that slope of a high mountain. In the southern part of the monastery, at the foot of the Magharda mountain range, the Araks River flows.
mgt: The Holy Saviour Monastery of Julfa (Armenian: Սուրբ Ամենափրկիչ վանք) was an Armenian Apostolic monastery located in Jugha, Nakhichevan, dating back to the 9th–10th centuries. Believed to have been founded no later than the 10th century, it was destroyed between 1997 and 2003, along with its adjacent cemetery containing 47 tombstones.

trgt: Orange Roses, the second album by English singer-songwriter Rebecca Hollweg, was released on 10 March 2008 on Emu Records. Its songs have a country tinge. Produced in London by Andy Hamill and mixed by Brad Nunn of 4 Hero, it features the same musicians as on Rebecca Hollweg's debut album June Babies and a sleeve design by Jeb Loy Nichols.
mgt: *Orange Roses* is the second studio album by singer-songwriter Rebecca Hollweg, released on 10 March 2008 through Emu Records. Produced by Andy Hamill, the album blends folk, jazz, pop, and country influences. It received critical acclaim, with reviewers praising its songwriting and emotional depth, particularly the tracks "These Are My Tears" and "Orange Roses."

trgt:
W. & J. Sloane was a luxury furniture and rug store in New York City that catered to the prominent, including the White House and the Breakers, and wealthy, including the Rockefeller, Whitney, and Vanderbilt families.

After a long period of prosperity and prominence, the firm went through a short period of decline and was forced to file for bankruptcy in 1985.
mgt: W. & J. Sloane was a prominent American retail company specializing in rugs, furniture, and home furnishings, founded in 1843 by William Sloane in New York City. Renowned for introducing oriental carpets to the U.S. and setting national decorating trends, it served elite clients, including the White House. After decades of success, the company declined following its 1955 acquisition, filing for bankruptcy in 1985.

trgt:
CCM is a Canadian bicycle manufacturer based in Montreal, Quebec.

"CCM" was originally an initialism for Canada Cycle & Motor Co. Ltd. However, the company would eventually split into two separate entities both maintaining the CCM trademark, one maintaining bicycle manufacturing and the other, CCM (The Hockey Company), producing hockey equipment.
mgt: CCM (Canada Cycle & Motor Co. Ltd.) was a Canadian bicycle manufacturer founded in 1899 through the merger of four major Canadian bicycle companies. Based in Weston, Ontario, and later Montreal, CCM became a dominant force in Canadian bicycle production, accounting for 85% of the market at its peak. The company also diversified into motor vehicles, establishing the Russell Motor Car Company in 1904. During World War II, CCM produced bicycles for the Canadian military. Despite its success, financial struggles led to bankruptcy in 1983, after which its assets were acquired by Procycle Group Inc. Today, CCM-branded bicycles are sold in Canada, and the company is remembered for its iconic models like the Flyte, Mustang Marauder, and Targa.

trgt: Peredur (IPA-cy, Old Welsh Peretur) is the name of a number of men from the boundaries of history and legend in sub-Roman Britain. The Peredur who is most familiar to a modern audience is the character who made his entrance as a knight in the Arthurian world of Middle Welsh prose literature.
mgt: Peredur is a figure from early Welsh tradition, associated with the Hen Ogledd (Old North) and celebrated as a hero in the 6th-century Battle of Arfderydd alongside his brother Gwrgi. He appears in historical sources like the *Annales Cambriae* and Welsh Triads, which recount his lineage, battles, and tragic death. Peredur is also featured in early Welsh poetry, Geoffrey of Monmouth's works, and the Middle Welsh romance *Peredur son of Efrawg*, where he is reimagined as an Arthurian knight akin to Percival.

trgt: "Take Me for a Ride" is a song recorded by British group Steps, released in July 2021 as the second single from their seventh studio album, What the Future Holds Pt. 2 (2021). The single peaked at number 27 on UK Download chart.
mgt: "Take Me for a Ride" is a 2021 electropop single by British group Steps, released on July 29, 2021, as part of their album *What the Future Holds Pt. 2*. The song explores themes of gaslighting in relationships and marked a darker, more modern direction for the band. Its music video, directed by Jordan Rossi and featuring choreography by Mark Jennings, was released on August 26, 2021, showcasing a fresh, contemporary style while maintaining Steps' signature essence.

trgt: Benedictine College Preparatory is a private Roman Catholic military high school in Goochland, Virginia. It is owned and operated by the Benedictine Society of Virginia, part of the American-Cassinese Congregation. Benedictine offers education through a private military institute model, which has long been a traditional form of education for young men in Virginia.
mgt: Benedictine College Preparatory is a private, all-boys Roman Catholic military high school in Richmond, Virginia, founded in 1911 by Benedictine monks. Known for its rigorous academics, military leadership program, and athletics, the school relocated to Goochland County in 2013. Its alumni include notable athletes, such as NFL and NBA players, and an Olympic gold medalist.

trgt: The Fairmont Norfolk is a historic hotel in Nairobi, Kenya, and is managed by the luxury hotel chain Fairmont Hotels and Resorts. The hotel has 170 rooms and three restaurants, and consists of five main blocks, each ranging from 1 to 2 stories high. The hotel has a signature Tudor style of architecture that has been maintained since its original construction.
mgt: Fairmont The Norfolk Hotel, located on Harry Thuku Road in Nairobi, Kenya, is a historic luxury hotel that opened on December 25, 1904. Built by Maj. C.G.R. Ringer and R. Aylmer Winearls, it served as a refuge for early settlers and a base for explorers and hunters. Over the years, it has hosted notable figures such as Theodore Roosevelt, Kofi Annan, and Meryl Streep. The hotel has undergone significant expansions and renovations, including rebuilding after a bombing in 1980. Now managed by Fairmont Hotels and Resorts, it features 170 rooms, five dining outlets, and various facilities, including a health center and tropical gardens.

trgt: "Imagine" is a song performed by Norwegian singer Tone Damli as a duet with Eric Saade. The song is from her fifth studio album Looking Back (2012). It was written per request by and features vocals from Swedish singer Eric Saade. It was released in Norway as a digital download on 27 April 2012.
mgt: "Imagine" is a 2012 pop single by Norwegian singer Tone Damli featuring Swedish artist Eric Saade, from Damli's album *Looking Back*. Released on April 27, 2012, the song was written by Lukasz Duchnowski, Jason Gill, Leslie Tay, Eric Saade, and Julimar Santos. Its music video, filmed in Ibiza, depicts a love story between the two artists. Damli and Saade performed the song on Norwegian television and during the VG Lista 2012 tour.

trgt: Cape Newenham LRRS Airport (IATA: EHM, ICAO: PAEH, FAA LID: EHM) is a military airstrip located one nautical mile (1.2 miles; 1.9 km) southeast of Cape Newenham, in the Dillingham Census Area of the U.S. state of Alaska. It is not open for public use.
mgt: Cape Newenham LRRS Airport (IATA: EHM, ICAO: PAEH) is a United States Air Force military airstrip located in Cape Newenham, Alaska. Built in 1951, it supports the Cape Newenham Long Range Radar Station, providing access for maintenance personnel and supplies. The gravel runway measures 3,950 by 150 feet.

trgt:
Our Revolution: A Future to Believe In is a book by U.S. Senator from Vermont Bernie Sanders, published by Thomas Dunne Books in November 2016.

It was released on November 15, 2016, a week after the election of Donald Trump. The book was written in the context of Sanders's 2016 presidential campaign and aimed to explain some of its rationale.
mgt: *Our Revolution: A Future to Believe In* is a 2016 book by U.S. Senator Bernie Sanders, detailing his 2016 presidential campaign and progressive political agenda. The memoir addresses issues such as climate change, income inequality, free education, and healthcare reform, while recounting his campaign's challenges and successes. The book became a bestseller and received critical acclaim, including a Grammy nomination for its audiobook.

trgt:
Final Account is a 2020 German-language documentary film directed and produced by Luke Holland, who died shortly after post-production was completed on June 10, 2020. The film follows the last living generation of German participants in Adolf Hitler's Third Reich.

Final Account premiered at the 77th Venice International Film Festival on September 2, 2020. It was released on May 21, 2021, by Focus Features.
mgt: *Final Account* is a 2020 documentary film directed by Luke Holland, exploring the memories and personal reflections of former members and witnesses of Adolf Hitler's Third Reich. Through over 300 interviews conducted over a decade, the film examines themes of complicity, responsibility, and national identity in relation to Nazi war crimes. Premiering at the Venice International Film Festival, it received critical acclaim for its chilling and thought-provoking content.

trgt: Michael Van Wijk (born 30 September 1952) is an Indian-born English bodybuilder, professional sportsperson, actor and TV presenter. He is most well known for his role as Wolf on British TV endurance sports game show Gladiators, a role he held for all its eight series (1992–1999).
mgt: Michael Van Wijk (born 30 September 1952) is an English bodybuilder, actor, and former television personality, best known for his role as "Wolf" on the British TV series *Gladiators*. He ran gyms in London, later moved to New Zealand, and competed in Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu.

trgt: Elections were held in the Australian state of Queensland on 19 May 1956 to elect the 75 members of the state's Legislative Assembly. The Labor government was seeking its ninth continuous term in office since the 1932 election; it would be Vince Gair's second election as Premier.
mgt: The 1956 Queensland state election was held on 19 May 1956 to elect all 75 members of the Legislative Assembly. The Labor Party, led by Vince Gair, retained power. However, internal party conflicts led to Gair's expulsion in 1957, triggering political realignments and an early election.

trgt: The Kokerboom–Auas High Voltage Power Line, also Keetmanshoop–Dordabis High Voltage Power Line, is a high voltage electricity power line, under construction, connecting the high voltage substation at Kokerboom, near Keetmanshoop, in the ǁKaras Region to another high voltage substation at Auas, near Dordabis, in the Khomas Region of Namibia.
mgt: The Kokerboom–Auas High Voltage Power Line is a 400kV, 800-kilometer (500-mile) transmission line under construction in Namibia. Developed by NamPower, it connects Kokerboom in the ǁKaras Region to Auas near Windhoek, traversing three regions. Scheduled for completion in 2024, it aims to enhance grid reliability and support power imports and exports.

trgt:
William Le Baron Jenney (September 25, 1832 – June 14, 1907) was an American architect and engineer who is known for building the first skyscraper in 1884.

In 1998, Jenney was ranked number 89 in the book 1,000 Years, 1,000 People: Ranking the Men and Women Who Shaped the Millennium.
mgt: William Le Baron Jenney (September 25, 1832 – June 15, 1907) was an American architect and engineer, widely regarded as the "Father of the Skyscraper." He designed the Home Insurance Building in Chicago (1884–1885), the first fully metal-framed skyscraper. Jenney's innovations in steel-frame construction revolutionized architecture, enabling the development of taller buildings. Educated at École Centrale Paris, he applied engineering principles to architecture and influenced the Chicago School. Jenney also taught future architectural leaders, including Louis Sullivan and Daniel Burnham. His legacy includes significant contributions to urban planning and modern architecture.

page_title: American Association of Colleges of Nursing
trgt: The American Association of Colleges of Nursing (AACN) is a national organization of nurses in the United States that is dedicated to advancing nursing education. It was established in 1969, and represents nursing schools at 840 universities and colleges in the United States.
mgt: The American Association of Colleges of Nursing (AACN), founded in 1969, is a national organization headquartered in Washington, D.C., representing 840 member institutions. It provides scholarships for nursing students, promotes academic nursing leadership, and supports healthcare improvement initiatives.

trgt: Theodorick Bland (December 2, 1708 – 1784), also known as Theodorick Bland, Sr. or Theodorick Bland of Cawsons, was Virginia planter who served as a member of the first Virginia Senate, as well as a militia officer and clerk of Prince George County, Virginia.
mgt: Theodorick Bland (December 2, 1708 – June 1, 1784) was an American planter, politician, and member of the Virginia Senate from 1776 to 1779, representing Isle of Wight, Surry, and Prince George Counties. A member of the prominent Bland and Randolph families, he managed several plantations, including Kippax and Cawsons, and later moved to Amelia County. Bland played a role in supporting the American Revolution, including raising funds to replace gunpowder seized by British forces. He was also a militia colonel and descended from Theodorick Bland of Westover, a notable early Virginian.

trgt: Douglas A. Keszler is a distinguished professor in the Department of Chemistry at Oregon State University, adjunct professor in the Physics Department at OSU and adjunct professor in the Department of Chemistry at University of Oregon. He is also the director of the Center for Sustainable Materials Chemistry, and a member of the Oregon Nanoscience and Microtechnologies Institute (ONAMI) leadership team.
mgt: Douglas Keszler is an American chemist known for his contributions to materials and inorganic chemistry. He earned his BS from Southwestern Oklahoma State University in 1979 and his PhD from Northwestern University in 1984 under James A. Ibers. A distinguished professor at Oregon State University since 2006, Keszler's research focuses on inorganic materials for next-generation electronic and energy devices, including advancements in aqueous-based synthesis and thin-film technologies.

trgt: Alexis Cohen (October 17, 1983 – July 5, 2009) was a two-time reality TV show contestant and singer on American Idol who directed an expletive-filled televised rant at the show's judges after comparing her singing style to vocalists Grace Slick, Janis Joplin, and Pat Benatar. She was called "Glitter Girl" in the media.
mgt: Alexis Erica Cohen (October 17, 1983 – July 5, 2009) was an American singer and aspiring veterinarian best known for her appearances on *American Idol* in 2008 and 2009. She gained attention for her outspoken reactions to criticism from the judges. Cohen died in a hit-and-run incident in Seaside Heights, New Jersey, in 2009.

trgt: The Ruben M. Benjamin House is a house in Bloomington, Illinois. It is a two-story rectangular building, styled in the Classical Revival architectural motif. It was built in 1856 John L. Routt, who would become the first governor of Colorado. Ruben M. Benjamin, an attorney known for litigation relating to railroad regulation, lived in the home for more than 60 years. The United States National Register of Historic Places added the Ruben M. Benjamin House in August 1978.
mgt: The Ruben M. Benjamin House, located at 510 E. Grove Street in Bloomington, Illinois, is a historic home built in 1856 by John L. Routt, who later became Colorado's first governor. Designed in the Classical Revival style, the house remains largely unchanged since the 19th century. It was the residence of prominent attorney Ruben M. Benjamin, known for his role in the Granger cases and advocating for government regulation of private enterprises. The house was added to the National Register of Historic Places in 1978.

trgt: Shade is a 2003 American neo-noir crime thriller film directed and written by Damian Nieman and starring Stuart Townsend, Gabriel Byrne, Thandiwe Newton, Jamie Foxx, Melanie Griffith and Sylvester Stallone. The film follows a trio of grifters who attempt to set up a legendary card shark nicknamed "The Dean". The film had a limited release in the United States on May 7, 2004.
mgt: *Shade* is a 2003 American neo-noir crime thriller film written and directed by Damian Nieman. Featuring an ensemble cast including Stuart Townsend, Gabriel Byrne, Thandiwe Newton, Jamie Foxx, and Sylvester Stallone, the film follows a group of hustlers navigating high-stakes poker games and elaborate cons. Premiering at the CineVegas International Film Festival, it had a limited theatrical release in 2004, grossing $458,144. Despite mixed reviews, it holds a 67% rating on Rotten Tomatoes.

trgt:
Janice Charette is a Canadian public servant serving as the clerk of the Privy Council and secretary to the Cabinet. She previously held the role from 2014 to 2016, before being appointed as the Canadian high commissioner to the United Kingdom in 2016.

She has been recognized as one of Canada’s Top 100 Most Powerful Women in 2013, 2014, and 2015.
mgt: Janice Charette is a Canadian civil servant and diplomat currently serving as the Clerk of the Privy Council and Secretary to the Cabinet, a role she first held from 2014 to 2016 and resumed in 2021. She previously served as Canada’s High Commissioner to the United Kingdom from 2016 to 2021. Charette has held several senior public service roles and is active in volunteer work.

trgt: Katja Hessel (born 5 May 1972) is a German lawyer and politician of the Free Democratic Party (FDP) who has been serving as Parliamentary State Secretary in the Federal Ministry of Finance in the coalition government of Chancellor Olaf Scholz since 2021. She has been serving as a member of the Bundestag from the state of Bavaria since 2017.
mgt: Katja Hessel (born 5 May 1972) is a German lawyer, tax consultant, and politician of the Free Democratic Party (FDP). She has been a member of the Bundestag since 2017, representing the Nuremberg North district. Previously, she served as State Secretary in the Bavarian State Ministry of Economic Affairs from 2008 to 2013.

trgt: Lieutenant Colonel Thomas Bernard Hackett VC (15 June 1836 – 5 October 1880) was born in Riverstown, County Tipperary and was an Irish recipient of the Victoria Cross, the highest and most prestigious award for gallantry in the face of the enemy that can be awarded to British and Commonwealth forces.
mgt: Thomas Bernard Hackett VC (15 June 1836 – 5 October 1880) was an Irish-born British Army officer and recipient of the Victoria Cross (VC), the highest military decoration for valor in the British armed forces. He was awarded the VC for his bravery during the Indian Mutiny in 1857. Hackett later achieved the rank of lieutenant colonel and served in the Crimean War, Indian Mutiny, and Third Anglo-Ashanti War. He died in a firearm accident in 1880, and his VC is displayed at the Imperial War Museum in London.

trgt: Fishguard and Goodwick is the name of a community that wraps around Fishguard Bay, on the northern coast of Pembrokeshire, Wales. It comprises the two towns of Fishguard and Goodwick, with their neighbourhoods of Dyffryn, Harbour Village, Penyraber, Lower Town, and Stop-and-Call. Within the community are two railway stations and Goodwick Ferry Terminal, which is the terminus of the A40 London to Fishguard Trunk Road.
mgt: Fishguard and Goodwick (Welsh: Abergwaun ac Wdig) are twin towns in Pembrokeshire, Wales, with a combined population of 5,407. Situated on Fishguard Bay, the area features a rich maritime history, dramatic coastal landscapes, and significant conservation areas. Historically separate, the towns merged in 1934 to form a single urban district. Today, they are governed by Fishguard and Goodwick Town Council and are known for their ferry terminal connecting to Ireland and their geological and historical significance.

trgt: The Dickinson Micropolitan Statistical Area, as defined by the United States Census Bureau, is an area consisting of two counties in North Dakota, anchored by the city of Dickinson. As of the 2010 census, the μSA had a population of 24,982 (a July 1, 2019 estimate placed the population at 32,417).
mgt: The Dickinson, North Dakota micropolitan area, as defined by the United States Census Bureau, had a population of 23,524 as of the 2000 census. Predominantly White, the area had a median household income of $32,597 and a per capita income of $16,058, with 1.02% identifying as Hispanic or Latino.

trgt: "We Say Mabuhay" (sometimes called the "Mabuhay March") is the common name for "Mabuhay, My Philippines", a march used as the honors music for the President of the Philippines. It was composed by Tirso Cruz, with lyrics by James King Steele.
mgt: "We Say Mabuhay" is a Filipino honors music composed by Tirso Cruz with lyrics by James King Steele, first performed in 1931 at the Manila Hotel. It serves as the honors music for the President of the Philippines, traditionally played during presidential inaugurations and other official events. The song gained prominence during World War II when it was used in U.S. government broadcasts and propaganda. The title features the Tagalog word "Mabuhay," meaning "long live."

trgt: Sir Robin Buchanan Nicholson, FRS FREng (born 12 August 1934) is a British industrial metallurgist and academic, who served as Chief Scientific Adviser, Cabinet Office, from 1983 to 1985. He then joined the board of Rolls-Royce plc, where he served until 2005. He was also a non-executive board member of BP plc and Pilkington plc.
mgt: Robin Nicholson (born 1934) is a British metallurgist, academic, and government adviser. He served as Chief Scientific Adviser in the UK Cabinet Office from 1983 to 1985 and held prominent roles in academia, industry, and public policy. A Fellow of the Royal Society and the Royal Academy of Engineering, Nicholson contributed to metallurgy research, engineering policy, and corporate governance, holding directorships at major companies like Rolls-Royce and BP.

trgt: In molecular biology, this protein domain belongs to the terpene synthase family (TPS). Its role is to synthesize terpenes, which are part of primary metabolism, such as sterols and carotene, and also part of the secondary metabolism. This entry will focus on the C terminal domain of the TPS protein.
mgt: The terpene synthase C terminal domain is a protein domain involved in the biosynthesis of terpenoids, a large class of metabolic molecules. It catalyzes the cyclization of geranyl diphosphate, stabilizing reactive intermediates. Found in the terpene synthase family, it exhibits structural conservation and is classified into six phylogenetic subgroups.

trgt: Bolesław Karol Szymański (born 22 April 1950 in Pasłęk) is the Claire and Roland Schmitt Distinguished Professor at the Department of Computer Science and the Founding Head of the Center for Network Science and Technology, Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute. He's known for multiple contributions into computer science, including Szymański's algorithm.
mgt: Bolesław Szymański (born April 22, 1950) is a Polish-American computer scientist and the Claire and Roland Schmitt Distinguished Professor of Computer Science at Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute. Known for his contributions to parallel and distributed computing, he directs the Social Cognitive Networks Academic Research Center and has received numerous awards, including IEEE Fellow and the Wilkes Award.
