trgt: Rishta Ho To Aisa (transl. Relation Means This) is a 1992 Indian Hindi-language drama film, produced by M.M.C. Cooper on the Marco Enterprises banner and directed by Kalptaru. Starring Jeetendra, Rishi Kapoor, Juhi Chawla, Sujata Mehta and music composed by Laxmikant–Pyarelal.
mgt: *Rishta Ho To Aisa* is a 1992 Indian Hindi-language drama film directed by Kalptaru and produced by M.M.C. Cooper. Starring Jeetendra, Rishi Kapoor, Juhi Chawla, and Sujata Mehta, the film follows a family torn apart by misunderstandings, memory loss, and betrayal, ultimately reuniting through love and redemption. The film features music composed by Laxmikant–Pyarelal.

trgt: Société Belge des Transports par Air SA, known by its short form Sobelair, was a Belgian charter airline from that operated from 1946 to 2004. It was headquartered in Brussels (later in Zaventem) and operated mostly non-scheduled passenger and cargo flights out of Brussels Airport.
mgt: Sobelair was a Belgian charter airline founded on 30 July 1946, with operations based at Brussels Airport. Initially focused on flights to the Belgian Congo, it later specialized in holiday charters. A subsidiary of Sabena from 1949 to 2001, Sobelair modernized its fleet over the decades, operating aircraft such as the Boeing 737 and 767. Following Sabena's bankruptcy in 2001, Sobelair faced financial difficulties, briefly operating under SN Brussels Airlines before declaring bankruptcy on 19 January 2004, ceasing operations after nearly 60 years.

trgt: Lincoln Home National Historic Site preserves the Springfield, Illinois home and related historic district where Abraham Lincoln lived from 1844 to 1861, before becoming the 16th president of the United States. The presidential memorial includes the four blocks surrounding the home and a visitor center.
mgt: The Lincoln Home National Historic Site, located in Springfield, Illinois, preserves the only home ever owned by Abraham Lincoln, where he lived from 1844 to 1861. Designated a National Historic Site in 1971, the 12-acre area includes restored homes from Lincoln's neighborhood, offering insights into his life and times.

trgt: William Ross Bond (December 4, 1918 – April 1, 1970) was a United States Army brigadier general who was killed by an enemy sniper in 1970 while commanding the 199th Infantry Brigade in South Vietnam. General Bond also served in World War II with the Army Rangers and was a prisoner of war.
mgt: William Ross Bond (December 4, 1918 – April 1, 1970) was a United States Army brigadier general who served in World War II, the Korean War, and the Vietnam War. A decorated officer, he was awarded multiple honors, including the Distinguished Service Medal and Silver Star. Bond was killed in action in Vietnam during Operation Toan Thang IV.

trgt: "Cookie" is the third single recorded by South Korean girl group NewJeans from their debut extended play (EP) New Jeans. It was released as their debut single by ADOR ahead of the EP as one of the its lead singles on August 1, 2022.
mgt: "Cookie" is a song by South Korean girl group NewJeans, released on August 1, 2022, as the third single from their debut self-titled extended play. While praised for its production and groove, the song faced controversy over alleged sexual innuendos in its lyrics, which some deemed inappropriate for a group of minors. NewJeans' agency, ADOR, denied the allegations, asserting the lyrics were intended for fans and lacked sexual connotations. The group promoted the song during their debut stage on *M Countdown*.

trgt: HMS Fairy was a three-funnel, 30-knot destroyer of the First World War. One of three similar ships built by Fairfields for the Royal Navy, she was ordered under the 1896–1897 Naval Estimates and the sixth Royal Navy ship to carry this name. She was classified, along with other similar ships, as a C-class destroyer in 1913. She sank in 1918 from damage inflicted by ramming and sinking the German submarine UC-75.
mgt: HMS *Fairy* was a Fairfield three-funnel, 30-knot destroyer of the Royal Navy, launched in 1897 and commissioned in 1898. Initially serving with the Channel Fleet, she was later classified as a C-class destroyer in 1913. During World War I, *Fairy* was engaged in convoy escort duties. On 31 May 1918, she sank after ramming the German submarine SM UC-75 off Flamborough Head. She earned the battle honour "Belgian Coast 1914–17."

trgt: Rangi Ruru Girls'School is a New Zealand private girls' day and boarding secondary school located in Merivale, an inner suburb of Christchurch. The school is affiliated to the Presbyterian Church, and serves approximately 698 girls from Years 7 to 13 (ages 10 to 18).
mgt: Rangi Ruru Girls' School is an independent, single-sex girls' school in Merivale, Christchurch, New Zealand, offering intermediate and secondary education (Years 7–13) with boarding facilities. Founded in 1889 by the Gibson family, it was renamed "Rangi Ruru" in 1891, a name gifted by Māori chief Pāora Taki. The school moved to its current site in 1923 and has been governed by a Presbyterian Church-affiliated board since 1946. Known for its strong academic performance, Rangi Ruru consistently ranks among New Zealand's top schools.

trgt: Andreia Alexandra Norton (born 15 August 1996) is a Portuguese footballer who plays as a forward for Benfica in the Campeonato Nacional de Futebol Feminino. She first played for her country in an under nineteen match in 2012 and debuted for the senior team four years later.
mgt: Andreia Alexandra Norton (born 15 August 1996) is a Portuguese professional footballer who plays as a forward for Benfica and the Portugal national team. Beginning her career in youth football, she has played for clubs in Portugal, Spain, Germany, and Italy, including Braga, SC Sand, and Inter Milan. Norton made her senior international debut in 2016, scoring the decisive goal that qualified Portugal for their first UEFA Women's European Championship. Known for her versatility and skill, she has been recognized as one of the top players in Portuguese women's football.

trgt: Saint Melchior, or Melichior, was purportedly one of the Biblical Magi along with Caspar and Balthazar who visited the infant Jesus after he was born. Melchior was often referred to as the oldest member of the Magi. He was traditionally called the King of Persia and brought the gift of gold to Jesus. In the Western Christian church, he is regarded as a saint (as are the other two Magi).
mgt: Melchior, traditionally known as one of the Three Magi or Wise Men, is venerated as a saint in various Christian traditions, including the Roman Catholic, Eastern Orthodox, Anglican, and Lutheran Churches. Believed to be the King of Persia, he is said to have followed the Star of Bethlehem to worship the Child Jesus, offering gold as a gift symbolizing Jesus' kingship. According to tradition, Melchior died on January 1, 55 AD, at the age of 116. His relics, along with those of the other Magi, are enshrined in the Shrine of the Three Kings at Cologne Cathedral. Melchior is commemorated on the Feast of Epiphany and is associated with various patronages, including travelers and pilgrims.

trgt: Merewether (/ˈmɛrɪˌwɛðər/) is a former Municipality and today a suburb of Newcastle, New South Wales, Australia, located 3 km (2 mi) from Newcastle's central business district with a population of around 11,000. The suburb stretches 3 km (2 mi) from Merewether Beach in the east to Adamstown in the west.
mgt: Merewether is a coastal suburb of Newcastle, New South Wales, Australia, established in 1890 and named after Edward Christopher Merewether. Known for its coal mining history, the area also features iconic beaches, including Merewether Beach and the Merewether Ocean Baths, the largest in the Southern Hemisphere. It is a hub for surfing, hosting Newcastle Surfest, and is home to professional surfer Mark Richards. The suburb includes several schools, historic sites, and recreational facilities, blending its industrial past with modern residential and leisure developments.

trgt: Sander Kaasjager (IPA-nl; born 21 June 1985), who plays under the pseudonym Vo0 (pronunciation: 'vu:), is a Dutch professional player of the first-person shooter games Painkiller, Quake II, Quake III, Quake 4, Quake Live and Quake Champions, and also World of Warcraft. He has won more titles and prize money—over US$250,000—at professional Painkiller tournaments than any other player.
mgt: Sander Kaasjager (born June 21, 1985), known by his gaming alias Vo0, is a Dutch former professional esports player renowned for his success in *Painkiller* and *Quake*. Competing for teams like Fnatic and compLexity Gaming, he won multiple world championships, including the 2004 CPL and ESWC titles, and was the Most Valuable Player of the 2005 CPL World Tour.

trgt: Laura Natalia Esquivel (born May 18, 1994, in Buenos Aires, Argentina), known professionally as Laura Esquivel, is an Argentine and Italian actress and singer, who gained international popularity for her debut acting role as Patricia "Patito" Castro in the popular Argentine children's telenovela, Patito Feo.
mgt: Laura Natalia Esquivel (born May 18, 1994) is an Argentine actress and singer known for her role as the lead in the popular teen telenovela *Patito Feo* (2007–2008). She gained early recognition on the reality show *Código F.A.M.A.* and has since achieved international success, particularly in Latin America and Europe, with sold-out concerts, award-winning soundtracks, and roles in Italian films.

trgt:
The 2012 West Oxfordshire District Council election took place on 3 May 2012 to elect members of West Oxfordshire District Council in Oxfordshire, England. One third of the council was up for election and the Conservative Party stayed in overall control of the council.

After the election, the composition of the council was:
mgt: The 2012 West Oxfordshire District Council election was held on 3 May 2012 to elect members of West Oxfordshire District Council in England. The Conservative Party retained control of the council despite losing three seats to Labour, ending with 41 councillors. Labour and the Liberal Democrats each held four seats, while voter turnout averaged 34.77%.

trgt: The Technicolor Time Machine is a 1967 science fiction novel by American writer Harry Harrison. It is a time travel story with comedic elements, which satirizes Hollywood. The story first appeared in Analog Science Fiction and Fact magazine, where it was serialized in three parts in the March–May 1967 issues, under the title "The Time-Machined Saga."
mgt: *The Technicolor Time Machine* is a 1967 science fiction novel by Harry Harrison. The story follows a struggling film director who uses a time machine to produce a historical movie about Viking settlers in Vinland, inadvertently becoming part of history himself. Combining humor and time travel, the novel explores paradoxes and the challenges of filmmaking. It was adapted into a BBC radio play in 1981.

trgt: A civil parish is a country subdivision, forming the lowest unit of local government in England. There are 264 civil parishes in the ceremonial county of Cambridgeshire, most of the county being parished; Cambridge is completely unparished; Fenland, East Cambridgeshire, South Cambridgeshire and Huntingdonshire are entirely parished. At the 2001 census, there were 497,820 people living in the parishes, accounting for 70.2 per cent of the county's population.
mgt: Civil parishes in Cambridgeshire are the smallest units of local government in the county, with origins in ecclesiastical divisions of the Church of England. Over time, they gained civil administrative responsibilities, including road maintenance and poor relief, formalized by the Poor Law Act 1601. Modern civil parishes were established by the Local Government Act 1894, and their role has evolved, with recent legislation encouraging the creation of new parishes and granting councils additional naming options.

trgt: Allison Jones (born 1955) is an American casting director who is credited for helping bring together realistic ensemble casts for such popular television shows as The Fresh Prince of Bel-Air (1990–1996), Freaks and Geeks (1999–2000), Curb Your Enthusiasm (2000–), Undeclared (2001–2002), Arrested Development (2003–2006), the American version of The Office (2005–2013), United States of Tara (2009–2011), Parks and Recreation (2009–2015), Brooklyn Nine-Nine (2013–2021), The Good Place (2016–2020), and What We Do in the Shadows (2019–).
mgt: Allison Jones (born July 13, 1955) is an American casting director known for her work in television and film. Active since 1982, she has cast acclaimed TV shows such as *Freaks and Geeks* (for which she won an Emmy), *The Office*, *Parks and Recreation*, and *Brooklyn Nine-Nine*. Her film credits include *The 40-Year-Old Virgin*, *Superbad*, *Bridesmaids*, and *Lady Bird*. A graduate of Pomona College, Jones is celebrated for shaping modern comedy through her casting choices.

trgt: Heinz Rein (pseudonym: Reinhard Andermann) was an influential German novelist writing before and after the Second World War. He became a major figure in the "rubble literature" period, and his famous novel Berlin Finale, published in 1947, was one of the first bestsellers of the German rebuilding period.
mgt: Heinz Rein (9 April 1906 – 16 January 1991) was a German writer and journalist, best known for his novel *Berlin Finale*, a seminal work of rubble literature depicting the end of World War II and postwar Berlin. Initially active in East Germany, he later moved to West Germany.

trgt: Jessica Ashley Karpov (born July 10, 1992), better known as Harloe (stylized in all caps), is an American singer and songwriter, based in Los Angeles. In addition to her career as a recording artist, Karpov co-wrote and co-produced four songs on Kelly Clarkson's 2017 album Meaning of Life, including the singles "I Don't Think About You"and"Heat". She has also written for Britney Spears, Charli XCX, Celine Dion, Zayn, K/DA, Sabrina Claudio, and Olivia Holt.
mgt: Harloe, born Jessica Ashley Karpov on July 10, 1992, in Queens, New York, is an American singer, songwriter, and music producer known for her work in contemporary R&B, soul, and pop. She began her career as Jessica Ashley, releasing the EP *Prelude* in 2012, before rebranding as Harloe in 2016. She has collaborated with artists like Robin Schulz and co-wrote the hit "POP/STARS" for Riot Games. Her debut EP, *Rivers Run Dry*, was released in 2020.

trgt:
L.A. Law: The Movie is a 2002 American made-for-television drama film based on the 1986–1994 television series L.A. Law which reunited most of the original cast, although not all prominent cast members returned, including Blair Underwood (Jonathan Rollins), Jimmy Smits (Victor Sifuentes), Amanda Donohoe (C.J. Lamb) and John Spencer (Tommy Mullaney). The film aired on NBC on May 12, 2002.

The film's initial working title was L.A. Law: Return To Justice.
mgt: *L.A. Law: The Movie* (also known as *L.A. Law: Return to Justice*) is a 2002 American drama television film and reunion of the series *L.A. Law*. Directed by Michael Schultz and written by William M. Finkelstein, it aired on NBC on May 12, 2002. Set eight years after the series finale, the film follows the lives of the McKenzie Brackman law firm members as they navigate personal and professional challenges, including legal battles and relationship conflicts.

trgt: Victor Lenard Shapiro (16 October 1924, Chicago – 1 March 2013, Riverside, California) was an American mathematician, specializing in trigonometric series and differential equations. He is known for his two theorems (published in 1957) on the uniqueness of multiple Fourier series.
mgt: Victor L. Shapiro (1924–2013) was an American mathematician known for his contributions to analysis. He earned his Ph.D. from the University of Chicago under Antoni Zygmund and held academic positions at Rutgers, Oregon, and UC Riverside. A prolific author, he published over 80 research articles and several books. Shapiro was elected a Fellow of the American Association for the Advancement of Science in 2003 and the American Mathematical Society in 2012.

trgt: The Rodeo–Chediski Fire was a wildfire that burned in east-central Arizona beginning on June 18, 2002, and was not controlled until July 7. It was the worst forest fire in Arizona's recorded history until June 14, 2011 when the Wallow Fire surpassed it. Several local communities, including Show Low, Pinetop-Lakeside, Heber-Overgaard Clay Springs and Pinedale, were threatened and had to be evacuated.
mgt: The Rodeo–Chediski Fire was a massive wildfire that burned 468,638 acres in Arizona's Coconino and Navajo counties from June 18 to July 7, 2002. It originated as two separate fires: the Rodeo Fire, caused by arson, and the Chediski Fire, accidentally started by a stranded motorist. The fires merged on June 23, destroying 426 structures and prompting the evacuation of around 30,000 people. Restoration efforts included aerial seeding and erosion control, while the disaster influenced forest management policies, such as the Healthy Forests Restoration Act. Legal actions followed, with the arsonist sentenced to 10 years in prison.

trgt: Henrique Honoré Dumont (July 20, 1832 in Diamantina – August 30, 1892 in Rio de Janeiro) was a Brazilian engineer, coffee farmer and father of Alberto Santos-Dumont. A son of French immigrants, he is considered one of the three kings of the coffee of his time, introducing modern methods to coffee farming.
mgt: Henrique Honoré Dumont (July 20, 1832 – August 30, 1892), known as the "Coffee King," was a Brazilian engineer and coffee farmer. A graduate of the École Centrale Paris, he revolutionized coffee production in Brazil through innovative techniques, creating one of the largest and most modern coffee farms in South America. Dumont also contributed to the development of the Mogiana Railroad, facilitating regional growth. Later in life, he introduced Brazil's first gasoline-powered car. He was the father of aviation pioneer Alberto Santos Dumont.

page_title: In the Heart of Kunoichi Tsubaki
trgt: In the Heart of Kunoichi Tsubaki (Japanese: くノ一ツバキの胸の内, Hepburn: Kunoichi Tsubaki no Mune no Uchi) is a Japanese manga series written and illustrated by Sōichirō Yamamoto. It has been serialized in Shogakukan's shōnen manga magazine Monthly Shōnen Sunday since January 2018. An anime television series adaptation by CloverWorks aired from April to July 2022.
mgt: *In the Heart of Kunoichi Tsubaki* (くノ一ツバキの胸の内) is a Japanese comedy manga series written and illustrated by Sōichirō Yamamoto. It began serialization in *Monthly Shōnen Sunday* in January 2018, with seven volumes released as of April 2022. An anime adaptation by CloverWorks aired from April to July 2022. The story follows Tsubaki, a skilled kunoichi curious about men despite her village's strict rules forbidding contact with them. The series received mixed reviews, with criticism of its premise and character designs but some praise for its art style.

trgt: "Let It Go" is a song by Hong Kong singer and actor Jacky Cheung (Cantonese pinyin: Hok Yau)(中文名:张学友)written by Roxanne Seeman, Daniel Lindstrom and Daniele Musto for Cheung's album Private Corner (2010). Cantonese lyrics were written by Kenny So (乔星 Qiao Xing). The song was released to radio by Universal Music in April 2010 as the fourth single and is the seventh track on the album.
mgt: "Let It Go" is a jazz and Canto-jazz single by Hong Kong singer Jacky Cheung, released in April 2010 as part of his album *Private Corner*. Written by Roxanne Seeman, Daniel Lindstrom, and Daniele Musto, with Cantonese lyrics by Kenny So, the song features a gospel choir and was produced by Andrew Tuason. It reached number 16 on the HMVHK sales chart and was performed during Cheung's *Private Corner* Mini Concert and his 1/2 Century Tour.

trgt: Daniel Roy Carpenter (born November 25, 1985) is a former American football placekicker. He was signed by the Miami Dolphins as an undrafted free agent in 2008 after playing college football for the University of Montana. He has also played for the Buffalo Bills.
mgt: Dan Carpenter (born November 25, 1985) is a former American football placekicker who played nine seasons in the National Football League (NFL). After a standout college career at the University of Montana, where he earned multiple All-Big Sky honors, Carpenter went undrafted in 2008 but signed with the Miami Dolphins, where he earned Pro Bowl honors in 2009. He later played for the Buffalo Bills, Arizona Cardinals, and New York Jets, achieving notable success with the Bills, including multiple game-winning field goals and AFC Special Teams Player of the Week awards. Carpenter retired with 236 career field goals made.

trgt: Vesna Škare-Ožbolt (IPA-sh; born 20 June 1961) is a Croatian politician. She was the 10th Minister of Justice of Croatia from 2003 until 2006. She was the second female holder of that office, as well as the second consecutive woman after Ingrid Antičević-Marinović.
mgt: Vesna Škare-Ožbolt (born 20 June 1961) is a Croatian politician and former Minister of Justice, serving from 2003 to 2006 in the cabinet of Prime Minister Ivo Sanader. A lawyer by profession, she played a key role in the peaceful reintegration of Eastern Slavonia into Croatia during the 1990s. Initially a member of the Croatian Democratic Union (HDZ), she later joined the Democratic Centre (DC), becoming its leader. As Minister of Justice, she pursued judicial reforms, including efforts to digitalize land registries. After her dismissal in 2006, she ran unsuccessfully in the 2009–10 Croatian presidential election.

trgt:
Fluorenone is an aromatic organic compound with the chemical formula C13H8O. It is bright fluorescent yellow in color and is a solid at room temperature.

It can be synthesised from fluorene with the addition of glacial acetic acid and sodium hypochlorite solution, undergoing an oxidation reaction.

It is used to make antimalaria drugs. It is used as a fragrance or odor agent in candles.
mgt: Fluorenone, also known as 9-fluorenone or diphenylene ketone, is an aromatic organic compound with the chemical formula C₁₃H₈O. It is a yellow solid, insoluble in water but soluble in organic solvents like alcohol and benzene. Fluorenone serves as a precursor to various azafluorenones, which are biologically significant compounds with antimicrobial, DNA-damaging, and anti-malarial properties. Azafluorenones are synthesized through methods such as N-oxime rearrangements, Diels-Alder reactions, and intramolecular acylation.

trgt: Nomads R.F.C. are a Hong Kong rugby union club, who play in G4S League Divisions 2,5 and 7 and are based in the Tai Hang Tung area in New Kowloon. Between 2008/09 the club has won over 6 different league and 7's trophies.
mgt: Nomads Rugby Football Club, founded in 1989, is a Hong Kong-based rugby union club competing in the G4S League Division 2, 5, and 7. Initially focused on developing local Chinese players, the club has since grown to include a diverse membership. Nomads achieved significant milestones, including fielding three teams by the 2009–10 season and securing league and tournament successes. The club collaborates with Flying Kukris RFC to promote youth rugby development.

trgt: Matthew Daniels (born September 27, 1989) is a former American football safety and current special teams coach of the Minnesota Vikings of the National Football League (NFL). He was signed by the St. Louis Rams as an undrafted free agent in 2012. He played college football for Duke.
mgt: Matt Daniels (born September 27, 1989) is an American football coach and former player who serves as the special teams coordinator for the Minnesota Vikings of the National Football League (NFL). A standout safety at Duke University, he played in the NFL from 2012 to 2015 before transitioning to coaching.

trgt: In medicine, Murphy's sign (also known as Sweeney’s sign) is a maneuver during a physical examination as part of the abdominal examination. It is useful for differentiating pain in the right upper quadrant. Typically, it is positive in cholecystitis, but negative in choledocholithiasis, pyelonephritis, and ascending cholangitis.
mgt: Murphy's sign is a clinical test used during abdominal examinations to identify upper right quadrant pain, often associated with gallbladder disease. It involves palpation below the right costal margin during inspiration. Named after American surgeon John Benjamin Murphy, it has high sensitivity but lower specificity, particularly in elderly patients.

trgt:
Rainbow War is a 1985 Oscar-nominated 19-minute animated/live action short film created for Expo 86, the 1986 World's Fair in Vancouver, British Columbia, Canada. It was directed by Bob Rogers and cinematography was done by Reed Smoot.

Notable cast members include Ryan Stiles and Colin Mochrie of Whose Line is it Anyway fame.
mgt: *Rainbow War* is a fantastical story about three isolated kingdoms—Blue, Red, and Golden—each devoted to a single color and distrustful of others. When a young boy from the Golden Kingdom invents a flying ring and explores the other lands, his actions spark a colorful conflict. The kingdoms engage in a vibrant "paint war," but through the chaos, they discover the beauty of blending colors, creating the first rainbow. This newfound harmony unites the kingdoms, symbolizing the power of collaboration and diversity.

trgt: Educational robotics teaches the design, analysis, application and operation of robots. Robots include articulated robots, mobile robots or autonomous vehicles. Educational robotics can be taught from elementary school to graduate programs. Robotics may also be used to motivate and facilitate the instruction other, often foundational, topics such as computer programming, artificial intelligence or engineering design.
mgt: Educational robotics is the use of robots as tools to support education and training, fostering skills in programming, artificial intelligence, and problem-solving. It is implemented in schools, summer camps, and universities, with applications ranging from early education to post-secondary degree programs. Educational robotics also benefits special education, enhancing cognitive, social, and personal skills.

trgt: The Virginia Convention of 1864 was an assembly of sixteen loyal Unionists during the American Civil War meeting under the auspices of Virginia's Restored Government. It abolished slavery in the state of Virginia, and framed the fundamental civil law that served Virginia government for six years through Appomattox, Presidential Reconstruction and Congressional Reconstruction.
mgt: The Virginia Constitutional Convention of 1864 was held from February 13 to April 11 in Alexandria, Virginia, during the American Civil War. Composed of delegates from Union-occupied counties, the convention abolished slavery without compensation, reformed voting procedures, and limited disenfranchisement to Confederate officeholders. Its constitution governed Union-controlled areas until replaced in 1870.

trgt: Crymogæa (from Greek Κρυογαια, "ice-land") is a book in Latin written by the Icelandic scholar Arngrímur Jónsson and published in Hamburg in 1609 and again in 1610. It was the first historical description of Iceland since Íslendingabók and the first comprehensive book about the history of Iceland.
mgt: *Crymogæa* is a historical work by Arngrímur Jónsson that chronicles Iceland's history, culture, and language. Divided into three parts, it covers Iceland's medieval golden age and subsequent decline. Published in the 17th century, it inspired Icelandic nationalism, revived interest in sagas, and introduced Norse mythology to Europe.

trgt: Robert Mackenzie Beverley (1798-1868) was an author, magistrate, and controversialist. He was born in the town of Beverley in Yorkshire, attended Richmond School, and matriculated at Trinity College, University of Cambridge in 1816. He received the degree of LL.B. in 1821, after which he lived at Beverley, in due course becoming a Justice of the Peace and Deputy Lieutenant.
mgt: Robert Mackenzie Beverley (died November 3, 1868) was an English writer and religious controversialist. Originally a Quaker, he left the Society of Friends during the Beaconite Controversy and joined the Plymouth Brethren. Beverley authored works on religion, politics, and science, including a critique of Charles Darwin's theory of evolution in *The Darwinian Theory of the Transmutation of Species* (1867).

page_title: Faculty of Social and Human Sciences
trgt: The Portuguese Faculdade de Ciências Sociais e Humanas (Faculty of Social and Human Sciences - FCSH) is an organic unit of the Universidade NOVA de Lisboa (NOVA). According to its statutes, “the Faculty of Social and Human Sciences of NOVA University of Lisbon is an institution dedicated to education, scientific research and cultural creation". The Faculty's own identity stems from the coexistence of social sciences with humanities, allowing an unusual interdisciplinarity in the Portuguese higher education panorama.
mgt: The Faculty of Social and Human Sciences (Faculdade de Ciências Sociais e Humanas, NOVA FCSH) is a public higher education institution in Lisbon, Portugal, established in 1977 as part of the Universidade NOVA de Lisboa. Located on Avenida de Berna, it offers undergraduate, master's, and doctoral programs across 12 departments, emphasizing interdisciplinary studies in social sciences and humanities. NOVA FCSH is home to 16 research centers, several libraries, and a robust internationalization strategy, including mobility programs like Erasmus.

trgt: Rhodium(III) chloride refers to inorganic compounds with the formula RhCl3(H2O)n, where n varies from 0 to 3. These are diamagnetic solids featuring octahedral Rh(III) centres. Depending on the value of n, the material is either a dense brown solid or a soluble reddish salt. The soluble trihydrated (n = 3) salt is widely used to prepare compounds used in homogeneous catalysis, notably for the industrial production of acetic acid and hydroformylation.
mgt: Rhodium(III) chloride (RhCl₃) is an inorganic compound that exists as a dark red, deliquescent solid. It is commonly encountered as its hydrated form, RhCl₃(H₂O)₃, which serves as a precursor to a variety of rhodium complexes used in catalysis and organometallic chemistry. Anhydrous RhCl₃ is prepared by reacting rhodium metal with chlorine gas. Rhodium(III) chloride is notable for its role in homogeneous catalysis, including hydroformylation and alkene hydrogenation.

trgt: The Bridge for Kids is a proposed bridge across the Carbon River in Orting, Washington, about a mile upstream of where it joins the Puyallup River. It would provide an emergency evacuation route for school children to escape a future lahar flow from Mount Rainier, consisting of an up to 10-meter (33 ft) high flood of mud, rock and boulders. As of 2016, the $40 million bridge was still in the planning phase.
mgt: The Bridge for Kids, officially known as the Orting Emergency Evacuation Bridge, is a proposed pedestrian suspension bridge in Orting, Washington, designed to provide rapid evacuation in the event of a lahar from Mount Rainier. Spanning 540 feet (160 m) across the Carbon River, the bridge would accommodate 12,000 people within a 40-minute evacuation window. Its design includes uneven footings and a steep slope, addressing the high lahar risk in the region's floodplain.

trgt: Manny Pacquiao vs. Jeff Horn, billed as the Battle of Brisbane, was a boxing match for the WBO welterweight championship. The event took place on July 2, 2017 at Suncorp Stadium in Brisbane, Australia. Horn won the fight by unanimous decision and claimed his first world title in boxing.
mgt: The boxing match between Manny Pacquiao and Jeff Horn took place on July 2, 2017, at Suncorp Stadium in Brisbane, Australia, for the WBO welterweight title. Horn won via a controversial unanimous decision, sparking widespread criticism from analysts and fans. Despite Pacquiao landing significantly more punches, the judges scored the fight 117–111, 115–113, and 115–113 in Horn's favor. The bout drew significant attention, with over 51,000 attendees and record-breaking viewership on ESPN. A WBO rescore upheld Horn's victory, though the decision remained contentious.

trgt: Baankey Ki Crazy Baraat (Baankey's Crazy Wedding Procession) is a 2015 Bollywood comedy film directed by Aijaz Khan, starring Rajpal Yadav, Vijay Raaz, Sanjay Mishra, Tia Bajpai, Rakesh Bedi, Satyajeet Dubey and the director himself among others. Hazel Keech has a special appearance. It was produced by Anita Mani and released on 28 August 2015.
mgt: *Baankey Ki Crazy Baraat* is a 2015 Indian Hindi-language comedy film directed by Aijaz Khan and written by Anita Mani. Starring Rajpal Yadav, Sanjay Mishra, Vijay Raaz, and Tia Bajpai, the film follows a bachelor whose family arranges a proxy groom for his wedding due to astrological issues. Despite its quirky premise and seasoned cast, the film received mixed to negative reviews, with critics praising performances but criticizing the convoluted plot and lack of humor.

trgt: The Art of Self-Defense is a 2019 black comedy martial arts film written and directed by Riley Stearns and starring Jesse Eisenberg, Alessandro Nivola and Imogen Poots. It had its world premiere at South by Southwest on March 10, 2019, and was released in the United States on July 12, 2019, by Bleecker Street.
mgt: *The Art of Self-Defense* is a 2019 black comedy film written and directed by Riley Stearns. Starring Jesse Eisenberg, Alessandro Nivola, and Imogen Poots, the film explores themes of toxic masculinity and self-empowerment through the story of a timid accountant who joins a karate dōjō after being attacked. Premiering at South by Southwest, it received critical acclaim for its dark humor and originality.

trgt: A fish flake is a platform built on poles and spread with boughs for drying cod on the foreshores of fishing villages and small coastal towns in Newfoundland and Nordic countries. Spelling variations for fish flake in Newfoundland include flek, fleyke, fleake, flaik and fleack. The term's first recorded use in connection with fishing appeared in Richard Whitbourne's book Newfoundland (1623, p. 57). In Norway, a flake is known as a hjell.
mgt: A fish flake is a traditional structure used in Newfoundland and other fishing communities for drying salted cod. Constructed from horizontal poles supported by upright posts, flakes allowed air to circulate beneath the fish as they dried in the sun. Permanent flakes were part of fishing village landscapes, often accompanied by fish stores for storage and gear. Temporary hand flakes were also used during bountiful seasons. Flakes were widely used until the 20th century, when freezing technology reduced the demand for dried fish.

trgt: The 2014 Vodacom Cup was played between 7 March and 16 May 2014 and was the 17th edition of this annual domestic cup competition. This edition of the Vodacom Cup was played between fourteen provincial rugby union teams in South Africa from the Currie Cup Premier and First Divisions, as well as the Limpopo Blue Bulls and Simba XV from Kenya.
mgt: The 2014 Vodacom Cup was a rugby union competition held from 7 March to 16 May 2014, featuring 16 teams from South Africa and Kenya. The Griquas claimed their fifth title, defeating the Golden Lions in the final. The tournament saw 466 tries scored across 63 matches, with JC Roos as the top point scorer (110) and Devon Williams and George Tossel as the top try scorers (9 each).

trgt:
Jessie Joe Nandye (born 22nd Aug 1985), also commonly known by his former name Jessie Joe Parker, is a Papua New Guinean rugby league footballer who plays as a centre for Whitehaven in Betfred League 1.

He is a Papua New Guinean international representative.
mgt: Jessie Joe Parker (born 22 August 1985) is a Papua New Guinean rugby league player and coach. He has represented Papua New Guinea in multiple international tournaments, including the Rugby League World Cups and the Four Nations. Parker played for clubs such as the Northern Pride, Featherstone Rovers, and Whitehaven, where he reached 100 appearances in 2016. He became player-coach of Hensingham ARLFC in 2022 and remains the club's top scorer.

trgt: Nerima Wako-Ojiwa (née Nerima Wako), is a Kenyan political analyst, who serves as the executive director of Siasa Place, an organization, led by young people that engages young Kenyans, educating them about the constitution, governance and electoral processes, through community engagements and social media.
mgt: Nerima Wako-Ojiwa (born 1989) is a Kenyan political analyst and corporate executive, serving as the executive director of Siasa Place, a youth-led organization she co-founded in 2015. She holds a Bachelor of Arts in Journalism and Sociology and a Master of Public Administration from Jacksonville State University. Born in Nairobi, Kenya, she developed her political acumen through campus leadership roles while studying in the United States. Nerima is married to politician Billian Ojiwa and is the niece of former Kenyan Senator Amos Wako.

trgt: The City of Greenwood Village is a home rule municipality located in Arapahoe County, Colorado, United States. The city population was 15,691 at the 2020 United States Census. Greenwood Village is a part of the Denver–Aurora–Lakewood, CO Metropolitan Statistical Area and the Front Range Urban Corridor.
mgt: Greenwood Village is a home rule municipality located in Arapahoe County, Colorado, United States. Incorporated on September 19, 1950, it is part of the Denver-Aurora-Lakewood Metropolitan Area and includes portions of the Denver Technological Center (DTC). The city had a population of 15,691 as of the 2020 Census. Originally developed in the 1860s as Greenwood Ranch, it transitioned from a gold rush settlement to a farming community by the early 1900s. Greenwood Village is known for its affluent population, high median household income, and notable attractions such as the Fiddler's Green Amphitheatre, an 18,000-seat outdoor venue. It is served by the Cherry Creek School District and hosts several major employers within the DTC.

trgt: Edmonton Public Schools (legally Edmonton School Division) is the largest public school division in Edmonton, the second largest in Alberta, and the sixth largest in Canada. The division offers a variety of alternative and special needs programs, and many are offered in multiple locations to improve accessibility for students. As a public school division, Edmonton Public Schools accepts all students who meet age and residency requirements set out in provincial legislation.
mgt: Edmonton Public Schools is the second-largest school district in Alberta, Canada, serving over 105,000 students across 212 schools as of 2021. Governed by a board of nine elected trustees, the district offers a range of programs, including regular, alternative, and special education, as well as early education and language immersion. Established in 1881, it pioneered site-based decision-making in Canada, fostering innovative educational models. Its headquarters is located at 1 Kingsway Avenue NW, Edmonton.

trgt: Going Blank Again is the second studio album by English rock band Ride, released on 9 March 1992 on Creation Records. It was produced by Alan Moulder, and peaked at No. 5 in the UK Albums Chart. In October 2009 the album was certified gold by the British Phonographic Industry for sales of over 100,000 units.
mgt: *Going Blank Again* is the second studio album by British shoegaze band Ride, released on 9 March 1992 through Creation and Sire Records. Produced by Alan Moulder and the band, it features ten tracks, including the singles "Leave Them All Behind" and "Twisterella." The album received critical acclaim for its layered vocals and shoegaze sound, and it has been reissued multiple times, including a 20th-anniversary edition in 2012.

trgt: Infanta Beatriz of Spain, Princess of Civitella-Cesi (Beatriz Isabel Federica Alfonsa Eugénie Cristina Maria Teresia Bienvenida Ladislàa de Borbón y Battenberg; 22 June 1909 – 22 November 2002) was a daughter of King Alfonso XIII of Spain and Victoria Eugenie of Battenberg, wife of Alessandro Torlonia, 5th Prince di Civitella-Cesi. She was a paternal aunt of King Juan Carlos I.
mgt: Infanta Beatriz of Spain (22 June 1909 – 22 November 2002) was a Spanish princess, the third child of King Alfonso XIII and Queen Victoria Eugenie. Born at the Royal Palace of La Granja, she lived through the Spanish monarchy's exile following the proclamation of the Second Republic in 1931. She married Alessandro Torlonia, 5th Prince of Civitella-Cesi, in 1935, renouncing her succession rights. The couple had four children and resided in Rome. Beatriz remained active in royal and social circles, supporting her family's claims to the Spanish throne and maintaining ties to Spain despite her exile. She passed away in Rome at the age of 93, as the last surviving child of Alfonso XIII.

trgt: The 1983 Barcelona City Council election, also the 1983 Barcelona municipal election, was held on Sunday, 8 May 1983, to elect the 2nd City Council of the municipality of Barcelona. All 43 seats in the City Council were up for election. The election was held simultaneously with regional elections in thirteen autonomous communities and local elections all throughout Spain.
mgt: The 1983 Barcelona City Council election was held on 8 May 1983 to elect all 43 members of the City Council of Barcelona. Pasqual Maragall of the Socialists' Party of Catalonia (PSC) was re-elected as mayor. The election used proportional representation with a 5% electoral threshold, and the mayor was indirectly elected by the council.

trgt:
Arbignieu (IPA-fr) is a former commune in the Ain department in the Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes region of eastern France. On 1 January 2016, it was merged into the new commune Arboys en Bugey.

The inhabitants of the commune are known as Arbignolans or Arbignolanes
mgt: Arbignieu is a former commune in the Ain department in the Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes region of France. Located near Belley, it became part of the commune of Arboys en Bugey in 2016. Known for its vineyards producing Roussette du Bugey-Arbignieu wine, the area features a mix of farmland and forest, with the Furans river forming its eastern border. The commune included the hamlets of Thoys, Slignieu, and Peyzieu and was traversed by the Tour du Bugey trail.

trgt: A limiting factor is a variable of a system that causes a noticeable change in output or another measure of a type of system. The limiting factor is in a pyramid shape of organisms going up from the producers to consumers and so on. A factor not limiting over a certain domain of starting conditions may yet be limiting over another domain of starting conditions, including that of the factor.
mgt: A limiting factor is a variable that restricts the growth, abundance, or distribution of a system, organism, or process. The concept is used across disciplines, including ecology, business, technology, and chemistry. In ecology, it is based on Liebig's Law of the Minimum, where growth is controlled by the scarcest resource. Limiting factors can be physical, biological, or circumstantial, and may lead to competition. In chemistry, they determine reaction yields and rates.

trgt: Dau al set was a Catalan avant-garde magazine founded in September 1948 by Joan-Josep Tharrats and directed by Joan Ponç. The magazine was produced by the artistic group Dau al Set. It was the continuation of another magazine called Algol (magazine) published by the same group. The magazine had eight pages measuring 250 mm × 175 mm (9.8 in × 6.9 in). The texts were written in Catalan and Spanish. The magazine continued publishing numbers until December 1951.
mgt: *Dau al Set* was a Catalan avant-garde magazine founded by Joan-Josep Tharrats in September 1948 and published until 1951. Initially influenced by Dadaism, it later adopted a surrealistic approach, becoming a platform for innovative artists. The magazine was associated with the *Dau al Set* group, which included poet Joan Brossa, philosopher Arnau Puig, and painters Antoni Tàpies, Joan Ponç, and Modest Cuixart. Recognized for its artistic contributions, it was named one of the 20 best avant-garde magazines of the 20th century by the Museum of Modern Art.

trgt: Anima Yell! (アニマエール!, Anima Ēru!) is a Japanese four-panel manga series by Tsukasa Unohana. It was serialized in Houbunsha's seinen manga magazine Manga Time Kirara Carat from its April 2016 to October 2020 issues and has been collected in five tankōbon volumes. An anime television series adaptation by Doga Kobo aired from October 7 to December 23, 2018.
mgt: *Anima Yell!* (アニマエール!, *Anima Ēru!*) is a Japanese four-panel manga series written and illustrated by Tsukasa Unohana. It was serialized in *Manga Time Kirara Carat* from February 2016 to August 2020, with five tankōbon volumes published. The story follows Kohane Hatoya, a high school girl who starts a cheerleading club. An anime adaptation by Doga Kobo aired from October to December 2018, running for 12 episodes. The series blends slice-of-life and sports elements, receiving mixed reviews for its characters and humor but praise for its animation and charm. Characters also appear in the mobile game *Kirara Fantasia*.

trgt: Roberto Gari (February 13, 1920 – January 22, 2008) was an American artist and actor. He was born in Brooklyn and appeared in vaudeville under the name of Jackie Hayes from the age of 4. He was best known for his role as Guy Blank, Jerri Blank's father, in the cult Comedy Central series Strangers with Candy.
mgt: Roberto Gari (February 13, 1920 – January 22, 2008) was an American artist and actor. Known for his Impressionist and Modernist portraits, his work was collected by notable figures, including Ronald Reagan and Judy Garland. Gari also had a diverse show business career, appearing on Broadway, in films, and on television, including *Strangers with Candy*.

page_title: Tales of the Heroes: Twin Brave
trgt: Tales of the Heroes: Twin Brave is an action game in the Tales series of games. published by Bandai Namco Games. It is considered a spinoff, and plays similarly to the Dynasty Warriors series. The game was released for the PlayStation Portable in Japan on February 23, 2012.
mgt: *Tales of the Heroes: Twin Brave* is a 2012 beat 'em up action game developed by Alfa System and published by Bandai Namco Games for the PlayStation Portable. Featuring characters from the *Tales* series, the game involves battling waves of enemies. It received mixed reception, with strong initial sales in Japan but lower overall performance compared to other *Tales* spin-offs.

trgt: Jim Radakovich is an American sculptor and painter living and working in New York City. He was a key figure in the East Village art scene in New York from 1982 to 1987 often showing together both Neo-Surrealist paintings and totem-like sculpture. He frequently exhibited with other artists who emerged at the time, including Kiki Smith, David Wojnarowicz, George Condo, Rick Prol, Peter Schuyff, Mark Kostabi and Marilyn Minter.
mgt: Jim Radakovich is an American painter and sculptor associated with the East Village art movement of the 1980s. Known for his Neo-Expressionist and Neo-Surrealist works, his art explores urban dramas, psychological ambiguity, and modern life through vibrant paintings and totemic sculptures. Radakovich gained prominence with solo and group exhibitions in New York and internationally, and his work is part of notable collections. He continues to exhibit in contemporary art shows.

trgt: Riffs is a live album by American saxophonist Jimmy Lyons. It was recorded on September 13–14, 1980 at Le Dreher, a jazz club in Paris, and was released in 1982 on the hat MUSICS label. The album features Lyons on alto saxophone, Karen Borca on bassoon, Jay Oliver on bass, and Paul Murphy on drums.
mgt: *Riffs* is a live album by American alto saxophonist Jimmy Lyons, released in 1982 on the hat MUSICS label. Recorded over two days in September 1980 at Le Dreher in Paris, the album features Lyons alongside bassoonist Karen Borca, bassist Jay Oliver, and drummer Paul Murphy. It showcases adventurous compositions and improvisations, highlighting Lyons' work outside of Cecil Taylor's ensembles.

trgt: Salah Shahrour (Lang-ar; born 1 February 1988 in Aleppo, Syria) is a Syrian footballer who plays as a defender for Al-Nejmeh, which competes in the Lebanese Premier League the top division level in Lebanon and is currently a member of the Syria national football team.
mgt: Salah Shahrour (born 1 February 1988) is a Syrian professional footballer who plays as a defender for Al-Nejmeh and the Syria national team. He began his career with Al-Ittihad, winning the 2010 AFC Cup, and represented Syria at the 2005 FIFA U-20 World Cup.

trgt: Murayama Tōan Antonio (村山等安) was a 17th-century Japanese magistrate of the city of Nagasaki (Nagasaki daikan, 長崎代官). He was born in Nagoya from a humble background, and he was a Christian. He played an important role in the handling of "Nanban trade" in Nagasaki with Christian powers, and led an invasion to Taiwan, before being executed for his Christian faith.
mgt: Murayama Tōan (died 1619) was a Japanese Christian merchant, magistrate of Nagasaki, and influential figure in the silk trade during the late 16th and early 17th centuries. Known for his ties to the Jesuits and his role in regulating Portuguese silk imports, he also led a failed 1616 expedition to Taiwan to establish direct trade with China. He was executed in 1619 following political and religious disputes.

trgt:
Future Strategic Tanker Aircraft (FSTA) is a British project to procure Airbus A330 Multi Role Tanker Transport aerial refuelling (AR) and air transport (AT) aircraft for the Royal Air Force, to replace older models such as the VC10s and TriStars.

After evaluation of bids the RAF selected the AirTanker consortium, owned by Cobham plc, EADS, Rolls-Royce plc, Thales UK and VT Group plc, in 2004 offering the Airbus A330 MRTT.
mgt: The Future Strategic Tanker Aircraft (FSTA) program is a Royal Air Force (RAF) initiative to replace its aging fleet of Vickers VC10 and Lockheed TriStar refueling aircraft. Launched in 1997, the program adopted a Private Finance Initiative (PFI) model, with AirTanker selected as the contractor in 2004. The program introduced 14 Airbus A330 MRTT aircraft, named "Voyager," beginning in 2011. Despite initial delays and financial scrutiny, the fleet achieved operational milestones, providing air-to-air refueling and transport capabilities.

trgt: Arthur Edward Booth Jr. (February 12, 1948 – July 11, 2021) was an American jazz bassist. His professional name was Juini Booth, though his nickname has been spelled Jiunie, Junie, Joony, Jooney, Joonie, Juni, Juney, and Junius, over the course of his career.
mgt: Juini Booth (February 12, 1948 – July 11, 2021) was an American jazz bassist known for his work on double bass and electric bass. Born in Buffalo, New York, he performed with prominent artists such as Art Blakey, McCoy Tyner, and Sun Ra's Arkestra, where he was a long-serving member. Booth's career spanned collaborations with notable musicians including Freddie Hubbard, Tony Williams, and Elvin Jones, as well as international tours and recordings. He passed away in Buffalo at the age of 73.

trgt:
System of a Down is the debut studio album by Armenian-American heavy metal band System of a Down, released on June 30, 1998, by American Recordings and Columbia Records.

The album was certified gold by the Recording Industry Association of America in February 2000. After the success of the band's following album, Toxicity (2001), System of a Down was certified platinum.
mgt: *System of a Down* is the debut studio album by American metal band System of a Down, released on June 30, 1998, through American Recordings and Columbia Records. Produced by Rick Rubin and the band, the album blends nu metal and alternative metal with themes of war, genocide, religion, and brainwashing. It received critical acclaim and is featured in *1001 Albums You Must Hear Before You Die*.

trgt: Prakash Nanjappa (born 29 February 1976) is an Indian shooter who competes in the 10 metre air pistol and 50 metre pistol events. He was the only Indian to win a medal in the 2013 ISSF World Cup, when he won bronze with a very minor difference between the second place in 10 metre air pistol event in Changwon, South Korea. In the same event, he won the silver medal at the 2014 Commonwealth Games in Glasgow.
mgt: Prakash Nanjappa (born 29 February 1976) is an Indian sport shooter specializing in the 10 metre air pistol and 50 metre pistol events. He has represented India in multiple international competitions, winning medals at the ISSF World Cup, Commonwealth Games, and Asian Games. Notably, he won a bronze at the 2013 ISSF World Cup and a silver at the 2014 Commonwealth Games. Despite a paralytic attack in 2013, he recovered to continue competing and represented India at the 2016 Rio Olympics.

trgt: Yawalapiti (Jaulapiti) is an Arawakan language of Brazil. The Agavotaguerra (Agavotoqueng) reportedly spoke the same language. Speakers of the language live in a village along the edge of the river Tuatuari, a tributary of the Kuluene River, located in the southern part of the Xingu Indigenous Park (Upper Xingu), in the state of Mato Grosso.
mgt: The Yawalapiti language is an Arawakan language spoken by the Yawalapiti people in the Xingu Indigenous Park, Mato Grosso, Brazil. As of 2006, it had only eight native speakers. The language features a complex phonemic inventory, agglutinative morphology with classifiers and derivational suffixes, and a subject-verb-object (SVO) word order.

trgt: Kathryn Michelle "Katy" Kinard (born September 16, 1980) is an American Christian musician and pianist, who primarily plays a style of contemporary worship music and Christian pop music. She has released four musical works, Headed Back (2005), You're Still Better (2007), Lullaby Hymns (2010), and God of Fireflies (2016).
mgt: Katy Kinard (born Kathryn Michelle Kinard, September 16, 1980) is an American Christian singer, songwriter, and pianist based in Nashville, Tennessee. Active since 2005, she has released four studio albums, including *Headed Back* and *God of Fireflies*. Kinard graduated from Belmont University.

trgt: I, Olga Hepnarová (Lang-cs) is a 2016 internationally co-produced drama film, directed by Tomáš Weinreb and Petr Kazda, about Olga Hepnarová (1951–1975). She was a Czechoslovakian mass murderer, who on 10 July 1973, killed eight people with a truck in Prague. The film was shown in the Panorama section at the 66th Berlin International Film Festival.
mgt: *I, Olga Hepnarová* is a 2016 Czech biographical drama film directed by Tomáš Weinreb and Petr Kazda. Starring Michalina Olszańska, the film explores the life and crimes of Olga Hepnarová, a woman who committed a mass murder in 1973. Praised for its stark cinematography and haunting narrative, it received mixed to positive reviews, with a 78% approval rating on Rotten Tomatoes.

trgt: Bethlehem Atlantic Works of East Boston, Massachusetts, was a shipyard in the United States from 1853 until 1984. It was owned by the Bethlehem Shipbuilding Company. It is located directly to the west of the East Boston Immigration Station. The company's offices were in the Atlantic Works Warehouse, built in 1893.
mgt: Bethlehem Atlantic Works was a prominent shipyard in East Boston, Massachusetts, operating from 1853 to 1984. Originally two separate shipyards, it became part of Bethlehem Steel in 1928 and played a key role in ship repair during the Civil War, World Wars, and beyond. Known for its advanced dry docks and marine railways, it was Boston's largest private ship repair facility for over a century. The shipyard faced racial violence during the 1970s and 1980s, leading to the Navy's withdrawal and its eventual closure. The site was later redeveloped, with portions now hosting luxury housing and the Atlantic Works Gallery.

trgt: "Just Got Started Lovin' You" is a song co-written and recorded by American country music artist James Otto. It was released in July 2007 as the first single from his album Sunset Man. On the Hot Country Songs chart dated for May 17, 2008, the song has also become Otto's first (and to date, his only) number-one hit. The song was also the number one song on Billboard's year-end 2008 Hot Country Songs chart.
mgt: "Just Got Started Lovin' You" is a song by American country music artist James Otto, released on July 3, 2007, as the lead single from his album *Sunset Man*. Co-written by Otto, Jim Femino, and D. Vincent Williams, the song was inspired by Otto's desire to create a "sexy" and romantic track, drawing comparisons to Conway Twitty. Produced by John Rich and Otto, the song received positive reviews for its smooth, romantic style. A music video directed by Ryan Smith accompanied the release.

trgt: Half Man Half Biscuit are an English rock band, formed in 1984 in Birkenhead, Merseyside. Known for their satirical, sardonic, and sometimes surreal songs, the band comprises lead singer and guitarist Nigel Blackwell, bassist and singer Neil Crossley, drummer Carl Henry, and guitarist Karl Benson.
mgt: Half Man Half Biscuit is an English indie rock band formed in 1984 in Birkenhead, Merseyside, by Nigel Blackwell and Neil Crossley. Known for their satirical lyrics referencing UK culture and geography, the band gained prominence with their debut album *Back in the DHSS*, which topped the UK Indie Chart. Despite a brief split in 1986, they reformed in 1990 and have since released numerous albums. Championed by DJ John Peel, the band is celebrated for their wit, humor, and unique blend of musical styles, earning them a cult following and critical acclaim as one of England's most distinctive bands.

trgt: Wild Things: Foursome is a 2010 erotic thriller film directed by Andy Hurst and starring Jillian Murray, Marnette Patterson, Ashley Parker Angel and John Schneider. It is a sequel to Wild Things: Diamonds in the Rough (2005) and the fourth and final film in the Wild Things series. The film was released on DVD on June 1, 2010.
mgt: *Wild Things: Foursome* is a 2010 American erotic thriller film directed by Andy Hurst and starring Jillian Murray, Marnette Patterson, Ashley Parker Angel, and John Schneider. The fourth installment in the *Wild Things* series, it follows a web of deceit, murder, and betrayal surrounding a wealthy inheritance. The film received largely negative reviews, with critics citing its lack of originality and tedious execution.

trgt: The Holy Science is a book written by Swami Sri Yukteswar Giri in 1894 under the title Kaivalya Darsanam. Sri Yukteswar states that he wrote The Holy Science at the request of Mahavatar Babaji. The book compares parallel passages from the Bible and Upanishads in order to show the unity of all religions.
mgt: *The Holy Science* is a book written by Sri Yukteswar Giri that explores the parallels between ancient Indian scriptures and the New Testament, particularly the Book of Revelation. Divided into four sections representing stages of knowledge, it introduces a unique interpretation of the Yuga Cycle, asserting that humanity is currently in the ascending Dwapara Yuga.

trgt:
Bunyan and Babe is a 2017 American computer-animated comedy film directed by Louis Ross, featuring the voices of John Goodman as Paul Bunyan and Jeff Foxworthy as Babe the Blue Ox. The film first premiered online on Google Play.

It has been made available to watch for free from January 12, 2017 to February 16, 2017. Then, it was theatrically released in 12 American markets on February 3, 2017.
mgt: *Bunyan and Babe* is a 2017 American animated film directed by Louis Ross and featuring the voices of John Goodman, Jeff Foxworthy, and Kelsey Grammer. Loosely based on the folklore of Paul Bunyan, the story follows two children who discover Bunyan and Babe the Blue Ox in a hidden lair and team up with them to stop an evil land developer from destroying a Minnesota town. The film received mixed reviews.

trgt:
Hallelujah, Baby! is a musical with music by Jule Styne, lyrics by Adolph Green and Betty Comden, and a book by Arthur Laurents. The show is "a chronicle of the African American struggle for equality during the [first half of the] 20th century."

The musical premiered on Broadway in 1967 and made a young Leslie Uggams a star. It won the Tony Award for Best Musical.
mgt: *Hallelujah, Baby!* is a 1967 Broadway musical with music by Jule Styne, lyrics by Adolph Green and Betty Comden, and a book by Arthur Laurents. The story follows Georgina, an ambitious African American woman navigating racism and societal challenges as she rises to stardom through the Great Depression, World War II, and the civil rights movement. The original production won five Tony Awards, including Best Musical.

trgt: Leigh Diffey (born 3 March 1971) is an Australian American auto racing commentator. His career began calling motorcycle races in his home country before moving to the United Kingdom to cover other forms of motorsport. Diffey then moved to the United States to join Speed Channel, while simultaneously working for Network Ten in Australia. Since 2013, he has served as a play-by-play announcer and studio host with NBC Sports in the United States.
mgt: Leigh Diffey (born 3 March 1971) is an Australian-American sports commentator known for his extensive work in motorsports broadcasting. Beginning his career in Australia, he covered events such as the Superbike World Championship and V8 Supercars before moving to the United States in 2001. Diffey has worked for NBC Sports since 2013, serving as the lead announcer for Formula One, IndyCar, NASCAR, IMSA, and AMA Supercross. He has also covered Olympic events, rugby, and track and field.

trgt: Sir Constantijn Huygens, Lord of Zuilichem (/ˈhaɪɡənz/ HY-gənz, also US: /ˈhɔɪɡənz/ HOY-gənz, 4 September 1596 – 28 March 1687), was a Dutch Golden Age poet and composer. He was secretary to two Princes of Orange: Frederick Henry and William II, and the father of the scientist Christiaan Huygens.
mgt: Constantijn Huygens (1596–1687) was a Dutch poet, composer, architect, and diplomat, renowned for his contributions to literature, music, and the arts during the Dutch Golden Age. A polymath, he served as secretary to the Princes of Orange and was knighted by both James I of England and Louis XIII of France. Huygens was a prolific writer, producing poetry, musical compositions, and architectural designs, including his estate, Hofwijck. He was a member of the Muiderkring, corresponded with prominent figures like René Descartes and John Donne, and was the father of the scientist Christiaan Huygens. His legacy is honored through the Constantijn Huygens Prize, a Dutch literary award established in 1947.

trgt: The Sumunkur Ainu (Katakana: スムンクㇽ, literally "west in people", i.e. "Western people") is the name of the Ainu subgroup living along the southern coast of Hokkaido, traditionally from Iburi to Hidaka. The subgroup is known to have fought with the Menasunkur Ainu to the east in the 17th century. In Japanese texts, they were also called the Haekuru Ainu (Ainu ‹See RfD› hay-kur) and the Sarunkuru Ainu (Ainu ‹See RfD› sar-un-kur, "plains-on-people", "people on the plains").
mgt: The Sumunkur Ainu were an Ainu subgroup historically residing along the Pacific coast of Hokkaido, Japan, from Niikappu to Shiraoi, with cultural and burial practices distinct from neighboring Ainu groups. Believed to descend from the ancient "Hinomoto" people, they came into conflict with the Menasunkur Ainu in the 1600s, culminating in the death of their chief Onibishi during Shakushain's Revolt. Following the revolt, Matsumae Domain's control over the Ainu, including the Sumunkur, significantly increased.

trgt: Helen Tufts Bailie (January 9, 1874 – May 1962) was a social reformer and activist. Tufts is known as outing the Daughters of the Revolution for having a blacklist about individuals and organizations, in 1928. This controversy led Tufts to be banned from the organization and to become an advocate for women's, labor, and social rights.
mgt: Helen Tufts Bailie (January 9, 1874 – May 1962) was an American social reformer and activist known for her work in women's rights, labor movements, and social justice. She gained prominence in 1928 for exposing blacklists maintained by the Daughters of the American Revolution (DAR), which targeted progressive individuals and organizations. Bailie also advocated for birth control legalization and opposed loyalty oaths for teachers.

trgt: Anas al-Kandari (1981 – October 8, 2002) was a Kuwaiti citizen. He died in a firefight on Faylaka Island, with United States Marines on October 8, 2002. A U.S. Marine was also killed in the incident as was Al Kandari's fellow fighter Jassem al-Hajiri.
mgt: Anas Al Kandari (1981 – October 8, 2002) was a Kuwaiti national who died during an attack on U.S. Marines on Faylaka Island, Kuwait. He reportedly spent time in Afghanistan and returned to Kuwait shortly before the September 11 attacks in 2001.

trgt: Mansfield is a borough located in east-central Tioga County, Pennsylvania, United States, in the Tioga River valley. It is situated at the intersection of U.S. Route 6 and U.S. Business Route 15, about 36 miles (58 km) southwest of Elmira, New York.
mgt: Mansfield is a borough in Tioga County, Pennsylvania, United States, settled in 1804 and incorporated in 1857. It is home to Mansfield University of Pennsylvania and is notable for hosting the first night football game under electric lights in 1892.

trgt: Boyd Gail Harris Jr. (October 15, 1931 – November 14, 2012) was an American professional baseball first baseman who played for the New York Giants and Detroit Tigers of Major League Baseball (MLB) during all or part of six seasons between 1955 and 1960. In 1958, Harris hit a career high 20 home runs with the Tigers. Harris batted and threw left-handed and was listed as 6 feet (1.8 m) tall and 195 pounds (88 kg).
mgt: Gail Harris (October 15, 1931 – November 14, 2012) was an American professional baseball player who played as a first baseman in Major League Baseball (MLB) for the New York Giants and Detroit Tigers from 1955 to 1960. He was the last player to hit a home run for the New York Giants before their move to San Francisco. Harris had a career batting average of .240 with 51 home runs and 190 RBIs in 437 MLB games.

trgt: Divided Soul: The Life of Marvin Gaye is a 1985 biography of American soul singer Marvin Gaye. The biography was written by music reviewer David Ritz including conversations he had with the singer, who put the biography together shortly after Gaye's death at the hands of his father Marvin Gay, Sr. in 1984.
mgt: *Divided Soul: The Life of Marvin Gaye* is a 1985 biography of American singer Marvin Gaye, written by David Ritz. Based on extensive interviews with Gaye, including during his self-imposed exile in Belgium, the book explores his troubled childhood, career with Motown, personal struggles, and relationships. Released after Gaye's death, it became a bestseller.

trgt: Cecil Lewis Alexander (born August 2, 1935) is a former influential Democratic politician and lobbyist in Arkansas. He was a member of the Arkansas House of Representatives, serving from 1963 to 1979, including a term as Speaker of the House. Alexander later became a lobbyist followed by a tenure as chair of the Arkansas Racing Commission.
mgt: Cecil L. Alexander (born August 2, 1935) is an American businessman, politician, and lobbyist who served as the 69th Speaker of the Arkansas House of Representatives from 1975 to 1977. A Democratic member of the Arkansas House from 1963 to 1979, he focused on promoting tourism in the Ozarks. After retiring from politics, Alexander became a prominent lobbyist and served on the Arkansas Racing Commission.

trgt: Samira Ćosović (Serbian Cyrillic: Самира Ћосовић; born 19 November 1973) is a politician in Serbia. She was a member of the National Assembly of Serbia from 2020 to 2022 and is currently the deputy mayor of Prijepolje. Ćosović is a member of the Social Democratic Party of Serbia (SDPS) and also of its regional affiliate, the Sandžak Democratic Party.
mgt: Samira Ćosović is a Serbian language and literature teacher and politician. She has been a member of the Prijepolje municipal assembly since 2008 and served as vice-president of the Sandžak Democratic Party's municipal board. Ćosović was a member of the National Assembly of Serbia from 2020 to 2022 and was appointed deputy mayor of Prijepolje in December 2021.

trgt:
"The Crocodile" is the fourth episode of the second season of the American ABC fantasy/drama television series Once Upon a Time, and the show's 26th episode overall, which aired on October 21, 2012.

It was co-written by David H. Goodman and Robert Hull, while being directed by David Solomon.

In this episode, Mr. Gold, David, and Ruby go searching for a missing Belle, while flashbacks show Rumpelstiltskin's past with Captain Hook.
mgt: "The Crocodile" is the fourth episode of the second season of the American fantasy drama series *Once Upon a Time*. Directed by David Solomon and written by David H. Goodman and Robert Hull, it originally aired on October 21, 2012. The episode explores the backstory of Captain Hook (Killian Jones) and his rivalry with Rumplestiltskin, while also focusing on Belle's struggle to assert her independence in Storybrooke. It received critical acclaim for its character development, action, and Colin O'Donoghue's performance as Captain Hook.

trgt: Vinita Marwaha Madill (born 9 January 1987) is a British Space Operations engineer and science communicator. She is based at the European Space Agency through TERMA B.V. in the Netherlands, where she is responsible for the development of the European Robotic Arm. She founded the platform Rocket Women, which uses stories and interviews to encourage women to study STEM.
mgt: Vinita Marwaha Madill (born 9 January 1987) is a British space operations engineer, advocate for women in STEM, and founder of the platform Rocket Women. She has worked with the European Space Agency, NASA, and the German Aerospace Center, contributing to astronaut training and space medicine. Recognized for her advocacy, she has been featured in *Elle* and *Fast Company* and regularly speaks on space and representation in science.

trgt: WiQuest Communications, Inc., was a fabless semiconductor company that designed and developed Wireless USB (WUSB) products using WiMedia Alliance technology for high-speed, short-range applications. WiQuest offered integrated circuits, software, and reference designs. WiQuest was headquartered in Allen, Texas, and had operations in Bangalore, India.
mgt: WiQuest Communications was a private computer hardware company founded in 2003 in Allen, Texas. Specializing in Wireless USB technology, it held an 85% market share in early 2008, with customers including Toshiba, Dell, and Lenovo. The company ceased operations on October 31, 2008, due to challenges facing Wireless USB technology and economic conditions. Its Bangalore operations were acquired by Staccato Communications.

trgt:
Margaret Dayton (born 1949) is an American politician from Utah. A member of the Republican Party, she served longer in the Utah Legislature than any other woman in Utah history. After serving a decade in the Utah House of Representatives, she unseated an incumbent senator in 2006 and served in that position until June 2018, when she resigned for medical reasons.

She represented the 15th Senate District which includes parts of Provo, Orem, Vineyard, Lindon and Pleasant Grove.
mgt: Margaret Dayton (born May 17, 1949) is an American politician and former registered nurse who served as a Republican member of the Utah State Legislature. She represented the 61st district in the Utah House of Representatives from 1996 to 2007 and the 15th district in the Utah Senate from 2007 to 2018. Known for her conservative positions, Dayton sponsored notable legislation on abortion data collection and housing policy. She is a recipient of the Friend of Taxpayer Award and an honorary doctorate from the Utah System of Higher Education.

trgt: The Philadelphia Jewish Film Festival (also known as the Gershman Philadelphia Jewish Film Festival (GPJFF)) is an annual film festival in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, in the United States that presents movies and film-related programs about the Jewish experience, culture, values, and legacy.
mgt: The Philadelphia Jewish Film Festival, founded in 1981, is the second-oldest Jewish film festival in the United States. Hosted by the Gershman Y in Philadelphia, it showcases Jewish-themed films annually, featuring international works and audience awards.

trgt: Stanstead is a village and civil parish in Suffolk, England. The name Stanstead comes from the Old English for "Stony place". Located off the B1066, it is around 4 miles (6 km) from Sudbury, and is part of Babergh district. It is about 2 kilometres (1 mi) from Glemsford, 12 miles (19 km) from Hadleigh, and 4 miles (6 km) from Lavenham.
mgt: Stanstead is a village and civil parish in the Babergh district of Suffolk, England, with a population of 319 as of the 2011 Census. Located in the Glem Valley northeast of Sudbury, the village's name derives from the Anglo-Saxon term for "stony place," reflecting its glacial flint-strewn fields. The village features a "T-shaped" layout and is served by local schools and limited bus services.

trgt: The agile gibbon (Hylobates agilis), also known as the black-handed gibbon, is an Old World primate in the gibbon family. It is found in Indonesia on the island of Sumatra, Malaysia, and southern Thailand. The species is listed as endangered on the IUCN Red List due to habitat destruction and the pet trade.
mgt: The agile gibbon (*Hylobates agilis*) is an endangered primate species in the gibbon family, Hylobatidae, native to the rainforests of Sumatra, the Malay Peninsula, and southern Thailand. Known for its brachiation and monogamous social structure, it primarily feeds on fruit but also consumes leaves, flowers, and insects.

trgt: St. John's Catholic Church is a historic church at 217 York Street in Bangor, Maine. Built in 1855 at the height of the anti-immigrant Know Nothing movement, it stands as a major symbol of the city's Irish-American heritage, and a high quality local example of Gothic Revival architecture. It was listed on the National Register of Historic Places in 1973.
mgt: St. John's Catholic Church is a historic Gothic Revival church located at 217 York Street in Bangor, Maine. Designed by architect Patrick Charles Keely, it was built in 1855–56 by local Irish laborers during the height of the anti-immigrant Know Nothing movement. The church features a 180-foot tower, a long nave, and an 1860 E. and G.G. Hook organ. It was added to the National Register of Historic Places in 1973 and now serves as part of St. Paul the Apostle Parish.

trgt: Planet Earth is a television and film documentary franchise produced and broadcast by the BBC. The franchise began in 2001 with the success of The Blue Planet. As of 2017, The Blue Planet has spawned 5 series and one feature film.
mgt: The *Planet Earth* franchise is a globally acclaimed series of nature documentaries produced by the BBC Natural History Unit. Originating with *The Blue Planet* (2001), the franchise includes the groundbreaking *Planet Earth* (2006) and its sequels, *Planet Earth II* (2016) and *Planet Earth III* (2022). Known for its cinematic quality, high-definition visuals, and focus on Earth's diverse habitats and species, the franchise has expanded to include companion books, films like *Earth* (2007), and live-action series such as *Planet Earth Live* (2012). Narrated by figures like Sir David Attenborough, the franchise has achieved critical and commercial success, becoming a landmark in natural history storytelling.

trgt: The Black sermonic tradition, or Black preaching tradition, is an approach to sermon (or homily) construction and delivery practiced primarily among African Americans in the Black Church. The tradition seeks to preach messages that appeal to both the intellect and the emotive dimensions of humanity. The tradition finds its roots in the painful experiences of blacks during slavery in the United States, as well as experiences during the Jim Crow era and subsequent discrimination.
mgt: The Black sermonic tradition is a distinctive style of African American preaching characterized by authenticity, rhetorical embellishments, challenges to societal structures, and a connection to ancestral struggles. It often incorporates musicality, hyperbole, and expressive body language. A notable feature is "whooping," a rhythmic and musical preaching style that emerged in the 19th century and remains influential. This tradition has also shaped African American literature and poetry, reflecting its enduring cultural and spiritual significance.

trgt: Hannah Mary Bardell (born 1 June 1983) is a Scottish politician who has served as Member of Parliament (MP) for Livingston since 2015. A member of the Scottish National Party (SNP), she served as its spokesperson for Digital, Culture, Media and Sport from 2018 to 2019.
mgt: Hannah Mary Bardell (born 1 June 1983) is a Scottish National Party (SNP) politician serving as the Member of Parliament (MP) for Livingston since 2015. She has held various spokesperson roles, including for Work and Pensions and Digital, Culture, Media and Sport. Bardell is openly LGBT and has advocated for rehabilitation and inclusivity in society.

trgt: Marilyn J. Suiter is a geologist whose professional career has spanned teaching, working the oil and gas industry and public services. In her leadership roles at both the American Geosciences Institute and the National Science Foundation, Suiter has worked over decades to increase the ethnic diversity of the geosciences.
mgt: Marilyn Suiter is an American geoscientist and educator known for her contributions to geoscience education and diversity. She has held leadership roles at the American Geosciences Institute and the National Science Foundation, where she has advanced programs promoting diversity in the geosciences. Suiter is a recipient of the Geological Society of America's Bromery Award.

trgt: Therese Ann Markow is the Amylin Chair in Life Sciences at the University of California, San Diego. Her research involves the use of genetics and ecology to study the insects of the Sonoran Desert. She was awarded the Presidential Early Career Award for Scientists and Engineers in 2001 and the Genetics Society of America George Beadle Award in 2012. Her research received widespread attention for its alleged misuse of Native American genetic data.
mgt: Therese Ann Markow is an American geneticist and ecologist known for her research on Drosophila species and their ecological and evolutionary genomics. She has held prominent academic positions, including Regents Professor at Arizona State University and the University of Arizona, and Amylin Chair in Life Sciences at the University of California, San Diego. Markow has received numerous accolades, including the Presidential Early Career Award and the Genetics Society of America’s George Beadle Award. Her career has also been marked by controversy over the unauthorized use of Havasupai DNA samples.
