There is a provincial advocacy organisation Fédération des Francophones de Terre-Neuve et du Labrador, representing both the Peninsular French and Acadian French communities.
mgt:
Newfoundland French (français terre-neuvien) is a moribund dialect of French historically spoken on the Port au Port Peninsula in Newfoundland, Canada. With fewer than 500 native speakers remaining, it is considered endangered. The dialect originated from Breton, Norman, and Basque fishermen who settled in the region during the 17th and 18th centuries, despite restrictions on French settlement under British rule. Additional influences came from Acadian immigrants in the 19th century. 

The decline of Newfoundland French began in the mid-20th century, exacerbated by the establishment of an American air force base in Stephenville and the lack of French-language education. Assimilation into the anglophone majority, intermarriage, and mandatory English schooling further contributed to its decline. Since Newfoundland joined Canada in 1949, the dialect has been largely replaced by Acadian French or standard Quebec French. Efforts are underway to preserve and reintroduce Newfoundland French as part of the province's cultural heritage.

trgt: The 2000 Save Mart/Kragen 350 was the 16th stock car race of the 2000 NASCAR Winston Cup Series and the 12th iteration of the event. The race was held on Sunday, June 25, 2000, in Sonoma, California, at the club layout in Sears Point Raceway, a 1.99 miles (3.20 km) permanent road course layout. The race took the scheduled 112 laps to complete. At race's end, Hendrick Motorsports driver Jeff Gordon would dominate the late stages of a chaotic race to win his 51st career NASCAR Winston Cup Series win and his first of the season. To fill out the podium, Sterling Marlin of Team SABCO and Mark Martin of Roush Racing would finish second and third, respectively.
mgt: The 2000 Save Mart/Kragen 350 was the 16th race of the 2000 NASCAR Winston Cup Series, held on June 25, 2000, at Sears Point Raceway in Sonoma, California. The race took place on the 1.99-mile road course, which featured the Chute layout, a configuration used exclusively for NASCAR events. Jeff Gordon of Hendrick Motorsports won the race, leading 43 of the 112 laps, while Rusty Wallace of Penske-Kranefuss Racing secured the pole position with a lap time of 1:10.652. The event was broadcast on ESPN, with commentary by Bob Jenkins, Ned Jarrett, and Benny Parsons. The race covered a total distance of 218.288 miles, with an average speed of 78.789 mph.

trgt:
Hainan HNA Infrastructure Investment Group Co., Ltd. formerly Hainan Island Construction Co., Ltd. is a Chinese listed company based in Haikou. in mid-2016 the company acquired HNA Infrastructure Group (Chinese: 海航基础产业集团) in a reverse IPO from intermediate parent company HNA Infrastructure Holding Group (Chinese: 海航基础控股集团), which HNA Infrastructure Group is the parent company of HNA Real Estate and HNA Airport Group (Chinese: 海航机场集团, in turn HNA Airport Group is the parent company of HNA Airport Holdings (Group) (Chinese: 海航机场控股(集团)) and HNA Airport Holdings is the parent company of Sanya Phoenix International Airport Co., Ltd.); HNA Airport Group is the largest shareholder of Haikou Meilan International Airport (19.58% as at October 2016), which in turn the largest shareholder of Hong Kong listed company HNA Infrastructure. In December 2016 a proposed capital increase of the HK-listed company was announced. HNA Infrastructure Investment Group would purchase a minority share directly. Haikou Meilan International Airport was the second-largest shareholder of Hainan Airlines; Hainan Airlines, however, also owned a minority stake in Haikou Meilan International Airport.

The registered office was located in Meilan District, but the general office was located in West Zone 21/F, HNA Building on 7 Guoxing Avenue.
mgt:
HNA Infrastructure Investment Group Co., Ltd. is a Chinese public company specializing in real estate and transport infrastructure, including the construction and operation of airports and seaports. Founded on May 12, 1993, and headquartered in Haikou, Hainan, the company has undergone several name changes, reflecting its evolving focus and ownership structure. Initially established as The First Investment and Merchant, it became a listed company on the Shanghai Stock Exchange in 2002 and was later renamed Hainan Zhuxin Investment, Hainan Island Construction, and finally HNA Infrastructure Investment Group in 2015.

The company is a subsidiary of HNA Infrastructure Holding Group, part of the larger HNA Group, and has played a significant role in HNA's infrastructure development strategy. Over the years, it has been involved in complex ownership restructurings, including reverse takeovers and share transfers among HNA subsidiaries. Notable financial activities include the issuance of bonds to fund infrastructure projects, such as the Nanhai Pearl Artificial Island. HNA Infrastructure Investment Group's operations and ownership are closely tied to HNA Group's broader business interests, making it a key player in China's infrastructure and real estate sectors.

trgt:
In object-oriented programming, method cascading is syntax which allows multiple methods to be called on the same object. This is particularly applied in fluent interfaces.

For example, in Dart, the cascade:

is equivalent to the individual calls:

Method cascading is much less common than method chaining – it is found only in a handful of object-oriented languages, while chaining is very common. A form of cascading can be implemented using chaining, but this restricts the interface; see comparison with method chaining, below.
mgt: Method cascading is a programming technique that allows multiple method calls or property accesses on the same object without repeatedly referencing the object. It is commonly used in fluent interfaces and is supported in languages like Smalltalk, Dart, and Visual Basic. Cascading simplifies code by reducing redundancy, particularly when working with complex expressions or temporary variables. Unlike method chaining, cascading evaluates to the original object rather than the method's return value. It was first introduced in Smalltalk and later adopted in other languages with variations in implementation.

trgt:
The Sugar Loaf Islands (often Sugarloaf; Lang-mi, lit. 'the islands') are a collection of five small uninhabited islands and several sea stacks near Port Taranaki, New Zealand.

The largest, Moturoa Island, covers approximately 1.4 hectares (3.5 acres). Motumahanga is the island furthest from shore, at approximately 1.5 kilometres (0.93 miles).

Ngā Motu was one of the first areas inhabited by descendants of Te Whiti o Rongomai, and the islands and reefs were all named by Ngāti Te Whiti. The island group was given its English name in 1770 by James Cook because the reminded him of the way sugar was stored in heaps in Europe.

The Sugar Loaf Islands Marine Protected Area (SLIMPA) was established in 1991 to protect the area from oil exploration. This strengthened the protection that had been in place since the formation of a marine park in 1986.

In 2013 New Plymouth district councillors unanimously agreed to gift the protected area back to the government for treaty settlement negotiations with Taranaki and Te Āti Awa iwi.
mgt:
The Sugar Loaf Islands (Ngā Motu in Māori) are a group of volcanic islands and rock outcrops located near Port Taranaki, New Zealand. Divided into inner and outer groups, the islands include Mataora (Round Rock), Pararaki (Seagull Rock), Motuotamatea (Snapper Rock), Motumahanga (Saddleback Island), and Moturoa, along with smaller outcrops such as Waikaranga (Seal Rocks) and Whareumu (Lion Rock). The islands, formed from volcanic activity approximately 1.7 million years ago, are composed of porphyritic hornblende andesite and are the type locality of taranakite, a phosphate mineral.

Historically, the islands served as hunting, fishing, and refuge grounds for the Taranaki and Te Āti Awa peoples. In 1991, the Sugar Loaf Islands Marine Park was established, protecting 752.5 hectares of land and sea from fishing and mining. The area is home to species such as reef herons, little blue penguins, New Zealand fur seals, and orcas, and lies along migration routes for oceanic birds and cetaceans.

trgt:
Ultraman Powered is a fighting game developed by Tose and published by Bandai for the 3DO Interactive Multiplayer. The player takes on the role of the extraterrestrial superhero Ultraman, tasked with protecting the Earth from destructive aliens and monsters. Gameplay primarily consists of one-on-one battles where the player must deplete an adversary's health meter using both basic and special fighting techniques. The game also contains 3D rail shooter sections and a two-player versus option.

The game is based on the 1993 television series Ultraman: The Ultimate Hero, known in Japan as Ultraman Powered. The game was created using digitized backgrounds and character sprites from this series and uses clips from it as full-motion video (FMV) cutscenes. Ultraman Powered was released exclusively in Japan on March 20, 1994 as a launch title for the 3DO in that region. Global critical reception for the game has been mixed with many reviewers complimenting its graphics but panning its slow gameplay and controls.
mgt:
*Ultraman Powered* is a 1994 fighting video game developed by Tose and published by Bandai for the 3DO console. Based on the 1993 home video series *Ultraman: The Ultimate Hero* (known in Japan as *Ultraman Powered*), the game features the titular superhero defending Earth from alien and monster threats. Players engage in one-on-one battles using basic attacks and special moves powered by a replenishing energy meter. The game includes single-player modes such as Visual Mode, which features FMV clips from the series, and Battle Mode, which combines rail-shooter segments with traditional fighting gameplay. A multiplayer Versus Mode allows two players to compete.

The game was a launch title for the Japanese 3DO market and was praised for its digitized graphics and character sprites, which were adapted from the series. However, its slow gameplay and poor controls received criticism. Despite mixed reviews, *Ultraman Powered* remains notable for its connection to the *Ultraman* franchise and its early use of FMV and pre-rendered visuals.

trgt:
Heifer International (also known as Heifer Project International) is a global nonprofit working to eradicate poverty and hunger through sustainable, values-based holistic community development. Heifer International distributes animals, along with agricultural and values-based training, to families in need around the world as a means of providing self-sufficiency. Recipients must agree to "pass on the gift" by donating animal offspring, as well as sharing the skills and knowledge of animal husbandry and agricultural training with other impoverished families in the community. The organization receives financial support from the Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation, BlackRock, Cargill, Mastercard Foundation, Walmart and the W. K. Kellogg Foundation.

Based in Little Rock, Arkansas, United States, Heifer International started with a shipment of 17 heifers to Puerto Rico in 1944.
mgt:
Heifer International is a global nonprofit organization founded in 1944 by Dan West, a Church of the Brethren relief worker, to combat hunger and poverty through sustainable development. Headquartered in Little Rock, Arkansas, the organization began by providing livestock, starting with heifers, to impoverished families, enabling them to achieve self-sufficiency. Its model, "Passing on the Gift," encourages recipients to donate the first offspring of their livestock to another family, fostering community development. Over time, Heifer expanded its scope to include a variety of animals and agroecological initiatives worldwide.

The organization has received numerous accolades, including the Conrad N. Hilton Humanitarian Prize and recognition from U.S. presidents. Despite its impact, Heifer has faced scrutiny over its agricultural assistance model. It continues to focus on empowering rural communities through sustainable practices and economic opportunities.

trgt:
Anything But Conservative (Lang-fr or N'importe quoi d'autre que conservateur), also known as the ABC campaign and Vote ABC, is a political campaign which aims to defeat the Conservatives in Canadian federal elections. It encourages strategic voting.

Canadian federal elections use a first-past-the-post voting system, which allows a party with minority support among the population to win the majority of the seats in the Parliament. Given the make-up of existing political parties in the 21st century in Canada, the centre and centre-left vote tends to split between the federal NDP, Liberals and Greens, which allows for the Conservatives to win a disproportional number of seats in the parliament compared to their popular support.

It is estimated by ABC campaigners that widespread strategic voting in 2015 federal elections decreased the number of Conservative seats by over 72.
mgt:
"Anything But Conservative" (ABC) refers to a series of strategic voting campaigns in Canada aimed at preventing the election of Conservative Party candidates. The term gained prominence during the 2008 federal election, when Newfoundland and Labrador Premier Danny Williams launched the ABC campaign in response to what he viewed as a broken promise by Prime Minister Stephen Harper regarding equalization payments. The campaign successfully shut out federal Conservative candidates in Newfoundland and Labrador, though it had limited impact nationally.

The ABC strategy has since been associated with grassroots efforts to encourage voters to support the most viable non-Conservative candidate in their ridings. Similar campaigns emerged in subsequent elections, including the 2015 federal election, where strategic voting contributed to a Liberal majority government. The movement has been supported by unions and advocacy groups, though it has also faced criticism for its impact on smaller progressive parties like the NDP.

trgt: Sheikh Ahmad bin Ali bin Abdullah bin Jassim bin Mohammed Al Thani (Lang-ar; 1922 – 25 November 1977) was the Emir of Qatar who ruled from 1960 to 1972. Qatar's financial status witnessed significant improvements during his reign as a result of the enrichment and discovery of several new oil fields. Qatar also gained its independence as a sovereign state in September 1971 under his rule. He was deposed in February 1972 by his cousin, Khalifa bin Hamad Al Thani.
mgt: Ahmad bin Ali Al Thani (1922 – 25 November 1977) was the Emir of Qatar from 1960 to 1972. He ascended to the throne following his father’s abdication and oversaw Qatar’s independence from Britain in 1971. His reign was marked by significant economic growth driven by oil discoveries and the establishment of modern administrative systems. However, his rule faced internal dissent, including nationalist movements. In 1972, he was deposed in a bloodless coup by his cousin, Sheikh Khalifa bin Hamad Al Thani. Ahmad died in exile in 1977.

trgt:
This is Orson Welles is a 1992 book by Orson Welles (1915–1985) and Peter Bogdanovich that comprises conversations between the two filmmakers recorded over several years, beginning in 1969.: xxiv The wide-ranging volume encompasses Welles's life and his own stage, radio, and film work as well as his insights on the work of others. The interview book was transcribed by Bogdanovich after Welles's death, at the request of Welles's longtime companion and professional collaborator, Oja Kodar.

In addition to more than 300 pages of interviews, the book includes an annotated chronology of Welles's career, a summary of the alterations made in Welles's 1942 masterpiece, The Magnificent Ambersons, and notes on each chapter by film scholar Jonathan Rosenbaum, who edited the volume.

A second edition of This is Orson Welles was published in paperback in 1998, with a new introduction by Bogdanovich and excerpts of a 58-page memo Welles wrote Universal Pictures about the editing of his 1958 film Touch of Evil. The memo was used to create a director's cut of the film released in 1998.

The 1992 audiobook version of This is Orson Welles was nominated for a Grammy Award for Best Spoken Word or Non-Musical Album.
mgt:
*This Is Orson Welles* is a 1992 book by filmmaker Peter Bogdanovich and actor-director Orson Welles, published by HarperCollins. The book is a compilation of interviews between the two, recorded over several years in the United States, Mexico, and Europe. It offers insights into Welles's life, career, and creative process, as well as his perspectives on filmmaking and the industry. The interviews are loosely organized, reflecting the conversational nature of their discussions, and were extensively revised by Welles before his death in 1985.

The project originated in 1968 when Welles suggested to Bogdanovich, a rising filmmaker and admirer of Welles's work, that they collaborate on a book. Work on the book was intermittent, with delays caused by Welles's other commitments, including an offer to write his memoirs. After Welles's death, Bogdanovich resumed work on the project with the assistance of film critic Jonathan Rosenbaum, who edited the extensive transcripts into the final published version.

The book also includes a detailed chronology of Welles's career and serves as a significant resource for understanding his artistic legacy. It was released in print and audiobook formats and remains a key text for fans and scholars of Welles and cinema history.

page_title: Prostitution in the Democratic Republic of the Congo
trgt:
Prostitution in the Democratic Republic of the Congo is legal but related activities are prohibited. The Congolese penal code punishes pimping, running a bawdy house or brothel, the exploitation of debauchery or prostitution, as well as forced prostitution. Activities that incite minors or promote the prostitution of others have been criminalised. The government does little to enforce the law. During the colonial era and the years that followed independence, the Ministry of Health issued calling cards identifying professional sex workers and provided them with medical health checks. However, this system was abandoned in the 1980s. Public order laws are sometimes used against sex workers. Street prostitutes report harassment, violence and extortion from the police. UNAIDS estimated there are 2.9 million sex workers in the country.

Food insecurity and extreme poverty are now the main reasons why women in the DRC become prostitutes. Traders make up the majority of clients, along with officials working for national and international NGOs. Many sex workers earn between $2 and $5 and payment is sometimes made in the form of food or other goods. Prostitutes working in bars and nightclubs receive between $10 and $20, and are known as "Londoners" as they dress like British girls on a Saturday night out. "VIP prostitution" operates from hotels, with sex workers earning between $50 and $100. Some Congolese prostitutes are from abroad or homeless children who have been accused of witchcraft.
mgt:
Prostitution in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) is widespread and driven by poverty, with sex workers facing significant health and safety risks. The country has one of the highest HIV prevalence rates among sex workers in Central Africa, at 5.7% compared to 0.7% in the general population, according to a 2016 UNAIDS report. Efforts by organizations like Médecins Sans Frontières aim to promote condom use among sex workers, though reluctance among clients remains a challenge.

Child prostitution is a significant issue in the DRC, often overlooked by authorities. Non-governmental organizations (NGOs) such as Association de Solidarité Internationale (ASI) and Reiper work to address the problem. Reports have documented sexual abuse of children by soldiers during the country's wars, as well as instances of child exploitation by criminal gangs and peacekeeping forces.

The DRC is also a source, destination, and transit country for sex trafficking. Women and children are subjected to forced marriage, sexual slavery, and trafficking both within the country and abroad. Some families are deceived into sending their children to cities like Kinshasa under false promises of education, only for them to be exploited. While the 2006 sexual violence statute criminalizes sexual slavery, trafficking, and forced prostitution, enforcement remains inadequate. The U.S. Department of State ranks the DRC as a Tier 3 country for human trafficking.

trgt:
William Forsythe (born June 7, 1955) is an American actor. He is best known for his portrayal of tough-guy, criminal characters, and has starred in films such as American Me (1992), Raising Arizona (1987), Dick Tracy (1990), Gotti (1996), The Rock (1996), and The Devil's Rejects (2005). He has also played recurring characters in television series such as Boardwalk Empire (2010) and Justified (2010).

Forsythe was nominated for the Independent Spirit Award for Best Supporting Male for his performance in the indie film The Waterdance.
mgt: William Forsythe (born June 7, 1955) is an American actor known for his versatile roles in film and television. Born in Brooklyn, New York, he began his career with minor roles and guest appearances in popular TV shows like *CHiPs* and *Hill Street Blues*. Forsythe gained recognition for his performances in films such as *Once Upon a Time in America* (1984), *Raising Arizona* (1987), and *Dick Tracy* (1990). He has portrayed notable characters, including Al Capone in *The Untouchables* (1993) and John Wayne Gacy in *Dear Mr. Gacy* (2010).

trgt:
A marine evacuation system (MES) is a lifesaving device found on many modern passenger ships or oil rigs consisting of an inflatable slide or escape chute where a passenger can evacuate straight into waiting life rafts. Developed in 1979 by RFD, a New Zealand-based company that distributes safety equipment; MES is replacing traditional davit-launched life rafts used on ships.

MESs are common on high speed craft, where weight and evacuation times must be kept to a minimum, although many conventional ferries and cruise ships are now fitting MESs to complement or replace lifeboats.

The main manufacturers of MES are Survitec Group (RFD Beaufort and Brude Safety) Zodiac, Viking, ALBE Engineering & Consulting GmbH in Germany and Liferaft Systems Australia. They take very little space on deck, positioned on the front of the embarkation deck and on the sides of the ship. MES requires little time and effort to assemble, which is crucial in times of emergency.
mgt:
A marine evacuation system (MES) is a life-saving appliance designed for the rapid and efficient evacuation of passengers and crew from ships during emergencies. Over the past 30 years, it has gained popularity for its ability to evacuate over 700 people within 30 minutes, making it a critical safety feature in the maritime industry. The system has been rigorously tested under challenging conditions, including Beaufort Sea State 6 winds and 3-meter wave heights, and complies with the EU Directive of Marine Equipment 96/98/EC and IMO Resolution A689(17).

The MES is designed for quick deployment, requiring only one or two crew members to activate. Once deployed, a chute extends into the water, and an inflatable life raft is automatically activated within 90 seconds, with some systems inflating in under a minute. Passengers descend through air-cushioned chutes into the rafts, which are capable of accommodating over 700 individuals. The system consists of five main components.

trgt: St Richard's Church is an Anglican church in the town of Haywards Heath in the district of Mid Sussex, one of seven local government districts in the English county of West Sussex. The present reinforced concrete and brick structure replaced a temporary building which was a daughter church to Haywards Heath's parish church, St Wilfrid's; the new church soon became parished in its own right to reflect the rapid population growth in the northern part of the town. English Heritage has listed the 1930s building at Grade II for its architectural and historical importance.
mgt: St Richard's Church is a Grade II listed Church of England parish church in Haywards Heath, West Sussex, dedicated to Richard of Chichester. Founded in 1937 and consecrated in 1938, it replaced an earlier "tin tabernacle" chapel built in 1897. Designed by architect L. Keir Hett in an Art Deco and Scandinavian style, the church is notable for its reinforced concrete construction, cruciform layout, and steeply pitched roof. The parish, established in 1939, serves the northern part of Haywards Heath. The church features stained glass, carved fittings, and a tiled crucifix on its east side.

trgt:
The 1986 New York Mets season was the Mets' 25th season in the National League. They improved from a 98–64 record in 1985 to finish the season with a franchise record 108–54 record, giving them the division title. They went on to defeat the Houston Astros in six games in the NLCS and the American League champion Boston Red Sox in seven games in the World Series. This is their last championship to date.

The 1986 Mets are one of just three National League teams (the other two are the 1975 Cincinnati Reds and the 2022 Los Angeles Dodgers) to have won twice as many games as lost in the regular season since 1909. The team is often regarded as one of the best of all-time, being cited in 2021 by ESPN as a top contender for best MLB team of the previous half-century from 1970 to 2020.
mgt:
The 1986 New York Mets season was one of the most memorable in Major League Baseball history, culminating in the team winning the World Series. Managed by Davey Johnson, the Mets dominated the National League East with a franchise-record 108 wins, finishing 21.5 games ahead of the second-place Philadelphia Phillies. The team featured a mix of rising stars like Dwight Gooden and Darryl Strawberry, and seasoned veterans such as Keith Hernandez and Gary Carter. 

The Mets' postseason run included a dramatic National League Championship Series victory over the Houston Astros and a legendary World Series against the Boston Red Sox. Game 6 of the World Series, highlighted by Mookie Wilson's ground ball through Bill Buckner's legs, remains one of baseball's most iconic moments. The Mets ultimately defeated the Red Sox in seven games, securing their second championship in franchise history. The season was later chronicled in the ESPN documentary *Once Upon a Time in Queens*.

trgt:
Portugal is generally considered as successful in upholding the civil liberties and protecting the human rights of its citizens. Portugal has proved to be determined in promoting and respecting human rights at an international and national level. The country's minister of Justice as of September 2018, Francisca Van Dunem, said that Portugal has had "a good track record" on human rights but violations still do persist.

Portugal is a member state of the European Union and therefore its citizens are protected by its Charter of Fundamental Rights. In addition to being a member of the European Union, Portugal is also a member state of the United Nations and has signed the Universal Declaration of Human Rights.
mgt: Human rights in Portugal have undergone significant progress, particularly following the 1974 Carnation Revolution, which ended decades of authoritarian rule and established a democratic government. The 1976 Constitution guaranteed fundamental rights, and Portugal became an active member of international human rights organizations, including the United Nations and the European Union. The country has made advancements in areas such as healthcare, gender equality, LGBT rights, and refugee protections. Notable achievements include the decriminalization of homosexuality in 1982, the legalization of same-sex marriage in 2010, and progressive abortion laws. However, challenges remain, including child poverty, housing conditions for marginalized communities, and allegations of police mistreatment, prompting calls for further reforms and oversight.

trgt: The Jamaat al Muslimeen coup attempt was an attempt to overthrow the government of Trinidad and Tobago, instigated on Friday, 27 July 1990. Over the course of six days, Jamaat al Muslimeen, a radical extremist Islamist group, held hostages (including Prime Minister A. N. R. Robinson and other government officials) at the Red House and at the headquarters of the state-owned national television broadcaster, Trinidad and Tobago Television (TTT). On 1 August, the insurgents surrendered. They were charged with treason, but were ordered released by the Court of Appeal. Twenty four people were killed and many more were injured in the coup.
mgt: The Jamaat al Muslimeen coup attempt was an armed insurrection in Trinidad and Tobago from July 27 to August 1, 1990, led by Yasin Abu Bakr and the Jamaat al Muslimeen. The group stormed the Red House, the seat of Parliament, taking Prime Minister A. N. R. Robinson and other officials hostage, while also seizing a television station. The coup resulted in 24 deaths, widespread looting, and significant property damage. After six days, the insurgents surrendered under an amnesty agreement. The event exposed political, economic, and security vulnerabilities in the country, leading to long-term investigations and reforms.

trgt:
Mijar is a village in the Manglore taluk in the city of Mangaluru of Dakshina Kannada district of the Indian state of Karnataka. Tenkamijaru Grama Panchayat which is 30 km from Mangalore, 12 km from the famous pilgrimage site Jain Kashi. The Grama Panchayat Contains 2 Revenue Villages, cultivation as the main source of income, the level of education and the quality of life of the people are in general. Gram Panchayat consists of Scheduled Castes, Scheduled Tribes, Backward Classes and Others.Hindu Muslim Christian, Jains lead lives in harmony.

The village has a total of 7 wards and has 19 members, Anganwadi Primary School, High School and PU College are located in the Gram Panchayat area. Includes 2 Engineering Colleges and MBA Courses. Ayurvedic herbal preparation parmesis, operating cashew factory, are useful in improving the quality of life of people.The Panchayath has its own fixed and variable assets and is always responsive to people's problems

There are mosques and temples in the Panchayath area, The temple of Lord Vishnu and Somanatheshwara the village headman The Dargah of Muslims in Thodar is religiously famous, People are living in communion with social politics and religion.
mgt:
Mijar, also known as Mijaru, is a village in the Dakshina Kannada district of Karnataka, India. Located in the Tulu Nadu region, it falls under the Mangalore taluk and is governed by a gram panchayat. The village spans an area of 23.84 square kilometers and had a population of 8,480 as of the 2011 census. The primary languages spoken in Mijar are Tulu, Kannada, Konkani, and Beary.

Situated along National Highway 169, Mijar is approximately 30 kilometers from Mangalore city and 20 kilometers from Mangalore International Airport. It is also close to other towns such as Moodbidri, Udupi, and Karkala. The village is home to several educational institutions, including the Mangalore Institute of Technology and Engineering and Alvas Institute of Engineering and Technology.

Mijar is known for its religious significance, with numerous temples such as Sri Vishnumoorthi Temple, Sri Somanatheshwara Temple, and Sri Bhoothanatheshwara Temple. The Nandini River, originating from Kanakabettu in Mijar, flows westward, encircling the Sri Kateel Durgaparameshwari Temple. Historically, women in the village engaged in rolling beedies as a source of income.

trgt: The Western Tapes, 1983 is an EP by American country rock band Lone Justice, released on 23 November 2018 by Omnivore Recordings. It consists of demo recordings made in 1983 prior to their self-titled debut release in 1985 and was recorded by the original Lone Justice lineup consisting of Maria McKee, Ryan Hedgecock, Dave Harrington and Don Willens. All tracks are previously unissued, except "Drugstore Cowboy" which was included on the compilation album This World Is Not My Home in 1999. David Mansfield, who has toured with Bob Dylan and later became a member of Bruce Hornsby and the Range, guests on pedal steel guitar and fiddle.
mgt: *The Western Tapes, 1983* is an EP by the American country rock band Lone Justice, released on November 23, 2018, by Omnivore Recordings. The six-track collection features demo recordings from 1983, including five tracks produced by Marvin Etzioni at the Record Plant in Los Angeles and one earlier track recorded at Mystic Sound. The EP showcases the band's early sound, blending country, rock, and alternative influences, and includes early versions of songs later re-recorded for their 1985 debut album. Critics praised the EP for its raw energy, authenticity, and Maria McKee's standout vocals, highlighting its significance in capturing Lone Justice's formative years.

trgt:
Youngblood is the third studio album by Australian pop rock band 5 Seconds of Summer. The album was initially set to be released on 22 June 2018, but was later brought forward to 15 June. Four singles were released in support of the album: "Want You Back", "Youngblood", "Valentine" and "Lie to Me". The album was released to positive reviews, with critics praising the lyrical maturity and the sound change from their previous work. In support of the album, the band embarked on the Meet You There Tour.

As well as appearing on a multitude of weekly and 2018 and 2019 year-end charts over several countries and receiving multiple certifications, the album's title track, "Youngblood", impacted the ARIA decade-end chart (2010-2019), which ranks the most popular songs of the decade, at number thirty-seven. In the ARIA Australian decade-end chart (2010-2019), ranking the most popular songs of the decade by Australian artists, "Youngblood" ranked at number four. "Youngblood" sold over five million adjusted copies and is the most streamed Australian song of all time on Apple Music. According to ARIA certifications,"Youngblood" is the eleventh best-selling single in Australian history. Youngblood was the most-streamed Australian album of 2018. In February 2020, the band were honored in the APRA AMCOS' The 1,000,000,000 List, for "Youngblood" having been streamed over 1 billion times.
mgt:
*Youngblood* is the third studio album by Australian pop band 5 Seconds of Summer, released on June 15, 2018, through Capitol Records. The album marked a significant stylistic shift for the band, embracing a polished pop sound with influences from 1980s music, moving away from their earlier pop-punk roots. Produced by a team including Andrew Watt, Louis Bell, and Mike Elizondo, the album was recorded primarily in Los Angeles and features singles such as "Want You Back," "Youngblood," "Valentine," and "Lie to Me." The title track, "Youngblood," achieved global success, becoming the most-streamed Australian song of all time and earning multiple platinum certifications worldwide.

The album received generally positive reviews, with critics praising its mature sound and strong songwriting, though some noted the loss of the band's earlier edge. *Youngblood* debuted at number one in Australia and the United States, making 5 Seconds of Summer the first Australian act to achieve three consecutive number-one albums in the U.S. The album was supported by the 5SOS III promotional tour and the Meet You There Tour, both of which received critical acclaim. *Youngblood* was a commercial success, earning accolades such as the ARIA Award for Best Group and recognition as one of the best albums of 2018 by *Alternative Press*.

trgt:
Shochiku Studio Co., Ltd. (株式会社松竹撮影所, Kabushiki gaisha Shōchiku Satsueijo) is a Japanese film and production studio company of Shochiku Group, which has been producing movies and dramas for roughly a century, being the second-oldest motion picture company in Japan.

The company has production bases in Kyoto and Tokyo since its inception in the 1920s, and the long-established Shochiku Kyoto Studio (松竹京都撮影所, Shōchiku Kyōto Satsueijo) is equipped with a studio facility that is deeply rooted in tradition.

It has also worked on the production of Hollywood films such as "The Last Samurai".

Shochiku Studio is known for productions of many Japanese period dramas, movies, TV and commercials.
mgt: Shochiku Studio (株式会社松竹撮影所) is a Japanese film studio and subsidiary of Shochiku, founded on October 15, 2008, and headquartered in Kyoto, Japan. It operates two main divisions: Kyoto Studio and Tokyo Studio. The studio traces its origins to the early 20th century, with Shochiku's establishment of the Kamata Studio in Tokyo in 1920. Over the decades, Shochiku Studios played a pivotal role in Japanese cinema, producing influential films and collaborating with renowned directors such as Yasujirō Ozu and Nagisa Ōshima. Today, Shochiku Studio continues to serve as a key production hub for Japanese filmmaking, with its primary operations based in Kyoto.

trgt: Necedah Shrine, officially the Queen of the Holy Rosary, Mediatrix of Peace Shrine, is a Marian shrine located in Necedah, Wisconsin. On November 12, 1949, Mary Ann Van Hoof (1909–1984) reported receiving a vision from the Blessed Virgin Mary. She claimed that in subsequent visions she was told to "bring the truth to people" through prayer and the rosary. The Roman Catholic Church investigated and found the reported visions and other phenomena indisputably faked, and when Van Hoof and her followers refused to desist, put her under interdict. Van Hoof and her followers then left the Roman Catholic Church and joined themselves to the Old Catholic movement.
mgt: The Necedah Shrine is a religious site in Necedah, Wisconsin, associated with reported Marian apparitions experienced by Mary Ann Van Hoof between 1949 and 1950. Van Hoof claimed to receive messages from the Virgin Mary, which included warnings of global chastisement and support for the Tridentine Mass. Despite attracting thousands of pilgrims, the Roman Catholic Church declared the apparitions false in 1955, and in 1975, Van Hoof and her followers were placed under interdict, leading to a schism with the Church. The shrine now operates independently, affiliated with a conservative Old Catholic organization, and includes a school, visitor center, and ongoing construction of a "House of Prayer."

trgt: Joseph Tanke (Ph.D., Boston College; born 1978) is an American philosopher and Professor of Philosophy at the University of Hawaii at Manoa. His work focuses on Continental philosophy, the history of philosophy, aesthetics, art theory, and historical ontology. Tanke is known for his analysis of major figures in the French philosophical tradition, such as Michel Foucault and Jacques Rancière. His book Foucault's Philosophy of Art: A Genealogy of Modernity is one of the first systematic presentations of Michel Foucault's writings on visual art. Tanke is the first scholar to publish a comprehensive critical introduction to the thought of Jacques Rancière.
mgt: Joseph Tanke is an American philosopher and scholar specializing in aesthetics and philosophy. He has held academic positions at institutions such as the California College of the Arts and the University of Hawaii at Manoa, where he directs the International Cultural Studies Graduate Certificate Program. Tanke has also served as the Critical Studies Fellow at the Cranbrook Academy of Art. He is the editor of the Global Aesthetic Research book series and the author of notable works, including *Foucault's Philosophy of Art: A Genealogy of Modernity* and *Jacques Rancière: An Introduction—Philosophy, Politics, Aesthetics*.

trgt: Shikantaza (只管打坐) is Dogen's Japanese translation of the Chinese phrase zhǐguǎn dǎzuò (只管打坐 / 祇管 打坐), "just sitting." The phrase was used by his teacher Rujing, a monk of the Caodong school of Zen Buddhism, to refer to the meditation-practice called "Silent Illumination" (Chinese: 默照禅), or "Serene Reflection," taught by the Caodong master Hongzhi Zhengjue (1091-1157). In Japan, it is associated with the Soto school. In shikantaza one does not focus attention on a specific object (such as the breath); instead, practitioners "just sit" in a state of conscious awareness.
mgt: Shikantaza, often translated as "just sitting," is a form of meditation central to the Soto school of Zen Buddhism. The term originates from the Sino-Japanese reading of the Chinese phrase zhǐguǎn dǎzuò, meaning "nothing but sitting" or "just sitting." Popularized by the Japanese Zen master Dōgen, shikantaza emphasizes wholehearted, single-minded sitting meditation without specific focus or goal. Rooted in the silent illumination practice of the Chinese Caodong school, it is characterized by open awareness and alertness, reflecting the reality of life in a state of quiet mindfulness.

trgt:
Elizabeth Parr-Johnston, CM (born Elizabeth H. Parr in 1939 in New York, NY) is a Canadian business woman. She is the Managing Partner of Parr-Johnston Consultants, an economic policy consultancy based in Chester Basin, Nova Scotia. Parr-Johnston is a past president of two Canadian Universities, a recipient of the 125th Anniversary of the Confederation of Canada Medal in 1992, the Queen Elizabeth II Golden Jubilee Medal in 2002 and the Order of Canada in 2008.

Raised in Essex Fells, New Jersey, she attended Grover Cleveland High School (since renamed as James Caldwell High School).
mgt: Elizabeth Parr-Johnston CM (born 1939) is an American-born Canadian economist, academic, and former university administrator. She served as the 16th president of the University of New Brunswick (1996–2002) and the 8th president of Mount Saint Vincent University (1991–1996). Parr-Johnston has held prominent roles in academia, the private sector, and the Canadian federal government, including her involvement in the Iran hostage crisis. She was appointed to the Order of Canada in 2008 for her contributions to education. Parr-Johnston resides in Nova Scotia and continues to be recognized for her leadership and public service.

trgt:
Twentieth Century is the eighteenth studio album by American country music band Alabama, released in 1999 by RCA Records. It produced the singles "(God Must Have Spent) A Little More Time on You", a collaboration with 'N Sync, "Small Stuff", "We Made Love" and "Twentieth Century", which respectively reached numbers 3, 24, 63, and 51 on the Hot Country Songs charts. In addition, "(God Must Have Spent) A Little More Time on You" was the band's last top ten hit on the country charts.

"(God Must Have Spent) A Little More Time On You" was originally recorded by 'N Sync in 1998 on their first album *NSYNC.

The album peaked at No. 5 on Billboard Country Albums chart and No. 51 on the Billboard 200.
mgt:
*Twentieth Century* is a studio album by the American country music band Alabama, released on June 15, 1999, by RCA Nashville. Produced by the band alongside Don Cook, the album was recorded at Soundshop Recording Studios in Nashville, Tennessee, and features a runtime of 45 minutes and 36 seconds. It includes tracks such as "Twentieth Century," "Mist of Desire," "Life's Too Short to Love This Fast," and a cover of NSYNC's "God Must Have Spent a Little More Time on You." 

The album received mixed reviews, with Stephen Thomas Erlewine of AllMusic rating it 2 out of 5 stars, praising its technical precision but criticizing its lack of distinct identity. Notably, Alabama's drummer Mark Herndon was not credited on the album.

trgt:
The 2018 Atlanta United FC season was the second season of Atlanta United FC's existence, and the tenth year that a professional soccer club from Atlanta, Georgia competed in the top division of American soccer. Atlanta played their home matches at Mercedes-Benz Stadium and were coached by Gerardo "Tata" Martino. Outside of MLS, the team participated in the 2018 U.S. Open Cup and the 2018 MLS Cup Playoffs, as well as various preseason competitions.

On November 17, 2017, Atlanta United announced the launch of a reserve team, Atlanta United 2, to play in what is currently named the USL Championship at the start of the 2018 season.

With a win over Chicago Fire on October 21, Atlanta qualified for the 2019 CONCACAF Champions League by having the best aggregate record across the 2017 and 2018 MLS seasons. The club later qualified for the competition by winning MLS Cup 2018, so their previously-held spot was allocated to the team with the second best aggregate record, New York Red Bulls.
mgt:
The 2018 Atlanta United FC season was the club's second season in Major League Soccer (MLS). Under the leadership of head coach Gerardo Martino, the team played its home matches at Mercedes-Benz Stadium in Atlanta, Georgia. Atlanta United finished second in both the Eastern Conference and overall league standings during the regular season. The team went on to win the MLS Cup, defeating the Portland Timbers in the final on December 8, 2018, in front of a record-setting home crowd of 73,019. This marked the club's first MLS Cup victory.

Josef Martínez was a standout player, finishing as the league's top scorer with 31 goals in the regular season and 35 in all competitions. The team also achieved its biggest win with a 5–0 victory over LAFC on April 7 but suffered its biggest defeat in a 4–0 loss to the Houston Dynamo on March 3. The season also included participation in the U.S. Open Cup, where Atlanta reached the Round of 16.

trgt: Cardea or Carda was the ancient Roman goddess of the hinge (Latin cardo, cardinis), Roman doors being hung on pivot hinges. The Augustan poet Ovid conflates her with another archaic goddess named Carna, whose festival was celebrated on the Kalends of June and for whom he gives the alternative name Cranê or Cranea, a nymph. Ovid's conflation of the goddesses is likely to have been his poetic invention, but it has also been conjectured that Carna was a contracted form of Cardina, and at minimum Ovid was observing that their traditions were congruent.
mgt: Cardea is a Roman goddess associated with doorways, thresholds, and hinges, often linked to the protection of homes and boundaries. In Roman mythology, she is connected to Forculus and Limentinus, minor deities of doorways and thresholds, and her role reflects rituals marking sacred space and boundaries. Ovid's *Fasti* recounts her transformation from the nymph Cranaë, who was raped by Janus and granted power over door hinges as compensation. Cardea is also associated with the whitethorn, a plant used to ward off harmful influences, and her mythology intertwines with Roman beliefs about domestic protection and liminality.

trgt: Ja Lama (Lang-mn, also known as Dambiijantsan, Lang-mn or Dambiijaa, Lang-mn; 1862–1922) was an adventurer and warlord of unknown birth and background who fought successive campaigns against the rule of the Qing dynasty in western Mongolia between 1890 and 1922. He claimed to be a Buddhist lama, though it is not clear whether he actually was one, as well as a grandson and later the reincarnation of Amursana, the Khoid-Oirat prince who led the last great Mongol uprising against the Qing in 1757. He was one of the commanders of Mongolian forces that liberated Khovd city from Qing control in 1912.
mgt: Ja Lama (born Dambiijantsan, 1862 – 1922) was a controversial figure in Mongolian history, known as a guerrilla leader, militant Buddhist monk, and warlord. Of uncertain origins, he claimed Kalmyk heritage and Russian citizenship. He played a significant role in Mongolia's struggle for independence from Qing China, contributing to the 1912 capture of Khovd, the last Qing stronghold in western Mongolia. Renowned for his brutality, Ja Lama ruled western Mongolia through fear, amassing wealth and power. After falling out of favor with both Russian and Mongolian authorities, he was executed in 1922 by order of the new communist government.

trgt: Somalia first participated at the Olympic Games at the 1972 Summer Games in Munich, West Germany; the Somali Olympic Committee being recognised by the International Olympic Committee shortly prior. The nation has sent athletes to compete in most Summer Olympic Games since then, boycotting in 1976 due to the inclusion of New Zealand, and in 1980 when it joined with the American-led boycott. It also did not compete in 1992 due to the ongoing effects of a famine. Somalia entered their largest contingent of athletes at the 1984 Summer Olympics, a total of seven.
mgt: Somalia has participated in the Summer Olympics since its debut at the 1972 Games in Munich, though it has never won a medal. The Somali Olympic Committee, founded in 1959 and recognized by the International Olympic Committee in 1972, oversees the nation's participation. Due to the Somali Civil War, many athletes have faced challenges training domestically, with some, like Mo Farah, representing other nations and achieving significant success. Somalia began including female athletes in 2000, despite societal challenges. Notable figures include Samia Yusuf Omar, whose tragic story highlighted the struggles of Somali athletes amid conflict.

trgt: Hope Hibbard (December 18, 1893- May 12, 1988) was an American biologist, cytologist, zoologist, and professor of zoology. Born in Altoona, Pennsylvania, she conducted research in the fields of histology and marine biology, utilizing organisms such as silkworms, limpets, earthworms, and frogs. Hibbard dedicated most of her life to education as a professor at multiple institutions, including Bryn Mawr College, Elmira College, and Oberlin College. She received accolades for her research and academic merits, such as the Sarah Berliner Research Fellowship and the Adelia A. Field Johnston Professor of Zoology. Hibbard is not only remembered for her intellectual endeavors, but for her support of women in the scientific sphere and her involvement in associations that forwarded female roles in science and research, such as the American Association of University Women (AAUW).
mgt:
Hope Hibbard (December 18, 1893 – May 12, 1988) was an American zoologist, professor, and advocate for women in science. She earned degrees in zoology from the University of Missouri and Bryn Mawr College, later completing a doctorate of science at the Sorbonne. Hibbard began her academic career at Elmira College before spending 34 years at Oberlin College, where she became the first woman to hold the title of Adelia A. Field Johnston Professor of Zoology and served as department chair. Her research spanned cytology, histology, and marine biology, and she was a trustee at the Woods Hole Marine Biological Laboratory.

A vocal advocate for women in science, Hibbard encouraged women to balance professional and personal lives. She was active in organizations like the AAUW and inspired future generations through her writings and leadership. Oberlin College established the Hope Hibbard Memorial Award in her honor.

trgt:
Aussie Gold Hunters is an Australian factual television show which follows crews of gold prospectors in Australia. The series is produced by Electric Pictures and began on the Discovery Channel on 15 September 2016. The series has been a ratings success and gone on to air in 125 countries including the United Kingdom.

In August 2022 the series was renewed for a eighth and ninth season, with season eight to premiere in 2023. Season seven concluded on 13 October 2022.
mgt: *Aussie Gold Hunters* is an Australian factual television series that premiered on the Discovery Channel on September 15, 2016. Produced by Electric Pictures, the series follows gold miners across Western Australia, Victoria, and Queensland as they use various techniques to meet their seasonal gold targets. The show has been a domestic and international ratings success, airing in 125 countries and reaching an estimated 40 million viewers. It has been renewed through its ninth season, with the eighth season introducing new teams.

trgt: Norman Louis Haggett, MBE (8 July 1926 – 22 February 2018) was an English first-class cricketer, Royal Air Force (RAF) officer and educator. Initially working as a school teacher after briefly serving with the Royal Navy in the latter years of the Second World War, Haggett joined the RAF to play for the Royal Air Force cricket team, following the recommendation of a squadron leader. His career with the RAF saw him become a member of the RAF Falcons parachute display team. After leaving the RAF in 1981, he returned to teaching, only retiring aged 91 in 2017.
mgt: Norman Louis Haggett MBE (8 July 1926 – 22 February 2018) was an English cricketer, Royal Air Force (RAF) officer, and educator. A right-handed batsman, he played four first-class cricket matches for the Combined Services cricket team between 1962 and 1964, scoring 204 runs. Serving in the RAF from 1956 to 1981, he rose to the rank of squadron leader, became a parachute instructor, and was a member of the RAF Falcons display team. After retiring, he taught at several schools, including Radley College, where he worked until 2017. Haggett was appointed MBE in 1981.

trgt:
The Subnetwork Access Protocol (SNAP) is a mechanism for multiplexing, on networks using IEEE 802.2 LLC, more protocols than can be distinguished by the 8-bit 802.2 Service Access Point (SAP) fields. SNAP supports identifying protocols by EtherType field values; it also supports vendor-private protocol identifier spaces. It is used with IEEE 802.3, IEEE 802.4, IEEE 802.5, IEEE 802.11 and other IEEE 802 physical network layers, as well as with non-IEEE 802 physical network layers such as FDDI that use 802.2 LLC.

The SNAP and LSAP fields are added to the packets at the transmitting node in order to allow the receiving node to pass each received frame to an appropriate device driver which understands given protocol.
mgt: The Subnetwork Access Protocol (SNAP) is a networking protocol used to extend the capabilities of the IEEE 802.2 Logical Link Control (LLC) layer by enabling the multiplexing of more protocols than can be distinguished by the 8-bit Service Access Point (SAP) fields. It is commonly used with various IEEE 802 physical network layers, such as IEEE 802.3 (Ethernet), IEEE 802.11 (Wi-Fi), and non-IEEE 802 networks like FDDI. SNAP employs a 5-octet header, including a 3-octet Organizationally Unique Identifier (OUI) and a 2-octet protocol ID, to identify protocols. It is essential for networks where the MAC layer lacks an EtherType field, allowing for protocol differentiation and private protocol spaces.

trgt: Stowaway is a 1936 American musical drama film directed by William A. Seiter. The screenplay by William M. Conselman, Nat Perrin, and Arthur Sheekman is based on a story by Samuel G. Engel. The film is about a young orphan called "Ching Ching" (Shirley Temple) who meets wealthy playboy Tommy Randall (Robert Young) in Shanghai and then accidentally stows away on the ocean liner he is travelling on. The film was hugely successful, and is available on videocassette and DVD.
mgt: *Stowaway* is a 1936 American musical comedy film directed by William A. Seiter and starring Shirley Temple, Robert Young, and Alice Faye. The story follows a young orphan, Barbara "Ching-Ching" Stewart, who accidentally becomes a stowaway on an ocean liner and plays matchmaker for two passengers, leading to an unexpected romance and adoption. The film features musical numbers by Harry Revel and Mack Gordon, including "Goodnight, My Love." Praised for its humor and performances, *Stowaway* was a box office success and remains a notable entry in Temple's filmography.

trgt:
Atlantic Coast Line 1504 is a 4-6-2 USRA Light Pacific steam locomotive built in March 1919 by the American Locomotive Company (ALCO) of Richmond, Virginia, for the Atlantic Coast Line Railroad (ACL) as a member of the P-5-A class. It was assigned to pull ACL's mainline passenger trains until it was retired in 1952 and subsequently donated to the city of Jacksonville, Florida, where it sat on static display as the only original USRA Light Pacific steam locomotive to be preserved. In 1990, No. 1504 was designated as a National Historic Mechanical Engineering Landmark by the American Society of Mechanical Engineers (ASME).

As of 2022, the locomotive is being restored to operating condition for use in excursion service on the South Central Florida Express shortline railroad in Clewiston, Florida as part of U.S. Sugar's (USSC) heritage tourist passenger train named the Sugar Express, where it will eventually ran alongside ex-Florida East Coast Railway (FEC) No. 148.
mgt:
Atlantic Coast Line No. 1504 is a historic USRA Light Pacific steam locomotive built by the American Locomotive Company (ALCO) between 1919 and 1920 for the Atlantic Coast Line Railroad (ACL). Originally classified as P-5 and later reclassified as P-5-A, it hauled premier passenger trains such as the Florida Special and Dixie Flyer at speeds of up to 80 mph. After ACL transitioned to diesel locomotives in the late 1940s, No. 1504 was reassigned to freight service until its retirement in 1952.

Preserved by ACL in 1960, the locomotive was displayed in Jacksonville, Florida, and designated a National Historic Mechanical Engineering Landmark in 1990. In 2021, it was sold to U.S. Sugar Corporation for restoration and future use in excursion service on the South Central Florida Express. Currently undergoing a three-year operational restoration, No. 1504 remains the only surviving USRA Light Pacific in near-original condition.

page_title: Additional Civil Lord of the Admiralty
trgt: The Additional Civil Lord of the Admiralty or formally the Office of the Additional Civil Lord of the Admiralty sometimes called the Department of the Additional Civil Lord of the Admiralty was a member of the Board of Admiralty first from 1882 to 1885 and then again from 1912 to 1919 who was mainly responsible for administration of contracts for matériel for the Fleet, supervision of the contracts and purchase department and general organisation of dockyards within the Admiralty.
mgt: The Additional Civil Lord of the Admiralty was a position on the Board of Admiralty in the United Kingdom, established in 1882 and held by individuals who were neither naval officers nor politicians. Initially created to assist with shipbuilding and dockyard management, the role was abolished in 1885 but reinstated in 1912. During World War I, additional civil lords were appointed to manage the increasing workload, with responsibilities including ship repairs and procurement. The position was permanently abolished in 1919.

trgt:
Ribadesella (Asturian: Ribeseya) is a small 84-square-kilometre (32 sq mi) municipality in the Autonomous Community of the Principality of Asturias, Spain. Known for its location on the Cantabrian Sea, at the outlet of the River Sella, Ribadesella is a town that forms part of the Picos de Europa (Peaks of Europe). It is bordered on the east by Llanes, on the south by Cangas de Onís and Parres, and on the west by Caravia. Ribadesella is the home town of Queen Letizia of Spain.

On the first weekend of August, the Descenso Internacional del Sella (International Descent of the Sella) takes place; kayakers from all over the world gather to attempt the final 20 km of the Sella River in record time. The town is also known for its prehistoric cave, known as the cave of Tito Bustillo, which is open to visitors all year round. Ribadesella is also visited for its history, sport activities, scenery, natural amenities, and food.
mgt:
Ribadesella is a municipality in the autonomous community of Asturias, Spain, located along the northern coast. It covers an area of 84.37 km² and has a population of 5,730 (2018). The town is known for its rich history, including the Tito Bustillo Cave, a UNESCO World Heritage Site featuring prehistoric Magdalenian-era cave art. The first recorded mention of Ribadesella dates back to the 1st century BC, with references by the Greek geographer Strabo.

The town is divided into distinct areas, including the historic Casco Antiguo, the Gran Vía de Agustín Argüelles, and the Paseo Marítimo del Puerto. Ribadesella hosts several cultural events, most notably the International Descent of the Sella, a canoe race held annually in August, accompanied by the Fiesta de Les Piragües. Other traditional festivals celebrate local religious and culinary heritage, such as the Cheese Festival and Fish Festival, as well as Semana Santa processions and celebrations.

trgt: Dessie Glynn (7 June 1928 – 6 January 2017) was a Republic of Ireland international footballer who played for Drumcondra and Shelbourne in the late 1940s and 1950s, scoring 111 goals in his League of Ireland career. Glynn was also Drumcondra's all-time top goalscorer with 96 goals between 1949 and 1956. Eamon Dunphy described Glynn as "a splendidly versatile centre-forward, a scorer and maker of goals". Glynn grew up in Drumcondra, Dublin, was educated at St. Vincent's C.B.S. and worked for the Irish civil service. In 1958 he spent nine months in hospital, suffering from tuberculosis – a condition which effectively ended his playing career. He later coached in New York.
mgt: Desmond "Dessie" Glynn (7 June 1928 – 6 January 2017) was an Irish footballer who played as a forward, most notably for Drumcondra and Shelbourne in the League of Ireland. Glynn was a prolific scorer, netting 96 league goals for Drumcondra and finishing as the league's top scorer in the 1950–51 season with 20 goals. He won the League of Ireland title with Drumcondra in his debut season and an FAI Cup in 1953–54. Glynn also earned two caps for the Republic of Ireland, scoring the winning goal on his debut against West Germany in 1951. He later played for Shelbourne, where he was the club's top scorer in 1956–57.

trgt: Collar × Malice is a visual novel video game developed and published by Idea Factory under their Otomate brand. It was released for PlayStation Vita on August 18, 2016, in Japan, and by Aksys Games in North America and Europe on July 28, 2017. A Nintendo Switch port was also released in 2020. The game was well received by critics, and sold well in Japan for a niche game of its genre and platform. A fan disc, Collar × Malice: Unlimited, was released on July 26, 2018. An anime film adaptation is set to premiere in 2023.
mgt: *Collar × Malice* is a visual novel developed by Idea Factory under their Otomate brand and published in Japan in 2016 for the PlayStation Vita, with subsequent releases on the Nintendo Switch. The game follows Ichika Hoshino, a rookie police officer in Shinjuku, Tokyo, who becomes entangled in the "X-Day Incident," a series of murders orchestrated by the terrorist group Adonis, after a poison-filled collar is placed around her neck. The game received praise for its suspenseful narrative and character development. A fan disc, *Collar × Malice: Unlimited*, and an anime film adaptation were later released.

trgt:
Jefferson Andrés Lerma Solís (born 25 October 1994) is a Colombian professional footballer who plays as a central midfielder for Premier League club AFC Bournemouth and the Colombia national team. He has also been used as a defensive midfielder and as a right-back.

A youth product of Atlético Huila, Lerma played for three years in Colombia before joining Spanish side Levante on loan in 2016. That same year, Levante bought Lerma outright on a four-year contract. Lerma became Premier League side AFC Bournemouth's club record transfer fee in 2018 as he joined The Cherries for a reported €30 million.

Lerma first played with Colombia's Olympic team, representing them at the 2016 Summer Olympics in Rio. He made his international debut for Colombia the following year in a friendly against South Korea. He was part of the Colombia squad that participated at the 2018 World Cup, and represented his nation at the 2019 edition of the Copa América.
mgt:
Jefferson Andrés Lerma Solís (born 25 October 1994) is a Colombian professional footballer who plays as a defensive midfielder for Premier League club AFC Bournemouth and the Colombia national team. Lerma began his career with Atlético Huila in Colombia, where he made his professional debut in 2013 and became a key player. In 2015, he joined Spanish club Levante, initially on loan, and helped them achieve promotion to La Liga during the 2016–17 season. 

In 2018, Lerma signed with Bournemouth for a club-record fee, becoming a fan favorite for his tough-tackling style. He played a pivotal role in Bournemouth's promotion back to the Premier League in the 2021–22 season. Internationally, Lerma has represented Colombia since 2017, featuring in the 2018 FIFA World Cup and the 2019 Copa América. Known for his versatility and physicality, he has also scored key goals for both club and country.

trgt:
My Mowgli Boy (Chinese: 我的莫格利男孩) is a 2019 Chinese urban romance comedy television series directed by Peng Xuejun. It stars Ma Tianyu as Mowgli and Yang Zi as Ling Xi in the lead roles. It premiered on iQIYI on August 29, 2019 and finished broadcasting on October 10, 2019. It also aired primetime on Shenzhen Satellite TV starting from April 12th 2020.

The series tells the story of Mo Ge Li, a boy raised in the jungle by the forest caretaker, who is accidentally brought into the city by Ling Xi, an independent entrepreneurial woman and called on people to protect the environment.
mgt: *My Mowgli Boy* is a 2019 Chinese romantic comedy television series starring Ma Tianyu and Yang Zi. Directed by Peng Xuejun and written by Liu Fei, Liu Chenguang, and Shi Shi, the series aired on iQiyi from August 29 to October 10, 2019, spanning 50 episodes. The story follows Mo Ge Li, a boy raised in the forest, and Ling Xi, an independent career woman who inadvertently brings him to the city. As they navigate their differences, they develop a romantic bond and work together to promote environmental protection. The series was praised for its environmental themes and fashion sense.

trgt:
The Schillinger System of Musical Composition, named after Joseph Schillinger (1895–1943) is a method of musical composition based on mathematical processes. It comprises theories of rhythm, harmony, melody, counterpoint, form and semantics, purporting to offer a systematic and non-genre approach to music analysis and composition; a descriptive rather than prescriptive grammar of music.

While it influenced some prominent figures, such as Lawrence Berk (founder of the Berklee College of Music) and George Gershwin (likely influencing the piece "I Got Rhythm Variations"), it began to fall out of favor in the 1960s after receiving criticisms for being over-complicated and pseudo-scientific, and was removed from the Berklee curriculum.
mgt: The Schillinger System is a method of musical composition developed by Joseph Schillinger, a Russian-American composer and theorist. It is based on mathematical principles and scientific analysis, aiming to provide composers with tools to organize sound and create music free from traditional constraints. Schillinger's system emphasizes universal patterns and applies mathematical concepts to rhythm, melody, and structure, offering techniques for planning and executing large musical works. While the system was influential, it was also criticized for its complexity and dense presentation. Schillinger taught prominent musicians, including George Gershwin and Glenn Miller, and his ideas influenced jazz education and institutions like Berklee College of Music.

trgt:
Arthur Rackham RWS (19 September 1867 – 6 September 1939) was an English book illustrator. He is recognised as one of the leading figures during the Golden Age of British book illustration. His work is noted for its robust pen and ink drawings, which were combined with the use of watercolour, a technique he developed due to his background as a journalistic illustrator.

Rackham's 51 colour pieces for the early American tale Rip Van Winkle became a turning point in the production of books since – through colour-separated printing – it featured the accurate reproduction of colour artwork. His best-known works also include the illustrations for Peter Pan in Kensington Gardens, and Fairy Tales of the Brothers Grimm.
mgt:
Arthur Rackham (19 September 1867 – 6 September 1939) was a British illustrator renowned for his contributions to children's literature and fantasy art during the "Golden Age" of British book illustration (1890–1914). Known for his distinctive pen and ink drawings combined with subtle watercolors, Rackham's work brought a renewed sense of excitement to book illustration, coinciding with advancements in printing technology. His illustrations for works such as *Rip Van Winkle* (1905) and *Peter Pan in Kensington Gardens* (1906) solidified his reputation, and his art was widely exhibited, including at the Louvre.

Rackham's imaginative style, blending Nordic and Japanese influences, inspired numerous artists and filmmakers. His works remain popular, with many books still in print. He died in Limpsfield, Surrey, in 1939, leaving a lasting legacy in the world of illustration.

trgt:
Charles-Joseph Natoire (3 March 1700 – 23 August 1777) was a French painter in the Rococo manner, a pupil of François Lemoyne and director of the French Academy in Rome, 1751–1775. Considered during his lifetime the equal of François Boucher, he played a prominent role in the artistic life of France.

He is remembered above all for the series of the History of Psyche for Germain Boffrand's oval salon de la Princesse in the Hôtel de Soubise, Paris, and for the tapestry cartoons for the series of the History of Don Quixote, woven at the Beauvais tapestry manufacture, most of which are present at the Château de Compiègne.
mgt: Charles-Joseph Natoire (1700–1777) was a French Rococo painter and director of the French Academy in Rome. Born in Nîmes, he trained under prominent artists Louis Galloche and François Lemoyne, winning the Prix de Rome in 1721. Natoire gained recognition for his historical and decorative works, including royal commissions for Versailles, Fontainebleau, and the Gobelins and Beauvais tapestry manufactories. Appointed director of the French Academy in Rome in 1751, he shifted focus to mentoring students and overseeing their artistic progress. Later in life, he embraced religious themes and created numerous drawings of the Roman Campagna. He retired in Castel Gandolfo, where he died in 1777.

trgt:
Magnolia is a small village in Gloucester, Massachusetts, located on the Manchester-by-the-Sea, Massachusetts town line in the southwestern part of the city. Straddling the town line between the two communities is Surf Park, a two-acre swath of land that has scenic views of Kettle Cove.

Magnolia has its own library, restaurants, and shops. Another feature of Magnolia is its beach, which goes by the official name Gray Beach and is a part of Coolidge Reservation. Union Congregational Church is located in Magnolia, as is Hammond Castle. The village has its own Women's Community Club, which has been in existence for over 60 years.
mgt: Magnolia is a village in Gloucester, Massachusetts, United States, known for its history as a summer resort destination. Following the Civil War, it became popular among Bostonians, including notable figures like Unitarian minister James Freeman Clarke, who built a summer home there in 1879. By the early 20th century, Magnolia was renowned for its luxurious accommodations, such as the Oceanside Hotel and Casino, which hosted prominent figures like John Philip Sousa and Lucille Ball. Wealthy families built grand mansions along Shore Road, while local businesses and immigrant workers supported the vibrant summer community. Magnolia remains a picturesque coastal village with historical significance.

trgt: Baaji (‹See RfD› transl. elder sister) is a 2019 Pakistani romantic drama film, directed by Saqib Malik and produced by his Production company Page 33 Films. The film features Meera as Shameera Osman Khalid Butt as Rohail Khan/ Khalid Osman, Amna Ilyas as Neha, Mohsin Abbas Haider as Ajji, Ali Kazmi as Rammy, Nayyar Ejaz, and Nisho in pivotal roles. It is a dramatic tale of a fading Lollywood actress Shameera (Meera), who is not accepting that now her position in the industry is not much valuable. The story revolves around the relationship of Shameera with a Salon girl Neha (Amna Ilyas) who later becomes an assistant of Shameera and make this opportunity happens of working with an American Based Pakistani Director Rohail Khan (Osman Khalid Butt). Neha who belongs to a conservative lower middle class family then also make herself involved in Shameera's comeback movie titled Waapsi by helping them in rehearsals and script making so far she get a chance to take a supporting role in the movie. The film includes Ali Kazmi as Raamy (Shameera's Boyfriend) who is highly aggressive about Shameera. Mohsin Abbas Haider plays the love interest of neha's character, while the Legendary actor Nayyer Ejaz supports the film by his great comics and unforgettable role as Chand Kamal, film also includes numerous well-known faces from the industry as in cameo appearances.
mgt:
*Baaji* (Urdu: باجی) is a 2019 Pakistani drama film directed by Saqib Malik and written by Irfan Ahmed Urfi. Produced under the banner of Page 33 Films and distributed by ARY Films, the movie stars Meera, Osman Khalid Butt, Amna Ilyas, Mohsin Abbas Haider, and Ali Kazmi. The film, which explores themes of ambition and the fading glory of Lollywood, was released on June 28, 2019, and received critical acclaim for its portrayal of Pakistan's cinematic heritage and the performances of its cast, particularly Meera and Amna Ilyas.

The film's promotional campaign, including its teaser and theatrical trailer, garnered significant attention on social media. Despite controversy surrounding the song "Gangster Guriya," featuring Mehwish Hayat, the film benefited from the publicity and achieved commercial success, grossing Rs. 120 million (US$420,000) domestically against a budget of Rs. 60 million (US$210,000). *Baaji* was also released internationally by B4U Films and later became available for streaming on Amazon Prime Video in the United States and the United Kingdom in August 2020.

Critics praised the film for its vibrant yet dark depiction of Lollywood's legacy, with Hassan Hassan of Galaxy Lollywood rating it 3.5 out of 5 stars. The film was also celebrated for its nostalgic homage to Pakistan's film industry.

trgt: Rabbi Menachem Mendel Monsohn (Hebrew: מנחם מענדל מאנזאהן‎; October 13, 1895 – September 3, 1953) was a member of the Monsohn family of Jerusalem, born in the Old City of Jerusalem. He was a great-grandson of Abraham-Leib Monsohn, one of the founders of the Ashkenazi Old Yishuv of Jerusalem in the early nineteenth century, and a son of Abraham-Leib Monsohn II, a founder of the A.L. Monsohn Lithography in Jerusalem. After marrying, Monsohn lived with his family in the Batei Broide section of Nachlaot, Jerusalem, which provided housing for rabbis and their families. In 1924 Monsohn immigrated to the United States with a group of rabbis from Eretz Israel, settling in Brooklyn, New York, where he served as rabbi of Congregation Ezrath Israel on Gates Avenue, in the Bedford-Stuyvesant section, until his death in 1953. His book, Mi-Peninei Ha-Rambam: Bi’ur ‘al ha-Torah, a compendium of Maimonides’ commentaries on the Pentateuch, arranged by the compiler in order of the Torah chapters, first appeared in Brooklyn c.1925 and was reprinted there several times in the early 1930s. In 2006 it was re-released by Mossad Harav Kook of Jerusalem, which also published an English translation, Pearls of the Rambam (tr. Avraham Berkovits) c. 2008. Some of the early editions included a Yiddish introduction to the life of Maimonides.
mgt:
Menachem Mendel Monsohn (October 13, 1895 – September 3, 1953) was a Jerusalem-born rabbi, scholar, and author, known for his work *Mi-Peninei Ha-Rambam* (*Pearls of the Rambam*), a study of Maimonides' writings. Ordained at the age of 16 by Rabbi Abraham Isaac Kook, the first Ashkenazic chief rabbi of British Mandatory Palestine, Monsohn demonstrated exceptional intellectual ability from a young age. He initially worked at his family's lithographic press in Jerusalem but left due to ethical objections over its printing of a church calendar.

Monsohn later settled in Brooklyn, New York, where he brought his wife, Zipporah Yehudit, and their children. In Brooklyn, he struggled financially but dedicated himself to publishing *Mi-Peninei Ha-Rambam*, using the printing skills he had acquired in Jerusalem. The work was published in multiple editions in Hebrew between 1932 and 1939 and posthumously in Jerusalem in 2006, as well as in English in 2008. Monsohn's legacy is tied to his contributions to Jewish scholarship and his devotion to preserving and disseminating Maimonides' teachings.

trgt:
Armet Francis (born 29 January 1945) is a Jamaican-born photographer and publisher who has lived in London since the 1950s. He has been documenting and chronicling the lives of people of the African diaspora for more than 40 years and his assignments have included work for The Times Magazine, The Sunday Times Supplement, BBC and Channel 4.

He has exhibited worldwide and his work is in collections including those of the Victoria & Albert Museum and the Museum of London. One of his best known photographs is 1964's "Self Portrait in Mirror".
mgt: Armet Francis (born January 29, 1945) is a British photographer of Jamaican origin, known for his pioneering work documenting the African diaspora and co-founding Autograph ABP, an association promoting Black photography. His career began in the 1960s, transitioning from fashion photography to social documentary projects, including his lifelong series *The Black Triangle*. He became the first Black photographer to hold a solo exhibition at The Photographers' Gallery in London in 1983. Francis's work has been featured in major exhibitions, including *Roots to Reckoning* and *Staying Power*, and is held in prominent public collections.

trgt:
Duško Knežević (Montenegrin Cyrillic: Душко Кнежевић) is the founder and president of the board of directors of Atlas Group Corporation (AGC), a financial group based in Montenegro consisting of companies with interests in banks in Serbia, Cyprus, Russia and Great Britain.

In addition to his business interests, he is the founder and president of the board of two business-oriented universities, in Montenegro and in Serbia. In April 2010, Knežević became a member of Clinton Global Initiative.

Knežević founded the Atlas Foundation in 2010. The mission of the Foundation is to support the creation of new societal values and economic empowerment of citizens through enhancement of business environment and corporate social responsible practices. Improving regional cooperation is one of the key goals of the Atlas Foundation. Knežević organised the Inaugural Conference “Balkan Networking for Social Empowerment of South-Eastern Europe” that was held in Budva in May 2011, under the auspices of the Government of Montenegro and Atlas Foundation, and in partnership with Clinton Global Initiative.
