The song was nominated for the Golden Globe Award for Best Original Song.
mgt: "Winter" is a song by the Irish rock band U2, written for the 2009 war film *Brothers* directed by Jim Sheridan. Composed by the band with lyrics by Bono, the song underwent several iterations, evolving from a rock arrangement to a stripped-down version inspired by The Edge's original piano composition. The lyrics, influenced by Tobey Maguire's character in the film, explore themes of hidden emotions and the experiences of soldiers in Afghanistan. Although initially intended for U2's album *No Line on the Horizon*, it was ultimately excluded but featured in the film's closing credits. "Winter" was nominated for a Golden Globe Award for Best Original Song in 2010.

trgt: The Pin Up Girls are a girl group and dance troupe, founded by New York City native Vixen Romeo in 2005, which began as a burlesque-style performance group based in Los Angeles. Performing at Hollywood's most notorious venues such as The Viper Room Key Club and Roxy the girls quickly gained local attention with their girl-on-girl themed, tribal fusion belly dance, burlesque and hip hop routines. Between 2006-2008 The Pin Up Girls started to become poster girls for the lesbian scene with performances for Curve (magazine), a guest appearance on LOGO network's reality series Curl Girls, a web series segment on AfterEllen, a performance for the LGBT community hosted by Jane Lynch, and performances in Margaret Cho's Sensuous Woman Show. In 2008 The Pin Up Girls first recorded single "There She Goes.She's Real Fly"was picked up to be played on Showtime's hit lesbian series The L Word. In 2009 The Pin Up Girls music video,"There She Goes.She's Real Fly" premiered on Logo (TV channel) (an MTV network), on New Now Next Pop Lab. The Pin Up Girls' "Girl Candy," filmed in N.Y. and L.A., was released in 2011. The Pin Up Girls' "Pretty Things", featuring actress Elaine Hendrix, was filmed in L.A. by Director Joe LaRue in 2012 and was released in June 2012.
mgt:
The Pin Up Girls are a Los Angeles-based performance group and music act that blends pop, hip hop, electronic, and post-punk influences. Formed in 2005 under the direction of Vixen Romeo, the group began as a dance troupe combining burlesque, ballet, hip hop, and belly dance. Initially performing covers of classics like "Fever" and "Criminal," they transitioned to creating original music in 2007 when their song "There She Goes...She's Real Fly" was featured on Showtime's *The L Word*. The track, co-written by Romeo and Top DeWilde, gained further exposure on networks such as Logo, MTV Hive, VH1, and Spike.

Over the years, The Pin Up Girls expanded their performances to include fire tribal dancing, live zills, and doumbek playing. They have released several original songs, including "Girl Candy," "Boushie B*tch," and "Pretty Things," along with three music videos. Known for their dynamic and eclectic style, the group continues to perform and produce music, maintaining their presence in the indie music and performance scene.

trgt:
Formula One 99 is a racing video game developed by Studio 33 and published by Psygnosis for PlayStation and Microsoft Windows. It is the sequel to the 1998 video game Formula 1 98 and was based on the 1999 Formula One World Championship.

Following the disappointment of Formula 1 98, and the subsequent split with Visual Science, Psygnosis hired Studio 33 to develop Formula One 99, after their successful development of Newman/Haas Racing the previous year. During production of the game, Sony completed a full acquisition of Psygnosis, making this the final Formula One game to be published under the Psygnosis name.

The game is unique in having substitute drivers appear in the game as they did in the real 1999 season (such as Mika Salo replacing Michael Schumacher for the races between Austria and Europe). A new grid editor tool was also introduced, allowing players to customise the starting grid to their own liking before a race. Despite the lack of an arcade mode that had featured in previous titles, the game was widely praised as an overwhelming improvement in comparison to the 1998 game.
mgt:
*Formula One 99* is a racing video game developed by Studio 33 and published by Psygnosis as part of the *Formula One* series. It was released for the PlayStation in 1999 across Europe, Japan, and North America, with a Microsoft Windows version following later that year. The game features single-player and multiplayer modes and aims to deliver an authentic Formula One racing experience.

The PlayStation version of *Formula One 99* received favorable reviews from critics. Official UK PlayStation Magazine praised it for revitalizing the series with improved gameplay and a strong sense of speed. GameSpot highlighted its excellent controls and extensive settings, while IGN described it as the best Formula One game available on the PlayStation. In Japan, where it was published by Sony Computer Entertainment, the game received a score of 28 out of 40 from *Famitsu*. The game was noted for addressing issues from its predecessor and delivering a satisfying experience for fans of the sport.

trgt:
Camping World Stadium is a stadium in Orlando, Florida, located in the West Lakes neighborhood of Downtown Orlando, west of new sports and entertainment facilities including the Amway Center, the Dr. Phillips Center for the Performing Arts, and Exploria Stadium. It opened in 1936 as Orlando Stadium and has also been known as the Tangerine Bowl and Florida Citrus Bowl. The City of Orlando owns and operates the stadium.

Camping World Stadium is the current home venue of the Citrus Bowl and the Cheez-It Bowl. It is also the regular host of other college football games including the Florida Classic between Florida A&M and Bethune-Cookman, the MEAC/SWAC Challenge, and the Camping World Kickoff. The stadium was built for football and in the past, it has served as home of several alternate-league football teams. From 2011 to 2013, it was the home of the Orlando City SC, a soccer team in USL Pro. From 1979 to 2006, it served as the home of the UCF Knights football team. It was one of the nine venues used for the 1994 FIFA World Cup. The stadium has also hosted the NFL's Pro Bowl four times.
mgt:
Camping World Stadium, located in Orlando, Florida, is a multi-purpose stadium owned by the City of Orlando and operated by Orlando Venues. Originally built in 1936 as a Works Progress Administration project during the Great Depression, it has undergone numerous renovations and expansions, most notably in 1989 and 2014. The stadium has a current seating capacity of 60,219, expandable to 65,194, and features modern amenities, including a 20,000-square-foot plaza deck and upgraded seating.

The venue has hosted a wide range of events, including college football bowl games like the Citrus Bowl and Cheez-It Bowl, professional football games, high school championships, and international soccer matches, including the 1994 FIFA World Cup and Copa América Centenario. It has also been the site of major entertainment events, such as WrestleMania XXIV and 33, which set attendance records.

Previously known by various names, including the Tangerine Bowl and Florida Citrus Bowl, the stadium was renamed Camping World Stadium in 2016 after a naming rights agreement. Its rich history and versatility have made it a prominent venue for sports and entertainment in Central Florida.

page_title: University of Florida clinical toxicology distance education program
trgt:
The University of Florida's (UF) online clinical toxicology distance education programs cater to working professionals, including physicians, nurses, physician assistants, first responders, and poison control center professionals. The programs focus on toxicants, drugs of abuse, drug analysis, and biotransformation, as well as the treatment of poisoned or overdosed patients. Classes are taught by internationally recognized faculty with expertise in clinical toxicology, medicine, pharmacy, and pharmacology.

Each course within UF's program is conducted online and is made up of specific topic modules. Most modules contain course notes supplemented with images, animations, and case studies. Students are provided constant access to course modules, which are released over the duration of the UF semester.
mgt: The University of Florida's clinical toxicology distance education program, offered through the UF College of Pharmacy in Gainesville, Florida, provides advanced education for health science professionals. The program includes a Master of Science (MS) in Clinical Toxicology and a graduate certificate in Clinical Toxicology, both delivered entirely online. The MS program consists of 32 credits and focuses on medical and toxicological principles, drug analysis, and treatment of poisoning or overdoses, catering to professionals such as pharmacists, nurses, and first responders. The 15-credit graduate certificate offers foundational knowledge in toxicology for those with a bachelor's degree in health or science-related fields, emphasizing toxicants encountered in poison control and emergency settings.

trgt: Collective motion is defined as the spontaneous emergence of ordered movement in a system consisting of many self-propelled agents. It can be observed in everyday life, for example in flocks of birds, schools of fish, herds of animals and also in crowds and car traffic. It also appears at the microscopic level: in colonies of bacteria, motility assays and artificial self-propelled particles. The scientific community is trying to understand the universality of this phenomenon. In particular it is intensively investigated in statistical physics and in the field of active matter. Experiments on animals, biological and synthesized self-propelled particles, simulations and theories are conducted in parallel to study these phenomena. One of the most famous models that describes such behavior is the Vicsek model introduced by Tamás Vicsek et al. in 1995.
mgt:
Collective motion refers to the coordinated behavior of self-propelled particles, both biological and synthetic, in response to external or self-generated gradients. This phenomenon is observed in micromotors and nanomotors, which can exhibit behaviors such as schooling, exclusion, and chemotaxis. For instance, silver chloride micromotors form schools under UV light, while silver orthophosphate microparticles transition between schooling and exclusion behaviors in response to stimuli like ammonia and hydrogen peroxide. These behaviors have practical applications, such as designing logic gates and transporting microscale cargo.

Chemotaxis, the directed movement of particles along chemical gradients, has been demonstrated in systems like enzyme-coated particles and Au-Pt nanorods. This property enables applications in drug delivery, sensing, and lab-on-a-chip devices. Additionally, enzyme chemotaxis has been used to study metabolon formation and separate active from inactive enzymes in microfluidic systems.

trgt: Polytrauma and multiple trauma are medical terms describing the condition of a person who has been subjected to multiple traumatic injuries, such as a serious head injury in addition to a serious burn. The term is defined via an Injury Severity Score (ISS) equal to or greater than 16. It has become a commonly applied term by US military physicians in describing the seriously injured soldiers returning from Operation Iraqi Freedom in Iraq and Operation Enduring Freedom in Afghanistan. The term is generic, however, and has been in use for a long time for any case involving multiple trauma.
mgt: Polytrauma refers to the condition of sustaining multiple traumatic injuries, often life-threatening, which can result from high-velocity incidents such as motor vehicle crashes in civilian settings or blast injuries in military contexts. In civilian medicine, initial diagnosis typically involves imaging to identify critical injuries, while military polytrauma often includes traumatic brain injuries, burns, fractures, and sensory impairments. Advances in medical care have improved survival rates, though long-term physical and psychological challenges remain. In the U.S., specialized rehabilitation centers managed by the Department of Veterans Affairs provide comprehensive care for veterans, including screening and treatment for deployment-related traumatic brain injuries.

trgt:
A leg is a weight-bearing and locomotive anatomical structure, usually having a columnar shape. During locomotion, legs function as "extensible struts". The combination of movements at all joints can be modeled as a single, linear element capable of changing length and rotating about an omnidirectional "hip" joint.

As an anatomical animal structure it is used for locomotion. The distal end is often modified to distribute force (such as a foot). Most animals have an even number of legs.

As a component of furniture, it is used for the economy of materials needed to provide the support for the useful surface such as the table top or chair seat.
mgt: A leg is a structural component of gross anatomy found in many animals, including humans, mammals, and insects, and is used for support, locomotion, or both. In humans, a leg typically consists of bones, muscles, tendons, ligaments, blood vessels, nerves, and skin, while in insects, the exoskeleton replaces the function of bones and skin. The definition of a leg can vary, sometimes including the foot or hip joint. In tetrapods, the term "leg" may refer to the entire limb, while in human anatomy, it specifically denotes the segment between the knee and ankle. Legs can also be robotic or prosthetic, serving functional or artificial purposes.

trgt: Akumaa Mama Zimbi, also known professionally as Dr. Joyce Akumaa Dongotey-Padi, is a Ghanaian television and radio broadcast journalist, women's rights activist, blogger, marriage counselor, and an actress in Ghana. Akumaa is known for her fascinating, yet sexually stimulating, ways of expressing "Medaase" (Thank you). Her exceptional ways of wearing her headgear also comes off as a distinct Akumaa brand. Little is known about Akumaa's early life as well as her family and educational background. However, it is known that her father is a policeman and her husband is a businessman to whom she has been married for over twenty-four years
mgt: Akumaa Mama Zimbi, born Dr. Joyce Akumaa Dongotey-Padi, is a Ghanaian actress, radio and television presenter, marriage counselor, and philanthropist. She gained prominence in the 1990s through her role in the popular *Cantata Show* and transitioned into radio broadcasting in the early 2000s, eventually becoming the host of the *Odo Ahomaso Show* on Adom TV, which focuses on relationship and marital issues. Akumaa is also the founder of the Mama Zimbi Foundation, established in 2004, which empowers underprivileged women, widows, and children in Ghana. Her work has earned her national recognition, including awards for her contributions to radio and television.

trgt: AAA vs MLW (also known as MLW x AAA and AAA vs. MLW Super Series) was a professional wrestling supercard event co-produced by the U.S-based Major League Wrestling (MLW) and the Mexico-based Lucha Libre AAA Worldwide (AAA), in partnership with Promociones EMW. It was held at Auditorio Fausto Gutierrez in Tijuana, Baja California, Mexico on March 13, 2020. The event was taped to be aired on future episodes of MLW Fusion; they would be the last episodes to air before the promotion went on hiatus due to the COVID-19 pandemic.
mgt: AAA vs MLW was a professional wrestling event co-promoted by Major League Wrestling (MLW), Lucha Libre AAA Worldwide (AAA), and Promociones EMW. It took place on March 13, 2020, at the Auditorio Fausto Gutierrez in Tijuana, Baja California, Mexico, and aired from April 11 to May 9, 2020. The event featured nine scripted matches involving wrestlers from both promotions, portraying either rudos (heels) or técnicos (faces). This marked the first co-promoted event between MLW and AAA, following their working agreement established in 2018.

trgt: The Dornier Do 11 was a German heavy bomber, developed in secret in the early 1930s. It was originally called the Dornier F before being renamed by the Reichsluftfahrtministerium (RLM) in 1933, and was considered a heavy bomber at the time. It came into service in 1932, a continuation of a line of bomber designs from the Dornier Do P in 1930, and the Dornier Do Y in 1931. The line would continue to develop with the Dornier Do 13 and Dornier Do 23.
mgt: The Dornier Do 11 was a German aircraft developed in the early 1930s, initially designed as a heavy bomber but publicly presented as a freight transport. It featured a retractable undercarriage, though this was often locked down due to mechanical issues. The Do 11 served as a trainer for the secret Luftwaffe and was the first Dornier bomber to use two engines instead of three or four. Despite modifications, the aircraft suffered from significant design flaws, leading to its replacement by the improved Do 23 by 1936.

trgt: The Prospect Hill neighborhood in North Omaha, Nebraska is one of the oldest neighborhoods in the city. In addition to being home to some of the city's oldest structures, the neighborhood is also the site of the Prospect Hill Cemetery which is located between North 31st and 33rd Streets and Parker and Grant Streets. The neighborhood's boundaries are North 30th Street from Hamilton Street to Lake and up to Creighton Boulevard; then over to Blondo and up to North 38th Street to Hamilton. The Omaha Belt Line ran near the northwest corner of the neighborhood. The neighborhood is also home to the historic Franklin Elementary School.
mgt: Prospect Hill is a historic neighborhood in Omaha, Nebraska, known for its cemetery and public housing developments. Established in the 1850s during the Scriptown affair, the area became home to Prospect Hill Cemetery, with its first official burial in 1858. The cemetery includes the graves of over 800 African Americans, including at least 10 Buffalo Soldiers. In the 1940s, the U.S. government constructed nearly 700 public housing units in the neighborhood, including Hilltop Homes, Spencer Homes, and Pleasantview Homes. Today, Salem Baptist Church occupies the former site of Hilltop Homes, reflecting the neighborhood's evolving history and significance.

trgt: Madden NFL 25 is an American football sports video game based on the National Football League and published by EA Sports. It was released for PlayStation 3 and Xbox 360 in August 2013. Instead of numbering it Madden NFL 14 with the year like in previous versions, the "25" in the title refers to the 25th anniversary of the Madden NFL series. The eighth-generation console versions of Madden NFL 25 are the very first games to run on EA Sports's Ignite game engine. However, the seventh generation versions still run on EA's previous game engine, Impact. The seventh-generation versions featured former Detroit Lions running back Barry Sanders on the cover, while the eighth-generation versions featured Minnesota Vikings running back Adrian Peterson, and were released as launch titles for the PlayStation 4 and Xbox One in November 2013.
mgt:
*Madden NFL 25* is a 2013 sports video game developed by EA Tiburon and published by EA Sports as part of the *Madden NFL* series. Released for PlayStation 3, Xbox 360, iOS, and Android on August 27, 2013, it later launched as a title for PlayStation 4 and Xbox One. The game introduced the Ignite engine for next-gen consoles, offering enhanced graphics, real-time physics, and improved gameplay mechanics, including more realistic player movements and environmental interactions. It also marked the return of team relocation in the Connected Franchise mode.

Barry Sanders, a Hall of Fame running back, was featured on the cover for seventh-generation consoles, while Adrian Peterson appeared on the next-gen versions. The game received mostly positive reviews for its gameplay improvements but faced criticism for commentary and repetitive elements. Despite mixed reception for its mobile versions, *Madden NFL 25* sold over 3 million copies.

trgt:
The Stone Flower (Russian: Каменный цветок, romanized: Kamennyy tsvetok) is a 1946 Soviet fantasy film directed by Aleksandr Ptushko. It is an adaptation of Pavel Bazhov's story of the same name, in turn based on Ural region Russian folklore. It also incorporates plot elements from the stories "The Mistress of the Copper Mountain" and "The Master Craftsman".

The Stone Flower was theatrically released by Mosfilm on 28 April 1946. It was the Soviet Union's first color film shot on Agfacolor negative film seized in Germany, and was entered into the 1946 Cannes Film Festival. It was a success at the box-office in the year 1946 in USSR, it was seen by 23.17 million viewers.
mgt: *The Stone Flower* (Russian: Каменный цветок, *Kamenny tsvetok*) is a 1946 Soviet fantasy film directed by Aleksandr Ptushko and produced by Mosfilm. Based on Pavel Bazhov's Ural folktale of the same name, the film tells the story of Danilo, a talented gemcutter who seeks to capture the true beauty of nature in his craft, leading him to encounter the mystical Mistress of the Copper Mountain. The film explores themes of artistic ambition, love, and sacrifice. Praised for its performances, *The Stone Flower* introduced the popular Russian catchphrase "How did that Stone Flower come out?" Bazhov appreciated the film but noted it lacked authentic Ural cultural elements.

trgt:
Richard Mellon Scaife (/skeɪf/; July 3, 1932 – July 4, 2014) was an American billionaire, a principal heir to the Mellon banking, oil, and aluminum fortune, and the owner and publisher of the Pittsburgh Tribune-Review. In 2005, Scaife was number 238 on the Forbes 400, with a personal fortune of $1.2 billion. By 2013, Scaife had dropped to number 371 on the listing, with a personal fortune of $1.4 billion.

During his life, Scaife was known for his financial support of conservative public policy organizations over the past four decades. He provided support for conservative and libertarian causes in the United States, mostly through the private, nonprofit foundations he controlled: the Sarah Scaife Foundation, Carthage Foundation, and Allegheny Foundation, and until 2001, the Scaife Family Foundation, now controlled by son David.
mgt:
Richard Mellon Scaife (July 3, 1932 – July 4, 2014) was an American billionaire, philanthropist, and media owner, best known for his conservative political activism and ownership of the *Pittsburgh Tribune-Review*. A member of the influential Mellon family, Scaife inherited significant wealth and used it to support conservative and libertarian causes, including funding organizations like The Heritage Foundation and the Hoover Institution. He also contributed to arts, education, and public policy initiatives, as well as non-political causes such as environmental conservation and population control.

Scaife expanded the *Tribune-Review* into a competitor of the *Pittsburgh Post-Gazette* and was a prominent critic of President Bill Clinton, funding investigations into Clinton's administration. Despite this, he later developed a friendship with Clinton. Scaife died of cancer in 2014, leaving a complex legacy of political and philanthropic influence.

trgt: "Remember a Day" is a song by the British rock band Pink Floyd, written and sung by their keyboardist Richard Wright, appearing on their second album, A Saucerful of Secrets (1968). It was performed by Pink Floyd only once, as an encore in May 1968; it was subsequently performed by David Gilmour in September 2008 in memory of Wright, who had recently died of cancer, on Later. with Jools Holland, and by Nick Mason during his Saucerful of Secrets tour. The dreamy, poetic lyrics are about nostalgia for the lost paradise of early childhood.
mgt: "Remember a Day" is a song by the English rock band Pink Floyd, written and sung by keyboardist Richard Wright. It was recorded in 1967 at De Lane Lea Studios in London and appears on their second studio album, *A Saucerful of Secrets* (1968). The track features contributions from Syd Barrett on guitar and producer Norman Smith on drums. Although planned as a single in the UK, it was only released as a single in the United States. In 2008, David Gilmour performed the song live as a tribute to Wright following his death.

trgt:
Force Majeure (IPA-fr; Lang-sv, "tourist") is a 2014 internationally co-produced black comedy film written and directed by Ruben Östlund. It follows the marital tension resulting from an apparent avalanche in the French Alps, during which the husband prioritizes his own escape over the safety of his wife and two children. The title used for the film in some English-speaking countries comes from force majeure, a contractual clause freeing both parties from liability in the event of unexpected disasters.

Force Majeure was acclaimed upon release, with critics praising its script and cinematography. It won the Best Film award at the 50th Guldbagge Awards, and was named one of the best films of 2014 by various publications.
mgt:
*Force Majeure* is a 2014 satirical drama film written and directed by Ruben Östlund. The film follows a Swedish family on a ski vacation in the French Alps whose lives are upended after the father’s reaction to a controlled avalanche calls his character into question. Exploring themes of masculinity, family dynamics, and human behavior in crises, the film blends dark humor with uncomfortable drama. 

Filmed at Les Arcs ski resort in France, *Force Majeure* drew inspiration from viral videos and Östlund's background in ski filmmaking. It premiered in the Un Certain Regard section at the 2014 Cannes Film Festival, where it won the Jury Prize, and received critical acclaim, earning a 94% approval rating on Rotten Tomatoes. The film won multiple awards, including six Guldbagge Awards, and was Sweden's submission for the Best Foreign Language Film at the 87th Academy Awards.

trgt:
Bernard Vince (born 2 October 1985) is a former Australian rules footballer who played for the Adelaide and Melbourne Football Club in the Australian Football League (AFL). He has since become involved in the football media, working for Triple M and Fox Footy.

At the time of his retirement, Vince was only one of seven players in VFL/AFL history to have played 100 or more games at two clubs and win the best and fairest award at both clubs.
mgt: Bernard Vince (born 2 October 1985) is a former Australian rules footballer who played as a midfielder for the Adelaide Football Club and Melbourne Football Club in the Australian Football League (AFL). Originally from Stansbury, South Australia, he was drafted by Adelaide in 2005 and won the Malcolm Blight Medal in 2009. After being traded to Melbourne in 2013, he won the Keith 'Bluey' Truscott Medal in 2015. Vince retired in 2018 and is married with one son.

trgt:
Mesa/Boogie (also known as Mesa Engineering) is an American company in Petaluma, California, that manufactures amplifiers and other accessories for guitars and basses. It has been in operation since 1969.

Mesa was started by Randall Smith as a small repair shop which modified Fender amplifiers, particularly the diminutive Fender Princeton. Smith's modifications gave the small amps much more input gain, making them much louder as well as creating a high-gain, distorted guitar tone. Prominent early customers included Carlos Santana, and Ron Wood and Keith Richards of The Rolling Stones. Exposure from these top players helped to establish Mesa/Boogie's position on the market, and it is frequently referred to as the first manufacturer of boutique amplifiers.
mgt: Mesa/Boogie is an American company that designs and manufactures amplifiers for guitars and basses. Founded in 1969 by Randall Smith in Petaluma, California, the company gained recognition for its innovative amplifier designs, including the high-gain Mark I and the Rectifier series, which became staples in modern rock music. Originally starting as a small repair shop, Mesa/Boogie evolved into a prominent amplifier manufacturer, with Smith pioneering advancements such as cascading gain stages. The company relocated to Petaluma in 1980 and continued to expand its product line over the decades. In 2021, Mesa/Boogie was acquired by Gibson, with Smith joining Gibson as a "Master Designer." The company remains a significant name in the music industry.

trgt:
Day of Defeat is a team-based multiplayer first-person shooter video game set in the European theatre of World War II on the Western front. Originally a modification of the 1998 game Half-Life, the rights of the modification were purchased by Valve and released as a full retail title in 2003.

Set in the midst of World War II, Day of Defeat includes no single-player campaign, with focus left only on the game's multiplayer aspects. The game favors teamwork and features objective-based gameplay in combination with its system of classes. Maps are primarily made up of narrow paths, all of which typically lead to a few key locations. An official remake of the game, Day of Defeat: Source, was released by Valve in 2005.
mgt:
*Day of Defeat* is a multiplayer first-person shooter video game developed by Valve. Originally released as a modification for *Half-Life* in 2000, it was later acquired by Valve, who released a stand-alone retail version in May 2003 for Microsoft Windows, with subsequent versions for OS X and Linux in 2013. Set in the European Theatre of World War II, the game features squad-based combat between the Allied and Axis forces, emphasizing teamwork and historical accuracy in weaponry and gameplay mechanics.

The game received generally favorable reviews, praised for its pacing, attention to detail, and promotion of teamwork, though criticized for its aging visuals. It was succeeded by *Day of Defeat: Source* in 2005, a remake using Valve's Source engine that introduced updated graphics, physics, and gameplay improvements.

trgt:
Gabby La La (born Gabriel Lang on May 23, 1979) is a vocalist and multi-instrumentalist (including sitar, ukulele, accordion, theremin and toy piano), signed to Prawn Song Records. Her music is self described as "fun, unique, crazy (and) kooky". La La's debut album, Be Careful What You Wish For., is a collaboration with Les Claypool that though "peculiar. reveals gems of pop merriment."

Gabby La La is primarily a solo artist but, at times, she has been a member of Weapon of Choice, and she was a member of Les Claypool's Fancy Band.
mgt: Gabby La La (born Gabby Lang, May 23, 1979) is an American experimental musician, singer, and songwriter from Petaluma, California. Known for her high-pitched vocals and eclectic performances, she plays a variety of instruments, including sitar, ukulele, accordion, theremin, and toy piano. A protégé of Les Claypool, she has performed in his Fancy Band and collaborated with artists such as Snoop Dogg, Jane Wiedlin, and The Coup. La La has also performed at Bonnaroo and taught music to young girls. She is recognized for her colorful stage presence and unique musical style.

trgt: The Coaldale Big Green was an early professional football team based in Coaldale, Pennsylvania. The club played as an independent until joining the Anthracite League in 1924. After leaving the league in 1924, the team spent its 1925 season as an independent, then joined the short-lived Eastern League of Professional Football in 1926. While the most well known Anthracite League team is the Pottsville Maroons, which jumped to the National Football League in 1925 and immediately established itself as a championship contender, the most consistently successful club in the coal region was the Big Green. Coaldale won the coal region's Curran Cup in 1921, 1922 and 1923.
mgt: The Coaldale Big Green was an American football team based in Coaldale, Pennsylvania, that operated from 1912 to 1933. Originally formed as Coaldale Football by the Coaldale Athletic Association and James H. Gildea, the team adopted the "Big Green" name in 1917 after introducing green jerseys. Known for its reliance on local talent, the team won the Curran Cup in 1921, 1922, and 1923. In 1924, Coaldale helped establish the Anthracite League but left after disputes over player recruitment rules. The team later joined the Eastern League of Professional Football in 1926 before returning to independent play and folding around 1933.

trgt:
VirusTotal is a website created by the Spanish security company Hispasec Sistemas. Launched in June 2004, it was acquired by Google in September 2012. The company's ownership switched in January 2018 to Chronicle, a subsidiary of Google.

VirusTotal aggregates many antivirus products and online scan engines called Contributors. In November, 2018, the Cyber National Mission Force, a unit subordinate to the U.S. Cyber Command became a Contributor. The aggregated data from these Contributors allows a user to check for viruses that the user's own antivirus software may have missed, or to verify against any false positives. Files up to 650 MB can be uploaded to the website, or sent via email (max. 32MB). Anti-virus software vendors can receive copies of files that were flagged by other scans but passed by their own engine, to help improve their software and, by extension, VirusTotal's own capability. Users can also scan suspect URLs and search through the VirusTotal dataset. VirusTotal uses the Cuckoo sandbox for dynamic analysis of malware. VirusTotal was selected by PC World as one of the best 100 products of 2007.
mgt:
VirusTotal is an online service that analyzes files and URLs for viruses, worms, trojans, and other types of malicious content. Launched in June 2004 by Hispasec Sistemas, the platform allows users to upload files or submit URLs to be scanned by multiple antivirus engines and tools. It supports a wide range of languages and is headquartered in Dublin, Ireland. VirusTotal was acquired by Google in 2012 and later became part of Google's cybersecurity subsidiary, Chronicle, in 2018.

The service offers various tools, including browser extensions, a mobile app for Android, and uploaders for Windows, macOS, and Linux, enabling users to scan files and URLs directly from their devices. It also provides a public API for automating tasks such as file uploads, URL scans, and report retrieval, though usage restrictions apply. VirusTotal retains uploaded files and shares them with anti-malware companies to enhance threat detection. While its Windows Uploader was discontinued in 2017, the platform remains active and widely used for cybersecurity purposes.

trgt:
Club Deportivo Corporación Arturo Fernández Vial is a football club in Chile, from the Concepción area, in the Bio-Bio Region. The team was founded on June 3, 1903, and they currently play in the second level of Chilean football, the Primera B.

Arturo Fernández Vial identifies itself since its beginning with the national corporation of railroad workers, and it was the most popular team in southern Chile. Its traditional rival is Deportes Concepción. Also, the team's "hinchada" (die-hard supporters) are known as La Furia Guerrera.
mgt: Club Deportivo Corporación Arturo Fernández Vial, commonly known as Fernández Vial, is a Chilean football club based in Concepción. Founded on June 3, 1903, the club was named in honor of Admiral Arturo Fernández Vial, a national figure admired for his role in resolving a labor dispute. Known by nicknames such as "La Máquina Aurinegra," the team plays its home matches at Estadio Municipal de Concepción, which has a capacity of 30,448. Fernández Vial has a storied history, including a remarkable rise from the third to the first division in just two years (1981–1982). Despite financial struggles in recent decades, the club maintains a loyal fanbase and currently competes in Chile's Primera B.

trgt:
Kamaal the Abstract is the third studio album by American hip hop artist Q-Tip, released September 15, 2009, on Battery Records. Recorded in 2001, the album is a departure from his solo debut album Amplified (1999). Kamaal the Abstract is an eclectic album that features Q-Tip rapping, singing, and exploring his jazz influences. The album contains introspective and life-related lyrical themes.

The album was originally planned for release in 2002 as the follow-up to Amplified. However, it was shelved by Q-Tip's record label at the time, Arista, who doubted its commercial potential. It eventually leaked onto the internet, while the distribution of promotional copies led several publications to run reviews of the album.

After its official release in 2009, the album debuted at number 77 on the U.S. Billboard 200 chart, selling 6,000 copies in its first week. Upon its release, Kamaal the Abstract received positive reviews from most music critics.
mgt:
*Kamaal the Abstract* is the second studio album by American rapper and producer Q-Tip, released on September 15, 2009, through Battery Records. Originally recorded in 2001, the album was shelved by Arista Records due to concerns over its commercial viability but gained a cult following after promotional copies leaked online. The album marks a departure from Q-Tip's mainstream-oriented debut, *Amplified* (1999), showcasing an eclectic blend of neo-soul, jazz fusion, funk, and hip hop, with improvisational arrangements and introspective lyrics. Its instrumentation features electric piano, flute, deep organs, and guitar grooves, drawing comparisons to works by Prince, D'Angelo, and the Soulquarians collective.

Upon its eventual release, *Kamaal the Abstract* received generally positive reviews for its experimental sound, though some critics noted its uneven execution. It debuted at number 77 on the Billboard 200 and includes the single "Barely in Love." The album is considered a bold, genre-defying effort in Q-Tip's discography.

trgt:
Download coordinates as:

The Grand Loop Road is a historic district which encompasses the primary road system in Yellowstone National Park. Much of the 140-mile (230 km) system was originally planned by Captain Hiram M. Chittenden of the US Army Corps of Engineers in the early days of the park, when it was under military administration. The Grand Loop Road provides access to the major features of the park, including the Upper, Midway and Lower geyser basins, Mammoth Hot Springs, Tower Fall, the Grand Canyon of the Yellowstone and Yellowstone Lake.

A number of other historic districts adjoin the Grand Loop in Yellowstone, including Mammoth Hot Springs, North Entrance Road, Roosevelt Lodge, Lake Fish Hatchery and Old Faithful historic districts.

Grand Loop Rd. includes unsigned portions of US 20, US 89, US 191 and US 287, all of which traverse the park. The road was named by Harry W. Frantz in 1923, who served as the park's publicist before beginning a long career as a journalist.
mgt:
The Grand Loop Road is a historic roadway in Yellowstone National Park, Wyoming, that forms a figure-eight loop connecting many of the park's most notable natural features and landmarks. Constructed by the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers and later maintained by the National Park Service, the road was added to the National Register of Historic Places on December 23, 2003. 

The route begins near Mammoth Hot Springs at the park's headquarters and traverses diverse landscapes, including Golden Gate Canyon, Obsidian Canyon, and the Norris Geyser Basin. It passes iconic sites such as Sheepeater Cliff, Old Faithful in the Upper Geyser Basin, Yellowstone Lake, and the Grand Canyon of the Yellowstone. The road also crosses the Continental Divide and Dunraven Pass, offering access to geysers, waterfalls, and scenic valleys. 

The Grand Loop Road connects with several entrance roads, providing access to neighboring areas like Grand Teton National Park and Cooke City, Montana, making it a central feature of Yellowstone's infrastructure.

trgt:
Lashkargāh (Lang-ps; Lang-fa), historically called Bost or Boost (بست، بوست), is a city in southwestern Afghanistan and the capital of Helmand Province. It is located in Lashkargah District, where the Arghandab River merges into the Helmand River. The city has a population of 201,546 as of 2006. Lashkargah is linked by major roads with Kandahar to the east, Zaranj on the border with Iran to the west, and Farah and Herat to the north-west. It is mostly very arid and desolate. However, farming does exist around the Helmand and Arghandab rivers. Bost Airport is located on the east bank of the Helmand River, five miles north of the junction of the Helmand and Arghandab rivers. Because of the trading hubs, it is Afghanistan's second largest city in size, after Kabul and before Kandahar.

After several weeks of fighting in the Battle of Lashkargah, the city was captured by the Taliban on 13 August 2021, becoming the fourteenth provincial capital to be seized by the Taliban as part of the wider 2021 Taliban offensive.
mgt:
Lashkargah (Pashto: لشکرگاه), also known as Bost, is a city in southern Afghanistan and the capital of Helmand Province. Situated at the confluence of the Helmand and Arghandab Rivers, it serves as a trading and transit hub with a population of approximately 276,831 as of 2015. The city has historical significance, dating back to the 9th century as part of the Saffarid Empire and later serving as a Ghaznavid military barracks town. Over the centuries, it was governed by various empires, including the Ghorids, Timurids, Safavids, and the Durrani Empire.

Modern Lashkargah was developed in the 1950s as part of the Helmand Valley Authority irrigation project, modeled after the Tennessee Valley Authority in the United States. The city features American-style urban planning, though decades of conflict have altered its landscape. Lashkargah has been a focal point in Afghanistan's recent history, witnessing battles during the Soviet invasion, Taliban rule, and U.S.-led military operations. It fell under Taliban control in August 2021. The city is also known for its cultural heritage, including the Ghaznavid fortress of Lashkari Bazar and Qala-e-Bost.

trgt:
"I Am Real" is the debut single by Australian hotelier Simon van Kempen. It was released on 21 April 2011, through JSM Music, during his wife Alex McCord's tenure on the American reality television series The Real Housewives of New York City. The song was written by Allison Beth Simon, Jon Jason Appleton, and Koki Saito, while production was handled by Joel Simon, Ross Hopman, and Saito. During its development, van Kempen and McCord met with the team of writers to ensure that the song's lyrics would represent them accurately. A dance-pop and EDM track, "I Am Real" contains tongue-in-cheek lyrics that address van Kempen's critics. One lyric references a comment McCord used to describe fellow cast member Luann de Lesseps.

"I Am Real" received mostly unfavourable reviews from music critics, who dismissed van Kempen as a television personality who tried launching a singing career. It also received comparisons to "Money Can't Buy You Class" (2010) by de Lesseps. The song was unsuccessful, selling just 1,000 copies in the United States, as of September 2011. van Kempen has since performed "I Am Real" on Watch What Happens Live with Andy Cohen and at Manhattan's annual "Fashion Night Out" event in 2011.
mgt:
"I Am Real" is the debut and only single by Simon van Kempen, an Australian hotelier and husband of Alex McCord, a cast member of *The Real Housewives of New York City*. Released on April 21, 2011, the dance-pop and EDM track was written by Allison Beth Simon, Jon Jason Appleton, and Koki Saito, with production by Joel Simon, Ross Hopman, and Saito. The song, recorded at JSM Music Studios in New York City, reflects van Kempen's experiences with reality television, addressing criticism, class, and his interactions with cast members of *The Real Housewives*. Its lyrics include playful and self-deprecating commentary, such as the now-famous line "Thug in a cocktail dress," referencing a remark by McCord on the show.

Critics offered mixed to negative reviews, with some calling it a "musical punch line" and others appreciating its uniqueness among *Real Housewives* singles. Despite its notoriety, the song sold approximately 1,000 digital copies in the U.S. and was ranked among the franchise's least successful musical releases. Van Kempen promoted the track with live performances, including on *Watch What Happens Live* and at Manhattan's Fashion Night Out. The song's development and reception were later revisited in Bravo's 2017 web series *Throwback Bravo*.

trgt:
Kadampanad grama panchayath is the southernmost part of Pathanamthitta district. It comes under Parakkode block of Adoor taluk. The panchayath shared boundaries with Erathu panchayath in the north, Ezhamkulam panchayath in the east, Kunnathur panchayath and Kalladayar in the south and Pallikkal and Poruvazhy Panchayaths in the west. Kakkathikkunnu in the north and Pandimalakkunnu (Pandimalappuram) in the south stands as two strong forts in this panchayath which balances the climatic conditions of this area.

Kadampanad, Kallukuzhi, Thuvayoor and Mannadi areas form Kadampanad panchayath. Mannadi is known as the place where Veluthambi Dalawa, the great freedom fighter, died.
mgt: Kadampanad is a village in the Pathanamthitta district of Kerala, India, known for its historical and cultural significance. It was historically part of ancient Tamil Nadu and later came under the rule of the Kulasekhara and Venad kings before becoming part of Travancore. The village spans 23.95 km² and features diverse geography, including rivers, hilly areas, and paddy fields. Kadampanad is home to numerous religious sites, including St. Thomas Orthodox Cathedral and the Kadampanad Bhagavathy Temple, reflecting its cultural diversity. Tourist attractions include the Veluthambi Dalawa memorial and the Aravakkalu Chani Cave in Mannadi.

trgt: Shanghai Street is a 2.3 km long street in the Jordan, Yau Ma Tei and Mong Kok areas of Kowloon, Hong Kong, China. Completed in 1887 under the name of Station Street (差館街), it was once the most prosperous street in Kowloon. It originates from the south at Austin Road, and terminates in the north at Lai Chi Kok Road. Parallel to Shanghai Street are Nathan Road, Temple Street, Portland Street, Reclamation Street and Canton Road. Though parallel, Shanghai Street was marked by 2- to 3-floor Chinese-style buildings while Nathan Road was marked by Western-style buildings.
mgt: Shanghai Street (Chinese: 上海街) is one of the oldest streets in Hong Kong, located in the Yau Ma Tei and Mong Kok districts of Kowloon. Originally called Station Street, it was renamed in 1909 to reflect Hong Kong's trade connections with Shanghai. The street, built on reclaimed land, was a major commercial hub from the late 19th to mid-20th century, featuring traditional Chinese shops and trades. Notable landmarks include the Engineer's Office of the Former Pumping Station, a Grade I historic building, and pre-war shophouses (tong lau), which are part of ongoing heritage conservation and revitalization efforts.

trgt: Barbara Sue Gilders (later Dudeck, born July 23, 1937) is a retired American diver. She competed in the 3 m springboard at the 1956 Summer Olympics and 1959 Pan American Games and finished fourth and third, respectively. Coached by four-time Olympic medalist, Clarence Pinkston, Gilders entered the Olympics as the 1956 AAU champion, and Olympic Trials silver medalist. Later she won the AAU indoor titles in the one-meter (1958) and three-meter springboard (1959). In June 1959, she won the Pan American Games trials; later that summer, in what would be her final international competition, Gilders won a bronze medal at the Pan American Games.
mgt: Barbara Sue Gilders (born July 23, 1937) is an American former diver who won a gold medal in the 3-meter springboard event at the 1959 Pan American Games in Chicago. She competed for the Detroit Athletic Club and was coached by Clarence Pinkston. Gilders is the younger sister of Fletcher Gilders, a two-time NCAA diving champion and Hall of Fame diving coach. She married John Dudeck, a former Michigan State University swimmer and nine-time All-American. Their daughter, Diane Dudeck, followed in her footsteps, winning the national indoor title in the one-meter springboard in 1981 and earning NCAA All-American honors in 1984.

trgt:
FC Stal Kamianske (Lang-uk) was a professional Ukrainian football club based in Kamianske, Dnipropetrovsk Oblast, Ukraine. After being relegated to the Ukrainian First League after the 2017–18 Ukrainian Premier League season, the club re-registered to Bucha and changed its name to PFC Feniks Bucha in June 2018. The club didn't play any game under the new name and was dissolved on 9 July 2018.

The club was sponsored by the Dnieper Metallurgical Combine (DMK) which is a member of the Industrial Union of Donbas (ISD).
mgt: FC Stal Kamianske was a Ukrainian football club founded in 1926 and dissolved in 2018. Based in Kamianske, the club underwent several name changes and periods of inactivity before becoming FC Stal Kamianske in 2016. The team competed in various leagues, including the Ukrainian Premier League, where it played from 2015 to 2018 after taking the place of the dissolved FC Metalurh Donetsk. Following relegation in 2018, the club re-registered as PFC Feniks Bucha but withdrew from competition due to financial difficulties. Its colors were silver, blue, and white.

trgt: William Scott Husel is an American intensive/critical care physician who was charged (and later acquitted) with 14 counts of first degree murder relating to the deaths of multiple patients from his care of terminally ill patients at Mount Carmel West and St. Ann's Hospitals in Columbus, Ohio. The prosecution in this case argued that Dr. Husel hastened the death of terminally ill patients. He turned himself in on June 5, 2019. After an extensive trial, Husel was found not guilty on all counts on April 20, 2022.
mgt: William Scott Husel (born November 11, 1975) is an American physician who was accused of prescribing excessive doses of fentanyl to patients during his tenure as an intensive-care doctor at Mount Carmel West Hospital in Columbus, Ohio. Between 2015 and 2018, 34 patient deaths were linked to his prescriptions, leading to his indictment on 25 counts of murder in 2019. His trial began in February 2022, with the defense arguing the medications were intended for palliative care. On April 20, 2022, Husel was acquitted of all charges. He later surrendered his medical license.

trgt: The FIVB Senior Continental Rankings is a ranking system for men's and women's national teams in volleyball. Divided to five continental confederations, the teams of the member nations of FIVB, volleyball's world governing body, are ranked based on their game results with the most successful teams being ranked highest. A points system is used, with points being awarded based on the results of all FIVB-recognised full international matches. The rankings are used in international competitions to define the seeded teams and arrange them in pools. Specific procedures for seeding and pooling are established by the FIVB in each competition's formula, but the method usually employed is the serpentine system.
mgt: The FIVB Senior Continental Rankings are a system used to rank national volleyball teams within their respective continental confederations. In 2019, the FIVB collaborated with Hypercube Business Innovation to develop a new ranking platform to address issues in the previous system, which limited ranking opportunities to teams participating in major events. The updated system, implemented on February 1, 2020, considers all match results from January 1, 2019, and aims to provide a fairer and more comprehensive evaluation of team performance. The rankings are consistently updated to reflect the latest results, including matches between teams within the same continental confederations across official competitions.

trgt:
Christian Malford is a village and civil parish in the county of Wiltshire, England. The village lies about 4+1⁄2 miles (7 km) northeast of the town of Chippenham. The Bristol Avon forms most of the northern and eastern boundaries of the parish. The hamlets of Thornend and Upper Town lie within the parish.

The unusual name is evidently a corruption of Christ mal Ford, Old English moel, mal being a mark: "Christ’s mal" is Christ's mark or sign, the cross. Thus the name signified "Cross Ford". Deeds from Glastonbury Abbey cartulary relate to Christmalford Manor: in AD 940 King Edmund granted Christmalford to St Dunstan, Abbot of Glastonbury. In 1086 the Abbey of Glastonbury remained tenant-in-chief of Cristemeleforee in the ancient Domesday hundred of Sterchelai (Startley). Christian Malford and the other parishes of Startley hundred were amalgamated with additional parishes to form the hundred of Malmesbury.
mgt:
Christian Malford is a village and civil parish in Wiltshire, England, located near the River Avon and approximately 5 miles northeast of Chippenham. The village had a population of 705 as of the 2011 census. It is notable for its Middle Jurassic fossil site, rediscovered in 2008, which contains exceptionally preserved specimens, including ammonites, fish, and squid-like cephalopods.

The parish church of All Saints, a Grade I listed building, dates to around 1300 and features elements from earlier periods, including a 12th-century font and 15th-century stained glass. A Congregational chapel, built in 1836 and later part of the United Reformed Church, closed in 1998.

Christian Malford is served by the B4069 road and is near the Great Western Main Line railway. The village has a primary school, a village hall, and a pub, the Rising Sun.

trgt:
GABA transporter 2 (GAT2; SLC6A13) also known as sodium- and chloride-dependent GABA transporter 2 is one of four GABA transporters, GAT1 (SLC6A1), GAT2 (SLC6A13), GAT3 (SLC6A11) and BGT1 (SLC6A12). Note that GAT2 is different from BGT1 despite the fact that the latter transporter is sometimes referred at as (mouse) GAT-2.

All these transporters are highly hydrophobic proteins with 12 transmembrane segments, extracellular glycosylation sites, and intracellular consensus sites for phosphorylation, and there is over 50% amino acid homology between each of them. Each binds GABA with varying affinities with BGT1 having the lowest affinity and GAT3 the highest. GAT2 (SLC6A13) is predominantly expressed in hepatocytes in the liver, but is also found in proximal tubules in the kidney as well as in the leptomeninges and in some blood vessels in the brain.
mgt: GABA transporter type 2 (GAT2) is a protein involved in the transport of gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA) and taurine across cell membranes. It is expressed in various tissues, including the brain, liver, and kidneys, though its physiological roles remain partially understood. In the brain, GAT2 facilitates the efflux of GABA and taurine through the blood-brain barrier, but it has a lower affinity for GABA compared to GAT1 and GAT3, which are more influential in neurotransmitter activity. In the liver, GAT2 is primarily involved in taurine transport and may play a role in GABA uptake from the intestine. In the kidneys, it is localized to the basolateral membranes of proximal tubules, though its function is unclear. While GAT2's clinical significance is undetermined, GABA transporters are linked to epilepsy and other neurological disorders.

trgt: WU polyomavirus (also known as WU virus, WUPyV, or Human polyomavirus 4) is a virus of the family Polyomaviridae. It was discovered in 2007 in samples of human respiratory secretions, originally from a child patient in Australia who presented with clinical signs of pneumonia and in whom other common respiratory viruses were not detected. Follow-up studies identified the presence of WU virus in respiratory secretion samples from patients in Australia and the United States, suggesting that, like other human polyomaviruses, WU virus is widely distributed.
mgt: WU polyomavirus (WU virus), also known as Human polyomavirus 4, is a species of virus in the family *Polyomaviridae* and genus *Betapolyomavirus*. Discovered in 2007 at Washington University, it was identified through shotgun sequencing of respiratory secretions from a pneumonia patient. The virus has a circular double-stranded DNA genome encoding five viral proteins. WU virus is globally distributed, with seroprevalence rates ranging from 54% to 97% in various populations, suggesting primary infection occurs in childhood. While its pathogenicity remains unclear, it may have potential relevance in immunocompromised individuals.

trgt:
The Ma Tante River is a tributary of the Franquelin River, flowing in the township of Franquelin, in the municipality of Franquelin, in the Manicouagan Regional County Municipality, in the administrative region of Côte-Nord, in the province of Quebec, in Canada.

Forestry is the main economic activity in this valley; recreational tourism, second.

The surface of this Middle North Shore river is usually frozen from the beginning of November to the end of April, except the rapids; however, safe circulation on the ice is generally from late November to early April.
mgt: The Ma Tante River is a short river in the Côte-Nord region of Quebec, Canada, located within the municipalities of Rivière-aux-Outardes and Franquelin in the Manicouagan Regional County Municipality. Originating from Ma Tante Lake at an elevation of 197 meters, the river flows for 5.4 kilometers through a forested area before emptying into the Franquelin River at an elevation of 151 meters. The river's name, formalized in 1974, is associated with the nearby Mon Oncle River, both of which reference familial terms. It was first documented on a 1933 map by the Ontario Paper Company.

trgt:
GRRR! is a greatest hits album by the Rolling Stones. Released on 12 November 2012, it commemorates the band's 50th anniversary. The album features two new songs titled "Doom and Gloom" and "One More Shot", which were recorded in August 2012.

"Doom and Gloom" peaked at No. 61 in the UK Charts, No. 26 on the Billboard Japan Hot 100 and No. 30 on the Billboard Rock Songs chart in October 2012. Rolling Stone named "Doom and Gloom" the 18th best song of 2012.

The album reached #3 on the UK Albums Chart and #19 on the Billboard 200.
mgt: *GRRR!* is a greatest hits album by the Rolling Stones, released on November 12, 2012, to celebrate the band's 50th anniversary. Spanning recordings from 1963 to 2012, the album features classic hits alongside two new singles, "Doom and Gloom" and "One More Shot." The cover artwork, created by Walton Ford, depicts a gorilla with the band's iconic tongue and lips logo, symbolizing their enduring legacy. While the band praised the irreverent design, fan reactions were mixed. The album was released in multiple formats, and an augmented reality app brought the cover to life with animation.

trgt:
Jacob "Jake" Blankenship (born March 15, 1994) is an American track and field athlete who competes in the pole vault. He holds a personal record of 5.80 m (19 ft 1⁄4 in) for the event, set in 2015. He placed third at the USA Outdoor Track and Field Championships in 2015.

Blankenship won medals at the World Youth Championships in Athletics in 2011 and NACAC Under-23 Championships in Athletics in 2014. He represents Tennessee Volunteers team collegiately and has had top three finishes both indoors and outdoors in NCAA competition, including winning the 2016 Outdoor Championships.
mgt: Jacob Blankenship is an American pole vaulter from Blacklick, Ohio, who gained prominence for his record-setting performances in high school and collegiate athletics. While attending Gahanna Lincoln High School, he set Ohio state records in pole vaulting and earned a bronze medal at the 2011 World Youth Championships. He competed collegiately for the University of Tennessee, where he achieved multiple podium finishes, including a third-place finish at the 2014 NCAA Outdoor Championships and a gold medal at the 2014 NACAC Under-23 Championships. Blankenship also placed third at the 2015 USA Outdoor Track and Field Championships.

trgt:
Horse pulling is a draft horse competition where horses in harness, usually one or two animals, pull a stone-boat or weighted sled and the winner is the team or animal that can pull the most weight for a short distance.

There are different weight classes and strict rules to avoid animal cruelty.

Draft breeds are commonly used, but here are also competitions for ponies, where they pull significant weight relative to their size, but usually for shorter distances than full-sized horses.

Related sports are tractor pulling and Canine Weight pulling. However, horses pull less weight than tractors, but much more than dogs.
mgt: Horse pulling is a competitive sport that originated from the agricultural practice of using draft horses for farm work. Farmers would challenge each other to see whose horses could pull the heaviest loads, evolving into an organized sport featuring teams of equine athletes trained specifically for competition. The sport often utilizes a dynamometer, a machine developed in the 1920s to measure the pulling power of horses, which is now used in competitions to test teams' strength. Events are governed by rules and regulations, such as those established by the Eastern Draft Horse Association, to ensure the safety and welfare of the horses.

trgt: Millennium Fantasy for Piano and Orchestra is a piano concerto written by the American composer Ellen Taaffe Zwilich. The work was commissioned by the pianist Jeffrey Biegel in association with the Adele Marcus Foundation, the Abraham J. & Phyllis Katz Foundation, the South Florida Council of the Chopin Foundation of the United States, Isa and Marvin Leibowitz, the American Music Center, and a consortium of 27 American orchestras. It was first performed by Jeffrey Biegel and the Cincinnati Symphony Orchestra under the direction of Jesús López Cobos in Cincinnati on September 22, 2000.
mgt: *Millennium Fantasy* is a composition for solo piano and orchestra by American composer Ellen Taaffe Zwilich. Written in two movements with a performance duration of approximately 20 minutes, the piece is based on the folk song "Come All You Fair and Tender Ladies," a melody Zwilich's grandmother sang to her in childhood. The work premiered to critical acclaim, with reviewers praising its vibrant dialogue between piano and orchestra and its broad appeal. A recording featuring pianist Jeffrey Biegel and the Florida State University Symphony Orchestra was released by Naxos Records in 2010.

trgt: Winter Haven Hospital is a hospital and health service system based in Winter Haven, Florida. The hospital is managed and supported by the Winter Haven Hospital Foundation—a 501(c)3 non-profit organization—and is owned by BayCare Health System, a group of 15 not-for-profit hospitals in the Tampa Bay Area. The Winter Haven Hospital system includes the hospital proper, the Community Blood Center, the Regency Medical Center for Women and Infants, the Winter Haven Ambulatory Surgery and Diagnostic Center, the Center for Behavioral Health, and seven Family Health Centers in Winter Haven and surrounding communities.
mgt: Winter Haven Hospital is a non-profit hospital located in Winter Haven, Florida, and is part of the BayCare Health System. Established in 1926, the hospital has grown significantly, with its main campus featuring 527 licensed beds and specialized facilities, including the Bostick Heart Center, Cassidy Cancer Center, and a certified Stroke Center. The hospital also operates the Regency Center for Women and Infants, seven Family Health Centers, and other satellite facilities. Known as one of the largest private employers in Polk County, it provides a wide range of medical services and has undergone numerous expansions and modernizations.

trgt: The San Lorenzo march is an Argentine military march, whose lyrics celebrate the role played by the Regiment of Mounted Grenadiers commanded by José de San Martín at the Battle of San Lorenzo during the Argentine War of Independence. Special mention receives the heroic sergeant Juan Bautista Cabral. The music was composed in 1901 by Uruguayan musician Cayetano Alberto Silva, and the lyrics in 1908 by Carlos Javier Benielli. It was later incorporated into the musical repertoires of other military bands around the world.
mgt: The "San Lorenzo March" is a military march composed by Cayetano Alberto Silva in 1901 and first performed on October 30, 1902, in Rosario, Argentina. Dedicated to General José de San Martín and the Battle of San Lorenzo, it later received lyrics by Carlos Javier Benielli in 1908. The march gained international recognition, being performed at events such as the coronations of King George V and Queen Elizabeth II and during the changing of the guard at Buckingham Palace. It is considered one of the finest military marches globally.

trgt:
Budha Subba Temple (Manghim) or Budha Subba (Lang-ne) is a famous religious shrine of the eastern people of Nepal which is located at Bijayapur of Dharan, Nepal. The site is located on the top of the Seuti river bank, to small distance east of the Dantakali Temple. It is taken as an important place for religious people. Within the temple, two earthen piles have been raised which have the tradition of worshiping as the Budha Subba. The place has uniquely tip-less bamboo plants. The tips of these bamboos are believed to be broken by shots of slingshot used by Budha subba.Regarded by many as one of the, if not "The Most important cultural site of Eastern Nepal." The temple is crowded by people in Saturdays. Generally at Saturdays, sacrifices of rooster and pigs are made. The temple is systematically handled since 2015 BS (1958-59 CE).

Historian Iman Xin Chemjong says Bijaypur was the capital of the Limbuwan (Pallo Kirat) kingdom during and after the reign of King Bijaynarayan Raya Khebang.
mgt:
Budha Subba Temple (बुढा सुब्बा) is a revered religious site located in Dharan, Sunsari District, Nepal, situated atop the Seuti River bank near the Dantakali Temple. Associated with Hinduism, the temple is steeped in local myths and historical significance. According to legend, the temple is named after a Subba hunter, Budha Subba, who meditated at the site after giving up hunting. The bamboo trees in the area are unique, as they lack tips, a phenomenon linked to the myth. Another belief ties the temple to the tomb of Budhhi Karna Khebang, the last Limbu King of Limbuwan, who was killed in 1773.

The temple is also associated with Lord Shiva and Goddess Parvati, as well as the Mahabharata figure Ekalavya. Visitors traditionally wrote names on bamboo trees to seek blessings for love, though this practice has been replaced by tying sacred threads. The temple remains a popular pilgrimage site, attracting devotees seeking spiritual fulfillment and good fortune.

trgt: Dolphin Tale 2 is a 2014 American family film written and directed by Charles Martin Smith as the sequel to his 2011 film Dolphin Tale which in-turn was based on the true story about a rescued bottlenose dolphin named Winter who made her final on-screen appearance in this film. In addition to Winter, most of the cast from the first film also reprise their roles including Harry Connick Jr., Ashley Judd, Nathan Gamble, Cozi Zuehlsdorff, Kris Kristofferson, Morgan Freeman, Austin Stowell, Tom Nowicki, Betsy Landin and Juliana Harkavy while Hope made her film debut. It was released on September 12, 2014, and tells the story of another dolphin at the Clearwater Marine Aquarium named "Hope". After Winter's elderly companion and surrogate mother, Panama, dies, Winter's future is in jeopardy, unless Sawyer, Clay, Hazel, and the rest of the team can find a new companion for her. The film received generally mixed to positive reviews from critics and grossed $57.8 million.
mgt:
*Dolphin Tale 2* is a 2014 American family drama film directed and written by Charles Martin Smith. A sequel to the 2011 film *Dolphin Tale*, it continues the story of Winter, a rescued dolphin with a prosthetic tail, and introduces Hope, another rescued dolphin. The film follows the staff at Clearwater Marine Aquarium as they navigate challenges, including Winter's loneliness after the death of her companion and the need to find her a new one to prevent her transfer to another facility. The movie stars Harry Connick Jr., Ashley Judd, Nathan Gamble, Cozi Zuehlsdorff, and Morgan Freeman.

Filmed at the Clearwater Marine Aquarium in Florida, production ran from October 2013 to January 2014, with the film released on September 12, 2014. It received mixed to positive reviews, praised for its family-friendly themes and emotional depth. A web series and discussions of a third installment followed, though Winter's death in 2021 shifted focus to Hope for any future projects.

trgt:
Henry Arinze Anthony Ugboma (born 15 November 1966) is a Nigerian Professor of Maternal-fetal medicine and the present Chief Medical Director of the University of Port Harcourt Teaching Hospital, Rivers State.

Henry A. A. Ugboma is an alumnus of the University of Port Harcourt School of Medicine. He is a consultant, Feto-Maternal and infectious gynecologist and a Fellow of the International College of Surgeons – FICS. He had training in Leadership and Administration Processes in Rivers State. In 2016, he obtained a certificate of Professional Management from the Nigerian Institute of Management (charted). He further obtained training in Information and Communication Technology. In 2017, he became a member of the Administrative Staff College of Nigeria (ASCON). Consequently, in 2019, he was conferred a Fellow of the Institute of Health Service Administrators of Nigeria (IHSAN).
mgt:
Henry A. A. Ugboma (born November 15, 1966) is a Nigerian professor of feto-maternal medicine, medical administrator, and Chief Medical Director (CMD) of the University of Port Harcourt Teaching Hospital (UPTH) since 2017. Born in Onitsha, Anambra State, he earned his medical degree from the University of Nigeria, Nsukka, and later became a Fellow of the West African Postgraduate Medical College and the International College of Surgeons. Ugboma has held various academic and administrative roles, including professor in the Department of Obstetrics and Gynaecology at the University of Port Harcourt, where he contributed significantly to the prevention of mother-to-child transmission of HIV.

As CMD, Ugboma modernized UPTH by acquiring advanced medical equipment and establishing new departmental units. He is married to Dr. Enighe Wanayo Ugboma, a radiologist, and they have five children. Ugboma has published extensively in medical research.

trgt:
Vlașca County is a former first-order administrative division of the Kingdom of Romania, in southern Muntenia, located between Bucharest and the Danube, which is now mostly the county of Giurgiu. The county seat was Giurgiu.

The county was located in the southern part of Greater Romania, in the southern part of the historical region of Muntenia. The county was bordered on the west by Teleorman County, on the northwest by Argeș County, on the north by Dâmbovița County, on the east by Ilfov County, and to the south by the Kingdom of Bulgaria. Its surface coincides in large part with that of the present Giurgiu County, but there are small parts of it that are included in today's Teleorman County.
mgt:
Vlașca County was a historic county in southern Romania, located in the region of Wallachia, with its administrative seat in Giurgiu. Established before 1601, the county covered an area of 4,494 square kilometers and had a population of 296,412 according to the 1930 census, predominantly Eastern Orthodox Romanians. Administratively, it was divided into five districts, later expanded to seven by the 1930s. 

In 1938, Vlașca County was dissolved and incorporated into Ținutul Argeș but was re-established in 1940 following the fall of King Carol II's regime. It was ultimately abolished in 1950 during the administrative reforms of the Communist regime. The county's urban population in 1930 was 31,016, with a similar ethnic and religious composition to the overall population.

trgt:
Pakistan has bilateral and multilateral trade agreements with many nations and international organizations. It is a member of the World Trade Organization, part of the South Asian Free Trade Area agreement and the China–Pakistan Free Trade Agreement. Fluctuating world demand for its exports, domestic political uncertainty, and the impact of occasional droughts on its agricultural production have all contributed to variability in Pakistan's trade deficit. The trade deficit for the fiscal year 2013/14 is $7.743 billion, exports are $10.367 billion in July–November 2013 and imports are $18.110 billion.

Pakistan's exports continue to be dominated by manpower export in the subcontinent, cotton textiles and apparel. Imports include petroleum and petroleum products, chemicals, fertilizer, capital goods, industrial raw materials, and consumer products.

On 12 December 2013, the European Union granted GSP Plus status to Pakistan until 2017, which enabled it to export 20% of its good with 0 tariff and 70 percent at preferential rates to the EU market. This status was given after the European Parliament passed the resolution by 406-186 votes.
mgt:
The foreign trade of Pakistan plays a significant role in the country's economy, with exports and imports contributing to its economic activity. In the fiscal year 2015–2016, Pakistan's exports amounted to $21 billion, while imports stood at $44.76 billion. Over the years, Pakistan's exports have grown substantially, increasing from $7.5 billion in 1999 to $18 billion in 2007–2008, and reaching $29 billion by April 2015.

Pakistan's key exports include rice, textiles, clothing, leather goods, sports goods (notably footballs), surgical instruments, cement, seafood, processed food, and defense equipment such as submarines and tanks. The country also exports engineering goods, software, and agricultural products like mangoes and kinnows. Cement is a notable export to regions like Asia, the Middle East, and India. Russia has emerged as a growing market for Pakistani exports.

Pakistan maintains trade relations with various countries and has business ties with numerous international companies, reflecting its integration into the global trade network.

trgt:
Randy Kaplan (b. Randall Leigh Kaplan) is an American songwriter, playwright, poet, and performer. He gained renown for his “not-JUST-for-kids” music in the mid-aughts, first in Brooklyn, New York, and later nationwide. Dedicated members of his fan base call themselves FORKs (Fans of Randy Kaplan). He was signed with myKaZoo Music for his CD dedicated to Country Blues and Ragtime music (Mr. Diddie Wah Diddie), which Universal Music released.

Randy has released six not-JUST-for-kids CDs (with his seventh, Shake It and Break It, released on May 10, 2019). Previous "kids" records include Five Cent Piece (2006), Loquat Rooftop (2008), The Kids Are All Id (2010), Mr. Diddie Wah Diddie (2012), Jam on Rye (2014), and Trippin'Round the Mitten (2017). Previous not-REALLY-for-kids records include Boyish Hips (1997), Reborn As Bees (1999), Lake Champions (1999), Miraculous Dissolving Cures (2001), Perfect Gentleman (2004), Ancient Ruins (2008), Durango (2008), and Songs For Old Lovers (2011). Randy has also released several digital singles, including "Don't Fill Up On Chips,""Nagasaki,""On the Phone on the Toilet,"and"Hugs for My Family, High Fives for My Friends."

Randy's songs have been included on many compilations, including those for Putumayo World Music, Fast Folk, and Park Slope Parents.
mgt:
Randy Kaplan is an American singer-songwriter, actor, and playwright known for his eclectic musical style that blends American roots, country blues, and comedic storytelling. Born and raised in Dix Hills, Long Island, Kaplan studied philosophy, writing, and literature at the University of Michigan and UCLA before pursuing acting and music. He began his career performing in theater, film, and television, including appearances on *Beauty and the Beast* and *Growing Pains*. In the early 1990s, he fronted the band "i" in Los Angeles before relocating to New York City, where he released his debut album, *Boyish Hips*, in 1997.

Kaplan is best known for his "not-JUST-for-kids" music, which has earned critical acclaim and numerous awards, including recognition from NPR, People magazine, and Nickelodeon Parents' Picks. His family-friendly albums, such as *Loquat Rooftop* and *Trippin' Round the Mitten*, feature a mix of original songs, reimagined classics, and humorous storytelling. Kaplan has also collaborated with bluegrass musician Mike West and contributed to compilations like Putumayo's *American Playground*. In addition to his music career, Kaplan has published poetry and co-created the musical comedy *Cleave*. He currently resides in a Detroit suburb with his wife and son.

trgt: Bellevue Plantation was the U.S. home of Catherine Willis Gray Murat, located in Tallahassee, Florida. It was purchased in 1854 after Catherine's second husband Prince Achille Murat (son of Joachim Murat, Napoleon's brother-in-law and King of Naples from 1808 to 1815) died in 1847. Murat was the great-grandniece of George Washington. She was a daughter of Colonel Byrd C. Willis (August 29, 1781 – 1846) and his wife Mary Lewis. Mary Lewis was the granddaughter of Fielding Lewis, George Washington's brother-in-law. Through the Lewis family, she was also a relative of explorer Meriwether Lewis.
mgt: Bellevue Plantation is a historic plantation located in Leon County, Florida, near Tallahassee. Named after the Hotel de Belle-Vue in Brussels, the plantation was home to Catherine Murat, a prominent figure involved in the preservation of George Washington's Mount Vernon through the Mount Vernon Ladies' Association. The plantation house, now part of the Tallahassee Museum, has been restored with period furnishings and includes reconstructed buildings such as a plantation kitchen and a slave cabin. Bellevue was added to the National Register of Historic Places on March 11, 1971, and serves as an educational site on plantation life.

trgt: The Open Siddur Project (Lang-he, IPA: pʁojeqt hassidduʁ hapatuaħ) is an open-source, web-to-print publishing and digital humanities project intent on sharing the semantic data of Jewish liturgy and liturgy-related work with free-culture compatible copyright licenses and Public Domain dedications. The project collaborates with other efforts in open-source Judaism in sharing content and code, advocates among related user-generated content projects to adopt Open Content licensing, and solicits copyright owners of related liturgical materials to share their work under free-culture compatible terms.
mgt: The Open Siddur Project is a digital humanities initiative founded by Aharon Varady in 2009, with Efraim Feinstein as lead developer. It aims to provide open-source tools and content for creating and sharing Jewish liturgy and related works, emphasizing inclusivity, pluralism, and free-culture principles. The project collects and digitizes historical and contemporary liturgical texts, offering them under open licenses.
